We find that emission-line gas is counter-rotating with respect to stellar component and that the [ ] 5007 line is blueshifted compared to other emission lines, possibly due to an outflow.

gmw_00009316.name: JADES+53.09731-27.84714
gmw_00009316.overview: JADES+53.09731-27.84714 is a faint galaxy candidate with a magnitude of 30.5 redward of Lyman-alpha [arXiv:2312.10033]. Photometric analysis favors a high-redshift solution of z=11.5 over a low-redshift alternative [arXiv:2312.10033].

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES+53.09731-27.84714 as a very faint z=11.5 candidate whose high-redshift SED fit is favored over a low-redshift solution constrained by F115W.

quotes.quote: JADES+53.09731-27.84714; NIRCam ID 74977 Figure fig:sed_74977 shows the best-fit SED for object JADES+53.09731-27.84714 (NIRCam ID 74977). The object is remarkably faint with m_ AB 30.5 redward of Lyman- , and has a best-fit redshift of z_a=11.5 . The best low-redshift solution has z_ low =2.7 , but exceeds substantially the observed F115W constraint.

gmw_00009317.name: JADES+53.02618-27.88716
gmw_00009317.overview: JADES+53.02618-27.88716 is a faint high-redshift galaxy candidate with a best-fit photometric redshift of approximately z=11.6 [arXiv:2312.10033]. The galaxy has a magnitude of 30.2 to 30.5 in the NIRCam long-wavelength channels, and its best-fit template shows Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2312.10033].

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES+53.02618-27.88716 as a faint z=11.6 candidate with long-wavelength NIRCam fluxes near 30 AB mag and a BAGPIPES check still supporting z>11.

quotes.quote: JADES+53.02618-27.88716; NIRCam ID 16699 Figure fig:sed_16699 shows the best-fit SED for object JADES+53.02618-27.88716 NIRCam ID 16699, hainline23 . The best-fit redshift is z_a=11.6 for this faint source, which has m_ AB 30.2-30.5 in the NIRCam long-wavelength channels. The best-fit template shows Lyman emission. The best low-redshift solution has z_ low =2.6 . We note that using the BAGPIPES SED-fitting code carnall18 to constrain the photometric redshift of this galaxy candidate provides a slightly lower redshift of z 11.3 , without Lyman- emission and with still substantial P(z>11) .

gmw_00009318.name: JADES+53.04017-27.87603
gmw_00009318.overview: JADES+53.04017-27.87603 is a faint galaxy candidate with a measured brightness of 30.2 magnitude in NIRCam long-wavelength filters [arXiv:2312.10033]. Its best-fit spectral energy distribution model indicates a high-redshift solution of z=12.1, while its best low-redshift alternative is at z=3.2 [arXiv:2312.10033].

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES+53.04017-27.87603 as a remarkably faint z=12.1 candidate whose best low-redshift solution is at z=3.2.

quotes.quote: JADES+53.04017-27.87603; NIRCam ID 33309 Figure fig:sed_33309 shows the best-fit SED for object JADES+53.04017-27.87603 NIRCam ID 33309, hainline23 . The best-fit SED model has z_a=12.1 , while the best low-redshift solution has z_ low =3.2 . The source is also remarkably faint, with m_ AB 30.2 in the NIRCam long-wavelength filters.

gmw_00009319.name: JADES+53.03547-27.90037
gmw_00009319.overview: JADES+53.03547-27.90037 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a redshift constrained to z = 12.4 [arXiv:2312.10033]. Photometry of the galaxy reveals flux densities of approximately 3.5 nJy and a strong break in the F150W band [arXiv:2312.10033].

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES+53.03547-27.90037 as a z=12.4 candidate, with about 3.5 nJy flux densities and photometry whose F150W break constrains the redshift.

quotes.quote: JADES+53.03547-27.90037; NIRCam ID 160071 Figure fig:sed_160071 shows the best-fit SED for object JADES+53.03547-27.90037 (NIRcam ID 160071). The flux densities of this object are f_ 3.5 nJy ( m_ AB 30 ). When fitting an SED model, the observed photometry, including the strong break in F150W, constrain the redshift to be z_a = 12.4 . The best-fit SED has Lyman line emission. The best solution at low redshift has z_ low = 3.4 .

gmw_00009320.name: JADES+53.02868-27.89301
gmw_00009320.overview: JADES+53.02868-27.89301 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a best-fit redshift of z=13.5 [arXiv:2312.10033]. It exhibits redward flux densities of 4 to 6 nJy, though certain color features and a marginal F162M detection suggest it could alternatively be a low-redshift contaminant [arXiv:2312.10033].

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES+53.02868-27.89301 as a z=13.5 candidate with 4-6 nJy redward fluxes, while noting F162M and color features that could indicate contamination.

quotes.quote: JADES+53.02868-27.89301; NIRCam ID 11457 Figure fig:sed_11457 shows the best-fit SED for object JADES+53.02868-27.89301 NIRCam ID 11457, hainline23 . The object has NIRCam flux densities redward of the break of f_ 4-6 nJy ( m_ AB 29.5-29.9 ), that constrain SED models to yield a best-fit redshift z_a=13.5 . The best-fit low-redshift solution SED at z_ low =3.6 exceeds the F090W and F150W constraints by several standard deviations. The second peak in the high-redshift p(z) at z 12.7 is driven by the marginal ( 2.3 ) detection in F162M, which if real would prefer a slightly lower redshift than the mode but still within our selection criteria. However, we caution that the F162M detection, the F182M-F210M color, and the rising SED shape longward of 3.5 m could indicate a potential low-redshift contaminant not well-modeled by our SED template set.

gmw_00009321.name: JADES+53.10762-27.86013
gmw_00009321.overview: JADES+53.10762-27.86013 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a best-fit redshift of z ~ 14.6 [arXiv:2312.10033]. Photometric observations of the candidate show NIRCam long-wavelength fluxes of 29.9 AB magnitude [arXiv:2312.10033].

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES+53.10762-27.86013 as a z=14.6 candidate with 29.9 AB long-wavelength fluxes, while noting template-induced Lyman emission and a weak F162M caveat.

quotes.quote: JADES+53.10762-27.86013; NIRCam ID 55733 Figure fig:sed_55733 shows the best-fit SED for object JADES+53.10762-27.86013 NIRCam ID 55733, hainline23 . The galaxy candidate has NIRCam long-wavelength fluxes of m_ AB 29.9 and a best-fit redshift of z_a 14.6 . The best low-redshift solution has z_ low =3.9 with 2 of the EAZY probability, although the corresponding SED model would substantially exceed the observed F150W. We comment that while the best-fit SED shows Lyman- emission, this feature is induced by the fitting process that mixes different SED templates with and without a Lyman- emission line and does not represent a physical prediction for, e.g., the line equivalent width. We note that this object shows F162M flux at 1.1 significance, and confirmation of this hint of a signal would negate a possible high-redshift solution.

gmw_00009322.name: D646-11
gmw_00009322.overview: D646-11 is a low surface brightness galaxy that displays scattered H-alpha emission [arXiv:1306.4278]. Its selected H II regions are not centrally concentrated, with the brightest shell-like region located in the outer disk [arXiv:1306.4278].

sources.summary: The paper describes D646-11 as an LSB galaxy with scattered H emission, noncentral HII regions, an outer-disk shell-like brightest region, and core filaments not selected as HII regions.

quotes.quote: Galaxy D646-11 (lower left) displays more scattered H emission. The selected HII regions are not centrally concentrated. In fact, the brightest region (more of a shell or bubble than a star complex) is located in the outer disk. There are several filaments and diffuse H regions in the core that were not selected as HII regions. The brighter HII regions are associated with bluer continuum colors, but this is not always the case for LSB galaxies as a whole (Pildis, Schombert Eder 1997).

gmw_00009323.name: WERLS 72964
gmw_00009323.overview: WERLS 72964 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:2309.06656]. It has a spectroscopic redshift of z=7.8881 and exhibits a photometric excess in IRAC Channel 2, which is likely caused by nebular emission contamination [arXiv:2309.06656].

sources.summary: The paper places WERLS 72964 in its secure LAE sample, reporting a z=7.8881 emission line, consistent CANDELS photometric redshift, IRAC detections, and possible nebular excess.

quotes.quote: WERLS 72964 is on mask wmmc06 with a SNR = 4.0 \ emission line corresponding to = 7.8881, and is in our secure sample. It lies within the COSMOS CANDELS coverage, is not in the COSMOS2020 catalog, and lies outside of PRIMER-COSMOS. We fit the target's CANDELS photometry (ID: 72964) and find a photometric redshift consistent with the spectroscopic solution within uncertainty, with =8.5^ +0.2 _ 0.7 . Further, the source is detected significantly in IRAC Channel 1 (3.6 ) and Channel 2 (4.5 ), with some photometric excess (0.33 mag) in Channel 2 where there should be contamination from + nebular emission at both the spectroscopic and photometric redshift solutions. Co-author vetting resulted in a score of 3.4/4 (a maximum score of 4 reflects the scenario wherein all 22 co-authors voted the source as secure).

gmw_00009324.name: WERLS 32350
gmw_00009324.overview: WERLS 32350 is a multi-component galaxy at a redshift of approximately 7.55 [arXiv:2301.07072, arXiv:2309.06656]. It exhibits strong rest-frame optical emission lines [arXiv:2301.07072], low gas-phase metallicity [arXiv:2301.07072], and a photometric excess indicating nebular emission [arXiv:2309.06656].

sources.summary: The paper describes CEERS-689 as a multi-component z=7.545 galaxy near CEERS-698, with strong optical lines, high ionization, low metallicity, and no detected Ly emission.

quotes.quote: This is a multi-component galaxy with integrated H-band magnitude ( H_ 160 =24.9 ). The NIRSpec micro-shutter covers one of the clumps with H_ 160 =26.2 . The strong rest-frame optical lines are detected ( [ O II ] , [ Ne III ] , H , H , [ O III ] ) indicating a systemic redshift of z_ sys =7.545 . This places this galaxy 3.1 pMpc away from CEERS-698.

The ionization-sensitive rest-frame optical line ratios (O32 =10.13 and Ne3O2 =0.71 ) point to a large ionization parameter, which is also recovered in the photoionization model fits ( U =-2.02 ). The gas-phase metallicity implied by the photoionization model fits is low ( 12+ [ O/H ] =7.84 ), consistent with the direct method value suggested by the tentative detection of [ O III ] 4363 (see Table tab:photoionization ). In spite of the intense ionizing nature of this source, we detect no Ly with NIRSpec, suggesting relatively weak Ly (EW <26.1 ) and correspondingly low transmission of Ly through the micro-shutter ( <0.055 ), with both corresponding to 3 limits.

sources.summary: The paper treats WERLS 32350 as a secure LAE with SNR=5.6 at z=7.5501, consistent CANDELS photometry, and Bagpipes evidence for nebular-emission excess.

quotes.quote: WERLS 32350 is on mask wmme01 with a SNR = 5.6 \ emission line corresponding to =7.5501 and is in our secure sample. It is selected from EGS CANDELS (ID: 32350) and is not within the CEERS JWST /NIRCam coverage. We fit the CANDELS photometry and find a photometric redshift of = 8.3^ +0.2 _ 1.3 , consistent within uncertainty with our derived = 7.5501. The Bagpipes SED fit suggests excess from + \ nebular emission, which is supported by the strong photometric excess in IRAC Channel 2 (4.5 ) of 0.91 mag.

gmw_00009325.name: WERLS 786362
gmw_00009325.overview: WERLS 786362 is a compact source with a tentative spectroscopic redshift of z=7.6810 [arXiv:2309.06656]. Its photometry and compact morphology are consistent with both a high-redshift galaxy and a brown dwarf [arXiv:2309.06656].

sources.summary: The paper presents WERLS 786362 as a tentative z=7.6810 LAE whose JWST/HST photometry fits the spectroscopic solution but is ambiguous with a brown-dwarf interpretation.

quotes.quote: WERLS 786362 is on mask wmmc05 and has a SNR = 4.8 \ emission line corresponding to =7.6810. It lies within the COSMOS CANDELS coverage and within PRIMER-COSMOS. This source was imaged by both NIRCam and MIRI with PRIMER, and has a faint detection in MIRI/F770W. We fit the target's JWST + HST photometry (CANDELS ID: 786362) and find a best-fit SED with = 7.41^ +0.09 _ 0.12 , consistent with the spectroscopic solution. Co-author vetting resulted in a score of 3.6/4.

The photometry for WERLS 786362 demonstrates a particularly blue slope at short wavelengths in addition to a red slope at long wavelengths (specifically, it is very blue in F115W F200W and red in F277W F444W), which could be indicative of a brown dwarf e.g. 2023hainline,2023burgasser,2023langeroodi . Its compact morphology, akin to the ``little red dots'' discovered recently in JWST images, is consistent with both a high redshift galaxy or a brown dwarf. We fit the photometry to a generated Bagpipes galaxy model with a very blue component with boosted \ and a red dusty component with boosted , as well as a brown dwarf with T=1000 K and log g=3.0 from the Sonora models 2021marley , and find that both models plausibly match the data.

gmw_00009326.name: WERLS 29712
gmw_00009326.overview: WERLS 29712 is a high-redshift candidate galaxy located in the UDS field [arXiv:2309.06656]. It has a candidate Lyman-alpha emission line at a spectroscopic redshift of z=7.1353, although its faint 2D spectral signal places it in a tentative sample [arXiv:2309.06656].

sources.summary: The paper places WERLS 29712 in the tentative sample, reporting a candidate z=7.1353 line, UDS photometry with a low-redshift PDF component, and faint 2D spectral signal.

quotes.quote: WERLS 29712 has a candidate \ line at SNR = 4.1 with = 7.1353. It is on mask wmmu01 in the UDS field. We fit the target's UDS CANDELS (ID: 29712) and PRIMER-UDS photometry and find = 6.8^ +0.1 _ 5.5 , with a small peak in the photometric redshift PDF at z 1-2 , and 9 of the total PDF at z<3 . Co-author vetting resulted in a score of 2.9/4. This lower redshift solution is inconsistent with the spectroscopic solution; given this discrepancy along with the faint signal in the 2D spectrum, we place it in the tentative sample.

gmw_00009327.name: WERLS 6931
gmw_00009327.overview: WERLS 6931 is a tentative Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy candidate located in the UDS field [arXiv:2309.06656]. It has a candidate emission line corresponding to a redshift of z = 8.1993, though photometric redshift fitting also indicates a substantial probability of a lower redshift [arXiv:2309.06656].

sources.summary: The paper treats WERLS 6931 as a tentative z=8.1993 LAE candidate, noting SNR=6.0, UDS photometry with substantial low-redshift probability, and limited high-redshift PDF support.

quotes.quote: WERLS 6931 has a candidate \ line with SNR = 6.0, corresponding to = 8.1993. It is on mask wmmu01 in the UDS field. We fit the target's CANDELS (ID: 6931) and PRIMER-UDS photometry and find = 6.8^ +0.8 _ 5.3 , with a peak in the photometric redshift PDF at z 1-2 , and 24 of the total PDF at z<3 . Co-author vetting resulted in a score of 2.7/4. While the \ solution falls within the broader high redshift peak of the photometric redshift PDF, given that only 35 of the redshift PDF is at z>7 , we place it in the tentative sample.

gmw_00009328.name: WERLS 9030
gmw_00009328.overview: WERLS 9030 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy candidate [arXiv:2309.06656]. Spectroscopic observations suggest a redshift of z = 7.6862, which is consistent with its photometric redshift solutions within errors [arXiv:2309.06656].

sources.summary: The paper describes WERLS 9030 as a tentative moderate-SNR z=7.6862 LAE candidate with PRIMER imaging, photometric consistency within errors, and a competing low-redshift PDF peak.

quotes.quote: WERLS 9030 is on mask wmmc02 and has a moderate \ emission line candidate detection at SNR = 4.4 corresponding to = 7.6862. It lies within the COSMOS CANDELS and PRIMER-COSMOS coverage, and is not in the COSMOS2020 catalog. This source was imaged by both NIRCam and MIRI with PRIMER, and has a faint detection in MIRI/F770W. We fit the target's CANDELS (ID: 9030) and PRIMER photometry and find a photometric redshift solution of = 7.0^ +0.4 _ 5.6 , consistent with the spectroscopic solution within errors, with a smaller peak in the photometric redshift PDF at z 1-2 , and 22 of the total PDF at z<3 . This lower redshift solution is inconsistent with the spectroscopic solution; fixing the SED to the \ returns a solution wherein the F814W flux is partially contaminated by \ emission, which is consistent with its MOSFIRE spectrum.

gmw_00009329.name: WERLS 20710
gmw_00009329.overview: WERLS 20710 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate located within the COSMOS CANDELS and PRIMER-COSMOS coverage fields [arXiv:2309.06656]. It has a moderately detected emission line corresponding to a spectroscopic redshift of 7.5295, although its photometric redshift is somewhat inconsistent with this spectroscopic solution [arXiv:2309.06656].

sources.summary: The paper classifies WERLS 20710 as tentative, with a moderately detected z=7.5295 emission-line candidate, COSMOS/PRIMER coverage, and photometric redshift somewhat inconsistent with spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: WERLS 20710 is on mask wmmc02 and has a moderately detected (SNR = 5.2) \ emission line candidate corresponding to = 7.5295. It lies within the COSMOS CANDELS coverage and within PRIMER-COSMOS. Co-author vetting resulted in a score of 2.7/4. We fit the target's JWST + HST photometry (CANDELS ID: 20710) and find a photometric redshift of = 6.5^ +0.08 _ 4.8 , somewhat inconsistent with the spectroscopic solution; given this we place this source in our tentative sample.

gmw_00009330.name: WERLS 13312
gmw_00009330.overview: WERLS 13312 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with a tentative spectroscopic redshift of z=8.1962 [arXiv:2309.06656]. It is classified as a Lyman-alpha emitter candidate, though its spectral energy distribution remains poorly constrained due to sparse photometry [arXiv:2309.06656].

sources.summary: The paper classifies WERLS 13312 as a tentative z=8.1962 LAE candidate whose CANDELS SED matches the spectroscopic solution but is poorly constrained by sparse photometry.

quotes.quote: WERLS 13312 is on mask wmmc02 and has a moderately detected (SNR = 5.4) \ emission line candidate corresponding to = 8.1962. It lies within the COSMOS CANDELS coverage but not in PRIMER-COSMOS or COSMOS-Web, and is not in the COSMOS2020 catalog. We fit the target's CANDELS photometry (ID: 13312) and find a best-fit SED with = 8.1^ +0.1 _ 1.5 , consistent with the spectroscopic solution. However, the SED appears poorly constrained as the target is undetected in Y band and bluer, and is not detected in IRAC. Co-author vetting resulted in a score of 3.2/4.

gmw_00009331.name: WERLS 450980
gmw_00009331.overview: WERLS 450980 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate selected from the COSMOS2020 catalog [arXiv:2309.06656]. It is tentatively identified as a Lyman-alpha emitter at a spectroscopic redshift of z=7.0925, which is well-matched by its photometric redshift [arXiv:2309.06656].

sources.summary: The paper treats WERLS 450980 as a tentative z=7.0925 LAE candidate, arguing Ly-alpha is more likely than an [O II] doublet and noting matching COSMOS2020 photometry.

quotes.quote: WERLS 450980 is selected from the COSMOS2020 catalog, is on mask wmmc01, and is in our tentative sample. It has candidate \ emission detected at SNR = 6.7, giving = 7.0925. There is a much fainter positive signal with SNR = 3.9 just blueward of the candidate emission line; this could indicate the signal is the \ doublet rather than . Assuming the brighter emission line candidate is \ with rest frame wavelength 3729 , that would place the source at z=1.639 , and we would expect to find the rest-frame 3727 \ emission line 6 \ blueward.

Here, the fainter signal is nearly twice that separation, at 11 \ from our candidate emission line. Therefore, we find the \ line identification to be more likely. Using the COSMOS2020 photometry (ID: 450980), we find the photometric redshift to be well-matched to the spectroscopic solution, with = 7.3^ +0.1 _ 0.8 . Co-author vetting resulted in a score of 2.7/4.

gmw_00009332.name: WERLS 29881
gmw_00009332.overview: WERLS 29881 is a tentative Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located in the EGS field [arXiv:2309.06656]. It represents a high-redshift candidate at a spectroscopic redshift of z=8.3868, showing possible contamination from nebular emission in its infrared photometry [arXiv:2309.06656].

sources.summary: The paper describes WERLS 29881 as its highest-redshift tentative LAE, with z=8.3868, inconsistent best-fit photometry partly reconciled by Ly-alpha contamination, and possible nebular excess.

quotes.quote: WERLS 29881 is in our tentative sample and is our highest redshift LAE, detected at SNR = 4.6 with = 8.3868. It is on mask wmme01 in the EGS field, but lies outside of the CEERS coverage. We fit the target's CANDELS photometry (ID: 29881) and find a best-fit SED with = 6.4^ +0.4 _ 4.9 , with a smaller peak in the photometric redshift PDF at z 1 , and 10 of the total PDF at z<3 . This is inconsistent with the spectroscopic solution; fixing the SED to the \ returns a solution wherein the HST /F814W flux is partially contaminated by \ emission, which is consistent with its MOSFIRE spectrum.

Further, while the source is undetected in IRAC Channel 1 (3.6 ), it is detected in IRAC Channel 2 (4.5 ), where there should be contamination from + \ nebular emission at the spectroscopic redshift. Co-author vetting resulted in a score of 3.3/4. While co-authors ranked this detection highly, given the line detection is at the edge of the MOSFIRE wavelength coverage, it is statistically more likely to be noise, and we place it in the tentative sample.

gmw_00009333.name: WERLS 40898
gmw_00009333.overview: WERLS 40898 is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 7.82 [arXiv:2301.07072, arXiv:2309.06656]. It hosts a young stellar population with low metallicity and exhibits extreme gas ionization conditions alongside strong Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2301.07072, arXiv:2309.06656].

sources.summary: The paper describes CEERS-1027 as a young, metal-poor z=7.819 galaxy with extreme ionization, strong Ly, UV metal lines, and a relatively low Ly escape fraction.

quotes.quote: This moderately bright ( H=26.5 ) galaxy is covered in the NIRCam footprint, with an SED that indicates a young stellar population ( 3.4 Myr), efficient ionizing photon production ( [ _ ion / erg ^ 1 \ Hz ] =25.8 ), and very strong nebular emission ( [ O III ] +H EW =3276 ). The NIRSpec spectrum reveals numerous emission lines, confirming the systemic redshift ( z_ sys =7.819 ) and placing this galaxy 5.2 pMpc from EGS-zs8-1. The line ratios of CEERS-1027 are the most extreme in the sample, with both O32 ( >30.22 ) and Ne3O2 ( >1.80 ) pointing to gas with very extreme ionization conditions. The BEAGLE models reproduce the lines with very low metallicity ( 12+ [ O/H ] =7.58 ) and large ionization parameter ( U =-1.31 ).

Tentative detection of [ O III ] 4363 reveals a similarly low metallicity ( 12+ [ O/H ] =7.74 ) using the direct method. The UV spectrum reveals reasonably strong Ly (EW =20.4 ) and lower S/N detections of C IV (EW =5.7 ), He II (EW =3.9 ), and C III ] (EW =12.9 ). As discussed in Section sec:uv_lines , the UV line ratios are consistent with powering by low metallicity stars.The Ly emerges with a velocity offset of 323 18 km s ^ 1 with respect to the systemic redshift (Fig. fig:lya_offset ). In spite of the large Ly EW, we infer a relatively low escape fraction for Ly ( 0.085 0.018 ). suggesting the vast majority of the line emission is not making its way through the NIRSpec micro-shutter.

sources.summary: The paper says WERLS 40898 lacked a WERLS MOSFIRE detection, but CEERS/NIRSpec secured z=7.820 with multiple optical lines and showed strong Ly-alpha emission hidden by sky-line contamination.

quotes.quote: MOSFIRE Primary target WERLS 40898 is on mask wmme03 in the EGS field and had no \ detection in the WERLS MOSFIRE data. It was observed by CEERS with JWST /NIRSpec (MSA ID: 1027) with both PRISM mode and with medium resolution in the G140M, G235M, and G395M filters. As reported in 2023arrabalharo , the CEERS reduction secures the redshift of the source via multiple strong, rest-frame optical emission lines (including + ), with = 7.820 ^ +0.001 _ 0.001 see also 2022heintz,2023sanders . The medium resolution spectrum also shows strong \ emission at 10732.1 , and 2023tang find a fairly large \ velocity offset from the systemic redshift of 323 km s ^ 1 .

Based on the strength of the NIRSpec detection, we expect \ to be detectable by WERLS for this source. However, the spectrum is contaminated by a sky line at the observed \ wavelength. Additionally, mask wmme03 was less than half complete, with only 8 ABBA sequences taken (out of our goal of 20+ sequences or about 4 hours of total exposure time).

gmw_00009334.name: WERLS 35089
gmw_00009334.overview: WERLS 35089 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy located in the EGS field [arXiv:2309.06656]. Observations securely identify the galaxy at a spectroscopic redshift of z=6.959, where its Lyman-alpha emission line falls into a detector gap [arXiv:2309.06656].

sources.summary: The paper says WERLS 35089 had no WERLS Ly-alpha detection, but CEERS/NIRSpec PRISM data securely identify it at z=6.959, with Ly-alpha falling in the detector gap.

quotes.quote: WERLS 35089 is a MOSFIRE Primary target on mask wmme01 in the EGS field and had no \ detection in the WERLS MOSFIRE data, corroborated by the MOSFIRE Y band non-detection in 2022jung . It was observed by CEERS with JWST /NIRSpec (MSA ID: 716) with PRISM mode only. The CEERS reduction shows a single bright emission line, which given photometric redshift priors can be identified securely as , with = 6.959 (Arrabal Haro et al. in prep); the redshift is also consistent with \ falling in the detector gap for these observations. While there is no detectable \ emission line in the spectrum, the \ break is detected, which anchors the spectroscopic redshift solution.

gmw_00009335.name: SDSS1151+4711
gmw_00009335.overview: SDSS1151+4711 is a disturbed galaxy merger that exhibits a double-nucleated infrared structure [arXiv:1501.00325]. The system is best fit by a disk component plus a point source, displaying extended low surface brightness emission and broad oxygen emission line wings [arXiv:1501.00325].

sources.summary: The paper presents SDSS1151+4711 as a disturbed double-nucleated merger with extended emission, disk-plus-point-source structure, compact OIII components, and broad line wings.

quotes.quote: The three objects with double-nucleated IR structure (SDSS0952+2552, SDSS1151+4711, SDSS1502+1115) show clear merger features.

SDSS0952+2552 and SDSS1151+4711 both show extended low surface brightness emission on scales 10 kpc. SDSS1502+1115 on the other hand, while clearly disturbed and showing several distinct knots of emission in is compact and shows only weak tidal features. Note that this source shows the largest separation between the nuclei, indicating an early merger stage. However, it should be noted that these three sources were chosen to have double-nuclei, clear merger features are therefore expected. The X-ray binary AGN SDSS1715+6008 stands out since it shows no signs of merging or disturbance down to very low surface brightnesses ( 27 mag/arcsec ^ 2 in ). The structures seen in the outskirts of the galaxy are consistent with background sources. Both SDSS0952+2552 and SDSS1151+4711 are best fit by a disk component plus point source, though with considerable residuals due to the disturbed nature.

In SDSS1151+4711 (Fig. F:twod_1151 ), two very compact OIII components are detected, one associated with the Type 1 AGN, the other at the location of the suspected secondary Type 2 AGN. The two components are not connected and appear as distinct clumps. Kinematically, the two OIII components are not separated (Fig. F:kinematics ). The OIII profiles shows a marginally separated component in the blue wing of the line. The general OIII profile is broad, with substantial red wings (out to 1000 km s ^ 1 in the central region), and even broader blue wings ( > 1500 km s ^ 1 ).

gmw_00009336.name: 2MASS J10065085+0141342
gmw_00009336.overview: 2MASS J10065085+0141342 is a quiescent post-starburst or E+A galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.04573 [arXiv:2003.05470, arXiv:2003.09427]. Its archival spectrum displays strong Balmer absorption lines and lacks common emission lines, indicating an early-type galaxy with very little UV light contribution [arXiv:2003.05470, arXiv:2003.09427].

sources.summary: The host SDSS J100650.86+014134.0 is described as a quiescent post-starburst or E+A galaxy with strong Balmer absorption and redshift-derived absolute magnitude.

quotes.quote: The transient was detected at = 10:06:50.872, = +01:41:34.10, placing it 0.2 0.8 " gomez from the centre of SDSS J100650.86+014134.0 , an apparently quiescent or post-starburst galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.04573 . Assuming a flat CDM cosmology with H_ 0 = 69.3 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ m = 0.286 , and _ = 0.712 Hinshaw13 , the redshift of the host galaxy suggests an absolute g band magnitude of 20.2 mag.

Archival host galaxy observations An archival spectrum of the host galaxy was available from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS; sdss ). The spectrum is displayed in Figure fig:hostsub . The lack of common emission lines such as H and [ O II ] , combined with strong Balmer absorptions, indicate that this is a quiescent, Balmer-strong galaxy, otherwise known as a post-starburst or E+A galaxy (i.e. an elliptical galaxy showing absorption lines from A-type star atmospheres). TDEs are over-represented in such galaxies by a factor of 30-35 arcavi14,french16,graur18 , possibly because these galaxies have higher central stellar densities than the background population graur18,lawsmith17 . A stellar population synthesis model is fit to archival host photometry in gomez .

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASS J10065085+0141342 as the inactive host of AT 2018hyz, with an E+A/post-starburst SDSS spectrum, low UV contribution, and systemic emission features.

quotes.quote: The transient aligns with the nucleus of the galaxy 2MASS J10065085+0141342 at z=0.0457 that is abscent of pre-flare nuclear activity. An archival Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) spectrum of the host galaxy displays strong Balmer absorption lines that are characteristic of an E+A galaxy, a rare subtype of post-starburst galaxies in which TDEs are preferentially discovered Arcavi2014,2016ApJ...818L..21F,2017ApJ...850...22L,2018ApJ...853...39G .

Given that the host galaxy is of an early type that contributes very little of the observed UV light ( u>19.9 mag), we did not attempt host galaxy subtraction for the \ light curves presented in fig:lightcurve .

As can be seen from fig:opt_spectra , the only features in the residual spectrum is a narrow \ emission ( 920 60 ) at a systemic velocity of the host galaxy and a weak N 3 + He 2 complex (red dashed line in fig:fwhm ).

gmw_00009337.name: UDG 317
gmw_00009337.overview: UDG 317 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Coma cluster [arXiv:2004.11388]. It features an offset X-ray source with a coincident optical counterpart [arXiv:2102.11285, arXiv:2004.11388], which may represent an off-center active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2102.11285, arXiv:2004.11388].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UDG 317 as one of three Coma UDGs with a nearby X-ray source and finds an optical counterpart, making an off-centre AGN interpretation more likely but uncertain.

quotes.quote: We detect three X-ray sources with detection significance 5 that could be off-centre AGN within 5 arcsec from the centre of the UDG 317, UDG 432, and UDG 535. We identify an optical counterpart for the X-ray source associated with the UDG 317, suggesting that this source is more likely an off-centre AGN.

It is possible that the X-ray sources associated with the UDG 317, UDG 432, and UDG 535 are not AGN in these galaxies, but are foreground or background objects. Therefore, their existence within a radius of 5 arcsec from the centre of the UDGs is due to random matches.

We identified an optical source within a radius of 5 arcsec from the UDG 317 centre, matching the position of the X-ray source. However, no optical counterparts were detected for the X-ray sources associated with the UDG 432 and UDG 535. Also, we checked images of these UDGs in FIR and other wave bands, but we did not find any significant sources in the field of these galaxies. Using the Subaru images, kovacs2020ultra also identified an optical counterpart for the X-ray source associated with the UDG 317. The detection of an optical counterpart implies that the X-ray source associated with the UDG 317 is an off-centre AGN.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UDG 317 as a Coma ultra-diffuse galaxy with an offset X-ray source and a coincident optical source that may be an off-center AGN.

quotes.quote: Using the large, 5 , search radius, we identified two galaxies, UDG 317 ( r_ eff =2.7 ) and UDG 412 ( r_ eff =1.8 ), which match with a detected X-ray source. The Chandra images of these two galaxies and the associated X-ray point sources are shown in Figure  fig:6 . We note that the relatively extended nature of the point sources is due to the 4 size of the weighted point spread function at the location of both X-ray sources. The offset between the galaxy centroid and the X-ray source positions is 3.0 and 3.2 for UDG 317 and UDG 412, respectively.

Interestingly, we identify an optical source that is coincident with the X-ray source identified in UDG 317. The presence of the optical counterpart suggests that this source is an AGN. Indeed, if the X-ray source was an X-ray binary or a ULX, the donor star would be too faint to be detected at the distance of the Coma cluster. However, it is still possible that the AGN is not residing in the galaxy, but it is a foreground or, more likely, a background object. To conclusively determine whether this source is truly an off-center AGN associated with UDG 317, the redshift of the UDG and the optical source should be measured.

gmw_00009338.name: UDG 412
gmw_00009338.overview: UDG 412 is a passive ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Coma cluster [arXiv:2004.11388]. It hosts an off-center, luminous X-ray source that is likely a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2004.11388].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UDG 412 as a passive Coma ultra-diffuse galaxy with an offset luminous X-ray source whose hardness ratio could indicate an AGN.

quotes.quote: Using the large, 5 , search radius, we identified two galaxies, UDG 317 ( r_ eff =2.7 ) and UDG 412 ( r_ eff =1.8 ), which match with a detected X-ray source. The Chandra images of these two galaxies and the associated X-ray point sources are shown in Figure  fig:6 . We note that the relatively extended nature of the point sources is due to the 4 size of the weighted point spread function at the location of both X-ray sources. The offset between the galaxy centroid and the X-ray source positions is 3.0 and 3.2 for UDG 317 and UDG 412, respectively.

The X-ray sources in UDG 317 and UDG 412 Assuming that the X-ray point sources are in the Coma cluster, the observed net count rates of ( 3.7 0.3) 10^ 5 \ s^ 1 and ( 5.5 0.3) 10^ 5 \ s^ 1 correspond to luminosities of L_ 0.5-7keV =(1.3 0.1) 10^ 39 \ erg \ s^ 1 and L_ 0.5-7keV = (2.5 0.1) 10^ 39 \ erg \ s^ 1 for UDG 317 and UDG 412, respectively. If these UDGs reside in the Coma cluster and the X-ray sources are within the galaxies, they are most likely off-center low-luminosity AGN. Indeed, the luminosity of both X-ray sources exceeds 10^ 39 \ erg \ s^ 1 , which is above the cut-off luminosity of low-mass X-ray binaries 2004MNRAS.349..146G . While high-mass X-ray binaries or ultra-luminous X-ray (ULXs) sources may reach luminosities in excess of 10^ 39 \ erg \ s^ 1 , these types of sources are associated with active star formation 2003MNRAS.339..793G,2004MNRAS.347L..18K,2009ApJ...703..159S,2012MNRAS.419.2095M . However, UDG 317 and UDG 412 have color indeces of B-R=0.91 and B-R=0.92 , which suggests that these are passive galaxies without significant star formation 2015ApJ...807L...2K .
We do not detect an optical counterpart of the X-ray source in UDG 412.

gmw_00009339.name: dw0009-25
gmw_00009339.overview: dw0009-25 is a faint dwarf galaxy candidate located in projection close to the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 24 [arXiv:2008.08954]. Due to this proximity, it is tentatively assigned as a satellite of NGC 24 pending follow-up distance measurements [arXiv:2008.08954].

sources.summary: The paper identifies dw0009-25 as a faint dwarf-galaxy candidate near NGC 24, gives possible satellite-scale size and luminosity, and assigns it to NGC 24 pending distances.

quotes.quote: The other dwarf galaxy candidate dw0009-25 was too faint to get detected by MTObjects (see next paragraph).

Both dwarf galaxy candidates dw0009-25 and dw0010-25 are in projection close to the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 24 and also close to each other (see Fig. field ). Their on-sky separation is only 0.11 deg and 0.40 deg from NGC 24, which at its distance ( D=7.3 Mpc) corresponds to 14.5 kpc and 52.5 kpc, respectively. Assuming that the two dwarfs are satellites of NGC 24, the effective radius and absolute magnitude would be r_ eff =283 pc and M_r=-8.7 mag for dw0009-25 and r_ eff =1069 pc and M_r=-11.9 mag for dw0010-25.

The physical properties of the dwarf galaxy candidate then would boil down to M_r=-7.2 mag and r_ eff =144 pc for dw0009-25 and M_r=-10.4 mag and r_ eff =542 pc for dw0010-25. How do these quantities compare to the structural parameters of other known dwarf galaxies? 2020MNRAS.491.1901H studied a population of over 2000 dwarf galaxies in the nearby universe and presented the structural parameters in their Figure 11. Our dwarf galaxy candidates are compatible with these dwarf galaxies at either distance, so no potential membership can be confirmed or excluded. Also the integrated (g-)_0 color of 0.87 mag and 0.55 mag are consistent with the mean color of 0.46 0.26 mag of dwarfs in the Cen A group 2018A&A...615A.105M , being part of the same survey. Due to their proximity we assign dw0009-25 and dw0010-25 to NGC 24 until follow-up distance measurements can confirm their true association.

gmw_00009340.name: dw0010-25
gmw_00009340.overview: dw0010-25 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy candidate located in close projection to the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 24 [arXiv:2008.08954]. It is also situated very close to the interacting background spiral galaxy LEDA 133604 [arXiv:2008.08954].

sources.summary: The paper presents dw0010-25 as a visual dwarf-galaxy candidate near NGC 24, estimates satellite-like properties, but notes a possible background-contamination caveat.

quotes.quote: Our visual search revealed one good dwarf galaxy candidate which we dubbed dw0010-25, and another potential candidate named dw009-25 (see Fig. dwarf ).

Both dwarf galaxy candidates dw0009-25 and dw0010-25 are in projection close to the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 24 and also close to each other (see Fig. field ). Their on-sky separation is only 0.11 deg and 0.40 deg from NGC 24, which at its distance ( D=7.3 Mpc) corresponds to 14.5 kpc and 52.5 kpc, respectively. Assuming that the two dwarfs are satellites of NGC 24, the effective radius and absolute magnitude would be r_ eff =283 pc and M_r=-8.7 mag for dw0009-25 and r_ eff =1069 pc and M_r=-11.9 mag for dw0010-25.

There is one caveat, dw0010-25 being very close to the interacting background spiral LEDA 133604 (see Fig. dwarf ), which is at a redshift of z=0.065 1996ApJS..107..201L , i.e., 290 Mpc away from us. At that distance dw0010-25 would have an absolute magnitude of M_r=-19.8 mag and an effective radius of r_ eff =42 kpc, which seems highly unrealistic.

gmw_00009341.name: Bulb
gmw_00009341.overview: Bulb is a type II active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2005.01732]. The galaxy is surrounded by extended Lyman-alpha and helium II nebulae that stretch toward the southwest, with the helium II emission reaching a radial distance of 50 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2005.01732].

sources.summary: The paper treats Bulb as a type II AGN source with detected Ly and He II nebulae; the He II emission reaches 50 pkpc, and both emissions extend southwest.

quotes.quote: Our sample consists of 4 type II AGNs, of which the Cdfs 04 and Cdfs 15 have been selected using the MUSE--WIDE survey Urrutia2019 , UDF 09 is found in the MUSE--Deep survey Bacon2017 and the Bulb AGN was taken from .

The Bulb shows extended \ emission out to a maximum radial distance of 50 pkpc. The UDF 09 source shows compact emission with only faint extended substructures out to 25--30 pkpc from the center of the AGN.

The extended Ly emission around the four sources shows a variety of shapes and sizes, as can be seen in fig:SBmaps and fig:SBmaps_2 . The Bulb Ly nebula is extended towards the south--west. Also the Bulb's \ emission extends towards the south--west.

gmw_00009342.name: SDSS J1042-0018
gmw_00009342.overview: SDSS J1042-0018 is a broad-line active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2202.04795]. Although it features apparent broad emission lines, its narrow emission line flux ratios cause it to be misclassified as an H II galaxy on the BPT diagram [arXiv:2202.04795].

sources.summary: The paper argues SDSS J1042-0018 is a broad-line AGN misclassified as an HII galaxy in BPT diagrams, with star-forming contributions favored over compressed high-density narrow-line regions.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1042-0018 a broad line AGN but mis-classified as a HII galaxy in the BPT diagram by flux ratios of narrow emission lines Vol.0 (20xx) No.0, 000--000 page 1 Cao Yi 1 Zhao SiDan 1 Zhu XingYu 1 Yu HaiChao 1 Wang YiWei 1 Zhang XueGuang ^ * 1 School of Physics and technology, Nanjing Normal University, No. 1, Wenyuan Road, Nanjing, 210023, P. R. China; blue xgzhang@njnu.edu.cn Received 20xx month day; accepted 20xx month day In the manuscript, we discuss properties of the   which is a broad line AGN but mis-classified as a HII galaxy in the BPT diagram (   called as a mis-classified broad line AGN). The emission lines around H and around H are well described by different model functions, considering broad Balmer lines to be described by Gaussian or Lorentz functions. Different model functions lead to different determined narrow emission line fluxes, but the different narrow emission line flux ratios lead the   as a HII galaxy in the BPT diagram. In order to explain the unique properties of the mis-classified broad line AGN , two methods are proposed, the starforming contributions and the compressed NLRs with high electron densities near to critical densities of forbidden emission lines. Fortunately, the strong starforming contributions can be preferred in the SDSS J1042-0018.

Cao et al. mis-classified broad line AGN   (=SDSS J104210.03-001814.7) is a common broad line AGN with apparent broad emission lines, as the shown spectra in Fig.  spec from SDSS (Sloan Digital Sky Survey). However, based on flux ratios of narrow emission lines well discussed in Section 2,   can be well classified as a HII galaxy in the BPT diagram bpt81, kb01, ka03, kb06, ke13a, kb19, zh20 . Therefore, in the manuscript, some special properties of   is studied and discussed.

gmw_00009343.name: AzTEC15
gmw_00009343.overview: AzTEC15 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:1509.07147] that is associated with Spitzer infrared emission [arXiv:1509.07147]. Its 3 GHz radio major axis FWHM is approximately 10.0 kiloparsecs, which is in good agreement with its ultraviolet and optical extent [arXiv:1509.07147].

sources.summary: The paper argues the 3 GHz source is physically related to AzTEC15 because ALMA gives nearly perfect positional agreement, and its radio size matches the UV/optical extent.

quotes.quote: The diameters were found to be 1.4 0.2 kpc ( 0 18^ +0.02 _ 0.04 ) for AzTEC10, and 10.0 1.6 kpc ( 1 28^ +0.22 _ 0.20 ) for AzTEC15. The 3 GHz major axis FWHM of AzTEC15 ( _ maj =1 21^ +0.29 _ 0.32 ) is in good agreement with its UV/optical extent, while AzTEC10 was not detected at 3 GHz.

Towards AzTEC15 the projected separation between the 890 m and 3 GHz positions is fairly large, i.e. 1 19 . The 890 m detection of AzTEC15 by Younger et al. (2009) was only of moderate significance ( 4.4 ), and no 1.4 GHz counterpart was detected, but it was found to be associated with Spitzer IR emission. The 890 m position uncertainty reported by Younger et al. (2009) is 0 3 in right ascension and 0 2 in declination. The 1 36 0 78 resolution ALMA 1.3 mm observations towards AzTEC15 (M. Aravena et al., in prep.), however, show a perfect positional coincidence ( 0 03 offset) with our 3 GHz source of 5.4 significance, and hence it is physically related to AzTEC15.

gmw_00009344.name: PKS 1055+201
gmw_00009344.overview: PKS 1055+201 is a Fanaroff-Riley class II radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of z=1.11 [arXiv:astro-ph/0607276]. It hosts a narrow X-ray jet aligned with its radio jet, as well as extended, curved regions of residual jet plasma [arXiv:astro-ph/0607276].

sources.summary: The paper reports PKS 1055+201 as a z=1.11 FR II quasar with a radio-aligned X-ray jet, extended residual jet plasma, and curved X-ray regions around the jet.

quotes.quote: This letter reports rich X-ray jet structures found in the \ observation of PKS 1055+201. In addition to an X-ray jet coincident with the radio jet we detect a region of extended X-ray emission surrounding the jet as far from the core as the radio hotspot to the North, and a similar extended X-ray region along the presumed path of the unseen counterjet to the Southern radio lobe. Both X-ray regions show a similar curvature to the west, relative to the quasar. We interpret this as the first example where we separately detect the X-ray emission from a narrow jet and extended, residual jet plasma over the entire length of a powerful FRII jet.

One of the extreme cases discovered in our survey for X-ray emission from radio-bright quasar jets Marshall05a, Gelbord05a is the FR II radio source PKSB1055+201 PKS 1055+201 Shimmins68 . Those authors associated it with the previously discovered 4C20.24 Pilkington65 . Bolton68 identified the object as a quasar at redshift z=1.11.

gmw_00009345.name: HUDF-J2
gmw_00009345.overview: HUDF-J2 is a galaxy candidate whose spectral energy distribution has been interpreted as either a massive post-starburst system at a redshift of approximately 6.5 [arXiv:astro-ph/0509768] or a dust-rich starburst galaxy at a lower redshift of 2.3 to 2.6 [arXiv:astro-ph/0703387]. The high-redshift interpretation is considered unlikely due to the absence of expected bright neighbor galaxies in its vicinity [arXiv:0711.0467].

sources.summary: The paper presents HUDF-JD2 as a remarkable high-redshift galaxy candidate whose SED indicates a massive post-starburst system at z 6.5, with dusty lower-redshift alternatives considered less favored.

quotes.quote: Using observations at optical, near-infrared and mid-infrared wavelengths from the Hubble and Spitzer Space Telescopes and the European Southern Observatory Very Large Telescope, we find one particularly remarkable candidate, which we designate as HUDF-JD2. Its spectral energy distribution has distinctive features that are consistent with those of a galaxy at z 6.5 , observed several hundred million years after a powerful burst of star formation that produced a stellar mass of 6 10^ 11 M_ (for a Salpeter IMF). We interpret a prominent photometric break between the near-infrared and Spitzer bandpasses as the 3646 \ Balmer discontinuity.
Alternative models of dusty galaxies at z 2.5 are possible but provide significantly poorer fits to the photometric data.

We present optical and near-infrared spectroscopy which has, thus far, failed to detect any spectral features. This non-detection helps limit the solution in which the galaxy is a starburst or active galaxy at z 2.5 , but does not rule it out. If the high-redshift interpretation is correct, HUDF-JD2 is an example of a galaxy that formed by a process strongly resembling traditional models of monolithic collapse, in a way which a very large mass of stars formed within a remarkably short period of time, at very high redshift.

sources.summary: The paper reinterprets HUDF-J2 as more likely a dusty z=2.3-2.6 starburst than a z=6.5 post-starburst, with observed 24 micron emission naturally reproduced.

quotes.quote: HUDF-J2 revisited Using the same modeling technique and spectral templates described above, we have examined the optical ( ) drop-out object HUDF-J2 discussed by Mobasher \ (2005) and more recently by Dunlop \ (2006). This object was also selected by Yan \ (2004) on the basis of its red 3.6 / band flux. Their full sample, named ``IRAC'' EROs (IEROs), will be discussed below (Sect. \ s_yan ). Available photometry Here we have used two datasets published for this object.

Adopting the photometry from (ii), an excellent fit ( 2=0.9) is found with the HR10 template and no additional extinction for = 2.31 (blue line in Fig. \ fig_sed_mob ). Interestingly, both fits reproduce quite well the 24 \ flux observed by MIPS/Spitzer (Mobasher \ 2005), which was not included in our fit procedure. This lends further credit to the explanation of HUDF-J2 as a dust rich galaxy at z 2.3 .

sources.summary: The paper argues that if JD2 were at z 6.5, HUDF should contain many bright i-dropout neighbors; their absence rules out that high-redshift interpretation.

quotes.quote: The mean abundance of such galaxies in the absence of JD2 is much less than unity, and indeed, no such objects have been detected in the HUDF Bouwens06,Bouwens07 . Figure Pdist shows that there should be a mean of 10 very bright i-dropout LBGs in hosts with masses larger than within an angular Lagrangian radius of 175'' of JD2, where this is a lower limit due to the fact that LBGs should be more clustered than halos ST03 . The probability, given by equation ( PN ), of detecting no such galaxies in this region is P(N=0 | M_ JD2 ) 3 10^ 4 . Thus, we can rule out JD2 at redshift z 6.5 with 99.7 confidence.

gmw_00009346.name: NGC 4448
gmw_00009346.overview: NGC 4448 is an isolated, regularly rotating galaxy observed at a high inclination [arXiv:2210.09383]. It features a compact neutral hydrogen distribution, with an optical disk that extends beyond its gas disk [arXiv:2210.09383].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4448 as an isolated, regularly rotating, high-inclination galaxy with compact H I and an optical disk extending beyond the gas disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 4448 is an isolated, regularly rotating galaxy seen at a high inclination. The \ distribution in the galaxy appears to be rather compact despite having an average line width (see Table table:hosts ). The optical disk extends beyond the \ disk which gives the impression of the gas in the outer regions being stripped. The galaxy IC 3334 is also detected in the data cube albeit with a significantly higher systemic velocity.

sources.summary: The paper leaves NGC 4448 out of the EPG analysis because disc masking removes nearly all usable anomalous emission in its low-resolution datacube.

quotes.quote: In NGC 4274 and NGC 4448, instead, the masking of the \ disc leaves virtually no EPG emission to work with. These are two of the systems with the fewest resolution elements; their datacubes show very asymmetric \ profiles which - according to our mask - are largely caused by beam-smearing rather than by the presence of kinematically anomalous gas. Our method relies on the modelling of a kinematically distinct component and can not be used here, but we can not exclude that some EPG may actually be present in these systems.

gmw_00009347.name: NGC 0672
gmw_00009347.overview: NGC 0672 is a moderately inclined, late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1012.0816, arXiv:0808.1789]. It is interacting with the neighboring irregular dwarf galaxy IC 1727 [arXiv:1012.0816, arXiv:1909.04048], a process that has created a shared, disturbed neutral hydrogen distribution and a connecting filament [arXiv:1012.0816, arXiv:1909.04048].

sources.summary: HALOGAS presents NGC 672 as a moderately inclined spiral interacting with IC 1727, with shared disturbed H I, continuum detections, interface emission, and unsettled gas likely available for later accretion.

quotes.quote: NGC 672 is a moderately inclined spiral which has a neighboring irregular galaxy, IC 1727. In Figure figure:n0672 , NGC 672 is the brighter galaxy centered in the frames, and IC 1727 is located to its southwest. At the adopted distance listed in Table table:sample (7.6 Mpc), the linear projected separation between the two galaxies is 16.3 kpc. In the optical image, there is no clear sign of a strong disturbance in NGC 672.

In our observations, we detect a total mass of 3.78 10^9 M_ , of which approximately 60 is associated with the primary, NGC 672. Both galaxies are clearly detected in continuum emission. The \ distribution shown in Figure figure:n0672 reveals clearly the connection between the two galaxies. Each of the two galaxies appears to be relatively undisturbed within their optical extent, but the outskirts and interface region are more complicated.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 672 as a late-type member of the NGC 672/IC 1727 pair, with HII regions and two fitted star-formation bursts including a very recent event.

quotes.quote: The NGC 672 and IC 1727 galaxies were both classified as late-type (e.g., Combes et al. \ 1980). NGC 672 was classified Sc ^ + (Holmberg 1958) and later as SBc (de Vaucouleurs et al. \ 1976).

34 HII regions were reported in NGC 672 and 28 in IC 1727 (e.g, Hodge 1982, Hodge Kennicutt 1983). Both NGC 672 and IC 1727 have colours typical of field galaxies. These fitted the models reasonably well ( ^ 2 < 3 ), indicating two SF bursts at similar times for both galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper models interacting NGC 0672 with IC 1727 contamination, blanking part of the approaching side and finding an unreproduced HI filament likely linked to interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 0672. This system is interacting with a companion, IC 1727, an irregular dwarf which strongly contaminates the \ flux at negative (approaching) velocities (see also maps in Appendix app:maps ). The rotation curve of NGC 0672 has been derived using only the receding side of the disc, and a significant portion of the approaching side has been blanked and added to the internal mask (as it appears from the white square regions in the slices along the major axis). An \ filament is visible in the major axis slices at 2' and 1' at about the systemic velocity, possibly caused by the interaction between the two galaxies. The filament is not reproduced by our model, as expected. Note that in this galaxy we have used >0 as an additional prior.

gmw_00009348.name: NGC 4062
gmw_00009348.overview: NGC 4062 is a late-type galaxy characterized by a disk structure [arXiv:1909.04048]. The galaxy features barely visible anomalous gas, and its extraplanar gas scale radius remains unconstrained [arXiv:1909.04048].

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4062 has barely visible anomalous gas and skeptical modeling conditions, though tests suggest recovery remains possible and its EPG scale radius is unconstrained.

quotes.quote: NGC 4062. The anomalous gas is barely visible in pv slices parallel to the major axis, and is virtually absent along the minor axis. Given the limited resolution and number of voxels to fit, the application of our model to this system must be taken with some skepticism. However, tests on mock data have shown that even in this condition our model can recover the correct parameter within a 2 uncertainty, as we discuss in Appendix app:mcmctest .
For three galaxies (NGC 2541, NGC 4062 and NGC 4414) we find a flat posterior in the R_ g parameter, meaning that the EPG scale radius for these systems is unconstrained.

gmw_00009349.name: 4C+55.16
gmw_00009349.overview: 4C+55.16 is a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy located at the center of a luminous, cool-core galaxy cluster [arXiv:1103.2585, arXiv:2308.07306]. The galaxy features a compact core and two diffuse radio lobes that align with X-ray cavities in the surrounding intracluster medium [arXiv:2207.05088, arXiv:2308.07306].

sources.summary: The paper includes 4C+55.16 as a powerful radio galaxy in a luminous cluster, estimates its cluster luminosity from Chandra data, and notes little long-term variation.

quotes.quote: Our sample also includes the powerful radio galaxy, 4C+55.16 ( z=0.2412 ). 4C+55.16 is not part of any of the catalogues, but is embedded in a luminous cluster that has an X-ray luminosity within 200 kpc larger than many of the other clusters in our sample (see Table tab5_2 ). It is therefore reasonable to assume that if it were part of the catalogues, its X-ray luminosity would be larger than 3 10^ 44 , and therefore should be included in our sample.

For 4C+55.16, we compute a rough estimate of the cluster luminosity by fitting mekal model to the 0.5-7 Chandra emission within the central 200 kpc. The Galactic absorption is kept frozen at the Kal2005440 value and the background is taken as a region located on the same detector but far from any cluster emission.
4C+55.16 and PKS0745-19 are two example that show no significant variation on 5-10 year time scales, see Hla2011415 and Sanders et al. in preparation.

sources.summary: The paper presents 4C+55.16 as an X-ray-bright, radio-powerful galaxy whose AGN outbursts, cavities, and iron-rich plume interact with and heat the surrounding cool-core cluster medium.

quotes.quote: We present a detailed X-ray analysis of 4C+55.16, an unusual and interesting radio galaxy, located at the centre of a cool core cluster of galaxies. 4C+55.16 is X-ray bright ( (cluster) 10^ 45 ), radio powerful, and shows clear signs of interaction with the surrounding intracluster medium. By combining deep Chandra (100 ks) with 1.4 GHz VLA observations, we find evidence of multiple outbursts from the central AGN, providing enough energy to offset cooling of the ICM ( P_ bubbles =6.7 10^ 44 ). Furthermore, 4C+55.16 has an unusual intracluster iron distribution showing a plume-like feature rich in Fe L emission that runs along one of the X-ray cavities. The excess of iron associated with the plume is around 10^7M_ .

sources.summary: The paper uses 4C+55.16 as a real LOFAR FRI radio-galaxy test case, describing its compact core, diffuse lobes, flux density, and spectral-index modelling.

quotes.quote: In addition to the simulated observation, we use the FRI radio galaxy 4C+55.16 RA 08^ h 34^ m 54 . ^ s 90 , Dec +55^ 34'21 . ''07 in J2000; pilkington_scott:1965,charlot_etal:2020 , because it has both diffuse and compact emission regions in its lobes and core. We use an 8 h LOFAR-VLBI HBA observation, that have been processed and calibrated by timmerman_etal:2022a . The dataset covers 120--165 MHz, with 16 s and 0.5 MHz of time and frequency resolutions, respectively.

4C+55.16 has a total flux density of 8.7 Jy at 143 MHz, dominated by the diffuse emission from its lobes that accounts for approximately 60 of the the total emission, while the remaining 40 is concentrated in the core. The core consists of two components with an angular separation of 160 mas, both with inverted spectra ( >0 ) in the HBA frequency range, but with different spectral indices whyborn_etal:1985 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C+55.16 as a compact flat-spectrum radio source with two steep-spectrum lobes; both lobes align with X-ray cavities, with a clearer rim around the southern cavity.

quotes.quote: The structure of 4C+55.16 consists of a bright compact flat-spectrum component in the center with two steeper-spectrum radio lobes extending about 9 arcseconds in the northwestern and southern directions, similar to as observed by xu95 . The southern lobe coincides with a very clear cavity with an X-ray bright rim almost fully surrounding the cavity. The northern radio lobe also coincides with a cavity, but the extent of this cavity is less pronounced hlavacek11 .

gmw_00009350.name: SDSSJ1331+3628
gmw_00009350.overview: SDSSJ1331+3628 is a spiral galaxy that acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:1609.05477, arXiv:2102.12614]. The galaxy features a counter-rotating, supermassive core that may have formed during a past merger event [arXiv:1609.05477, arXiv:2102.12614].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J1331+3628 as a spiral strong lens with a counter-rotating core, constrained by lensing, stellar dynamics, mass-to-light gradients, and possible merger-driven structure.

quotes.quote: We investigate the matter distribution of a spiral galaxy with a counter-rotating stellar core, SDSS J1331+3628 (J1331), independently with gravitational lensing and stellar dynamical modelling. By fitting a gravitational potential model to a quadruplet of lensing images around J1331's bulge, we tightly constrain the mass inside the Einstein radius R_ ein =(0.91 0.02)'' ( 1.83 0.04 kpc ) to within 4 : M_ ein = (7.8 0.3) 10^ 10 M _ . We model observed long-slit major axis stellar kinematics in J1331's central regions by finding Multi-Gaussian Expansion (MGE) models for the stellar and dark matter distribution that solve the axisymmetric Jeans equations. The lens and dynamical model are independently derived, but in very good agreement with each other around R_ ein . We find that J1331's center requires a steep total mass-to-light ratio gradient.

A dynamical model including an NFW halo (with virial velocity v_ 200 240 40 km s ^ 1 and concentration c_ 200 8 2 ) and moderate tangential velocity anisotropy ( _z 0.4 0.1 ) can reproduce the signatures of J1331's counter-rotating core and predict the stellar and gas rotation curve at larger radii. However, our models do not agree with the observed velocity dispersion at large radii. We speculate that the reason could be a non-trivial change in structure and kinematics due to a possible merger event in J1331's recent past.

sources.summary: SDSSJ1331+3628 is presented as a spiral strong lens with a counter-rotating massive core, complex rotation curve, and mass reconstruction suggesting a past merger.

quotes.quote: Galaxy J1331 SDSSJ1331+3628 (J1331) is a spiral galaxy with a counter-rotating massive core , where just like J2141, the inclination of this galaxy allows to get its rotational velocity values in function of the galactocentric radius. J1331 is localet in RA = 202.9188 ^ and DEC = 36.469990 ^ , and in the observation of this system Treut et.al (2011) observed 2 distinct redshifts within a radius of 1 arcs ( z_ L =0.113, z_ s =0.254 ), given the GLE in this galaxy. The images of this galaxy, were obtained by means of the SWELLS(Sloan WFC Edge-on late-type lens survey)(WFC-Wide Field Camera) ; In figure fig:image_gal2 these images are illustrated with the HST telescope in the F450W and F814W filters, where the high size of its core is evidenced, for this reason in the right side it is evidenced a slit done by Trick et. al , where they reconstructed the brightness surfaces. The images produced for the GLE are shown in figure fig:lens_gal2 , which are labeled with letters A,B,C y D, also it is important to clarify that the other 3 unlabeled images do not belong to this group, since according to what Trick et.al indicate these are part of a stellar formation region.

Regarding dynamical aspect of J1331, Dutton et al.(2013) obtained its rotational velocity values with the use of Keck I telescope by means of a LRIS spectrograph (Low Resolution Imaging Spectrograph) , where these data were obtained of the absortion lines M_ gb (5177 A ), Fe_ II (5270.5335 A ) and Fe_ II (5406 A ), while the gas velocity was estimated with emision lines H_ (6563 A ) and N_ II (6583 A ). An important aspect of J1331 is its supermassive core, according to what is exposed by other authors about half the brightness is enclosed in the effective radius illustrated in figure fig:curvrot_gal2 . For this reason, this galaxy has been and object of interest in different works , where a possible merger event in the past of this system which changes its structure and kinematics is speculated.

Analysis of the mass reconstruction for J1331 From the mass reconstructions performed for this galaxy with Galrotpy and Gallenspy , it follows that about of the 78 of the mass of J1331 is enclosed in the effective radius, this confirmed the presence of a supermassive core, which due to presenting a negative direction in its rotation opens the possibility to think that this galaxy is the result of a merger process between two stellar systems, with angular momentum oriented in distinct orientations.

gmw_00009351.name: PKS 0601-70
gmw_00009351.overview: PKS 0601-70 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of 2.409 [arXiv:2001.10777]. The active galactic nucleus exhibits a possible 450-day quasi-periodic variability in its gamma-ray light curves, which corresponds to a period of 0.35 years in the host galaxy frame [arXiv:2001.10777].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PKS 0601-70 as an FSRQ at redshift 2.409 with possible 450-day gamma-ray quasi-periodicity, corresponding to a 0.35-year host-frame period.

quotes.quote: The results show that besides several objects that have been reported in previous works, an additional source, the FSRQ PKS 0601-70 has a possible quasi-periodic variability of 450 days in its ray light curves with the significance of >3 .

Besides the objects reported in previous works, a FSRQ PKS 0601-70 is found having possible quasi-periodic variabilities in its ray light curves. Three BL Lacs (TXS 0518+211, S5 0716+714 and B2 2234+28 A), and a FSRQs (PKS 0250-225) present signals with significance of 2 . It is interesting that the observed period of PKS 0601-70 is 1.22 year, and the corrected period in the host galaxy frame is 0.35 year (see Table result_period ).
Besides, we apply a ARIMA model e.g., Kelly to describe the light curves of PKS 0601-70 and its results are shown in Appendix.

gmw_00009352.name: NGC 725
gmw_00009352.overview: NGC 725 is a nearby host galaxy with a generally accepted distance modulus of 31.55 mag [arXiv:1212.6893]. This value corresponds to a distance of approximately 20.4 Mpc, though alternative calculations place the galaxy at 24 Mpc [arXiv:1212.6893].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 725 as SN 2009ip's nearby host, comparing accepted and Hubble-flow distances and motivating a direct supernova distance estimate.

quotes.quote: The distance modulus for the galaxy NGC 725, where the SN 2009ip is situated, is generally accepted as = 31.55  mag Smith2010b which corresponds to 20.4 Mpc.

Since the host galaxy NGC 725 is relatively close, the Hubble parameter determined only from the redshift measurements can be incorrect because of a large peculiar velocity of the galaxy. On the other hand, the distance to the galaxy NGC 725 calculated with the generally accepted value H_0 = 71  km/s/Mpc can be significantly different from the distance obtained by other methods. For instance, Levesque2012 uses the distance to NGC 725 equal to 24 Mpc.

gmw_00009353.name: 2MASXJ00350521-6741147
gmw_00009353.overview: 2MASXJ00350521-6741147 is an elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.071 [arXiv:1410.6473]. It is likely interacting with a neighboring elliptical galaxy and features a large half-light radius of about 28 to 30 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1410.6473].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASXJ00350521-6741147 as the closest possible elliptical host, gives its redshift and projected SN offset, and argues the transient lies in the host outskirts.

quotes.quote: On October 16.21 ut , we took PESSTO spectra of the two elliptical galaxies, probably interacting, at coordinates = 00^ h 35^ m 04^ s .47 , = -67^ o 41'02''.7 (triangle in Fig.  fig:fc ) and = 00^ h 35^ m 05^ s .21 , = -67^ o 41'14''.7 (square in Fig.  fig:fc ), finding redshifts z=0.074 and z=0.072 , respectively. The second is also listed in the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database as 2MASXJ00350521-6741147 at redshift z=0.071 . If one of the two galaxies is the host, the transient would be at a projected distance of 49.9 kpc ( 35") from the first galaxy, or 40.2 kpc ( 30") from the second.

In our deepest images ( VrI ), we performed aperture photometry on the possible host (the closest). We found that the radius containing half of the light of the galaxy is R _ 1/2 28 kpc, similar to the value R _ 1/2 30 kpc measured using the V magnitude of the galaxy and a S ersic index of n=4 and reference therein gr13 . Thus is likely located in the extreme outskirts of the host, in the halo region.

gmw_00009354.name: SBS 0748+499
gmw_00009354.overview: SBS 0748+499 is a nearby Seyfert galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0244 [arXiv:astro-ph/0509572]. Structural profile decomposition indicates that the galaxy has an intermediate SBab morphology, although it lacks a clearly present typical spiral structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0509572].

sources.summary: The paper studies SBS 0748+499 as a nearby Seyfert galaxy, reporting its coordinates, redshift, BVRI imaging, photometry, and intermediate SBab morphology from profile decomposition.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The coordinates of the Seyfert galaxy SBS 0748+499 are: = 07 ^ h 51 ^ m 51.9 ^ s and = + 49 ^ 48 51 6 (J2000).

quotes.quote: The coordinates of the Seyfert galaxy SBS 0748+499 are: = 07 ^ h 51 ^ m 51.9 ^ s and = + 49 ^ 48 51 6 (J2000). Based on low-resolution optical spectroscopy, its redshift is z=0.0244 . We have obtained broad band BVRI images of SBS 0748+499 in the Johnson-Cousins system with the 1.5m telescope at the OAN-SPM.

Through polygonal and elliptical ( 9 kpc ) apertures we obtained that M_ B =-19.9 . This value is in the range given by Bo02 for a sample of Sy galaxies ( 18.7 to 22.3 ) with an average value M_ B =-20.7 . From the above estimate, it is clear that SBS 0748+499 is 1.2 mag fainter than the mean value M_ B =-21.1 reported for normal S0a--Sa UGC sample galaxies RH94 .

Using the classification scheme established for a sample of field galaxies from HST Medium Deep Survey S97 , we can see that the obtained value B/D=0.58 in the R band for SBS 0748+499 falls in the range 0.5<B/D<1.7 , so this object can be classified as an intermediate type galaxy (SBab). However, the typical spiral structure it is not clearly present in this object.

gmw_00009355.name: AGC 112521
gmw_00009355.overview: AGC 112521 is a very low-surface-brightness dwarf irregular galaxy [arXiv:0808.1789] classified as an extremely metal-deficient galaxy [arXiv:1501.05159]. It features a single star-like H II region [arXiv:0808.1789] and has experienced two modeled star-formation bursts [arXiv:0808.1789].

sources.summary: The paper identifies AGC 112521 as an XMD galaxy with very low luminosity; its weak emission line gives only an upper-limit measurement and is consistent with one of the lowest-metallicity regions.

quotes.quote: Three of the \ regions that we observed (AGC 110482 b , AGC 112521, AGC 111977) had such weak \ lines that we were unable to determine an accurate measurement of . Thus, these three \ regions have been plotted with arrows indicating the measured \ is an upper limit. The lack of distinguishable \ emission is consistent with these being among the lowest metallicity regions (see Table tab:abun ).
Notice that the abundances for AGC 112521 and AGC 111164 qualify them both as XMD galaxies which is consistent with them being the two lowest luminosity galaxies for which we obtained usable spectra.

sources.summary: The paper describes AGC 112521 as a very low-surface-brightness dwarf irregular with one star-like HII region and two modeled star-formation bursts.

quotes.quote: AGC 112521: this dIrr galaxy shows a very low surface brightness on the blue image. It has F(H )=4.39 10^ 15 erg/cm ^ 2 /s and shows a single star-like HII region south of the main diffuse body. The model fits indicate that the first SF burst took place more than 10 Gyr ago accounting for 37 10 of the present luminosity, while the second burst took place 7 Myr ago.

gmw_00009356.name: CDFS-LAE1
gmw_00009356.overview: CDFS-LAE1 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy located at a redshift of z=6.9245 [arXiv:1903.09047]. Non-detections of high-ionization lines indicate that its hydrogen-ionizing emission is dominated by normal star formation rather than an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1903.09047].

sources.summary: The paper confirms CDFS-LAE1 as a z=6.9245 Lyman-alpha galaxy and uses NIR non-detections of high-ionization lines to argue normal star formation dominates.

quotes.quote: Figure 1 shows the imaging cutouts of the two observed LAEs -- CDFS-LAE1 (RA=03:33:05.95 DEC=-28:24:59.10 J2000) and CDFS-LAE51 (RA=03:32:51.68 DEC=-28:32:42.51 J2000). The \ redshifts for LAE1 and LAE51 are 6.9245 and 6.931. The \ line fluxes are 21.8 1.8 10^ 18 \ for LAE1 and 15.6 1.8 10^ 18 \ for LAE51 (table 1). The \ line luminosities are 1.21 10^ 43 \ for LAE1 and 0.87 10^ 43 \ for LAE51.

In the FIRE spectra of CDFS-LAE1, the \ line is clearly detected with line flux of 20.6 1.4 10^ 18 \ and intrinsic FWHM of 218 . In CDFS-LAE1, the 2 upper-limits of the flux ratios of CIV 1548/ , / , OIII ] 1660/ , and CIII ] 1909/ \ are about 0.06 0.1. Therefore the contribution from an AGN is small and the H ionizing emission is dominated by normal star formation instead of AGN.

gmw_00009357.name: CDFS-LAE51
gmw_00009357.overview: CDFS-LAE51 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy located at a redshift of z=6.931 [arXiv:1903.09047]. It has an estimated star-formation rate of 54 solar masses per year [arXiv:1903.09047].

sources.summary: The paper confirms CDFS-LAE51 as a z=6.931 Lyman-alpha galaxy, measuring its line flux, width, luminosity, and inferred star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: Figure 1 shows the imaging cutouts of the two observed LAEs -- CDFS-LAE1 (RA=03:33:05.95 DEC=-28:24:59.10 J2000) and CDFS-LAE51 (RA=03:32:51.68 DEC=-28:32:42.51 J2000). The \ redshifts for LAE1 and LAE51 are 6.9245 and 6.931. The \ line fluxes are 21.8 1.8 10^ 18 \ for LAE1 and 15.6 1.8 10^ 18 \ for LAE51 (table 1). The full width at half maximum (FWHM, corrected for instrumental resolution) of \ line for LAE1 and LAE51 are 229 and 318 .

The \ line luminosities are 1.21 10^ 43 \ for LAE1 and 0.87 10^ 43 \ for LAE51. Assuming 10 \ escape fraction and / =8.7 in Case-B recombination and the prescriptions for calculating star-formation rates in Kennicutt Evans (2012), the corresponding star-formation rates are 75 M_ yr^ 1 for LAE1 and 54 M_ yr^ 1 for LAE51.

In the FIRE spectra of CDFS-LAE51, the \ line is not detected with a 3 flux upper-limit of 9.0 10^ 18 , which is slightly lower than the line flux in IMACS spectra. This could be caused by larger slit loss at poor seeing ( 1.0 1.2 ) and possibly larger blind-offset errors during the FIRE observation.

gmw_00009358.name: AS2COS0037.1
gmw_00009358.overview: AS2COS0037.1, also identified as AzTEC7, is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:2112.07430, arXiv:1312.1173]. Photometric redshift estimates place the source at approximately z = 2 [arXiv:1312.1173], and an adopted redshift of 1.90 has been utilized due to its location within an ALMA band-3 spectral scan gap [arXiv:2112.07430].

sources.summary: The survey reports AS2COS0037.1 as the only primary SMG without a detected line and adopts z=1.90 because photometric redshifts place it in the band-3 gap.

quotes.quote: For the 18 primary SMGs, 17 have yielded significant line detection based on LineSeeker , and AS2COS0037.1 is the only source that has none. Finally for AS2COS0037.1, the only target SMG that we do not obtain significant line detection, its spectrum is extracted using the synthesized beam but no correction to total is applied. Lastly, for the only SMG that has no line detection by far, AS2COS0037.1, the photometric redshift reported by Ikarashi et al. as well as the latest COSMOS2020 catalog ( 1.93^ +0.04 _ 0.07 and 1.98^ +0.03 _ 0.15 based on the LePhare and EAZY codes, respectively; Weaver:2021aa ) suggest that this SMG could be located within the known redshift gap of ALMA band 3 spectral scan, which is z=1.74-2.05 .

We therefore adopt 1.90 0.05 for AS2COS0037.1, which is simply the mean of the redshift gap with an uncertainty that makes the probability of it being outside the redshift gap equivalent to 3 . The corresponding redshifts for the matched SMGs are plotted in fig:A1 except for AS2COS0037.1 where no emission line is detected.

sources.summary: The paper describes AzTEC7 as a robust SMA-position identification at about z = 2 that would be recovered securely by all four original AzTEC-position association methods.

quotes.quote: AzTEC7. A robust single identification 0.23 arcsec from the SMA position. z_ phot and z_ LW are in good agreement that the source has a redshift z 2 . Like AzTEC5, this source would have been securely identified using all four types of statistical association on the basis of the original AzTEC position.

gmw_00009359.name: AS2COS0008.2
gmw_00009359.overview: AS2COS0008.2 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 3.5739 [arXiv:2112.07430]. It forms a gravitationally bound pair with a nearby companion galaxy at a projected distance of 20 to 30 kiloparsecs [arXiv:2112.07430].

sources.summary: The paper reports AS2COS0008.2 as the one companion SMG with a significant line detection, sharing the primary line redshift and forming a likely bound pair.

quotes.quote: We obtain a significant detection from AS2COS0008.2 and a marginal detection from AS2COS0001.2. The best-fit single Gaussian model suggests that the redshift of AS2COS0008.2 is 3.5739 0.0009 assuming that it is the same line as AS2COS0008.1, and the significance of the detection is at > 4 based on the velocity-integrated intensity over the FWHM of the best-fit model ( fig:fig8 ). Among the five companion SMGs we obtain significant line detection from one, AS2COS0008.2. Together with the previously reported line detection toward AS2COS0001.2, we find that the velocity differences of these two pairs are both within 500 km s ^ 1 , at the projected distances of 20-30 kpc, suggesting that they are gravitationally bound systems therefore confirmed physically associated pairs.

gmw_00009360.name: NGC1947
gmw_00009360.overview: NGC1947 is a peculiar early-type galaxy characterized by a dust lane aligned with its minor axis [arXiv:0811.1754]. The galaxy exhibits decoupled angular momentum, where its gas and dust rotate along the minor axis while its stars rotate along the major axis, likely due to an external accretion event [arXiv:0811.1754].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC1947 as a peculiar early-type minor-axis dust-lane galaxy, deriving photometric and kinematic evidence for decoupled gas, dust, and stellar angular momentum caused by external accretion.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present a detailed study of the peculiar early-type galaxy NGC1947. The main goal of this work is to constrain the dynamical status and the formation history of NGC1947 by comparing the observed properties with the predictions derived from different galaxy formation scenarios. To this aim, we derived the photometric and kinematical properties of NGC1947.
