The observed kinematics confirm the presence of two components with decoupled angular momentum: gas and dust rotate along the minor axis, while the rotation velocities of the stars are observed along the major axis. The complex structure observed in NGC1947 support the hypothesis that some kind of interactions happened in the evolution of this object. We analyzed two alternatives: a merging process and an accretion event. We discussed how the observed properties strongly suggest that the decoupled ring of gas and dust have been accreted from outside.

gmw_00009361.name: SDSS J155343.59+234825.4
gmw_00009361.overview: SDSS J155343.59+234825.4 is classified as a high-excitation radio galaxy [arXiv:1904.02175]. It exhibits unusually red WISE colors compared to passive wide-angle tail hosts, placing it near the sequence of more luminous blazars and flat spectrum radio quasars [arXiv:1904.02175].

sources.summary: The WAT catalog notes SDSS J155343.59+234825.4 as the sole high-excitation galaxy among its sources, lacking an [O III] luminosity measurement and showing unusually redder WISE colors compared with the passive WAT hosts.

quotes.quote: The only exception is SDSS J155343.59+234825.4, being a high excitation galaxy (HEG).

However, it is worth noting that for four sources (SDSS J144904.27+025802.7, SDSS J141927.23+233810.2, SDSS J222455.24-002302.3, SDSS J155343.59+234825.4) we were not able to retrieve information regarding the [ OIII ] line luminosity because this measurement was not present in the SDSS database.

Only one source (SDSS J155343.59+234825.4), classified as a HEG, has w_2-w_3 > 0.3 and it is located at the onset of the sequence defined by the more luminous objects such as blazars and flat spectrum radio quasars massaro12 .

gmw_00009362.name: DF20
gmw_00009362.overview: DF20 is an ultra-diffuse galaxy located in the Coma cluster region [arXiv:2310.09330]. Although resolved as two distinct objects in recent high-resolution survey data, its measured structural parameters remain reasonably consistent with earlier observations [arXiv:2310.09330].

sources.summary: The study re-detects DF20 among Coma ultra-diffuse galaxies, resolves it as two objects in the authors' data, and finds its measured structural parameters still reasonably consistent with van Dokkum et al.

quotes.quote: We detected all eight DF UDGs (DF10, DF12, DF18, DF20, DF23, DF25, DF26, and DF28) found by vanDokkum2015 in the region covered by our survey and successfully measured their structural parameters. In Figure fig:vDokkumcomp (left), we compare our measured structural parameters for those eight UDGs with the results of vanDokkum2015 .
Furthermore, we detected DF20 as two objects. However, our measured structural parameters for DF20 still agree reasonably well with those measured by vanDokkum2015 .

gmw_00009363.name: VLSB-A
gmw_00009363.overview: VLSB-A is a nucleated low surface brightness galaxy projected within the Virgo core [arXiv:1507.02270]. It features a bar-like component and a long, arcing tidal stream that suggests the galaxy may be orbiting within the M86 subgroup [arXiv:1507.02270].

sources.summary: The paper identifies VLSB-A as an extremely diffuse Virgo LSB galaxy with a nucleus, tidal stream, M86-subgroup kinematics, and likely tidal threshing into a UCD.

quotes.quote: Ellipticity estimates for VLSB-B and -C come from GALFIT modeling; VLSB-A is too low in surface brightness to yield a meaningful estimate. We report average colors for VLSB-A and -B, but cannot measure a color for VLSB-C as it falls outside the B imaging footprint of the Schmidt survey. The derived properties given in Table galprops show that, like the extreme Coma LSBs (vD15a, Koda 2015; K15), these objects are reasonably well characterized by exponential profiles ( n 1.0 ). They are large ( =40 -120 , or 3-10 kpc at the Virgo distance), extremely low in central surface brightness ( _ V,0 26.5 ), and fairly red ( =0.6--0.7).

VirgoLSB-A is projected deep within the Virgo core, 0.75 \ NW of M87 and 0.5 \ ESE of M86. It appears as a nucleated LSB galaxy with long, arcing tidal stream that runs NE-SW through the galaxy and extending off the frame in Figure imaging (the full extent of the stream can be seen in Figure 1 of Mihos 2005). The tidal stream is curved concave to M86 (and to the nearby galaxy pair NGC 4435/8), suggesting VLSB-A may be orbiting within the M86 subgroup rather than around M87 itself. The LSB component of the system is quite extended and shows a bar-like component oriented at 135 .

gmw_00009364.name: VLSB-C
gmw_00009364.overview: VLSB-C is a large, extremely low-surface-brightness galaxy candidate in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1507.02270]. It is characterized by an exponential structural profile and shows no signs of resolved stars or star clusters [arXiv:1507.02270].

sources.summary: The paper presents VLSB-C as a large, extremely low-surface-brightness Virgo candidate with exponential structure, no measured color, and no detected resolved stars or clusters.

quotes.quote: Ellipticity estimates for VLSB-B and -C come from GALFIT modeling; VLSB-A is too low in surface brightness to yield a meaningful estimate. We report average colors for VLSB-A and -B, but cannot measure a color for VLSB-C as it falls outside the B imaging footprint of the Schmidt survey. The derived properties given in Table galprops show that, like the extreme Coma LSBs (vD15a, Koda 2015; K15), these objects are reasonably well characterized by exponential profiles ( n 1.0 ). They are large ( =40 -120 , or 3-10 kpc at the Virgo distance), extremely low in central surface brightness ( _ V,0 26.5 ), and fairly red ( =0.6--0.7).

Figure ngvs shows no excess of of either point sources or resolved sources associated with VLSB-A or -C --- the discrete sources are consistent with pure background contamination. Thus, VLSB-A and -C are indeed diffuse, with no sign of either resolved stars or star clusters.

gmw_00009365.name: J1328+2752
gmw_00009365.overview: J1328+2752 is a nearby double-double radio galaxy hosted by a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2012.06290]. It features two highly misaligned pairs of radio jets and lobes [arXiv:2012.06290], double-peaked optical emission lines [arXiv:2012.06290], and two VLBI components separated by 6 parsecs [arXiv:2402.12521].

sources.summary: The paper presents J1328+2752 as a restarted double-double radio galaxy in a giant elliptical host, with misaligned lobes, double-peaked lines, and AGN activity.

quotes.quote: J1328+2752 has been classified as `double-double radio galaxy' (DDRG) in the literature 2000MNRAS.315..371S . At a redshift of z=0.0911 and a luminosity distance of 412 Mpc (assuming a CDM cosmology with H_0 =71 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ m =0.27 and _ vac =0.73) , it is one of nearby DDRG, affording us a close look at its nucleus. It contains two pairs of radio jets and lobes launched from its accreting supermassive black hole (SMBH) - accretion disk system, referred to as an AGN. Unlike typical DDRGs however, the two pairs of radio jets/lobes are highly misaligned with each other ( 30^ ; Nandi17 ) .

J1328+2752 is hosted by a giant elliptical galaxy which are thought to be built from multiple prior galaxy mergers see Appendix sec:SDSS for further details 1972ApJ...178..623T, 2017ARA&A..55...59N, 2020MNRAS.498.2766W . Each merger event should have deposited one or more SMBHs in their centres considering the fact that all galaxies host SMBHs in their centres Harms94,Genzel97 . The Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) optical spectrum of J1328+2752 shows double peaks in the [ Ne III ] 3869 , H 4342 , H 4863 , [ O III ] 4959 , [ O III ] 5007 , [ O I ] 6300 , H 6563 , [ N II ] 6548,6584 , and [ S II ] 6718,6732 emission lines.

sources.summary: The paper treats J1328+2752 as a dual-VLBI-component source whose dual-AGN nature remains unconfirmed, requiring dual-frequency observations; its components are separated by 6 pc.

quotes.quote: J1328+2752 is another source that shows dual VLBI components (see for a detailed discussion of this source). However, the dual AGN nature has not yet been confirmed due to the lack of spectral index information. have pointed out that the source possesses a flat spectrum core based on its VLA image. We must obtain near-simultaneous observations at dual frequencies to confirm the nature of both the VLBA components. The projected separation between the two components is equal to 6 pc Nandi2021 .

gmw_00009366.name: PKS 2326-502
gmw_00009366.overview: PKS 2326-502 is a blazar active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2302.14749]. Multi-wavelength monitoring of the source revealed a flaring state between 2013 and 2014, showing high-significance variability across millimeter, optical, and gamma-ray bands [arXiv:2302.14749].

sources.summary: The paper monitors the blazar PKS 2326-502 across millimeter, gamma-ray, and optical bands, finding a 2013-2014 flaring state and multi-wavelength correlations.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we focus on a single source, PKS 2326-502, which has extensive, day-timescale monitoring data in gamma-ray, optical, and now mm-wave between 2013 and 2016. We find PKS 2326-502 to be in a flaring state in the first two years of this monitoring, and we present a search for evidence of correlated variability between mm-wave, optical R band, and \ observations.

Although our SPTpol AGN monitoring campaign includes tens of sources, we choose to focus on the blazar PKS 2326-502 for this pilot study because of its long history of observations in multiple wavelengths e.g., dutka17 . PKS 2326-502 is among the targets of monitoring by both the \ Large Area Telescope (LAT) and Yale Small and Moderate Aperture Research Telescope System (SMARTS) Blazar Group collaborations. In particular, PKS 2326-502 has publicly available \ ( ) and SMARTS (optical) observations over most of the time period over which we have SPTpol data. In our study of PKS 2326-502, we use data from SPT, SMARTS, and LAT.

Multi-wavelength ( , optical, mm-wave) light curves for four years of monitoring of PKS 2326-502 are shown in Figure fluxes . We note that the raw statistical significance of the variability in all three bands is high: The typical S/N in a single 36-hour SPT light curve point is 50 in the quiescent state and over 200 in the flaring state, and the corresponding S/N for the 3-day \ light curve points are 1--2 and 7--10.

gmw_00009367.name: SDSS J090808.36 + 394313.6
gmw_00009367.overview: SDSS J090808.36 + 394313.6 is a hard X-ray source located at a redshift of approximately 0.45 [arXiv:0801.3341]. Its spectrum and high column density indicate that it is a highly obscured active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0801.3341].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J090808.36 + 394313.6 as a hard X-ray SDSS source whose spectrum, redshift, obscuration, and luminosity indicate a highly obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: The hard X-ray source to the west of \ (the blue source in Fig. fig:colour located at RA 09h08m08.3s, Dec +39d43m12s in J2000 coordinates) is an SDSS source and so, following the SDSS naming convention, is SDSS J090808.36 + 394313.6. It has a spectrum consistent with it being an obscured AGN (see Fig. fig:another ) with column density of N_ H 1.28^ +0.41 _ 0.36 10^ 24 \ when the power-law component is fixed at = 2 because it is otherwise unconstrained (although varying it between reasonable values does not significantly alter the inferred value of the column density). Adding an iron line redshifted from 6.4 \ improves the fit from 1.4 for 4 d.o.f. to 0.2 for 3 d.o.f., i.e. by ^2 = 5 .

The redshift is measured as z = 0.45^ +0.02 _ 0.08 . There are a total of 261 counts (159 background) in the 0.2-8 \ band, meaning that the derived quantities are somewhat uncertain; however, as Fig. fig:colour shows there are very few photons below 3 , this source must be a highly obscured AGN. The 2-10 \ band observed (absorbed) flux is F_ X = 3.1^ +5.7 _ 1.0 10^ 14 \ and the absorption-corrected X-ray luminosity in the same band is L_ X =1.3^ +0.3 _ 0.2 10^ 44 .

gmw_00009368.name: HE 0226-4110
gmw_00009368.overview: HE 0226-4110 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1307.6799] classified as a simple narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1909.13242]. Its broader X-ray spectrum can be modeled using two power-law components and an iron line [arXiv:1909.13242], and it features an associated absorption-line system [arXiv:1307.6799].

sources.summary: HE 0226-4110 has an associated absorber detected in multiple oxygen ionization stages and Lyman-series transitions, with profiles indicating no unocculted flux.

quotes.quote: HE 0226-4110 ( =0.4925) -- As presented and discussed by gan06a , this absorption-line system is detected in four adjacent ionization stages of oxygen - 832.927, 787.711, 629.720, and 1031.926, 1037.617 - as well as the Lyman series, 977.020, 765.148, 748.400, and 770.409. The Ly , 977.020, and 629.730 transitions reach zero flux levels, indicating no unocculted flux. Likewise, modulo a blend in the 1037.617 with Galactic 1548.204, the doublet is consistent with a 1:2 true optical depth ratio.

sources.summary: J0228 is classified as a simple NLS1 because a 2-12 keV power law fits well; its broader XMM spectrum can be modeled with two power laws plus an iron line.

quotes.quote: J0228 (IRAS 02262-4110) We reproduced the spectrum of J0228 between 2-12 keV with a simple power-law model and obtained a ^2_ = 1.05. This indicates that a power-law represents the data very well and thus it is classified as a S-NLS1. The XMM-Newton EPIC (PN+MOS1+MOS2) spectra of J0228 in the range of 0.2-12.0 keV can be modeled by two power-law components plus an iron line at the rest frame, as seen in Krumpe2010 .

gmw_00009369.name: HE 0515-4414
gmw_00009369.overview: HE 0515-4414 is a very bright, hyperluminous quasar located at a redshift of approximately 1.71 [arXiv:2112.05819, arXiv:1811.08752]. The system features a strong intervening absorption system at redshift 1.15 [arXiv:2112.05819], as well as associated absorption line systems that fully occult the background source [arXiv:1307.6799].

sources.summary: HE 0515-4414 has one secure associated absorption system and another probable system, with Lyman profiles showing full occultation and blends limiting partial-coverage tests.

quotes.quote: HE 0515-4414 ( =1.6736, 1.6971) -- There is one securely-identified associated absorption line system toward HE 0515-4414, at =1.69736 , that is detected in the Lyman series, 989.799, and the 1031.926,1037.617 doublet. These are shown in left panels of Figure fig:he0515 . The Ly \ and Ly \ profiles reach zero flux levels, indicating that the neutral species fully occult the background source. ( Ly \ appears to be blended with absorption unrelated to the absorption-line system.) The 1037.617 profile is blended, making it difficult to examine potential partial coverage. Another possible associated absorption-line system may reside at =1.6971 .

sources.summary: The paper treats HE 0515 4414 as a very bright southern quasar at redshift 1.71, observed with ESPRESSO to measure fine-structure-constant variation using its strong intervening absorber.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: J2000 right ascension 05 ^ h 17 ^ m 07.6 ^ s and declination 44^ 10 ^ 55.58 ^

quotes.quote: The strong intervening absorption system at redshift 1.15 towards the very bright quasar HE 0515 4414 is the most studied absorber for measuring possible cosmological variations in the fine-structure constant, . We observed HE 0515 4414 for 16.1 h with the Very Large Telescope and present here the first constraint on relative variations in with parts-per-million (ppm) precision from the new ESPRESSO spectrograph: = 1.3 1.3_ stat 0.4_ sys ppm .

We selected HE 0515 4414 as a high-value target because it is one of the brightest quasars in the southern sky, with an intervening absorption system at redshift =1.1508 which places the most important metal transitions within the LFC-calibrated wavelength range.

Observations HE 0515 4414 (J2000 right ascension 05 ^ h 17 ^ m 07.6 ^ s and declination 44^ 10 ^ 55.58 ^ ) was identified as a very bright ( m_ v 15.2 mag; Gaia G=14.90 mag) quasar at redshift =1.71 by Reimers_1998A&A...334...96R . It was part of the ESPRESSO Consortium's Guaranteed Time Observations (GTO) in two main runs: a visitor-mode run on 4--7 November 2018, and service mode observations between November 2019 and March 2020.

sources.summary: The paper uses HE 0515--4414 as a bright quasar sightline whose z=1.149 Fe II metal system was observed with high-S/N VLT/UVES spectra for line-center regression analysis.

quotes.quote: Figure 2 shows isolated and unsaturated Fe ii lines from the metal system at z = 1.149 toward the bright quasar HE 0515--4414. The spectra of this quasar were obtained with the UV-Visual Echelle Spectrograph (UVES) installed at the VLT/Kueyen telescope. The VLT/UVES data have a very high signal-to-noise ratio ( S/N 90-150 per resolution element) and a high resolution power of / 55 000 ( FWHM 5.5 km s ^ 1 ). The absorption systems from this data were analyzed in and . We used ( 10E4 )-( 10E7 ) to estimate the rest-frame equivalent widths W_ , the line centers _c , and their errors which are listed in Table 1.

sources.summary: The paper presents HE 0515-4414 as a hyperluminous quasar where ALMA detected an SZE wind signal, implying substantial kinetic luminosity and long-lived outflow enthalpy.

quotes.quote: For the hyperluminous southern quasar HE 0515-4414 (which also has L_ bol 10^ 15 L_ ), we detected a signal at 3.5 with a y_ peak =2 10^ 5 using a deep ALMA (12.4 hr time on source) observation. This detection corresponds to a kinetic luminosity in the winds of about 0.01 of L_ bol (Lacy et al. \ 2018). Nevertheless, the outflow in HE 0515-4414 still has a total enthalpy 3 10^ 53 W, comparable to the largest bubbles excavated by radio jets in radio mode feedback, and also seems to be long-lived (with a cooling time 0.6 Gyr), and so could be providing feedback in a similar manner to radio sources.

gmw_00009370.name: Mrk 132
gmw_00009370.overview: Mrk 132 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of 1.7322 [arXiv:1307.6799]. It features an associated absorption-line system with saturated, flat-bottomed Lyman series lines that indicate full occultation of the background source [arXiv:1307.6799].

sources.summary: Mrk 132 has an associated absorber detected across Lyman-series and metal-line transitions, including saturated flat-bottomed troughs that indicate full occultation.

quotes.quote: Mrk 132 ( =1.7322) -- This associated absorption-line system is detected in all Lyman series lines and includes a sharp Lyman limit break. The system is also detected in 1036.337, 977.020, and the 1031.926, 1037.617 doublet. Figure fig:mrk132sys shows velocity-aligned flux profiles of selected Lyman series and metal-line transitions. The Lyman series lines are saturated with flat-bottomed trough that reach zero flux indicating full occultation of the background by neutral species. Likewise, the shows no evidence for partial coverage via our test (Figure fig:mrk132pc ).

gmw_00009371.name: PG 1206+459
gmw_00009371.overview: PG 1206+459 is a hyperluminous infrared quasar [arXiv:2110.07373] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1307.6799, arXiv:2110.07373]. Its infrared emission is well-fitted by an extended dust torus model, showing no evidence of a starburst [arXiv:2110.07373].

sources.summary: PG 1206+459 contains an intrinsic absorber detected in Ly and the doublet, with strong smooth profiles suggesting an accretion-disk outflow mini-BAL.

quotes.quote: PG 1206+459 ( =1.0280) -- In the E230M spectrum of PG 1206+459, we detect an intrinsic absorption-line system at =1.0280 \ in Ly \ and the 1238.821,1242.804 doublet. The spectrum covers the rest-frame wavelength range 1120-1538, . This system has not been identified in other works and we present a velocity-stacked plot of the detected transitions in Figure fig:pg1206 . The absorption profiles are very strong and smooth, indicative of a possible origin in the accretion-disk outflow. This type of system has often been termed a ``mini-BAL'' e.g., cssg99,bhs97 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies QSO J1208+4540 as a hyperluminous infrared QSO whose infrared data favor an extended AGN dust torus without evidence for a starburst.

quotes.quote: QSO J1208+4540 (PG1206+459) is classified as QSO (activity type) and as Hyperluminous infrared galaxy (HyLIRG) at z= 1.16494 ( M_i=-29.26 0.50 ). M. Rowan-Robinson argued that hyperluminous galaxy with emission peaking at rest frame wavelengths 50 m are undergoing star formation at rates >10^3 M_ yr^ 1 . Infrared bump in spectrum corresponds to star formation or/and thermal emission caused by dusty torus. But in the same work he showed that for PG1206+459 the Infrared Astronomical Satellite (IRAS) 12 - 16 m data and the Infrared Space Observatory (ISO) 12 - 200 m data are well-fitted by the extended AGN dust torus model and there is no evidence for a starburst . In near-IR it was observed by WISE .

gmw_00009372.name: RX J1230.8+0115
gmw_00009372.overview: RX J1230.8+0115 is a quasar active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1307.6799] that exhibits high dust extinction [arXiv:0708.1083]. Its sightline, which passes through the Virgo cluster, contains several intrinsic absorption systems [arXiv:1307.6799] and shows evidence of sodium emission [arXiv:0708.1083].

sources.summary: RX J1230.8+0115 has several intrinsic systems near the quasar redshift, including partial-coverage systems and apparent line locking along the Virgo-cluster sightline.

quotes.quote: RX J1230.8+0115 ( =0.1000, 0.1058, 0.1093, 0.1172) -- There are several intrinsic systems within 4600 \ of the quasar redshift in this sight line passing through the Virgo cluster. These have been analyzed in detail by see their Table 1, and Figures 3 and 4 gan03b , who present evidence for partial coverage in the 1548.204, 1550.781 and 1238.821, 1242.804 doublets for the systems at =0.1000 , 0.1058, and 0.1093. The system at =0.1172 , which is at the quasar redshift, shows no evidence for partial coverage. In addition, the three systems showing partial coverage are also apparently line-locked, =0.1000 \ and 0.1058 in and Ly \ (transitions detected in spectra from the Far Ultraviolet Spectroscopic Explorer ), =0.1058 \ and 0.1093 in .

sources.summary: The paper identifies HE 1228 + 013 as highly reddened, with Na emission and high NELR Ly-fluorescence contribution compared with the BELR.

quotes.quote: For three sources, namely, Mrk 817, NGC 4151, and NGC 4593, the dust extinction is much below this value, whereas for another three sources, namely, Mrk 876, Ark 564, and HE 1228 + 013, it appears to be as high as A_ V 2 mag.
Conclusive evidence for the presence of 5896 in emission is observed only in HE 1228 + 013.

In four other sources, namely, HE 1228 + 013, Mrk 110, Mrk 335, and H 1934 063, the Ly fluorescence contribution is higher in the NELR than in the BELR, with the largest differences found for Mrk 110 ( 75 in the NELR vs. 50 in the BELR) and HE 1228 + 013 (a larger than unity photon flux ratio of 1.26 in the NELR vs. 85 in the BELR).

gmw_00009373.name: PG 1241+176
gmw_00009373.overview: PG 1241+176 is a quasar and active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of approximately 1.2717 [arXiv:1307.6799]. It features an associated absorption-line system detected in Lyman-alpha and the O VI doublet [arXiv:1307.6799].

sources.summary: PG 1241+176 has an associated absorption-line system in Ly and the doublet, with noisy blended data still consistent with full coverage.

quotes.quote: PG 1241+176 ( =1.2717) -- In the E230M spectrum of this quasar, we detect an associated absorption-line system in Ly \ and the 1031.926, 1037.617 doublet (Figure fig:pg1241 ). The core of the 1031.926 line in blended with the Galactic 2344.214 line. The data for the doublet are fairly noisy and the fluxes are consistent with full coverage in spite of the blend.

gmw_00009374.name: ESO563-G021
gmw_00009374.overview: ESO563-G021 is a disk galaxy that is relatively well fitted by a total-baryons-scaling model [arXiv:2105.00119]. Its rotation curve data provides stringent constraints on ultralight dark matter, conservatively excluding a mass range of 0.14 to 3.11 in units of 10^-22 eV [arXiv:2104.05159].

sources.summary: The paper finds ESO563-G021 gives the strongest SPARC rotation-curve constraint on ultralight dark matter, excluding m22 = 0.14-3.11 through its dark-halo mass limits.

quotes.quote: By examining the data of the Spitzer Photometry Accurate Rotation Curves (SPARC), we discover that the rotation curve data of 5 galaxies can give meaningful and stringent constraints for ULDM. In particular, one of the galaxies ESO563-G021 can conservatively exclude a mass range m_ 22 =0.14-3.11 , which is a crucial range for the ULDM proposal to explain the small-scale problems in the standard CDM model.

Among the results in Table 1, the constraints obtained by the ESO563-G021 galaxy are the most stringent. The excluded mass range m_ 22 =0.14-3.11 basically covers the excluded ranges based on the other four galaxies. In Fig. 2, we show the graphical analysis of the data from the ESO563-G021 galaxy. There are two data points ( r_i,M_i ) which can separate the allowed m_ 22 into two discrete regions (i.e. two pairs of lines are plotted). It has a relatively large minimum total dark matter halo mass M_j so that the excluded range (shaded region) is somewhat wider.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO563-G021 as relatively well fit by total-baryons scaling, with the HIDM gas-scaling solution requiring little or no dark-matter disk.

quotes.quote: ESO563-G021 is relatively well fitted by the total-baryons-scaling model. Here, the optimal fit for the HIDM gas-scaling model does not involve a DM disk. This example is typical of a subset of about 70 galaxies with little or no DM disk ( < 10^ 4 ).

gmw_00009375.name: IAC J010127-334319
gmw_00009375.overview: IAC J010127-334319 is an optical Einstein ring system located in the Sculptor constellation [arXiv:1605.03938]. It consists of a lenticular lens galaxy at a redshift of z=0.581 and a background starburst source galaxy at a redshift of z=1.165 [arXiv:1605.03938].

sources.summary: The paper reports IAC J010127-334319 as an almost complete optical Einstein ring, with a massive early-type lens at z=0.581 and a starburst source at z=1.165.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We report the discovery of an optical Einstein Ring in the Sculptor constellation, IAC J010127-334319, in the vicinity of the Sculptor Dwarf Spheroidal Galaxy. It is an almost complete ring ( 300^ ) with a diameter of 4.5 arcsec . The discovery was made serendipitously from inspecting Dark Energy Camera (DECam) archive imaging data. Confirmation of the object nature has been obtained by deriving spectroscopic redshifts for both components, lens and source, from observations at the 10.4 m Gran Telescopio CANARIAS (GTC) with the spectrograph OSIRIS.

Lens Using the template spectra by 1996ApJ...467...38K results that the spectrum of the lens galaxy fits well the spectrum of a S0 galaxy (see Figure fig:spec ), a typical early-type galaxy characterized by a large increase in flux from the UV part of the spectrum to the optical. The 4000 A Balmer discontinuity at 6330 A is noticeable. The redshift of the lens galaxy is z =0.581 0.001 and it has been determined from the measurements of: H 3835.4 , Ca K 3933.7 , Ca H 3968.5 , H 4141.8 , G-band 4307.7 , Mg-b2 5172.7 , Mg-b1 5183.6 (marked red features from left to right in Figure fig:spec middle panel). Source For the source galaxy we used the template spectra by 1994ApJ...429..582C and we found that the spectrum best fitting our observed spectrum corresponds to a starburst galaxy in the case of clumpy scattering slab , where it is assumed that clumped dust is located close to the source of radiation.

gmw_00009376.name: IFRS xFLS 478
gmw_00009376.overview: IFRS xFLS 478 is a high-redshift radio galaxy [arXiv:1607.02707]. It exhibits a steep radio spectrum with a spectral index of -1.14, showing a potential low-frequency turnover at 180 MHz [arXiv:1607.02707].

sources.summary: The paper observes IFRS xFLS 478 at 105 GHz, gives its radio/infrared position and SED, and finds a steep radio spectrum with possible low-frequency turnover.

quotes.quote: PdBI observations and ancillary data of IFRS xFLS 478 To complement the cm-wave observations described above, we observed one of the brightest IFRS in the Zinn2011 catalogue, IFRS xFLS 478 (35.8 mJy at 1.4 GHz), with the PdBI. The source is located in the xFLS Condon2003 field at RA 17h11m48.526s and Dec +59d10m38.87s (J2000). Zinn2011 found an uncatalogued IR counterpart of around 20 Jy at 3.6 m, resulting in a radio-to-IR flux density ratio S_ 1.4 GHz /S_ 3.6 m = 1831 .

Figure fig:radioSED_xFLS478 shows the radio SED of this source---including all available data between 150 MHz and 105 GHz---, similar to the SEDs shown in Fig. fig:radioSEDs . To describe the radio emission over this wide frequency range of almost three orders of magnitude, we followed the same approach as described in Sect. building_fitting_radioSEDs . Fitting a single power law to the data resulted in a spectral index = -1.14 0.02 . However, the low-frequency data at 150 MHz and 325 MHz depart from the single power law, indicating a potential turnover. An SSA model was found to better describe the data, resulting in a turnover at 180 MHz and a spectral index of 1.19 0.06 at the high-frequency side of the synchrotron bump.

gmw_00009377.name: IFRS CS618
gmw_00009377.overview: IFRS CS618 is a morphologically peculiar, double-lobed FRII radio galaxy with an angular size of approximately 1.7 arcminutes [arXiv:1607.02707]. The galaxy's total radio spectral energy distribution, as well as the individual SEDs of its core and two lobes, are well described by single power laws [arXiv:1607.02707].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IFRS CS618 as morphologically peculiar, a double-lobed FRII radio galaxy whose total and component radio SEDs are well described by single power laws.

quotes.quote: Radio SED of IFRS CS618 The IFRS CS618 is peculiar and has a different morphology than any other source in our IFRS sample. The 3.6 m map of CS618 overplotted by the 1.4 GHz ATLAS DR3 contours is shown in Fig. fig:S618 . It appears as a double-lobed FRII radio galaxy with an angular size of around 1.7 arcmin. Its total radio SED (containing the core and the two lobes) is well described by a single power law with a spectral index = -1.06 0.05 (see Fig. fig:radioSEDs ). All three components are separately detected at 325 MHz, 610 MHz, 1.4 GHz and 2.3 GHz, but are indistinguishable at 200 MHz, 843 MHz, and 844 MHz because of the lower resolution.

The radio SEDs of all three individual components are well described by single power laws. The central component (1.6 mJy at 1.4 GHz) is unresolved with a spectral index between 325 MHz and 2.3 GHz of +0.33 0.17 , whereas the two lobes are slightly resolved with 1.4 GHz flux densities of 34.5 mJy and 11.4 mJy, and spectral indices between 150 MHz and 2.3 GHz of 1.01 0.09 and 1.23 0.13 . These characteristics are common for FRII radio galaxies (e.g. \ Hovatta2014 ) and so CS618 seems to differ from the majority of IFRS in our sample, which are dominated by more compact and putatively younger radio sources as discussed above.

gmw_00009378.name: IFRS CS603
gmw_00009378.overview: IFRS CS603 is an infrared-faint radio source with a radio spectral energy distribution that follows a single power law between 800 MHz and 10 GHz [arXiv:1607.02707]. At higher frequencies, the spectrum turns upward, which is likely caused by an active galactic nucleus core dominating over the steep synchrotron emission of the lobes [arXiv:1607.02707].

sources.summary: The paper describes IFRS CS603 as having a radio SED that follows a power law to 10 GHz but turns upward at higher frequencies, probably from an AGN core.

quotes.quote: IFRS with an upturn in their radio SED The radio SED of IFRS CS603 follows a single power law in the frequency range between 800 MHz and 10 GHz. At higher frequencies, however, the SED departs from this power law, showing an increasing flux density with increasing frequency. This is indicated by the 18 GHz detection and clearly visible from the 20 GHz detection. There are two potential explanations for this behaviour. A flattening or upturning SED at high frequencies can be explained by a flat or inverted SED of an AGN core that is dominating over the steep synchrotron SED of the lobes at these frequencies.

Considering that IFRS are known to be AGN and that no evidence for heavy dust obscuration in IFRS has been found Collier2014 , the flat or inverted radio SED of an AGN core seems to be the most plausible explanation. This interpretation is consistent with results from Hogan2015a,Hogan2015b , who find that a fainter, flatter spectral core component is often present in the radio SEDs of brightest cluster galaxies (BCGs). However, higher-frequency observations of IFRS CS603 are needed to add evidence to this hypothesis.

gmw_00009379.name: CS164
gmw_00009379.overview: CS164 is a compact radio active galactic nucleus with an estimated redshift of approximately z=2 [arXiv:1607.02707]. High-resolution observations of the source reveal no resolved substructure, indicating an angular size of less than 1 arcsecond [arXiv:1607.02707].

sources.summary: The paper finds CS164 has a fitted radio turnover implying a rest-frame peak above 2 GHz if at z=2, compact size, and no resolved substructure.

quotes.quote: Going the other way around and using the fitted peak frequencies of 370 MHz and 680 MHz for ES1156 and CS164, the rest-frame peak frequencies are above 1 GHz and 2 GHz, respectively, assuming that these sources are at z 2 . Following the correlation presented by ODea1997 , these objects are expected to be smaller than 300 pc and 100 pc, respectively. In all available maps, CS164 and ES1156 were found to be very compact. In particular, CS164 was observed by Middelberg2011 at high angular resolution and did not reveal any substructure, suggesting an angular size of less than 1 arcsec, consistent with the linear size estimated from the observed turnover frequency.

gmw_00009380.name: NGC 6909
gmw_00009380.overview: NGC 6909 is classified as a giant elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0808.3573]. It exhibits very weak near-infrared sodium and calcium metal lines, a low velocity dispersion, and spectral-index behavior that suggests a unique chemical enrichment history [arXiv:0808.3573].

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 6909 as a giant elliptical with weak NIR metal lines, low velocity dispersion, and spectral-index behavior that may indicate unusual enrichment.

quotes.quote: Six of our galaxies are giant ellipticals (NGC 4472, NGC 4621, NGC 4649, NGC 4697, NGC 6909, IC 4296), five are intermediate-size ellipticals (NGC 4478, NGC 4564, NGC 4742, NGC 5077, NGC 5576), one is a bright dwarf elliptical (NGC 3641), and two are bulges of disk galaxies (NGC 4281, NGC 4594; our spectra are dominated by their spheroids). Among the galaxies, NGC 5077 ( 12) and NGC 6909 ( 14) possess very weak Na I , Ca I lines, and their relative errors are significant.

Three galaxies -- NGC 4281 ( 4), NGC 5077 ( 12) and NGC 6909 ( 14) -- are above the relation of S08 for the cluster stars, which hints at different chemical enrichment history with respect to the rest of the sample. Unfortunately the literature lacks much data about these objects and although NGC 6909 has the lowest velocity dispersion in our sample, these galaxies do not stand out in any respect, including in the optical Mg _2 versus diagram. Figure fig:IR_FeH shows the EW of the NIR spectral indices versus the Mg _2 measurements from ben93 . Na I , and to a lesser extent Ca I and CO, do show trends with respect to Mg _2 , with NGC 4281 ( 4), NGC 5077 ( 12) and NGC 6909 ( 14) standing out.

gmw_00009381.name: 2MASS J09023731 4813339
gmw_00009381.overview: 2MASS J09023731 4813339 is an extended, hard X-ray emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0391 [arXiv:0905.3309]. Near-infrared spectroscopy reveals broad emission lines that classify the object as a type 1 Seyfert galaxy [arXiv:0905.3309].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASS J09023731 4813339 as the extended infrared counterpart of the hard X-ray source and classifies it as a type 1 Seyfert galaxy at z=0.0391.

quotes.quote: We performed optical and near infrared observations on the counterpart 2MASS J09023731 4813339 with the ESO/NTT telescope on March 2007. We achieved photometry and spectroscopy in near infrared wavelengths and photometry in optical wavelengths. The accurate astrometry at both optical and near infrared wavelengths confirmed 2MASS J09023731 4813339 to be the counterpart of . However, the near infrared images show that the source is extended, thus excluding any Galactic compact source possibility.

The source spectrum shows three main emission lines identified as the HeI 1.0830 line, and the HI Pa and Pa lines, typical in galaxies with an active galactic nucleus. The broadness of these lines reached values as large as 4000 km s 1 pointing towards a type 1 Seyfert galaxy. The redshift of the source is z=0.0391 0.0004 .

gmw_00009382.name: NGC 3403
gmw_00009382.overview: NGC 3403 is a nearby spiral galaxy located at a distance of approximately 22.2 Mpc [arXiv:1908.03507]. It has a baryonic mass of 10^10 solar masses and exhibits a star formation rate of 0.23 solar masses per year [arXiv:1908.03507].

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 3403 as a possible Source 2 host: a nearby spiral in the localization region whose association would imply a small host/source dispersion measure.

quotes.quote: Notably, NGC 3403, a spiral galaxy with baryonic mass 10^ 10 M _ with a star formation rate of 0.23 0.09 M_ yr^ 1 erroz2016non at 22.2 Mpc ( z = 0.0042 ) is within the localization region of Source 2. If NGC 3403 is its host galaxy, then DM _ host +DM _ source 10 pc cm ^ 3 , suggesting that the FRB progenitor is situated either at the outskirts of the NGC 3403 disk or in its halo.
We found three catalogued sources that are not present in VLASS, one of them being NGC 3403.

gmw_00009383.name: SDSS J082905.01+571541.6
gmw_00009383.overview: SDSS J082905.01+571541.6 is a galaxy classified as a quasar that exhibits broad and narrow emission lines [arXiv:1104.2521]. However, its lack of a blue-rising continuum and the diffuse nature of its radio lobes suggest it may instead be a radio galaxy in a short, transitional phase [arXiv:1104.2521].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J082905.01+571541.6 as a quasar-catalog object with QSO-like emission lines but weak blue continuum, diffuse lobes, and a likely transitional radio-galaxy state.

quotes.quote: Only two of them -- SDSS J082905.01+571541.6 and SDSS J123915.40+531414.6 - were classified as QSOs and as such listed in the quasar catalogue based on Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) Data Release 7 DR7QSO . Some of their physical data taken from Shen2010 are quoted in Table qso . Their NVSS and FIRST radio maps along with SDSS spectra are shown in Figs. 082904+571541 and 123915+531416 , respectively. Although broad and narrow emission lines typical for QSOs are present there, the continua, unlike in QSOs, do not rise towards the blue end of the spectra. This makes labelling these two objects as quasars questionable. Instead, they should be named radio galaxies, but the diffuse nature of their radio lobes casts doubt on this classification as well.

Two objects in our sample -- SDSS J082905.01+571541.6 and SDSS J123915.40+531414.6 shown in Figs. 082904+571541 and 123915+531416 , respectively -- are also rare and accordingly likely to remain in a short, transitional phase.

gmw_00009384.name: PGC 128172
gmw_00009384.overview: PGC 128172 is a nearby galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.04 [arXiv:astro-ph/0606004]. It is observed practically edge-on and is situated near the error box of the gamma-ray burst GRB 060117 [arXiv:astro-ph/0606004].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC 128172 as a nearby z=0.04 galaxy in the GRB 060117 error box, but argues the optical transient is probably not associated with it.

quotes.quote: Soon after the trigger, a suspicion of an extremely low-redshift GRB was raised gcn4534 due to the presence of a nearby galaxy PGC 128172 with z=0.04 in the errorbox. However, this discovered transient lies 3 1 from this galaxy, accordingly the projected distance -- 160 kpc -- is approximately four times larger than the visible major diameter of this galaxy. Furthermore, the position angle of the transient with respect to the PGC 128172, which we observe practically edge-on, is 97 ^ . The association of the OT with this galaxy is, therefore, quite unlikely.

gmw_00009385.name: J0423B
gmw_00009385.overview: J0423B is the most massive galaxy in a z=0.633 absorber group [arXiv:1712.00014]. It exhibits luminous infrared galaxy-like molecular excitation and has a high far-infrared star formation rate [arXiv:1712.00014].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0423B as the CO-detected, most massive galaxy in a z=0.633 absorber group, with LIRG-like molecular excitation and high far-infrared SFR.

quotes.quote: The Nature of the galaxy J0423B Galaxy \ is detected in ^ 12 CO(2--1) and the ^ 12 CO(3--2) as well as our MUSE observations. Here we collect all this information and discuss the nature of this galaxy. In SubsecMolGas it is shown that the ratio of the ^ 12 CO(2--1) and the ^ 12 CO(3--2) emission line is consistent with \ being a LIRG. Furthermore, the SFR based on L_ FIR reported in SubsecSFR is 50 10 M _ yr ^ 1 . We compare this to the SFR from the dust-uncorrected [ OII ] line flux, which is 13 - 25 times lower.

gmw_00009386.name: target 1
gmw_00009386.overview: Target 1 is classified as a head-tail radio source [arXiv:2305.07561]. Its morphology features a southern hotspot and a single measurable lobe with spectral ages that increase toward the northern region [arXiv:2305.07561].

sources.summary: The paper classifies target 1 as a head-tail radio source with a one-lobe measurement, southern hotspot, and spectral ages increasing toward the northern lobe.

quotes.quote: Morphology Figure t1a shows the spectral age distribution for target 1 where the morphology in the spectral age map shows an increasing flux gradient towards the northern part of the source and indicates that the source morphology is that of a head-tail source, where the core is moving with respect to its environment and causing the ejected plasma to bend towards the movement axis. Hence we can only measure the size of a single lobe. The map shows a hot-spot near the southern region of the source. This region has an age which is consistent with zero which eventually grows to higher values exhibiting an increase in age of the source lobes towards the northern region.

gmw_00009387.name: target 2
gmw_00009387.overview: Target 2 is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley type-II radio galaxy [arXiv:2305.07561]. Its spectral-age map reveals hotspots located near the center of each lobe, with spectral ages increasing radially outward and toward the core [arXiv:2305.07561].

sources.summary: The paper interprets target 2 as an FR type-II radio source whose spectral-age map shows central lobe hotspots and ages increasing outward and toward the core.

quotes.quote: Figure t1b shows the spectral age distribution for target 2, where we observe hot-spots near the center of each lobe and the age eventually increases radially outwards and towards the core. This type of morphology is indicative of an FR type-II source. We see that the MeerKAT contours converge near the hot-spots with the host galaxy being near the center of the source where we expect the core to be, further confirming the morphology of the source.

gmw_00009388.name: target 3
gmw_00009388.overview: Target 3 is a radio galaxy that exhibits a Fanaroff-Riley II-like morphology [arXiv:2305.07561]. Its lobes feature hot-spot regions where the contour converges, showing spectral ages consistent with zero near the edges [arXiv:2305.07561].

sources.summary: The paper finds target 3 has FR II-like morphology but notes missing lobe-edge regions in the age analysis because lower-resolution, lower-sensitivity maps limit the result.

quotes.quote: For target 3 (in Fig. t1c ) we observe ages consistent with zero near the edge of the two lobes which indicate the hot-spot regions for the source. Furthermore, regions around the edge of the southern lobe and the top-right part of the northern lobe are missing in the analysis, as our age estimates are limited by the least resolved and lower sensitivity flux density maps. Although the hot-spots do emerge where the contour converges, clearly seen in the northern lobe.

gmw_00009389.name: target 5
gmw_00009389.overview: Target 5 is a radio galaxy classified as an anomalous Fanaroff-Riley Class I-like source [arXiv:2305.07561]. Its spectral age distribution reveals a large zero-age region around the center of its lobes [arXiv:2305.07561].

sources.summary: The paper flags target 5 as an anomalous FR I-like source where excluding poor GMRT maps reveals full lobe structure but leaves a large zero-age central region.

quotes.quote: Anomalous sources Figure t2a shows the spectral age distribution for target 5 from which we obtain age estimates using flux densities from four radio maps. We exclude the radio flux densities from the GMRT survey at 420 MHz, 460 MHz, and 610 MHz as this map has lower signal-to-noise and a lot of artifacts around the source. Excluding the maps for the source allows us to include emission from the entire structure of the lobes and the source. Another noticeable feature of the age map is the giant zero-age region around the center of the lobes. The possible explanation for such a distribution can come from the calibration errors for the maps corresponding to different frequencies or the quality of data obtained during observation.

gmw_00009390.name: target 14
gmw_00009390.overview: Target 14 is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley type-I radio galaxy [arXiv:2305.07561]. Its spectral age distribution shows that plasma flows out along the ejection axis and then spreads outward perpendicularly [arXiv:2305.07561].

sources.summary: The paper identifies target 14 as an FR type-I radio source with its lowest ages along the ejection axis and plasma spreading outward perpendicular to that axis.

quotes.quote: Figure t4b shows the spectral age distribution for target 14 which has the lowest age values along the ejection axis. The age morphology indicates that the plasma flows out along the ejection axis with no termination point and then eventually spreads outwards perpendicular to the ejection axis, these are characteristics of FR-type I sources. For target 5 (Fig. t2a , see Section AS ), 6 (Fig. t2b ), 17 (Fig. t5a ) and 28 (fig. t7d ) we observe similar morphology and features, hence these sources would also be expected to be FR type I.

gmw_00009391.name: target 18
gmw_00009391.overview: Target 18 is a radio galaxy that exhibits a flat spectrum across its entire structure [arXiv:2305.07561]. This flat-spectrum distribution corresponds to zero-age values throughout the source, though its exact morphological category remains difficult to classify without higher resolution data [arXiv:2305.07561].

sources.summary: The paper describes target 18 as anomalous, with a flat-spectrum, zero-age map throughout the source and no detected nearby contaminating MeerKAT emission.

quotes.quote: Figure t5b shows the spectral age distribution for target 18 where we observe that the entire source is filled with zero age values, corresponding to a flat spectrum everywhere in the source. We looked for emission near the source in the sensitive MeerKAT data but did not find any evidence of contaminating sources that could be responsible for such behavior. As we do not see any clear morphology type, it is difficult to categorise the source. High resolution data would be needed to understand the behavior of this source.

gmw_00009392.name: target 20
gmw_00009392.overview: The radio galaxy target 20 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2305.07561]. Spectral analysis reveals an unexpected zero-age distribution spread across the source, which persists even after excluding poor-quality GMRT survey maps [arXiv:2305.07561].

sources.summary: The paper finds target 20 has an unexpected zero-age distribution across the source, persisting after lower-quality GMRT maps are excluded from the analysis.

quotes.quote: Figure t5d shows the spectral age distribution for target 20 which does not show any age distribution with the zero-age region spread across the source. This is not something we expect for any type of morphology and hence points towards problems with the data quality. In order to check the flux maps in our analysis, we excluded maps from the GMRT survey at 610 MHz, 460 MHz, and 420 MHz as the data are of poor quality at these frequencies. We still observe similar behavior in our analysis and thus we can conclude that either the source type is different or the data quality is improper for our analysis.

gmw_00009393.name: target 24
gmw_00009393.overview: Target 24 is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley type-II radio galaxy [arXiv:2305.07561]. Observations reveal a jet structure and radio lobes emerging from the core of the host galaxy [arXiv:2305.07561].

sources.summary: The paper classifies target 24 as FR type-II from MeerKAT lobe and jet structure, while noting missing age-map structure caused by lower-quality non-MeerKAT maps.

quotes.quote: Figure t6d shows the spectral age distribution for target 24 which does not show any prominent structure looking at the age distribution. In the MeerKAT contours, we can see the structure of lobes emerging from the core (marked host galaxy position) with visible jet structure. These are characteristics of an FR type-II. From the age distribution map, we do not clearly see the lobe in the southern region and most of the northern lobe is missing. This is most likely due to the data quality from the maps other than MeerKAT.

gmw_00009394.name: target 25
gmw_00009394.overview: Target 25 is a radio galaxy classified as a wide-angle-tail source [arXiv:2305.07561]. It exhibits an increasing spectral age gradient around its core, which may be related to the timing of active galactic nucleus switch-off and switch-on events [arXiv:2305.07561].

sources.summary: The paper describes target 25 as a wide-angle-tail source with an age gradient around the core, possibly related to AGN switch-off/switch-on timing.

quotes.quote: Figure t7a shows the spectral age distribution for target 25 which shows an increasing age gradient around the core. The morphology is characteristic of a wide-angle tail type source where the age gradient grows away from the core. Another possible reason for such an age distribution could be a short time difference between AGN switch off and switch on where plasma injection was cut off. We also know that data quality plays a vital role so data from 144 MHz (LOFAR) and 610 MHz (GMRT) had to be removed. Data from more representative maps should be able to solve the problem and point out the reason for the anomaly.

gmw_00009395.name: z_ abs = 3.38639 DLA
gmw_00009395.overview: The z_abs = 3.38639 DLA is a high-redshift galaxy candidate [arXiv:astro-ph/0010427] located along the line of sight to the radio-loud quasar PKS 0201+113 [arXiv:1106.2471]. This absorber features turbulent gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0010427], a high spin temperature [arXiv:astro-ph/0010427], and experienced its last major star-formation episode at z ~ 4.3 [arXiv:astro-ph/0010427].

sources.summary: The paper uses PKS 0201+113 as the background quasar for ORT observations, describes it as an extremely compact active galactic nucleus, and considers possible continuum variability over months to years.

quotes.quote: Ooty Radio Telescope (Swarup et al. 1971) observations of PKS 0201+113 were carried out in two stretches, the first from the 29th of December, 1996 to the 5th of January, 1997 and the second from the 14th to the 17th of January, 1997. The source was observed for about 8 hours (broken up into several 2 to 2.5 hour sessions) each day, with a spectrum being recorded every 2 minutes.

PKS0201+113 is an active galactic nucleus which is extremely compact. Hence, variation of its continuum flux over time scales of 2 years would not be extremely surprising. Both the ORT and Arecibo observations did not have simultaneous measurements of the continuum flux but rely on earlier results.

We note that the VLA and WSRT estimates of the quasar flux differ by 20 per cent over a period of 6 months. However, even if this difference is interpreted as being entirely due to variablilty of the source, it is not quite enough to produce the observed differences in the spectra; a change in the flux density by a factor of 2 is required, on a time scale of 2 years.

sources.summary: The paper treats the z=3.38639 DLA toward Q0201+1120 as a high-redshift galaxy candidate in a larger overdensity, with turbulent gas, elevated metallicity, and an earlier star-formation episode.

quotes.quote: We present the results of a series of imaging and spectroscopic observations aimed at identifying and studying the galaxy responsible for the z_ abs = 3.38639 damped \ system in the z_ em = 3.61 QSO Q0201+1120. We find that the DLA is part of a concentration of matter which includes at least four galaxies (probably many more) over linear comoving dimensions greater than 5h^ 1 Mpc. The absorber may be a 0.7 L^ galaxy at an impact parameter of 15 h^ 1 kpc, but follow-up spectroscopy is still required for positive identification.

The gas is turbulent, with many absorption components distributed over 270 km s ^ 1 and a large spin temperature, T_s 4000 K. The metallicity is relatively high for this redshift, Z_ DLA 1/20 Z_ . From consideration of the relative ratios of elements which have different nucleosynthetic timescales, it would appear that the last major episode of star formation in this DLA occurred at z 4.3 , more than 500 Myr prior to the time when we observe it.

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS0201+113 as a radio-loud quasar with compact VLBI structure, a faint secondary component, and multiple absorption systems along the line of sight.

quotes.quote: PKS0201+113 \ is a radio loud quasar at a redshift of z 3.61 condon77,white93 . VLBI observations indicate that its linear size is small < 40 pc and has a smaller secondary component 2 south hodges84,morabito86,stanghellini91 . The secondary source emits on 1 of the total system flux (at 22 GHz). \ shows damped Lyman along the line of sight, several atomic lines, and 21 cm absorption at z 3.387 white93,oya98,kanekar07 .

gmw_00009396.name: G2
gmw_00009396.overview: G2 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy candidate identified as a Un-drop object [arXiv:astro-ph/0010427]. It has an estimated rest-frame ultraviolet luminosity of approximately 0.7 L* and is located near the quasar Q0201+1120, where it may act as a damped Lyman-alpha absorber [arXiv:astro-ph/0010427].

sources.summary: G2 is presented as a U_n-drop galaxy candidate near Q0201+1120 that could be the DLA absorber, with a 15 h^-1 kpc impact parameter and about 0.7 L* UV luminosity.

quotes.quote: Galaxies G2 and oM6, on the other hand, are both U_n drops and in principle could be at the redshifts of either the QSO or the DLA. A possible caveat to these conclusions is that the ground-based photometry of G1 and especially G2 is made somewhat uncertain by blending with each other and with the QSO. However, the WFPC2 F606W magnitudes of all the objects of interest here are consistent with the R and ( G - R ) ground-based measurements in Table 2.

Unfortunately our LRIS spectroscopy did not include object G2; in any case it would be very difficult to separate it from the QSO which is only 2.9 arcsec away and 6 magnitudes brighter. If this is the DLA, then its impact parameter from the QSO sight-line is 15 h^ 1 kpc (proper distance). Our estimated R 25.3 would imply that this galaxy has a rest-frame UV luminosity of about 0.7 L^ , adopting R = 24.54 = L^ at z 3 from the LBG luminosity function derived by Adelberger Steidel (2000) for our cosmology, and making the appropriate luminosity-distance and k corrections.

gmw_00009397.name: oM6
gmw_00009397.overview: The object oM6 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy companion of the QSO Q0201+1120 [arXiv:astro-ph/0010427]. It is resolved into two components, exhibits a Lyman-alpha emission line, and may be in the process of merging with the QSO host [arXiv:astro-ph/0010427].

sources.summary: The paper identifies oM6 as a Lyman-break galaxy companion of Q0201+1120, resolved into two components, with Ly emission, stellar continuum, and possible merger association with the QSO host.

quotes.quote: The object oM6 was among those observed; its spectrum, reproduced in the lower panel of Figure 1, exhibits a prominent \ emission line at z_ em = 3.645 . In some of the exposures the two components of oM6 are partially resolved, showing that the \ emission line is stronger in the western component of the pair in Figure 3. The emission line appears to be spectrally resolved; its profile is symmetric with FWHM 450 km s ^ 1 after correction for the instrumental resolution.

We conclude that the object oM6 is not the DLA absorber, but it is associated with the QSO, from which it is separated by only 19 h^ 1 kpc. There are several other known examples of such QSO companions; the properties of oM6 are similar, for instance, to those of the \ emitter at z = 4.695 near the QSO BR 1202 0725 discussed by Hu, McMahon, Egami (1996). What is particularly interesting, in the case of oM6, is the clear detection of a stellar continuum (see Figure 1). In at least this case, the companion appears to be a galaxy, possibly in the process of merging with the QSO host (Cen 2000), rather than simply a gas cloud lit up in \ by its proximity to the QSO.

gmw_00009398.name: EGS 13004291
gmw_00009398.overview: EGS 13004291 is a star-forming galaxy at redshift z=1.197 that lies significantly above the galaxy main sequence [arXiv:1703.02969]. It exhibits subthermal CO gas excitation and an enhanced dense gas fraction, but its star formation efficiency is consistent with main-sequence galaxies [arXiv:1703.02969].

sources.summary: The paper reports dense molecular-gas observations of EGS 13004291, finding tentative HCN/HNC detections, subthermal CO excitation, enhanced dense-gas fraction, and star-formation efficiency similar to main-sequence galaxies.

quotes.quote: We report observations of dense molecular gas in the star-forming galaxy EGS 13004291 ( z=1.197 ) using the Plateau de Bure Interferometer. We tentatively detect HCN and HNC J=2 1 emission when stacked together at 4 significance, yielding line luminosities of L'_ HCN (J=2 1) =(9 3) 10^ 9 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 and L'_ HNC (J=2 1) =(5 2) 10^ 9 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 respectively.

We also detect CO( J=2 1 ) emission in EGS 13004291, showing that the excitation in the previously detected CO( J=3 2 ) line is subthermal ( r_ 32 =0.65 0.15 ). We find a line luminosity ratio of / =0.17 0.07 , as an indicator of the dense gas fraction. This is consistent with the median ratio observed in z>1 galaxies ( / =0.16 0.07 ) and nearby ULIRGs ( / =0.13 0.03 ), but higher than in local spirals ( / =0.04 0.02 ). Although EGS 13004291 lies significantly above the galaxy main sequence at z 1 , we do not find an elevated star formation efficiency (traced by / ) as in local starbursts, but a value consistent with main-sequence galaxies. The enhanced dense gas fraction, the subthermal gas excitation, and the lower than expected star formation efficiency of the dense molecular gas in EGS 13004291 suggest that different star formation properties may prevail in starbursts.

gmw_00009399.name: Host galaxy of FRB 20210117A
gmw_00009399.overview: The host galaxy of FRB 20210117A is a dwarf galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.214 [arXiv:2211.16790]. It exhibits a low current star formation rate and lacks detectable radio emission [arXiv:2211.16790].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the FRB 20210117A host as a z=0.214 dwarf galaxy with little current star formation, low radio emission, older-source indicators, and excess DM from the FRB environment.

quotes.quote: The sub-arcsecond localization of the burst led to the identification of its host galaxy at a z=0.214(1) . Optical observations reveal the host to be a dwarf galaxy with little on-going star formation, very different to the dwarf host galaxies of known repeating FRBs 20121102A, and 20190520B. We find an excess DM contribution from the host and attribute it to the FRB's local environment. We do not find any radio emission from the FRB site or host galaxy. The low magnetized environment and lack of a persistent radio source (PRS) indicate that the FRB source is older than those found in other dwarf host galaxies and establish the diversity of FRB sources in dwarf galaxy environments.

The resulting model reveals a dwarf galaxy with a stellar mass of log(M _* /M _ ) = 8.56^ +0.06 _ 0.08 and mass-weighted age of 5.06^ +0.91 _ 1.34 Gyr (Gordon et al 2022., in prep.), a metric less biased by the youngest and brightest stars in the galaxy compared to traditional light-weighted ages Conroy2013 . The host has a low current star formation rate (SFR) with an average SFR over the past 100 Myr of 0.014^ +0.008 _ 0.004 M _ yr ^ 1 .

gmw_00009400.name: IC 698
gmw_00009400.overview: IC 698 is classified as a likely Sa spiral galaxy that exhibits irregular spiral structures in its outer disk [arXiv:1807.09955]. The galaxy is likely perturbed by a galaxy interaction, which may drive its high star formation efficiency [arXiv:1807.09955].

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 698 as an extreme SFE outlier, describing it as likely Sa with irregular outer spiral structure and probable interaction-driven perturbation.

quotes.quote: However, there are two extreme outliers (IC 676 and IC 698) deviating from the M_ SFR and f_ SSFR relations in Figure fig_HI_SFR_GM . Both galaxies have much lower f_ or higher SFE than the other counterparts with similar SFR and M_* . IC 698 is likely a Sa galaxy with obvious irregular spiral structures in the outer disk, and is likely perturbed by galaxy interaction, which may also drive the high SFE of this galaxy. SFE indicates the efficiency that galaxies convert the gas to molecular gas and then stars.

gmw_00009401.name: J 0240-231
gmw_00009401.overview: J 0240-231 is a quasar and a Gigahertz-peaked spectrum source [arXiv:2011.12592]. It is a highly polarized object with a complex Faraday spectrum and a flat-spectrum radio flux that exhibits variability [arXiv:2011.12592].

sources.summary: The paper describes J 0240-231 as a highly polarized QSO/GPS source with variable flat-spectrum flux and several Faraday components indicating a complex Faraday spectrum.

quotes.quote: AGN J0240 231 has been identified to be a QSO and also a Gigahertz-peaked spectrum (GPS) source Kuehr1981, Stanghellini1998a and our spectrum is in the range where synchrotron self absorption may become apparent. The results for the flat spectrum source J 0240-231 also indicate variability. The total flux of J 0240-231 in the 1.36 - 2.0 GHz frequency range has been reported to vary over the range 4.4 - 7.1 Jy Kuehr1981, Condon1998, Stanghellini1998a, Tingay2003a, Stanghellini2005 .

J 0240-231 : This is the most polarized source in our sample with several Faraday components that are above the detection threshold -- see Figure 0240-rmclean . Several Faraday components are seen in Figure 0240-rmclean and indicate that the fainter components are Faraday thick. Generally, the Faraday spectrum is complex with _ = 37.7 rad m ^ 2 .

gmw_00009402.name: Galaxy 178
gmw_00009402.overview: Galaxy 178 is a member of a substructure within the A2626 cluster [arXiv:2303.10141]. Although its low star formation rate and velocity suggest it could be a backsplash galaxy, its gas content indicates it is on its first infall trajectory [arXiv:2303.10141].

sources.summary: The paper describes Galaxy 178 as an A2626 substructure member whose compressed gas morphology suggests ram pressure stripping while its velocity and gas content imply first infall.

quotes.quote: Galaxy 178 is located in a substructure in A2626. Although it is not very close to the cluster core ( 1.5 R _ 200 ), the morphology hints at ongoing ram-pressure stripping. Its contours are compressed on the northern side of the disc and the outer contour seems to suggest that the gas is pushed to the south. Interestingly, galaxy 178 is moving at a velocity similar to the mean velocity of the cluster and has a low star formation rate. Therefore one may conclude that galaxy 178 is a backsplash galaxy, but the content of the galaxy suggests it is still on its first infall trajectory.

gmw_00009403.name: Galaxy 130
gmw_00009403.overview: Galaxy 130 is an early-type, barred ring galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0382 [arXiv:2303.10141]. It features central gas emission as well as clumps of gas within its faint outer stellar ring [arXiv:2303.10141].

sources.summary: The paper excludes Galaxy 130 as a foreground object and describes it as an early-type barred ring galaxy with gas emission in its center and outer stellar ring.

quotes.quote: Galaxy 130 is a foreground galaxy at a redshift of z _ HI =0.0382, and therefore is excluded from our overall analysis. Its optical morphology is that of an early-type, barred ring galaxy. emission is not only found in the bright central regions of the galaxy, but clumps of gas are also found in the faint outer stellar ring surrounding the bar, similar to NGC 4736 bosma1977 .

gmw_00009404.name: Galaxy 88
gmw_00009404.overview: Galaxy 88 is a star-forming galaxy with a regular morphology [arXiv:2303.10141]. Although previously considered a jellyfish candidate, it is classified as a non-jellyfish galaxy due to its regular structure and moderate gas deficiency [arXiv:2303.10141].

sources.summary: The paper treats Galaxy 88 as an A2626 SFMS outlier with regular morphology, moderate gas deficiency, short depletion time, and a rejected jellyfish classification.

quotes.quote: However, some galaxies (e.g. substructure galaxy 19 and non-substructure galaxy 88 in A2626 in Fig. fig:SFR_Mstar_tdep_HIdef ) have higher SFR than the SFMS. Their morphologies appear to be fairly regular (insets of the top left panel in Fig. fig:SFR_Mstar_tdep_HIdef ) while they are moderately deficient and have short depletion times. Galaxy 88 was identified as a jellyfish candidate galaxy from the B-band optical image by . However, since its morphology is regular and it is only moderately deficient, this galaxy was later identified as a non-jellyfish galaxy in .

gmw_00009405.name: galaxy 105
gmw_00009405.overview: Galaxy 105 is characterized by a strongly offset disc relative to its optical centre [arXiv:2303.10141]. It has nearby companion galaxies and exhibits a dusty optical color that suggests internal reddening [arXiv:2303.10141].

sources.summary: The paper uses galaxy 105 as a morphology example, noting a strongly offset disc, nearby companions among deficient Swarm galaxies, and dusty optical color.

quotes.quote: For example, galaxy 105 in Fig. fig:Amod_offset_class1 has a strongly offset disc with respect to the optical centre. Focusing on the morphologies of the most deficient galaxies in the Swarm (galaxy 25, 60, 70, 105 in Fig. fig:SFR_Mstar_tdep_HIdef ), we note that all of them have nearby companions, and two of these galaxies (galaxy 60, 105) have asymmetric or offset discs. From the optical colour, galaxy 105 seems dusty, that might have caused some internal reddening.

gmw_00009406.name: UGC9837
gmw_00009406.overview: UGC9837 is a nearly face-on spiral galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.009 [arXiv:1111.5205]. It is morphologically classified as SAB(s) and has been studied to map its ionized gas, local nebular reddening, oxygen abundance, and star formation rate [arXiv:1111.5205].

sources.summary: The paper studies UGC9837 with integral-field spectroscopy, deriving spatially resolved gas reddening, oxygen abundance, ionisation, and star formation maps, then simulating how those properties would appear at high redshift.

quotes.quote: We present here a detailed study of ionised gas in one galaxy, UGC9837. This Local Universe ( z 0.009 ) galaxy is positioned nearly face-on towards us, making it a good candidate for 2D galactic disk studies. The data were observed as part of the PMAS/PPAK, one of the worlds widest FOV IFU, survey of 48 nearby galaxies presented by esther11 . This survey was part of the preparatory phase of the CALIFA survey sanchez10,sanchez11b , a much larger IFS survey comprising observations of 600 nearby galaxies. We use our data to derive for UGC9837 2-D distributions of: 1) local nebular reddening estimates based on the Balmer decrement; 2) oxygen abundance distributions based on a suite of strong line diagnostics incorporating reddening-corrected H , H , [ O ii ] , [ O iii ] , [ N ii ] , and [ S ii ] line ratios; 3) measurements of ionisation structure in H ii regions and diffuse ionised gas using the well-known and most updated forbidden-line diagnostics in the oxygen and nitrogen lines; 4) the distribution of the surface star formation rate (SFR) traced by the H line intensity. The derived spatial distribution of these properties allows us to test the inside-out growing scenario of galaxies.

t UGC9837 is a spiral galaxy of magnitude 14.6 in the g band, located at redshift z 0.009 , and morphologically classified as SAB(s) devaucouleurs91 . In addition to the optical (SDSS), it has previously been observed at other wavelengths: UV GALEX, gildepaz07 , NIR 2MASS, skrutskie06 and IRAS, beichman88 , see Table tab:photom .

gmw_00009407.name: UGC 03685
gmw_00009407.overview: UGC 03685 is an Sb-type spiral galaxy that features both a bar and a ring structure [arXiv:1112.4058]. While its inclination remains highly uncertain, photometric data suggest the galaxy's disk may possess an abnormally low mass-to-light ratio [arXiv:1112.4058].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 03685 as a barred, ringed Sb galaxy with uncertain inclination; low disk M/L is supported for the photometric inclination but remains uncertain overall.

quotes.quote: UGC 03685 is an Sb-type galaxy. Just as NGC 6814 and NGC 5921, it has a bar and a ring, while having a small inclination. The inclination determined from the H_ kinematics is very uncertain ( i=12^o 16 ) (Epinat et al. 2008). According to our photometric data, i=40^o 15 (see Fig. 2), which is consistent with the NED ( 33^o ) and Hyperleda ( 55^o ) estimates.

In Table table4 , the disk rotational velocity v_ max disk estimated photometrically is compared with the total rotational velocities v_ max corresponding to i_1=12^o and i_2=40^o . The conclusion about an abnormally low mass-to-light ratio for the disk of UGC 03685 is confirmed if we take the photometrically estimated inclination i_2 and is not confirmed for i_1 . If we take into account the large error in the kinematic inclination estimate, then the conclusion about an abnormally low M/L remains possible, though uncertain.

gmw_00009408.name: NGC 1315
gmw_00009408.overview: NGC 1315 is an inactive control galaxy selected from the LLAMA sample [arXiv:1912.07734]. It has an estimated stellar velocity dispersion of 77 kilometers per second [arXiv:1912.07734].

sources.summary: The paper uses inactive control galaxy NGC 1315 to test AGN continuum contamination, combining its spectrum with a synthetic AGN continuum and showing the fit remains stable.

quotes.quote: First, we select an inactive control galaxy (NGC 1315) from the LLAMA sample; the stellar velocity dispersion of this galaxy is estimated as _ = 77 5 km s ^ 1 using pPXF. Then, the synthetic AGN continuum was combined with the NGC 1315 spectrum. As expected, the AGN continuum has no direct effect on the _ estimations for any reasonable AGN continuum level (< 70 ), if the continuum is modelled using an adequate number of additive polynomials. In the top panel of Fig. synthetic , we present a synthetic AGN spectrum which consists of the spectrum of the inactive galaxy NGC 1315 (shown as red line) and a fairly strong ( 70 ) model AGN continuum (blue line).

gmw_00009409.name: MCG-05-14-12
gmw_00009409.overview: MCG-05-14-12 is classified as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1912.07734]. The galaxy exhibits blue-shifted emission lines in its spectra, which indicate outflowing gas [arXiv:1912.07734].

sources.summary: The paper fits MCG-05-14-12 with a double-Gaussian broad-line profile, detects blue-shifted emission, classifies it as a narrow-line Seyfert 1, and applies an average rotation correction.

quotes.quote: In the case of MCG-05-14-12, NGC 1365 and NGC 2992 we detected blue-shifted emission lines in the spectra (> 500 km s ^ 1 ), which were also fitted with additional single Gaussian models. We excluded these blue-shifted emission lines, when we estimate our final BLR profiles of the LLAMA AGNs. We present the emission line fitting of our type 1 AGN sample in the Appendix (see appendix1 , appendix2 , appendix3 , appendix4 ). MCG-05-14-12 and MCG-06-30-15 both show low emission line widths (FWHM < 1700 km s ^ 1 ) and low [ O III ] /H ratios (0.2 and 0.9, respectively). According to the definition of narrow-line Seyfert 1 (NLS1) galaxies (FWHM < 2000 km s ^ 1 and [ O III ] /H < 3) reported by OsPo , we classify them as such.

However, We are still missing SINFONI observations for the following galaxies: MCG-05-14-12, NGC 4235, NGC 5128, and the spatially resolved stellar kinematics for NGC 3783, MCG-06-30-15. For these galaxies, the obtained stellar velocity dispersion estimates are reduced 10 the average galaxy rotation contribution to _ for the LLAMA sample (Shimizu et al. in preparation).

gmw_00009410.name: W0831+0140
gmw_00009410.overview: W0831+0140 is a high-redshift hot dust-obscured galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2104.09495]. The system exhibits a complex velocity field, which likely indicates the presence of multiple narrow-velocity components or merging clumps of galaxies [arXiv:2104.09495].

sources.summary: The paper robustly detects the line in W0831+0140 and highlights its complex velocity distribution, probably reflecting multiple components or merging clumps or galaxies.

quotes.quote: The line is robustly detected (SNR 20) in four out of the seven Hot DOGs in the sample: W0134--2922, W0220+0137, W0831+0140, and W2246--0526, the latter of which was already reported in DS2016 .

The redshifts derived from the line, z _ CII , for those galaxies in which the line is identified and the profile fully sampled have velocity offsets with respect to z _ UV of v -37 75, 989 74, 49 62 and --64 55 for W0134--2922, W0220+0137, W0831+0140, and W2246--0526, respectively (see Table  t:linefluxes ).

The case of W0831+0140 is of particular interest. In this source, the complex velocity field is likely caused by the presence of multiple narrow-velocity components that can be seen in the spectral cube, suggesting that this source could actually be a system of clumps or galaxies in the process of merging. However, higher angular resolution and SNR observations are needed to accurately disentangle these components spatially and in velocity space.

gmw_00009411.name: W0236+0528
gmw_00009411.overview: W0236+0528 is a high-redshift galaxy classified as a hot dust-obscured galaxy [arXiv:2104.09495]. Spectroscopic observations of the source show a tentative line detection alongside a possible alternate emission feature, though high noise levels limit definitive identification [arXiv:2104.09495].

sources.summary: The paper reports only a tentative line detection for W0236+0528, notes a possible alternate redder feature, and excludes it from later figures and discussion because of uncertainties.

quotes.quote: We tentatively detect the line in the spectrum of W0236+0528 at roughly the frequency (--161 ) where it was expected to appear, based on the z _ UV (see Figure  f:spectra ). However, there seems to be another spectral feature in emission detected at a SNR 4 in the adjacent, redder SPW, with a velocity offset of 950 . Given the mostly positive velocity offsets found for other Hot DOGs in the sample, we cannot discard that this feature could actually be the line. Unfortunately there is no additional spectroscopic information for this source, and the noise in the entire side-band is large. We attempted to create an image of the line using two velocity ranges encompassing the frequencies of the two possible feature identifications, but no source could be found in either.

gmw_00009412.name: NGC 5963
gmw_00009412.overview: NGC 5963 is a low-inclination disk galaxy [arXiv:1608.06264]. It features multiple tightly wound spiral arms, with four arms visible in its structure [arXiv:1608.06264].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5963 as a low-inclination disk with multiple tightly wound spiral arms whose rotation-curve shape MOND cannot reproduce.

quotes.quote: The 3.6 m and H images show multiple spiral arms in NGC 5963.
In particular, NGC 5963, the galaxy with the less inclination in our sample, shows the presence of four tightly wound spiral arms.

MOND is also unable to reproduce the shape of the rotation curve of NGC 5963; the observed rotation curve at the peak of the disc is still rising, but MOND predicts that it should slowly drop in a similar fashion as the Newtonian circular velocity does, because x>1 .

gmw_00009413.name: NGC 6689
gmw_00009413.overview: NGC 6689 is a high-inclination disk galaxy [arXiv:1608.06264]. Deprojection of the galaxy reveals signs of a spiral-like structure, and its kinematics show no detected deviations from circular motions [arXiv:1608.06264].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 6689 as a high-inclination disk with spiral-like structure, circular motions, and an acceptable MOND rotation-curve fit.

quotes.quote: The high inclination of NGC 6689 precludes determining details of its structure, though a deprojection shows signs of spiral-like structure both in the 3.6 m and H images.
In the case of NGC 6689, no deviations from circular motions were detected.
We see that the fit is good for NGC 3621 and NGC 5949, acceptable for NGC 6689, but poor for NGC 4605, 5963 and 6503.

gmw_00009414.name: DESJ2346-5203
gmw_00009414.overview: DESJ2346-5203 is a candidate lensed system containing a background quasar at a redshift of z=1.87 [arXiv:1808.04838]. The candidate deflector is an emission-line, late-type galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.48 [arXiv:1808.04838].

sources.summary: The paper models DESJ2346-5203 as a candidate lensed quasar system with an emission-line deflector, but concludes it is not a classic quadruply imaged quasar.

quotes.quote: Lens models and discussion of the case of DESJ2346-5203 The NTT spectra of DESJ2346-5203 (Figure fig:NTT_spec_D2346 ) are consistent with a small separation (subarcsecond separation) lens. The candidate deflector is an emission line galaxy at z_d=0.48 , while the source is a QSO at z_s=1.87 .
