This redshift is similar to those of EROs which have been discovered in near-IR surveys and have redshifts constrained by the shape of their continua in low-resolution near-IR spectroscopy (e.g. Cimatti et al. 1999) and also those found in front of the quasar QSO 1213-0017 (Liu et al. 2000).

gmw_00009466.name: NGC 4312
gmw_00009466.overview: NGC 4312 is a spiral galaxy containing a central bulge [arXiv:astro-ph/0512415]. The stellar population of this bulge exhibits hydrogen-beta emission that is greater than expected from oxygen III emission, which can lead to overestimations of its age [arXiv:astro-ph/0512415].

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 4312 as one of three spiral bulges whose Hβ-based ages are overestimated, producing too-low metallicities, while higher-order Balmer indices reduce the discrepancy.

quotes.quote: For three bulges (NGC 4157, NGC 4217, NGC 4312) \ indicates significantly older ages, all three well above the age of the universe. Interestingly, exactly those objects show H _ emission greater than expected from [ O iii ] (PS02). Therefore their ages must clearly be overestimated, a problem by which the higher-order Balmer line \ is much less affected. In these three cases \ yields metallicities that are too low in line with the overestimated ages.
NGC 4157, NGC 4217, NGC 4312 still show slightly older ages from , but the deviation approaches the general scatter in the relationship.

gmw_00009467.name: western source
gmw_00009467.overview: The western source is a low-mass companion galaxy to I Zw 1 that exhibits a predominantly old and evolved stellar population [arXiv:0705.2383]. It shows a lack of star formation activity and displays evidence of an ongoing tidal interaction with its host [arXiv:0705.2383].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the western source as I Zw 1’s likely low-mass companion, with old stellar populations, little recent star formation, and evidence for tidal interaction with the host.

quotes.quote: Furthermore, I Zw 1 shows indications for an ongoing minor merger with the companion galaxy marked as ``western source'' in Fig. fig:zwgeneral . This source is found with a velocity shift of only (170 110) \ km \ s^ 1 with respect to the systemic velocity of I Zw 1 and with stellar absorption features of an old stellar population 2001ApJ...555..719C . An ongoing merger with the ``western source'' is suggested by an extended tidal-tail structure in H i 1999ApJ...510L...7L and by a possible small-scale tidal bridge between the companion and the north-western spiral arm of I Zw 1 2003A&A...405..959S .
The red color of the western companion supports the scenario of a predominantly old and evolved stellar population (see Sect. sec:results.spectra ).

Although less conclusive in consequence of the low S/N, the H and K band spectra of the western companion (Fig. fig:compspek ) seem to support the findings by 2001ApJ...555..719C that the companion shows the spectral properties of an old stellar population. The lines in Fig. fig:compspek are marked at the redshift of z=0.0616 derived for the likely companion by 2001ApJ...555..719C . Many of these lines match with absorption features in the spectra. In particular, the hydrogen lines are found in absorption rather than emission. This suggests a lack of star formation activity in the western companion.

gmw_00009468.name: TXS 1541+050
gmw_00009468.overview: TXS 1541+050 is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy [arXiv:1910.00952]. It is identified as a candidate source of very-high-energy TeV gamma-ray emission that may be detectable by current ground-based Cherenkov observatories [arXiv:1910.00952].

sources.summary: The paper highlights TXS 1541+050 as an FR I radio galaxy among the best new TeV candidates, with simulated short CTA observations suggesting detectability by current Cherenkov observatories.

quotes.quote: Only potentially new TeV sources with a TS 100 in the 50 hours observations with E_ cut =0.5 TeV have been considered, i.e. 3C 78, 4C +39.12, B2 1113+29, Cen B, B2 1447+27 and TXS 1541+050. Not surprisingly, all of these sources are classified as FR I. Our likelihood analysis of the simulated five hours observations shows that 3C 78 and TXS 1541+050 can be detected with TS > 25 even with short integration times, in the case E_ cut =0.5 TeV. This suggests that a VHE detection of these sources is most likely already within reach of current ground-based ray observatories.

We have found that two FR I sources, i.e., 3C 78 and TXS 1541+050, would still be detected with >5 significance within 5 hours, under the assumption of E_ cut =0.5 TeV. This implies that these two sources are very likely within reach of current-generation Cherenkov observatories, if observed for a full targeted campaign, i.e. with an integration time of a few tens of hours.

gmw_00009469.name: SDSS J2129 0018
gmw_00009469.overview: SDSS J2129 0018 is an extremely red quasar [arXiv:1802.04272]. X-ray spectral analysis of the source reveals an absorbed power-law column consistent with its hardness-ratio analysis [arXiv:1802.04272].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J2129 0018 as a strongly detected extremely red quasar with enough XMM counts for spectral fitting and an absorbed power-law column consistent with hardness-ratio analysis.

quotes.quote: Specifically, SDSS J0006+1215 and SDSS J2129 0018 are the two most strongly detected objects with sufficient counts to enable spectral analysis.

In Figure pic:xray we show the X-ray spectra of two sources, SDSS J0006+1215 and SDSS J2129 0018, which have > 100 detected counts in their XMM data, sufficient to perform a spectral fit. For both sources, we fit a power-law model combined with foreground Galactic absorption determined from star92 and a rest-frame absorber which is intrinsic to the source.

For SDSS J2129 0018, the best-fit spectral model yields a similar absorbing column of N_ H (6.1 0.6) 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 , again with a marginally flatter spectral slope of 1.7 0.1 than the one we had assumed for the HRs. In this case, the spectroscopic N_ H value is consistent with the one resulting from our HR analysis.

gmw_00009470.name: host galaxy of GRB 011211
gmw_00009470.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 011211 is a Lyman-alpha emitting system located at a redshift of 2.1434 [arXiv:2105.09314]. It exhibits a multi-component morphology with extended emission and a redshifted line peak [arXiv:2105.09314].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 011211 host as a multi-component Lyman-alpha emitter with extended emission, redshift z=2.1434, a redshifted line peak, and low inferred SFR.

quotes.quote: The photometry of the afterglow and host galaxy of GRB 011211 has been published in Jakobsson2003 . The detection of emission through narrow-band filters was reported by Fynbo2003 . The host galaxy of GRB 011211 has a multi-component morphology Jakobsson2003,Fynbo2003 . The GRB site is in the southeast part of the system, while the emission peaks in the central-north part of the system and extends over the entire system.

From the [ OIII ] 4959,5007 , we derive a redshift of 2.1434 0.0001 . The peak is redshifted by 280 30 \ km \ s^ 1 compared to the host galaxy redshift. The spatial extension of the line in the 2D spectrum is 2 5, compared to 1 8 for the brightest nebular emission line [ OIII ] 5007 .

gmw_00009471.name: GRB 060926 host galaxy
gmw_00009471.overview: The GRB 060926 host galaxy is a high-redshift system located at a redshift of z=3.2090 [arXiv:2105.09314]. It exhibits a high star formation rate of 20.2 solar masses per year and features extended, asymmetric Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:2105.09314].

sources.summary: The paper presents first-time X-shooter observations of the GRB 060926 host, finding z=3.2090, blended host components, extended asymmetric Lyman-alpha emission, and high SFR.

quotes.quote: GRB 060926 In the VLT/FORS1 image (Prog. ID: 079.A-0253(A); P.I.: P. Jakobsson) shown in Fig. FCs , we see an offset between the GRB position and the brightest part of the system. Both are covered by the slit used for the host spectroscopic observations. In the X-shooter host galaxy observations obtained under program 085.A-0795(A) (presented here for the first time; PI: H. Flores; see Table Tlog ), we identify the [ OIII ] 5007 , [ OII ] 3726 , H and lines (see Fig. LyaLines ).

These lines, except , are used to derive a redshift of z=3.2090 0.0001 for the host galaxy which is consistent with the redshift derived in Kruhler2015 with a different data set. Even with seeing conditions of 0 8, from the emission lines it is not possible to separate the different parts of the host galaxy systems. The spatial extension of the line in the 2D spectrum is 2 7, compared to 1 4 for the brightest nebular emission line [ OIII ] 5007 .
We determine a SFR = 20.2 4.0 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 , assuming H = 2.86 H Osterbrock1989 . The emission from the host galaxy is an asymmetric line redshifted by 250 20 \ km \ s^ 1 from the systemic redshift.

gmw_00009472.name: GRB 070110 host galaxy
gmw_00009472.overview: The GRB 070110 host galaxy is a high-redshift galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.3523 [arXiv:2105.09314]. It is a star-forming galaxy with a star formation rate of 1.9 solar masses per year and exhibits asymmetric, redshifted emission [arXiv:2105.09314].

sources.summary: The paper reports previously unpublished UVB spectroscopy of the GRB 070110 host, measuring z=2.3523, SFR, asymmetric redshifted emission, and DLA/ISM properties.

quotes.quote: The near-infrared X-shooter spectrum of GRB 070110 host galaxy has been studied in Kruhler2015 , but the UVB spectrum has not been previously published. The [ OIII ] 4959,5007 , [ OII ] 3726 , H and lines are identified in the host galaxy spectrum (see Fig. LyaLines ), allowing a redshift determination of z=2.3523 0.0001 . The measured H flux corresponds to a SFR = 1.9 0.6 \ M_ \ yr^ 1 .

The emission from the host galaxy shows an asymmetric profile and is redshifted by 285 40 \ km \ s^ 1 from the systemic redshift (see also Table TLya for other properties). The spatial extension of the line in the 2D spectrum is 2 1, compared to 2 0 for the brightest nebular emission line [ OIII ] 5007 . The afterglow spectrum of GRB 070110 has been published in Fynbo2009 .

gmw_00009473.name: UDS19771
gmw_00009473.overview: UDS19771 is a foreground galaxy located approximately 3 arcseconds northwest of the high-redshift target UDS19627 [arXiv:1204.3099]. It has a photometric redshift of 0.58 and an estimated stellar mass of 10.32 dex [arXiv:1204.3099].

sources.summary: UDS19771 is the nearby foreground galaxy whose photometric redshift and stellar mass are used in a simple lens model for biasing UDS19627.

quotes.quote: The bright galaxy 3 north-west of the target is at lower redshift UDS19771, z_ phot =0.58 , williams2010 . This galaxy may be boosting the brightness of our target slightly, through the gravitational lensing effect (see Section sec:caveats )
There is a foreground ( z_ phot =0.58 ) galaxy 3 NW of UDS19627, which can potentially influence the fit. We perform three different fits to estimate the uncertainty it introduces.

We assume for the foreground galaxy a photometric redshift z_ l =0.58 and stellar mass (M_ *,l /M_ )=10.32^ +0.07 _ 0.05 , where both values are derived from broadband B, ,i ,z, J and K band photometry, using the same model library as for our main target. We then compare this stellar mass value to those of the SLACS lens galaxies bolton2006, grillo2010 to estimate the effective velocity dispersion _ SIS of a singular isothermal sphere (SIS) model that we adopt here to describe the total mass distribution of the lens.

gmw_00009474.name: Sculptor
gmw_00009474.overview: Sculptor is a dwarf spheroidal satellite galaxy of the Milky Way located in the Southern hemisphere [arXiv:2210.01220]. It lies at a distance of approximately 86 kiloparsecs, and its dark matter density distribution has been estimated using the dynamics of 1,365 member stars [arXiv:2210.01220].

sources.summary: Sculptor is the CTA mock-observation target, treated as a point-like Milky Way satellite dSph with known distance, Galactic coordinates, member-star dynamics, and J-factor profile.

quotes.quote: We focus on the dwarf spheroidal galaxy Sculptor, which is selected for the DM search programme of CTA . This article is organised as follows: in Sec. \ sec:exp_flux , we recall the ray flux computation and its key components. In Sec. \ sec:Astro_model , we describe the properties of the considered dwarf spheroidal galaxy Sculptor as well as the CTA mock data simulations.

In this work, we focus on Sculptor, a dSph satellite of the Milky Way located in the Southern hemisphere at Galactic coordinates ( =287.62 , b = -83.16 ) at a distance of 86 6 kpc. The dynamics of the dSph and hence its DM density distribution is estimated based on 1365 member stars . We use the J factor profile and its associated uncertainties provided in Ref. \ as a function of angular radius, whose total J factor reaches _ 10 (J/ GeV ^2 cm ^ 5 sr ) = 18.63^ +0.14 _ 0.08 .

gmw_00009475.name: COSMOS 0038+4133
gmw_00009475.overview: COSMOS 0038+4133 is a strong gravitational lens system with a configuration close to an Einstein ring [arXiv:1610.01599]. Nearby massive galaxies in its close proximity contribute external shear and convergence to the lens system [arXiv:1610.01599].

sources.summary: The paper applies its multi-plane lensing formalism to COSMOS 0038+4133, an Einstein-ring-like strong lens system whose nearby massive galaxies contribute external shear and convergence.

quotes.quote: We next apply our formalism to the strong lens system COSMOS 0038+4133 and its environment to test the capabilities of our method on real data. The lens system COSMOS 0038+4133 ( R.A. =10^ h 00^ m 38.2^ s DEC =+02^ 41 33 J2000) was chosen as our primary target as the configuration is close to an Einstein ring and there are massive galaxies in its close proximity that potentially add significant external shear and convergence contributions to the lens system. Detailed information about the lens system is provided in appendix ap:lens_description and the data, catalogs and derived stellar masses of the galaxies in appendix ap:cosmos_data .

Figure fig:shear_field illustrates the environment of COSMOS0038+4133 and the influence on the shear of the nearby galaxies in two different zoom-out regions. The shear estimate converges with a mask of 6.5 arcmin around the strong lens system.

gmw_00009476.name: TXS 1433+205
gmw_00009476.overview: TXS 1433+205 is a gamma-ray emitting Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.748 [arXiv:2212.08789]. Radio observations reveal a resolved core-lobe morphology consisting of a central core, an extended radio jet, and two lobes with hotspots [arXiv:2212.08789].

sources.summary: The paper identifies TXS 1433+205 as a z=0.748 ray-emitting FR II radio galaxy with resolved core-lobe morphology, not a BL Lac, and among the most distant known examples.

quotes.quote: In this Letter, we present the identification and multi-frequency properties of the ray detected object 4FGL J1435.5+2021, associated with the radio source TXS 1433+205 bayesian association probability 0.99, 2020ApJ...892..105A , as the most distant (as of now) ray emitting FR II radio galaxy at z=0.748 , revealed by the ongoing Very Large Array Sky Survey VLASS, 2020PASP..132c5001L . This object has been misclassified as a BL Lac in the 4LAC-DR2 catalog though we show that its multi-frequency properties resemble well that of ray detected FR II radio galaxies.

We browsed the VLASS quick look images https://science.nrao.edu/vlass/data-access/vlass-epoch-1-quick-look-users-guide of TXS 1433+205 and found a resolved radio morphology. As can be seen in Figure fig:1 , the object is resolved into three components besides the extended radio jet: a central core and two lobes with hotspots. These radio observations, along with the optical (Section sec5 ) and multi-wavelength (Section sec3 ) observations, unambiguously confirm that TXS 1433+205 is a ray emitting FR II radio galaxy.

gmw_00009477.name: Mrk 577
gmw_00009477.overview: The disk of the Seyfert galaxy Mrk 577 is largely featureless in the R band [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741]. Its nucleus is visible in narrowband emission, showing a slight northwest offset that may be caused by circumnuclear dust extinction [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741].

sources.summary: The paper finds Mrk 577 largely featureless outside its nucleus, with only nuclear narrowband emission, a slight northwest offset in one map, and no planar or extraplanar emission.

quotes.quote: The disk of Mrk 577 (Figure f:mrk577 ) 6 is remarkably featureless in the R band, and only the nucleus is visible in the and the maps. The nucleus in the map is slightly offset in the NW direction compared to the brightest peak in the R band. This may be due to dust extinction in the circumnuclear environment. No planar or extraplanar or emission is observed for this galaxy.

gmw_00009478.name: UGC 1479
gmw_00009478.overview: UGC 1479 appears relatively featureless in the R band, apart from its nuclear region [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741]. Its emission map reveals two spiral-arm-like structures, diffuse disk emission, and a nuclear extension resembling a bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 1479 as mostly featureless in broad band but showing spiral-arm-like emission, nuclear extension, diffuse disk emission, and no detected extraplanar emission.

quotes.quote: The R band image of UGC 1479 (Figure f:ugc1479 ) 7 appears relatively featureless apart from the prominent nuclear region. The map clearly shows emission from two spiral arm-like structures, as well as extended emission from the nucleus. The morphology of the latter is reminiscent of a bar joining the base of the two spiral arms. Diffuse emission is observed only within the disk, and extends 32 (10.2 kpc) to the N and 25 (8.0 kpc) to the S of the nucleus. The map also reveals emission from the nucleus, and faint diffuse emission from the disk, which seems offset to the E, possibly due to inclination effects.

gmw_00009479.name: Ark 79
gmw_00009479.overview: Ark 79 is a Seyfert galaxy that features a nuclear structure with two distinct narrowband components [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741]. These components include a primary broadband peak and a secondary nuclear component separated by approximately 850 parsecs, alongside a possible faint inner bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741].

sources.summary: The paper reports Ark 79 has a nuclear structure with two narrowband components, including a newly detected secondary nuclear component separated by about 850 pc.

quotes.quote: The R band image of Ark 79 (Figure f:ark79 ) 8 shows one nucleus and a structure which may be a faint inner bar. The nucleus is the main contributor to the emission in the and maps. When shown on a suitable greyscale, the narrowband maps reveal two components to the nucleus (see a zoomed-in version of the nuclear region in Figure f:ark79z ). 9 The brightest peak in is located to the E, centered on the broadband peak, and the fainter one is offset to the W. However, the two components have a similar brightness in , meaning that the / ionization ratio will be much larger for the offset peak compared to the actual center of the galaxy.

gmw_00009480.name: NGC 3735
gmw_00009480.overview: NGC 3735 is a highly inclined spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741, arXiv:2004.14383] with its southeastern side identified as the approaching side [arXiv:2004.14383]. The galaxy features compact and diffuse disk emission, alongside possible extraplanar regions that may indicate minor-axis outflows [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741].

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3735 has compact and diffuse disk emission, off-planar regions, and possible extraplanar emission that may signal minor-axis outflows.

quotes.quote: Strong emission from many compact regions in the disk, diffuse emission between these regions, and emission from an extended region around the nucleus are clearly visible in the map (Figure f:ngc3735 ). 16 The map shows a few compact regions, most of which are off-planar and have counterparts in the map. As shown in high contrast and maps (Figure f:fourha ), possible extraplanar regions emitting in , but barely visible in , are seen 38 (6.6 kpc) to the NE and 27 (4.7 kpc) to the SW of the nucleus. The high inclination of the galaxy makes these regions more likely to be extraplanar than in the galactic disk. The diffuse emission visible in the high-contrast map could, combined with these extraplanar regions, be signs of minor-axis outflows.

sources.summary: The paper traces NGC 3735 polarization far into the halo, subtracts a background polarized source from its field, and derives its approaching side from VLA H I data.

quotes.quote: Some of those with a smaller angular extent (hence without large-scale missing flux densities) like NGC 3735 or NGC 5775 show a P that can be traced in vertical direction up to 8 kpc or 10 kpc and P is still monotonically increasing.
Polarized background sources, not related to the galaxies, were found, one in each of the fields of NGC 3735, NGC 5775, and NGC 5907, and were subtracted, too.

For this galaxy we reduced HI data observed with the VLA and determined a velocity map of NGC 3735 as described and shown in Fig.  n3735HI in Appendix B . It shows that the south eastern side is the approaching one.

gmw_00009481.name: IC 1368
gmw_00009481.overview: IC 1368 is a galaxy featuring a bright nucleus that is extended along its minor axis [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741]. This nuclear region is surrounded by two strong absorption features, a possible cone-like structure, and a disk with diffuse emission and spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741].

sources.summary: The paper reports IC 1368 has X-shaped clustered H II regions, a minor-axis-extended nucleus, a possible cone-like nuclear structure, spiral-arm emission, and diffuse disk emission.

quotes.quote: The R band image of IC 1368 (Figure f:ic1368 ) shows an X-shaped region of possibly clustered H ii regions. In agreement with col96 , we find that the image shows a nucleus extended in the minor axis direction. Furthermore, we find evidence for a cone-like structure at the NW tip of the nucleus. In addition to faint diffuse emission from the disk, the emission map shows possible evidence for two spiral arms containing a few bright H ii regions. The image shows a bright nucleus surrounded by two strong absorption features, as well as diffuse disk emission.

gmw_00009482.name: UGC 12282
gmw_00009482.overview: UGC 12282 is an inclined spiral galaxy that features dusty regions between modest H II regions outlining its spiral arms [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741]. Its disk contains scattered H II regions, and it displays a ring of enhanced emission surrounding the nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0612741].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 12282 as an inclined spiral with dusty regions, modest spiral-arm H II regions, nuclear emission, a ring of enhanced emission, and little extranuclear emission in the other map.

quotes.quote: The R band image of UGC 12282 (Figure f:ugc12282 ) shows some dusty regions in between the modest H ii regions outlining the spiral arms, which are partially visible in this inclined spiral galaxy. The map shows emission from H ii regions scattered in the disk as well as from the nucleus. A ring of enhanced emission with radius 23 (7.6 kpc) is also visible. The map does not show much emission outside the nucleus.

gmw_00009483.name: GS-23077
gmw_00009483.overview: GS-23077 is a high-redshift emission line galaxy [arXiv:2405.05111]. Although previously associated with an overdensity at a lower redshift, revised spectroscopic analysis indicates that the galaxy is located at z=7.38 [arXiv:2405.05111].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GS-23077 as the sole match to a Helton et al. overdensity source, revising its redshift to z=7.38 by interpreting the strongest line as [O III].

quotes.quote: Helton2023 use FRESCO and JADES to study an overdensity at z=5.4 in GOODS-South. We find only one match, GS-23077. For this source ID 70 Helton2023 report a spectroscopic redshift of z=5.392 , whereas we find instead z=7.38 with q=2 . The strongest line identified is the same, but we interpret it as [ O iii ] 5008 rather than H at lower redshift. We note that our EAZY redshift for this source is z=5.438^ +0.010 _ 0.010 .

gmw_00009484.name: ID 1
gmw_00009484.overview: ID 1 is an extended, nearly edge-on disk galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.17 [arXiv:1512.00246]. Its morphology is characterized by weak central light that is poorly fit by a standard exponential disk model, possibly due to internal absorption [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 1 as a nearly edge-on extended disk at z 0.17 with weak central light, asymmetric emission lines, and off-centered star-forming structure.

quotes.quote: ID 1 Nearly edge-on ( i=75 ) and extended ( 4 \ in diameter) galaxy disk at z 0.17 , the lowest redshift of the sample, enabling the observation of both \ and \ strong emission lines with MUSE. The morphology is hardly reproduced by an exponential disk because the intensity is too low in the center. This might be because of some absorption not taken into account in the model.

gmw_00009485.name: ID 3
gmw_00009485.overview: ID 3 is a well-resolved, nearly face-on disk galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.56 [arXiv:1512.00246]. The galaxy's disk is clumpy, featuring bright emission regions along its spiral arms and an asymmetric line-flux distribution [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 3 as a well-resolved, nearly face-on disk at z 0.56 with clumpy spiral-arm emission and asymmetric line-flux structure.

quotes.quote: ID 3 This well-resolved ( 6 \ in diameter) galaxy is a nearly face-on ( i=16 ) disk at z 0.56 , allowing us to observe both \ (the brightest) and \ strong emission lines with MUSE. The disk of this galaxy is rather clumpy with bright \ regions along spiral arms. The flux distribution seems more clumpy and asymmetric for the \ line than for the \ doublet.

gmw_00009486.name: ID 4
gmw_00009486.overview: ID 4 is a large, highly inclined disk galaxy located at a redshift of z 0.56 [arXiv:1512.00246]. Its morphological center is slightly offset from the center of its external isophotes, and its model residuals reveal a spiral pattern [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 4 as a highly inclined large disk at z 0.56 with an offset center, spiral residuals, and a bright northeastern emission region.

quotes.quote: ID 4 Highly inclined ( i 75 ) and large ( 4 \ in diameter) disk at z 0.56 , allowing us to observe both \ (the brightest) and \ strong emission lines with MUSE. The morphological center is offset with respect to the center of external isophotes by less than 0.1 , which might be due to some absorption in this inclined disk. The morphological model residuals display a spiral pattern.

gmw_00009487.name: ID 5
gmw_00009487.overview: ID 5 is a compact galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.58 [arXiv:1512.00246]. The galaxy features a bright nucleus and two open arms that extend to the north and south [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 5 as a compact galaxy with a bright nucleus, open arms, difficult kinematic modeling, and apparently dispersion-dominated gas.

quotes.quote: ID 5 The inclination of this galaxy at z 0.58 is quite difficult to constrain with a value of 70 returned with Galfit, whereas both and \ give a value around 45 . This discrepancy probably comes from the fact that the emission of this galaxy is rather compact with a bright nucleus and two open arms extending north and south. Velocity fields derived from both \ and \ (the brightest) emission lines are consistent and show an observed regular pattern within 40 .

gmw_00009488.name: ID 6
gmw_00009488.overview: ID 6 is an asymmetric barred galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z 0.42 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It is a relatively large galaxy that exhibits an elongated, asymmetric spiral morphology extending toward the south [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 6 as an asymmetric barred galaxy at z 0.42 whose strong bar complicates morphology and affects the measured axis ratio and position angle.

quotes.quote: ID 6 The properties of this asymmetric barred galaxy at z 0.42 have already been discussed in . It is a rather large ( >2 \ in diameter) galaxy that clearly displays an elongated and asymmetric spiral morphology (extending southward) with a strong bar, which makes its morphological model difficult to adjust. In particular, the bar affects the determination of the axis ratio as well as of the true position angle.

gmw_00009489.name: ID 7
gmw_00009489.overview: ID 7 is a large, moderately inclined disk galaxy located at a redshift of z ~ 0.46 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It features an asymmetric spiral structure, with emission dominated by two bright regions situated northeast and southwest of its center [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 7 as a moderately inclined large disk at z 0.46 with asymmetric spiral structure and two bright emission regions.

quotes.quote: ID 7 Moderately inclined ( i 40 ) and large ( 3 \ in diameter) disk at z 0.46 , allowing us to observe both \ and \ (the brightest) strong emission lines with MUSE. This galaxy displays an asymmetric spiral structure that makes its morphology difficult to model accurately. The emission of this galaxy is dominated by two bright \ regions located toward the northeast and southwest directions with respect to the galaxy morphological/kinematic center.

gmw_00009490.name: ID 8
gmw_00009490.overview: ID 8 is a moderately inclined disk galaxy located at a redshift of z 0.58 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It exhibits an asymmetric broadband morphology, featuring a main rotating component and an eastern extension that may connect to a small, passive object [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 8 as a moderately inclined disk at z 0.58 with asymmetric broadband morphology and a rotating component plus eastern extension.

quotes.quote: ID 8 This moderately inclined ( i 60 ) disk at z 0.58 shows both \ (the brightest) and \ emission lines with MUSE. This galaxy displays an asymmetric broadband morphology as revealed by the model residuals. Velocity fields derived from these emission lines are consistent and show a main rotating component within 130 and an extension toward the east that may be linked to a (passive) small object seen in the HST image.

gmw_00009491.name: ID 9
gmw_00009491.overview: ID 9 is a large galaxy at a redshift of z 0.56 with a diameter of approximately two arcseconds [arXiv:1512.00246]. It features a bulge and a regular velocity field typical of early-type disks, with a maximum rotation velocity of 125 kilometers per second [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 9 as a large z 0.56 galaxy with a regular early-type disk velocity field, prominent bulge, and lower rotation velocity than previous data.

quotes.quote: ID 9 The properties of this large ( 2 \ in diameter) galaxy at z 0.56 have already been discussed in . The maximum rotation velocity of 125 \ derived with the disk modeling (both in 2D and with ) is significantly lower than the value ( 220 ) obtained with GIRAFFE data Puech+2008 . The velocity field of this galaxy is rather regular and typical of early-type disks with a prominent bulge.

gmw_00009492.name: ID 10
gmw_00009492.overview: ID 10 is a compact galaxy and the most massive member of a galaxy group at z ~ 1.28 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It exhibits an active galactic nucleus spectrum and is likely interacting with the galaxy ID 27, as evidenced by low-surface brightness tidal tails [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 10 as the sample’s most massive group member at z 1.28, interacting with ID 27 and showing compact AGN-like spectral properties.

quotes.quote: ID 10 This galaxy is the most massive among our sample ( M 10^ 10.8 ), is a member of a group at z 1.28 , and is probably interacting with the galaxy ID 27 as revealed by low-surface brightness tidal tails in between the two objects. The galaxy is rather compact ( 1 \ in diameter) in the HST images and its spectrum is typical of an AGN with prominent , , and \ emission lines. Its broadband morphology clearly shows a central component and two low-surface brightness arms.

gmw_00009493.name: ID 11
gmw_00009493.overview: ID 11 is a small, moderately inclined disk galaxy located at a redshift of z ~ 0.58 [arXiv:1512.00246]. Although its line flux maps appear circular due to beam size, high-resolution imaging reveals its inclined structure [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 11 as a small inclined disk at z 0.58 whose line maps appear circular but velocity fields show a regular pattern.

quotes.quote: ID 11 This moderately inclined ( i 60 ) and rather small ( 1 \ in diameter) disk at z 0.58 shows both \ and \ emission lines with MUSE. The line flux maps seem rather circular for such an inclined galaxy as seen at high resolution in the HST images. This might be because of the size of the beam with respect to the size of the galaxy.

gmw_00009494.name: ID 12
gmw_00009494.overview: ID 12 is a compact galaxy located at a redshift of z 0.67 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It features an eastern, low-surface-brightness jet-like structure that may be a relic of past gravitational interactions [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 12 as a compact z 0.67 galaxy with an eastern low-surface-brightness jet-like structure and a very low velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: ID 12 This galaxy at z 0.67 is very compact ( 0.5 \ in diameter) in the broadband HST images but shows (also in MUSE white-light image) an extended low-surface brightness jet-like structure eastward, which could be a relic of past gravitational interactions. The \ and \ emission-line flux maps are rather circular and their extent ( >2 \ in diameter) is probably only due to beam smearing and the very high S/N. With a rather low inclination ( i 37 ), derived both with Galfit and , the velocity gradient within 20 \ is very low.

gmw_00009495.name: ID 16
gmw_00009495.overview: ID 16 is a galaxy at redshift 0.46 that features a bright, compact central component embedded within a large, diffuse, and moderately inclined low-surface brightness disk [arXiv:1512.00246]. Its emission lines reveal regular velocity fields, though a nearby bright compact object seen to the east is at a different redshift [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 16 as a z 0.46 galaxy with a compact central component in a diffuse disk and regular emission-line velocity fields.

quotes.quote: ID 16 This galaxy at z 0.46 has a bright and compact central component embedded in a large, diffuse and moderately inclined ( i 20-30 ) low-surface brightness disk, allowing us to observe both \ and \ (the brightest) strong emission lines with MUSE. One bright compact object is also seen at about 1 \ eastward in the HST image but it is measured at a different redshift in the MUSE data. Velocity fields derived from these emission lines are consistent and show an observed regular pattern within 30 .

gmw_00009496.name: ID 17
gmw_00009496.overview: ID 17 is a galaxy located at a redshift of z 0.58 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It exhibits an asymmetric broadband morphology and displays a consistent velocity pattern within 60 km/s [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 17 as a z 0.58 galaxy with asymmetric broadband morphology, noisy border-of-field emission lines, and an observed velocity pattern.

quotes.quote: ID 17 This galaxy at z 0.58 exhibits an asymmetric broadband morphology. It is located at the border of the MUSE FoV and thus have , , and \ emission lines more noisy in the MUSE data. Velocity fields derived from these emission lines are however consistent and show an observed pattern within 60 .

gmw_00009497.name: ID 20
gmw_00009497.overview: ID 20 is a large galaxy located at a redshift of approximately z 0.43 [arXiv:1512.00246]. Its morphology is dominated by a strong central bar with arms extending from its ends, alongside a faint, low-surface brightness component [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 20 as a large z 0.43 galaxy dominated by a strong bar, arms, and a faint component that complicates morphology.

quotes.quote: ID 20 This galaxy at z 0.43 is rather large ( >2 \ in diameter). Its morphology seems to indicate that it is dominated by a strong bar with arms starting at the end of the bar. An extended low-surface brightness component (elongated in the east-west direction) seems to be observed slightly above the noise in the HST/F814W image.

gmw_00009498.name: ID 23
gmw_00009498.overview: ID 23 is a low-mass, nearly face-on disk galaxy at a redshift of z 0.56 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It exhibits a low surface brightness, numerous emission regions, and slight differences in its velocity-field orientations [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 23 as a low-mass, nearly face-on z 0.56 disk with low surface brightness, numerous emission regions, and differing velocity-field orientations.

quotes.quote: ID 23 HST images show a low surface brightness, rather large ( 2 \ in diameter) and nearly face-on disk with numerous \ regions for this low-mass galaxy at z 0.56 . Velocity fields derived from \ and \ emission lines slightly differ in the orientation of the major axis ( 20 ) as well as between the 2D and \ models. This might be due to the combination of an asymmetric line flux distribution with a better spatial resolution for the \ line.

gmw_00009499.name: ID 24
gmw_00009499.overview: ID 24 is a nearly face-on disk galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.97 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It features a regular velocity field, bright emission, and a slightly asymmetric broadband light distribution that deviates from a standard exponential disk [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 24 as a nearly face-on z 0.97 disk with bright emission, absorption lines, flat asymmetric light, and a regular velocity field.

quotes.quote: ID 24 This galaxy at z 0.97 is a nearly face-on disk (around 1 \ in diameter) with a bright \ emission together with \ and \ absorption lines. It displays a surprisingly flat but slightly asymmetric broadband light distribution that is not accurately described by an exponential disk. The velocity field derived from \ shows a regular pattern within 30 .

gmw_00009500.name: ID 26
gmw_00009500.overview: ID 26 is a nearly edge-on galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.22 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It is a low-mass system with a diameter of about 2 arcseconds and exhibits a regular velocity field consistent with a disk model [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 26 as the least massive sample galaxy, a nearly edge-on z 0.22 system with consistent regular velocity fields and disk-model fits.

quotes.quote: ID 26 This nearly edge-on galaxy at z 0.22 , of 2 \ size in diameter, is the less massive among our sample ( M 10^ 8 ). Velocity fields derived from \ and \ emission lines are consistent and show a regular pattern within 40 . Disk modeling (both in 2D and with ) reproduces well the observed velocity fields, but the \ analysis returns an underestimated disk inclination.

gmw_00009501.name: ID 27
gmw_00009501.overview: ID 27 is a large galaxy located in a group at a redshift of approximately 1.28 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It exhibits a highly disturbed morphology with a filamentary structure and tidal tails, indicating an active interaction or merger with the neighboring galaxy ID 10 [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 27 as a z 1.28 group member interacting with ID 10, with disturbed morphology, tidal tails, and possible merger-arm structure.

quotes.quote: ID 27 This large ( > 2 \ in diameter) galaxy is a member of a group at z 1.28 , has a very peculiar morphology, and is probably interacting with the galaxy ID 10 as revealed by low-surface brightness tidal tails in between the two objects. Its broadband morphology is very disturbed and cannot be reproduced by a simple bulge/disk decomposition. Indeed, it shows a main distorted elongation on top of a low-surface brightness filamentary structure, which may be a signature of a merger event with one tidal arm.

gmw_00009502.name: ID 28
gmw_00009502.overview: ID 28 is a galaxy with a diameter of 1.5 arcseconds and a peculiar, asymmetric broadband morphology [arXiv:1512.00246]. The galaxy features a flat center with a bright region offset to the north and an arm-like structure in the south [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 28 as a z 0.32 galaxy with peculiar asymmetric morphology, an offset bright region, and low velocity gradients across multiple emission lines.

quotes.quote: ID 28 This galaxy with a size of 1.5 \ in diameter has a peculiar morphology in HST images. It has an asymmetric broadband morphology that is surprisingly flat in the center with one bright region offset northward and an arm-like structure in the south. It is therefore not accurately described by an exponential disk model.

gmw_00009503.name: ID 32
gmw_00009503.overview: ID 32 is a small, highly inclined disk galaxy located at a redshift of z 0.56 [arXiv:1512.00246]. Its velocity fields, derived from emission lines, show a regular pattern that is well reproduced by disk modeling [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 32 as a highly inclined small disk at z 0.56 with regular velocity fields, successful disk modeling, and minor-axis dispersion enhancement.

quotes.quote: ID 32 This galaxy is a highly inclined and rather small ( 1 \ in diameter) disk at z 0.56 . Velocity fields derived from \ and \ emission lines are consistent and show a regular pattern within 60 . Disk modeling (both in 2D and with ) reproduces well the observed velocity fields.

gmw_00009504.name: ID 35
gmw_00009504.overview: ID 35 is a moderately inclined galaxy located in a group at a redshift of z 1.28 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It has an elongated asymmetric structure with a low-surface brightness component, and its velocity gradient suggests the presence of a rotating disk [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 35 as a z 1.28 group member with elongated asymmetric structure and a velocity gradient indicating a rotating disk.

quotes.quote: ID 35 This moderately inclined galaxy is also a member of the group at z 1.28 . It is 1 \ in diameter, shows a main elongation but also displays an asymmetric low-surface brightness component in the HST images. The \ velocity field displays a gradient along the same direction as the morphological elongation which seems to be an indication of a rotating disk ( 80 ).

gmw_00009505.name: ID 37
gmw_00009505.overview: ID 37 is a small galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1.1 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It appears elongated in Hubble Space Telescope images, while its central emission exhibits an almost flat velocity pattern [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 37 as a small elongated z 1.1 galaxy whose central emission has a flat velocity field and likely perturbed non-circular dynamics.

quotes.quote: ID 37 This galaxy at z 1.1 is rather small ( 1 \ in diameter) and seems to be rather elongated in HST images with a derived inclination of 70 from Galfit . However most of the \ emission probed with MUSE spectroscopy comes from the bright central regions. The velocity field derived from \ shows an almost flat pattern within 10 .

gmw_00009506.name: ID 38
gmw_00009506.overview: ID 38 is an elongated galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It exhibits a regular velocity field and rotation-dominated gas dynamics, with a morphology that may contain a bar-like structure [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 38 as an elongated z 1 galaxy with possible bar-like structure, a regular velocity field, and rotation-dominated gas dynamics.

quotes.quote: ID 38 This galaxy at z 1 has an elongated morphology. It could be a bar-like feature since it also has a lower surface brightness component that seems to have a slightly different orientation. However, the \ velocity field exhibits a regular pattern within 60 \ in the same direction of the main elongated structure.

gmw_00009507.name: ID 49
gmw_00009507.overview: ID 49 is a nearly edge-on disk galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 1 [arXiv:1512.00246]. It has a low-mass satellite galaxy situated 14 kiloparsecs to the south, which represents a potential future minor merger [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 49 as a nearly edge-on z 1 disk with a low-mass satellite and a regular X-shaped velocity-field pattern.

quotes.quote: ID 49 This galaxy is a nearly edge-on disk at z 1 with a diameter of about 1 . This galaxy has a low-mass ( 10^ 7.8 ) satellite, ID 149 in the redshift catalog of , located southward at a projected distance of 14 kpc and with a systemic velocity difference of 60 . With a stellar mass ratio of 1/25 , it can be considered a possible future minor merger (Ventou et al., in prep.).

gmw_00009508.name: ID 68
gmw_00009508.overview: ID 68 is a relatively small disk galaxy at a redshift of approximately 0.97 [arXiv:1512.00246]. Viewed nearly edge-on, the galaxy exhibits a slightly asymmetric broadband morphology, though its line flux morphology appears circular due to beam smearing [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 68 as a nearly edge-on small z 0.97 disk with asymmetric HST morphology and circular line flux likely from beam smearing.

quotes.quote: ID 68 This galaxy is a nearly edge-on and rather small ( 1 \ in diameter) disk at z 0.97 . Its broadband HST morphology appears slightly asymmetric. The \ line flux morphology is quite circular, probably due to beam smearing effect.

gmw_00009509.name: ID 72
gmw_00009509.overview: ID 72 is a small galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.84 [arXiv:1512.00246]. Its velocity field is compatible with a rotating disk viewed almost edge-on, and it features a nearby southward structure that may not be gravitationally linked [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 72 as a small z 0.84 galaxy whose nearby feature may be unrelated and whose velocity field matches an edge-on rotating disk.

quotes.quote: ID 72 This galaxy at z 0.84 is rather small ( <1 \ in diameter). HST images show a small feature about 0.2 \ southward, but this feature may not be gravitationally linked as it is not seen (or resolved) in MUSE data. The velocity field derived from \ shows a regular pattern within 60 , which is compatible (both in 2D and with \ modeling) with a rotating disk almost seen edge-on. We note, however, that inclination has been fixed to 74 5 with \ as a result of convergence problems.

gmw_00009510.name: ID 88
gmw_00009510.overview: ID 88 is an elongated galaxy located at a redshift of z = 1.36, making it the most distant system in its sample [arXiv:1512.00246]. It is identified as a merging system with a similar-mass companion located approximately 5 kpc to the south [arXiv:1512.00246].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 88 as the farthest sample galaxy, an elongated z 1.36 merging system with a similar-mass companion and regular but misaligned velocity field.

quotes.quote: ID 88 With a redshift z 1.36 and a size of 1 \ in diameter this galaxy is the farthest of our sample. It has an elongated broadband morphology identified as a merging system. This galaxy has indeed a similar mass ( 10^8 ) companion, not identified in the redshift catalog of , located southward at a projected distance of 5 kpc and with a systemic velocity difference of 20 .

gmw_00009511.name: PS J0630-1201
gmw_00009511.overview: PS J0630-1201 is a gravitationally lensed quasar system at a redshift of 3.34 [arXiv:1709.08975]. The system is lensed by two galaxies into a five-image configuration with a visible arc from the lensed quasar host galaxy [arXiv:1709.08975, arXiv:1803.07601].

sources.summary: The paper uses image B of lensed quasar PSJ 0630-1201 to demonstrate high-resolution FMM-IPM microlensing maps spanning ISCO-to-torus scales and BLR baseline behavior.

quotes.quote: To show this, we present here the case of image B in the high magnified lensed quasar PSJ 0630-1201 (Shajib et al. 2019). We have considered an spatial resolution of 0.475 light-days, enough to sample the ISCO in a 10^9M_ black hole, and a size of 1900 light-days, which covers the torus of scattered light in a typical case (Popovic et al. 2020). The map has =0.49 and =0.52 , with a magnification =-97 , and N_ pix =4000 4000 pixels, which implies a large number of deflectors (taken to have 0.2M_ ) N_*=1.4 10^7 .

The workload for this map is =2.15 10^ 16 . The execution time with the FMM-IPM code was roughly one hour ( t_ ex =3986 s ). The high number of lenses involved would have made this map a very heavy task even for GPUs (e.g. see typical execution times and performances for Gerlumph in Figures etvswl and pvswl ). Even for IPM, the map needs to shoot a huge amount of N_ rays 3.5 10^9 rays, which is equivalent to an array of 600000 6000 pixels (which would imply very high memory requirements for a Poisson solver).

From the histograms, it can be clearly seen that, while the smallest scales (ISCO and accretion disc) are strongly magnified, the BLR and the torus of scattered light are rather insensitive to microlensing and may be used as baseline for no microlensing.

sources.summary: The paper confirms PS J0630-1201 as a z=3.34 gravitationally lensed quasar system with two lensing galaxies, five images, an arc from the quasar host, and a successful two-ellipsoid model.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery, spectroscopic confirmation, and mass modelling of the gravitationally lensed quasar system PS J0630-1201. The lens was discovered by matching a photometric quasar catalogue compiled from Pan-STARRS and WISE photometry to the Gaia DR1 catalogue, exploiting the high spatial resolution of the latter (FWHM 0 1 ) to identify the three brightest components of the lens. Follow-up spectroscopic observations with the WHT confirm the multiple objects are quasars at redshift z_ q =3.34 . Further follow-up with Keck AO high-resolution imaging reveals that the system is composed of two lensing galaxies and the quasar is lensed into a 2 8 separation four-image cusp configuration with a fifth image clearly visible, and a 1 0 arc due to the lensed quasar host galaxy. The system is well-modelled with two singular isothermal ellipsoids, reproducing the position of the fifth image.

sources.summary: The paper says J0630-1201 was found through a Pan-STARRS plus WISE quasar catalog, with Keck data revealing two lens galaxies and a demagnified fifth image.

quotes.quote: J0630-1201 and J1606-2333. The free parameters for the pixel modelling are the positions of the quasar images and the lensing galaxy, as well as the flattening, size and S ersic index of the galaxy, all of which we assume to be the same across bands. The log likelihood is sampled using the emcee package foreman-mackey2013 to determine statistical uncertainties.

gmw_00009512.name: UGC 09379
gmw_00009512.overview: UGC 09379 is a massive galaxy with a redshift of approximately 0.025201 [arXiv:2004.10406]. It has a stellar mass comparable to the Milky Way and has hosted both PTF13ast and the broad-line Type Ic supernova SN 2020bvc [arXiv:2004.10406].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 09379 as the host of SN 2020bvc, gives its redshift and offset, and emphasizes that its stellar mass exceeds known GRB-SN and most Ic-BL SN hosts.

quotes.quote: The position of SN 2020bvc is 13 \ (7.2 kpc CDM cosmology of Planck2016 used throughout. ) from the center of UGC 09379 ( z=0.025201 0.000021 from NED ned.ipac.caltech.edu ), which also hosted PTF13ast GalYam2014 . UGC 09379 is a massive galaxy: estimate a stellar mass _ 10 (M/ ) = 10.28^ +0.01 _ 0.16 while the NASA-SDSS Atlas value ( Blanton2011 ) is _ 10 (M/ ) = 10.26 , comparable to the Milky Way and other large spirals. The stellar mass of UGC 09379 is larger than that of all known GRB-SN host galaxies Hjorth2012,Taggart2019 and similar only to the host galaxy of LLGRB 171205A/SN 2017iuk Delia2018,Wang2018,Izzo2019 , which had _ 10 (M/ ) = 10.1 0.1 Perley2017-host . The stellar mass of UGC 09379 is also larger than that of most Ic-BL SN host galaxies Modjaz2020 , with the exception of SN 2002ap M74: _ 10 (M/ ) = 11.52^ +0.05 _ 0.05 Kelly2012 and SN 1997ef UGC 4107: _ 10 (M/ ) = 10.55^ +0.07 _ 0.56 Kelly2012 .

gmw_00009513.name: UGC 2082
gmw_00009513.overview: UGC 2082 is an isolated, edge-on spiral galaxy classified as an Sc type [arXiv:1012.0816, arXiv:1510.08715]. It is a low surface brightness galaxy with a low star formation rate [arXiv:1012.0816], containing a warped neutral hydrogen disk that extends vertically into its halo [arXiv:1012.0816].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 2082 as an isolated edge-on low surface brightness galaxy with low star formation, detected H I, extended vertical gas, a warp, and possible filamentary halo gas.

quotes.quote: UGC 2082 is an edge-on, low surface brightness galaxy which appears to be isolated (hence the lack of environmental information in the UGC 2082 entry in Table table:environment ). It contains molecular gas matthews_gao_2001,matthews_etal_2005 , but has a low star formation rate. The SFR estimate listed in Table table:sample ( 0.023 M_ yr ^ 1 ) is based on FIR luminosity, and by this estimator UGC 2082 has one of the lowest SFRs in our sample.

With the HALOGAS \ observations, we detect a total \ mass of M_ HI = 2.54 10^ 9 M_ . The vertical distribution of \ emission is somewhat extended, reaching up to a maximum vertical extent of about 5 kpc, fully one third of the radial extent of the . The total \ contours along the major axis clearly show a slight warp in the sky plane, beginning somewhat beyond the optical radius. A warp component along the line of sight may be responsible for some of the apparent thickening of the disk.

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 2082 as a nearby Sc isolated galaxy with adopted distance 14.7 Mpc, literature H I masses, and a possible ALFALFA companion UGC 2082A.

quotes.quote: UGC 2082 is an Sc galaxy at a recessional velocity of 712 km s ^ 1 . We adopt the distance estimate of 14.7 Mpc from Tully et al. (2009). Previous Arecibo observations reported in Springob et al. (2005) give an H i mass (at our adopted distance) of 2.4 10^9 M_ and 3.1 10^9 M_ after correction of the flux for source size and self-absorption as for NGC 1156.

The companion of UGC 2082, referred to here as UGC 2082A, was discovered by the ALFALFA survey of the anti-Virgo region (Martin et al. 2009), where it is recorded as AGC 122400. It is dubious whether, given its projected distance from UGC 2082 and its separation in radial velocity, it is actually a companion of UGC 2082; we err on the side of including it in our analysis as a companion as this sets a lower limit on the significance of differences between our isolated galaxy sample and HIMFs from other regions (see next section).

gmw_00009514.name: NGC 5523
gmw_00009514.overview: NGC 5523 is an isolated Sc spiral galaxy located approximately 20.6 Mpc away [arXiv:1510.08715, arXiv:1611.05968]. Although it features a symmetric outer neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:1611.05968], its asymmetric inner structure and tidal stream suggest past minor mergers with low-mass companion galaxies [arXiv:1611.05968].

sources.summary: The paper argues that isolated spiral NGC 5523 shows a displaced bulge, tidal stream, regular outer disk, and other evidence for past soft minor mergers.

quotes.quote: Multi-band images of the very isolated spiral galaxy NGC 5523 show a number of unusual features consistent with NGC 5523 having experienced a significant merger: (1) Near-infrared (NIR) images from the Spitzer Space Telescope (SST) and the WIYN 3.5-m telescope reveal a nucleated bulge-like structure embedded in a spiral disk. (2) The bulge is offset by 1.8 kpc from a brightness minimum at the center of the optically bright inner disk. (3) A tidal stream, possibly associated with an ongoing satellite interaction, extends from the nucleated bulge along the disk.

The extreme isolation of NGC 5523 suggests that past mergers did not influence its evolution. However, its symmetric HI outer disk in combination with its asymmetric inner structure gives significant evidence for a past merger. Furthermore, the tidal stream, likely a remnant from an interaction with a bound dwarf companion, indicates a more-recent merger. We conclude that the asymmetric internal structure of NGC 5523 is the product of one or more past minor mergers with surrounding low-mass galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5523 as a nearby Sc isolated galaxy at 20.6 Mpc, with prior Arecibo H I masses and AGES observations of its surrounding field.

quotes.quote: NGC 5523 is another Sc galaxy, at a recessional velocity of 1050 km s ^ 1 . We adopt the distance estimate of 20.6 Mpc from Tully et al. (2009). Previous Arecibo observations reported in Springob et al. (2005) give an H i mass (at our adopted distance) of 3.6 10^9 M_ and 4.2 10^9 M_ after correction for source size and self-absorption as for NGC 1156 and UGC 2082.

Observations were carried out in 2004-5 (NGC 1156), 2009-10 (UGC 2082) and 2012-13 (NGC 5523). Data for all three fields were taken with the WAPP correlators using the standard AGES setup (Auld et al. 2007; Cortese et al. 2008; Minchin et al. 2010).

gmw_00009515.name: UGC 2082A
gmw_00009515.overview: UGC 2082A is a dwarf galaxy that was discovered by the ALFALFA survey [arXiv:1510.08715]. Although identified as a possible companion to UGC 2082, its large separation and weak tidal forces make interaction between the two galaxies unlikely [arXiv:1510.08715].

sources.summary: The paper treats UGC 2082A as an ALFALFA-discovered possible companion to UGC 2082, but argues its separation and weak tidal influence make significant interaction unlikely.

quotes.quote: The companion of UGC 2082, referred to here as UGC 2082A, was discovered by the ALFALFA survey of the anti-Virgo region (Martin et al. 2009), where it is recorded as AGC 122400. It is dubious whether, given its projected distance from UGC 2082 and its separation in radial velocity, it is actually a companion of UGC 2082; we err on the side of including it in our analysis as a companion as this sets a lower limit on the significance of differences between our isolated galaxy sample and HIMFs from other regions (see next section).

For the tidal forces to be significant, we would expect Q 10^ 2 or greater; we find that Q = 4 10^ 6 for NGC 1156A and 1 10^ 5 for UGC 2082A. For either of these to be significant would require the H i mass to total mass ratio of the companions to be 100 -- 1000 times more than that of the parents. It is therefore not surprising that we do not see evidence for interactions in the renzograms.

gmw_00009516.name: NGC 1156A
gmw_00009516.overview: NGC 1156A is a dwarf companion galaxy of NGC 1156 that was discovered in neutral hydrogen during the AGES survey [arXiv:1510.08715, arXiv:1112.2821]. The tidal forces between the two galaxies are calculated to be too small to produce observable interaction signatures [arXiv:1510.08715].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1156A as a newly detected AGES companion of NGC 1156 and finds its tidal force too small to cause significant interaction signatures.

quotes.quote: The companion of NGC 1156, referred to here as NGC 1156A, is a new detection by AGES, previously reported by Minchin et al. (2010) as AGES J030039+254656.

For the tidal forces to be significant, we would expect Q 10^ 2 or greater; we find that Q = 4 10^ 6 for NGC 1156A and 1 10^ 5 for UGC 2082A. For either of these to be significant would require the H i mass to total mass ratio of the companions to be 100 -- 1000 times more than that of the parents. It is therefore not surprising that we do not see evidence for interactions in the renzograms.

sources.summary: The paper describes AGES J030039+254656 as a dwarf galaxy discovered in H I near NGC 1156 and argues it is a physical companion in an isolated pair.

quotes.quote: Within the HI survey AGES, carried out with the Arecibo radiotelescope, a dwarf galaxy AGES J030039+254656 was discovered [ 20 ] with an apparent magnitude of B = 18.1^m and radial velocity of V_h = 308 km/s, located at an angular distance of 35^ (80 kpc) from NGC 1156. There is every reason to call this dwarf galaxy a physical companion of NGC 1156, the presence of which, however, does not distort the dynamic autonomy of this bright galaxy.

On the other hand, an example of a nearby isolated galaxy NGC 1156 and its dwarf companion AGES J030039+254656 indicates that the estimates of the orbital mass-to-K-luminosity ratio in these indisputably isolated pairs can reach values of around 200M_ /L_ .

gmw_00009517.name: VCC 297
gmw_00009517.overview: VCC 297 is an optical-counterpart galaxy located at an assumed distance of 28 Mpc [arXiv:1508.02825]. The galaxy has an absolute blue magnitude of 16.6 mag and has been detected in neutral hydrogen emission studies [arXiv:1508.02825].

sources.summary: The paper treats VCC 297 as the optical-counterpart galaxy in the field, comparing its VLA H I detection, blue magnitude, and gas-to-light scaling with nearby H I clouds.

quotes.quote: In this context, it is worth noting that the \ emission that we detect for VCC 297 (i.e. Source 4) from the VLA cube is in excellent agreement with that detected in an earlier Arecibo study giovanelli97 , indicating that, in general, the VLA observations are not systematically missing flux.

For our assumed distance of 28 Mpc, the absolute blue magnitude of the nearby galaxy VCC 297 is 16.6 mag. gavazzi06 . If the ratio of the \ mass to the blue luminosity in Source 1 is the same as that in VCC 297, one would expect it to have 13.2 mag., or an apparent magnitude of 18.2 mag. The corresponding numbers for Source 2 and Source 3 are 15.1 mag. and 17.0 mag., respectively, or apparent magnitudes of 16.7 mag. and 14.8 mag., respectively. Note that since we are scaling from the values for VCC 297, the apparent magnitudes that we estimate are independent of the assumed distance.

gmw_00009518.name: UGC 12138
gmw_00009518.overview: UGC 12138 is a Seyfert 1.8 active galaxy [arXiv:0907.0180]. It features compact near-ultraviolet emission concentrated within less than one arcsecond, which forms an extended halo around the nucleus that is not primarily composed of ionized gas [arXiv:0907.0180].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 12138 as having compact near-UV emission, most of it not from ionized gas, forming a halo around the nucleus visible in blue bands.

quotes.quote: UGC 12138 (Sy1.8) The emission is quite compact, concentrated in less than 1 arcsec. The reasonable values of f are too small to eliminate the nuclear emission in this case. Forcing a high value of f ( >0.8 ) the nuclear emission is subtracted, although the extended emission around it remains, and also negative residuals start to appear with f 0.5. Using a scaling factor of 0.35 yields that the contribution of the ionised gas is 18 per cent of the total near-UV light (once the Sy nucleus is subtracted). So most of the emission is not due to ionised gas in this object.

gmw_00009519.name: IRAS 08355-4944
gmw_00009519.overview: IRAS 08355-4944 is a merging system featuring remnant tidal tails that extend nearly 20 kpc [arXiv:1509.08468]. Its intense star formation is centrally concentrated within the inner 5 kpc, and the galaxy shows evidence of a blueshifted galactic wind [arXiv:1509.08468].

sources.summary: The paper describes remnant tidal tails, centrally concentrated star formation, and a blueshifted low-velocity shock component that may trace a galactic wind.

quotes.quote: IRAS 08355-4944 While HST I-band images show remnant tidal tails extending nearly 20 kpc, only the central 5 kpc or so appears to harbor the entirety of the intense ongoing star formation in this system. Our spectra are dominated by region emission, with evidence in some portions of the system of a blue-shifted component with low-velocity shock-dominated line ratios which could be associated with a galactic wind. This is in agreement with the IFS observations of Monreal10 .

gmw_00009520.name: IRAS F12592+0436
gmw_00009520.overview: IRAS F12592+0436 is a very dusty, late-stage galaxy merger [arXiv:1509.08468]. Its nuclear spectrum exhibits composite behavior dominated by shocks, while its outer gas and tidal tail show signatures of ongoing star formation with no indication of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1509.08468].

sources.summary: The paper describes this dusty late-stage merger as shock-dominated in its nuclear composite spectrum, with star formation signatures and no AGN indication in X-ray or IR data.

quotes.quote: IRAS F12592+0436 (CGCG 043-099) This late-stage merger is very dusty and subsequently the blue data are mostly too low S/N to measure any emission in single spaxels. The red data indicate low line ratio and gas in a tidal tail indicative of star formation and higher line ratio and data toward the nucleus. Our nuclear spectrum indicates composite behavior dominated by shocks. Poggianti00 found balmer emission and absorption indicative of star formation, with no indication of Seyfert behavior. X-ray and IR data indicate a galaxy with ongoing star formation and no indication of an AGN, consistent with our results Rush96,Petric11,Stierwalt13 .

gmw_00009521.name: IRAS F21330-3846
gmw_00009521.overview: IRAS F21330-3846 is a closely interacting galaxy pair that displays tidal tails [arXiv:1509.08468]. Both galactic disks are dominated by emission, while the ionized emission between the two systems shows elevated line ratios and velocity dispersion consistent with interaction-driven shock excitation [arXiv:1509.08468].

sources.summary: The paper describes this close pair as tidally tailed, mostly disk-emission dominated, with inter-galaxy high line ratios and velocity dispersion consistent with interaction-driven shocks.

quotes.quote: IRAS F21330-3846 (ESO 343-IG013) This closely interacting pair shows tidal tails in the available DSS image. Our IFS data show both discs are dominated primarily by emission. The line ratio diagnostic diagrams show a composite component, which corresponds primarily to the outskirts of the outskirts of the northern galaxy. The ionized emission between the two systems is also higher line ratio and velocity dispersion than the discs, consistent with shock excitation possibly caused directly by the interaction, as seen in systems like Stephan's Quintet and the Taffy Galaxies Cluver10,Guillard10,Peterson12 as opposed to outflow-driven shocks.

gmw_00009522.name: GS2 1406
gmw_00009522.overview: GS2 1406 is a high-redshift Lyman-break galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 7.452 [arXiv:1712.05807]. It features a Lyman-alpha emission line with a high equivalent width of 140.3 Angstroms [arXiv:1712.05807].

sources.summary: The paper reports GS2 1406 as a first-time z=7.452 Ly-alpha detection with stated position, line flux, high signal-to-noise, and unusually high equivalent width.

quotes.quote: This line is found in FIGS ID: GS2 1406 (ID z7 PAR2 2909 in Finkelstein2015 ), at a position of = 53.288090, =- 27.865408.

The EW _ Ly measurement for GS2 1406 is taken by comparing the grism-measured line flux to the best fit SED template at the spectroscopic redshift (z=7.452) immediately red-ward of the Ly line (average of 1220-1320 \ rest-frame) as the continuum value. The continuum flux is 1.25 10^ 19 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ^ 1 , giving GS2 1406 a EW _ Ly = 140.3 19.0 , much higher than any previously spectroscopically confirmed galaxy at z > 7, as shown in Figure fig:zvM .

The other is a first-time detection in GS2 1406, discovered photometrically as z7 PAR2 2909 by Finkelstein2015 . GS2 1406 has a detected emission line at 1.03 m, a line flux of (1.75 0.16) 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , and a line-flux signal-to-noise of 10.71.

gmw_00009523.name: B2 0207+38
gmw_00009523.overview: B2 0207+38 is a disk galaxy classified variously as an S0, a spiral, or a distorted Sa galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/9907009]. It exhibits a disk-like radio structure with no evidence of a radio core, jets, or radio lobes [arXiv:astro-ph/9907009].

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 0207+38 as S0- or spiral-like, possibly post-eruptive or distorted Sa, with disc-like radio structure and likely starburst nature.

quotes.quote: B2 0207+38 This source is described as being more similar to an S0 or to a spiral galaxy than an elliptical (Parma et al. 1986). It has also been called a post-eruptive Sa (Zwicky et al., 1968) or a distorted Sa (de Vaucouleurs, de Vaucouleurs Corwin 1975). The radio structure is disc-like and there is no sign of either a radio core, or of jets or radio lobes (Parma et al. 1986).

gmw_00009524.name: B2 2236+35
gmw_00009524.overview: B2 2236+35 is a radio source featuring large-scale, symmetric double radio jets embedded in a low-surface-brightness region [arXiv:astro-ph/9907009, arXiv:0908.4391]. Its two extended radio lobes are similar in size and strength, while its extended X-ray emission aligns with the radio jets [arXiv:astro-ph/9907009].

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 2236+35 as having symmetric double radio jets, similar extended lobes, X-ray emission aligned with the jets, and a point-source count fraction.

quotes.quote: B2 2236+35 This source has a double symmetric radio jet embedded in a low surface-brightness region. The two extended lobes are similar in strength and size (Morganti et al. 1987). The X-ray emission at radii greater than about 20 arcsec seems aligned with the radio jets in this source. Model fitting shows 25 per cent of the total counts to be in the point source.

sources.summary: The paper describes 2236+35 as a source with large-scale double radio jets in low-surface-brightness emission, unresolved on VLBA scales with no missing arcsecond core flux.

quotes.quote: 2236+35 UGC 12127 -- This source has a large-scale double radio jet embedded in a low-surface-brightness region with S symmetry ( ). In our VLBA image, the source is unresolved with total flux density 8.8 mJy and no arcsecond core flux is missing on the milliarcsecond scale.

gmw_00009525.name: GMP 3640
gmw_00009525.overview: GMP 3640 is a dense, massive galaxy that exhibits three bright filaments emerging from its main body [arXiv:2111.01140]. It displays pronounced truncation-age gradients and noticeable blue-band disturbances, which are likely driven by younger stellar populations rather than variations in stellar mass density [arXiv:2111.01140].

sources.summary: The paper reports GMP 3640 as a denser, more massive sample galaxy with pronounced truncation-age gradients, three bright filaments, and one of the strongest blue-band disturbance patterns.

quotes.quote: The radial gradients are more pronounced for denser and more massive galaxies, e.g. GMP 4348, GMP 2640, GMP 3640. The radially increasing gradients correspond to the outside-in star formation quenching expected from the ram pressure stripping.

Another galaxy, GMP 3640 has three bright filaments, emerging from its main body. Several galaxies show clumps or other irregularities in their central parts. GMP 4060 and GMP 3640 exhibit the most noticeable disturbances. However, they are substantially less significant in the Rc band compared to the bluer bands ( V and B ) and, therefore, they are likely caused by different stellar population properties (i.e. younger stellar age) rather than variations in the stellar mass density which would significantly affect the gravitational potential.

gmw_00009526.name: GMP 4188
gmw_00009526.overview: GMP 4188 is a low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy with a lower stellar mass that occupies a non-radial orbit [arXiv:2111.01140]. The galaxy exhibits localized kinematic disturbances associated with morphological irregularities, which are likely non-dynamical in origin [arXiv:2111.01140].

sources.summary: The paper presents GMP 4188 as a low-surface-brightness, lower-mass non-radial-orbit system with morphology-linked kinematic disturbances that the authors argue are not dynamical in origin.

quotes.quote: GMP 4188, GMP 4118, and J160215.29+160904.3 are examples of systems on non-radial orbits with lower stellar mass and therefore low surface brightness, which barely made it into the SDSS.

GMP 4060, GMP 2640 and GMP 4188 have noticeable kinematical disturbances localised in the area of significant morphological irregularities. Detailed analysis of high-resolution HST images for the brightest galaxy in our sample, GMP 2640, revealed a large number of luminous star clusters in its central part whose asymmetric distributions on the sides of the slit causes the non-uniform slit illumination and, hence, affects the radial velocity measurements. Disturbances of kinematics profiles of GMP 4060 and GMP 4188 are also of the same origin.
As we mentioned above, the kinematic disturbances in three galaxies, GMP 4060, GMP 2640 and GMP 4188 are unlikely to be of a dynamical origin.

gmw_00009527.name: N lsb10
gmw_00009527.overview: N lsb10 is an extremely low surface brightness dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0607476]. It is a large system with a limiting radius of 30 arcseconds, or approximately 2 kiloparsecs, and contains a resolved population of red giant stars [arXiv:astro-ph/0607476].

sources.summary: The paper confirms N lsb10 as an extremely low surface brightness Virgo dwarf, resolving its red giant stars, measuring its large radius, and deriving distance and metallicity from its color-magnitude diagram.

quotes.quote: N lsb10 is one of the most extreme cases found in the CCD survey, with a central B surface brightness of about 27.3 mag arcsec ^ 2 . It is also large, with a limiting radius (at 30 mag arcsec ^ 2 ) of 30 , or 2 kpc if it is indeed in the Virgo cluster. The question remained however of its true nature - while the object was definitely real, showing up on multiple exposures and in both colors, the possibility that it was a background object, as mentioned above, remained.

One of the principle targets, N lsb10, has a central intensity 4.5 magnitudes fainter than VCC941, so it is not surprising that the figure requires more scrutiny to find its resolved stars. As a visual confirmation of the increased star density here, an area near the edge of the ACS frame is also shown. Stars in this galaxy in fact extend well beyond the 22.5 diameter of the figure, going out to a radius of 40 . The automatic star finding work discussed below resulted in the detection of nearly as many stars in N lsb10 as in VCC941.

gmw_00009528.name: VCC941
gmw_00009528.overview: VCC941 is a dwarf galaxy in the Virgo cluster that lacks strong nucleation [arXiv:astro-ph/0607476]. Its stellar population is characterized by resolved red giant stars [arXiv:astro-ph/0607476], an old age of approximately 12 Gyr, and very low metallicity [arXiv:0706.4467].

sources.summary: The paper resolves VCC941 into red giant stars, describes it as a Virgo dwarf without strong nucleation, identifies a possible globular cluster, and compares its structure with N lsb10.

quotes.quote: The image of VCC941 will certainly come across best in the reproduction here, and demonstrates dramatically that the images do indeed resolve the red giant stars in this Virgo cluster galaxy. Showing no strong nucleation, VCC941 appears in this image as well-resolved as is the Local Group galaxy Leo I, seen from the ground. A possible globular cluster is seen directly west of the center. Anticipating the photometric calibrations presented below, this object has M _ V =-6 , and (V--I) _ 0 = 1.0, values consistent with a globular cluster.
Note that while N lsb10 is nearly a magnitude fainter than VCC941, the former is five times larger in effective radius.

sources.summary: The paper places VCC 941 in a very low-metallicity, old-age regime and says model discrepancies prevent a firm SBF-gradient interpretation.

quotes.quote: VCC 941 -- This galaxy's data match with models of very low metallicity ( Z 0.0004 ), and old ages ( t 12 Gyr). The SBF-gradient observed cannot be interpreted as related to age or metallicity variations because of the substantial differences between models in this metallicity regime. While BVA01 and MA06 models, in fact, predict that the observed gradient might be related to metallicity variations with the galaxy radius, the R05 and L02 are much more consistent with age variations.

gmw_00009529.name: N dSph
gmw_00009529.overview: N dSph is a faint dwarf spheroidal galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0607476]. It is characterized by an extremely low surface brightness and a stellar population with a distinctly blue giant branch [arXiv:astro-ph/0607476].

sources.summary: The paper treats N dSph as a faint Virgo dwarf spheroidal, finding a slightly more uncertain distance, a distinctly blue giant branch, and a color distribution unlike VCC941.

quotes.quote: N dSph appears to be more distant by about 0.2 mag, though the paucity of stars (not surprising given its luminosity of V=--9.6) makes the tip determination especially uncertain. Its giant branch is distinctly the bluest one of those presented here. A test confirming that was performed by selecting stars randomly from the cmd of VCC941 in numbers equal to the number of stars in the N dSph cmd. Out of 100 tests, no sample from VCC941 had the same color distribution as the stars in N dSph.

The two extremely low surface brightness galaxies observed provide a further test of the relative importance of total luminosity over surface brightness in determining mean abundance, and the N dSph galaxy provides another datum at the low luminosity end, where there is some real scatter, as well as disagreement about abundances for certain galaxies (e.g. 2002AJ....124..886D find [ Fe/H ] =-2.2 for And V, while 2005MNRAS.356..979M find -1.5).

gmw_00009530.name: SW2 lsb31
gmw_00009530.overview: SW2 lsb31 is an extremely low surface brightness dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0607476]. It lies between the spiral galaxy NGC 4407 and the brighter, nucleated dwarf elliptical galaxy VCC 871 [arXiv:astro-ph/0607476].

sources.summary: The paper confirms SW2 lsb31 as an extremely low surface brightness Virgo dwarf, resolves its stars, accounts for NGC 4407 contamination, and derives distance and metallicity from its color-magnitude diagram.

quotes.quote: The dwarf galaxy SW2 lsb31 was chosen because its surface brightness is low enough so that it was missed in the 1985AJ.....90.1681B photographic survey, yet not so low that its existence was in doubt. That dwarf lies between two larger galaxies, one an SB spiral (NGC4407) and the other a much brighter, nucleated dE (VCC871).

Moving to the SW2 field, the target galaxy SW2 lsb31 also has an extremely low central surface brightness, yet stars are clearly resolved here.
