While the combination of the USS and the absence of an optical match may suggest that the source represents an active high-redshift radio galaxy, a recent work on its resolved spectral properties suggests that its spectral index is extremely steep even for a high redshift radio source supporting the remnant interpretation (for a detailed discussion see 2021A&A...648A...9Morganti_resolvedsi ).

We confirm that some of the remnants (including some classified based on the USS emission) do show the presence of some level of nuclear radio activity (J1031+5915, J1045+5631, J1047+5550, J1052+5636, J1054+5505 and J1055+5635). The presence of cores (mean core radio luminosity of remnants being log_ 10 (L^ core _ 6000 MHz / W Hz ^ 1 ) = 23) means that not all remnants have a very low CP.

On the contrary, ultra-steep sources with high levels of CP for their total power might be hinting at a restarting jet activity in the nuclear regions (e.g. sources J1031+5915, J1047+5550, J1054+5505 and J1055+5635, marked with stars above the green line).

gmw_00009602.name: J1057+5911
gmw_00009602.overview: J1057+5911 is a remnant radio galaxy that exhibits a diffuse, amorphous morphology [arXiv:2106.13161]. It lacks a detected radio core and is characterized by a relatively low radio luminosity [arXiv:2106.13161].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1057+5911 as a diffuse amorphous remnant lacking a core detection, with constrained 1400 MHz flux and relatively low radio luminosity.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, all three of the sources showing amorphous morphology, J1029+5857, J1057+5911 and J1108+5831, are among the eight candidate remnant radio sources with no identification of the core.

The third source (J1057+5911) is not detected at 1400 MHz, but the WSRT upper limit measurement allowed us to put a tighter constraint on its total flux density at 1400 MHz, and therefore, we used this measurement for the calculation of its spectral index and the total radio luminosity presented in the following sections.

We note that the three amorphous remnant radio galaxies (J1029+5857, J1057+5911 and J1108+5831), which all interestingly lack a core detection at 6000 MHz, are among the least powerful ones, with radio luminosities lower than the median value of the remnant sample (median value of radio luminosities of remnants is log _ 10 (L _ 150 MHz / W Hz^ 1 ) = 26 ).

gmw_00009603.name: J1108+5831
gmw_00009603.overview: J1108+5831 is an amorphous remnant radio galaxy associated with the central galaxy of a cluster [arXiv:2106.13161]. It has three possible optical counterparts, exhibits low radio luminosity, and lacks a core detection at 6000 MHz [arXiv:2106.13161].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1108+5831 as an amorphous remnant in a cluster central galaxy, with multiple possible optical counterparts, no 6000 MHz core, and low radio luminosity.

quotes.quote: J1108+5831 has three possible OCs. For both aforementioned sources, we chose the optical galaxy located between the two higher flux density regions of the fossil lobes.

Three out of the latter five sources do not have a detection of the core at 6000 MHz, and two also have a very diffuse and amorphous morphology (J1057+5911 and J1108+5831 with the SB values of 20 mJy arcmin ^ 2 and 70 mJy arcmin ^ 2 , respectively, measured from the LOFAR18 images). This demonstrates that the `classic' selection based on USS cannot be used to identify all remnant radio galaxies, however, it remains a very good proxy for this class of sources. As already discussed in MB17 remnants with low CP and non-USS may represent the youngest tail of the remnant population. We also note that in two sources (J1045+5631 and J1047+5550) we see a flattening of the integrated spectrum towards higher frequencies. As can be seen from Fig. fig:remnants1 , in these sources central emission dominates the total flux density. Source J1047+5550 has USS at low frequencies, confirming its remnant nature, while the flat central emission and detection of the core, both at 6000 MHz, might point to a restart of the nuclear activity. Out of seven remnants without a core detection, four have USS, thus representing the classical prototype of a remnant radio galaxy. One source worth noting is J1055+5635, whose optical host remained unidentified, despite a detection of the core at 6000 MHz. While the combination of the USS and the absence of an optical match may suggest that the source represents an active high-redshift radio galaxy, a recent work on its resolved spectral properties suggests that its spectral index is extremely steep even for a high redshift radio source supporting the remnant interpretation (for a detailed discussion see 2021A&A...648A...9Morganti_resolvedsi ). We confirm that some of the remnants (including some classified based on the USS emission) do show the presence of some level of nuclear radio activity (J1031+5915, J1045+5631, J1047+5550, J1052+5636, J1054+5505 and J1055+5635). The presence of cores (mean core radio luminosity of remnants being log_ 10 (L^ core _ 6000 MHz / W Hz ^ 1 ) = 23) means that not all remnants have a very low CP. This expands previous findings (e.g. 2010evn..confE..89ParmaVLBI,2011A&A...526A.148Murgia2011 and 2016A&A...585A..29Brienza ) and suggests that the nuclear activity in these sources might never completely switch off but only dims. Alternatively, we might be witnessing the beginning of a new phase of jet activity while the USS remnant plasma is still visible (see e.g. 2012ApJS..199...27Saripalli, 2020A&A...638A..34Jurlin, 2021A&A...648A...9Morganti_resolvedsi ). Discerning between these two scenarios is not a trivial task. How weak the core radio luminosity should be in order to classify a source as a remnant is not well assessed, and it is a strong function of frequency. The mean CP of the remnants with core detections at 6000 MHz in our sample is 0.08 when considering the measurement of the total flux density at 1400 MHz, and 0.006 when considering the measurement of the total flux density at 150 MHz. The latter value is comparable to the mean CP value of sources with core detections at 6000 MHz equal to 0.005 considering the measurement of the total flux density at 150 MHz, reported by 2018MNRAS.475.4557Mahatma_remnants . A useful tool that can be used in this respect is the relation between the CP and the radio luminosity. Sources that have USS and very low levels of CP for the total power (see Fig. fig:deRuiter_plot ), might be interpreted as dying sources (J1101+5603, marked with a star below the green line). On the contrary, ultra-steep sources with high levels of CP for their total power might be hinting at a restarting jet activity in the nuclear regions (e.g. sources J1031+5915, J1047+5550, J1054+5505 and J1055+5635, marked with stars above the green line). Overall, the morphological selection based on the core prominence has been revealed to be complex, due to the scatter around the relation presented by 1990A&A...227..351DeRuiter , and due to the lack of high spatial resolution and deep images at 1400 MHz. Properties of the remnant radio sources and their host galaxies The 13 remnants show a variety of morphologies, from amorphous to double-lobed, which likely depends on the progenitor, as well as on the plasma dynamical evolution after the jets switch off. The observed linear sizes are in the range [ 214.40, 1385 ] kpc, including 3 giant radio galaxies (linear size > 0.7 Mpc; sources J1031+5915, J1047+5550 and J1052+5636). We note that the sample is biased towards radio galaxies with larger sizes due to the cut in the angular size of 60 ^ applied in the selection of the initial sample by MB17. This also influences the total radio luminosities of the sources, which is for the majority of the sources (18 out of 21) above L _ 150 MHz > 10^ 25 W Hz ^ 1 as can be seen in Table tab:derived_radio_values . Given the fact that the source luminosity is decreasing in the remnant phase due to radiative and expansion losses (see e.g. Fig. 2 in 2020MNRAS.496.1706Shabala ), we infer that during their active phase all remnants in our sample must have had a luminosity well above the classical FRI-FRII dividing line 1994ASPC...54..319OwenLedlow_FRIandII . While more recent work by 2019MNRAS.488.2701Mingo showed that such dichotomy is much less strict than previously thought, we can still conclude that the majority of the 13 remnants come from a population of powerful active radio sources with double morphologies. We note that the three amorphous remnant radio galaxies (J1029+5857, J1057+5911 and J1108+5831), which all interestingly lack a core detection at 6000 MHz, are among the least powerful ones, with radio luminosities lower than the median value of the remnant sample (median value of radio luminosities of remnants is log _ 10 (L _ 150 MHz / W Hz^ 1 ) = 26 ).
Sources J1054+5505 and J1108+5831 have a distance from the cluster centre of 2 and 1 kpc, respectively, and are therefore associated to the central galaxy of the cluster.

gmw_00009604.name: J1102+5857
gmw_00009604.overview: J1102+5857 is a radio galaxy cluster remnant associated with the cluster J110252.6+585731 [arXiv:2106.13161]. It is located at a projected distance of 124 kpc from the cluster center and lacks a detected core at 6000 MHz [arXiv:2106.13161].

sources.summary: The paper reports J1102+5857 as a cluster remnant with two possible optical identifications, a 124 kpc cluster-center separation, no detected 6000 MHz core, and unremarkable spectral index.

quotes.quote: Finally, source J1102+5857 has two possible optical identifications, and we chose the most central one as the most probable host.

The three sources, J1054+5505, J1102+5857 and J1108+5831, belong to the clusters J105401.2+550611 (z = 0.4), J110252.6+585731 ^ +RedMaPPer (z = 0.34; z ^ RedMaPPer = 0.36 0.02) and J110804.3+583141 (z = 0.15), as they are located at the same redshift and have a projected distance from the cluster centre of 2, 124 and 1 kpc, respectively.

Only three out of 13 remnants in our final sample (J1054+5505, J1102+5857 and J1108+5831) are located in one of the clusters listed in the 2012ApJS..199...34WenHanLiu and/or 2014ApJ...785..104Rykoff catalogues. Sources J1054+5505 and J1108+5831 have a distance from the cluster centre of 2 and 1 kpc, respectively, and are therefore associated to the central galaxy of the cluster. The third remnant lies instead at 124 kpc from the cluster centre. We do not find any common properties characterising all our remnant sources located in clusters. For example, source J1054+5505 has its core detected at 6000 MHz, while J1102+5857 and J1108+5831 do not. The spectral indices of these three cluster sources are not remarkable when compared with the rest of the sample.

gmw_00009605.name: J1029+5857
gmw_00009605.overview: J1029+5857 is a remnant radio galaxy that exhibits an amorphous morphology [arXiv:2106.13161]. It has two very close optical galaxies as possible hosts, lacks a detected core at 6000 MHz, and is characterized by a relatively low radio luminosity [arXiv:2106.13161].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1029+5857 as an amorphous remnant with ambiguous optical hosts, no 6000 MHz core, and relatively low radio luminosity.

quotes.quote: In the case of the source J1029+5857, there are two optical galaxies, very close to each other, as possible hosts.
Interestingly, all three of the sources showing amorphous morphology, J1029+5857, J1057+5911 and J1108+5831, are among the eight candidate remnant radio sources with no identification of the core.

We note that the three amorphous remnant radio galaxies (J1029+5857, J1057+5911 and J1108+5831), which all interestingly lack a core detection at 6000 MHz, are among the least powerful ones, with radio luminosities lower than the median value of the remnant sample (median value of radio luminosities of remnants is log _ 10 (L _ 150 MHz / W Hz^ 1 ) = 26 ).

gmw_00009606.name: J1031+5915
gmw_00009606.overview: J1031+5915 is a confirmed remnant radio galaxy with a giant linear size exceeding 0.7 megaparsecs [arXiv:2106.13161]. It exhibits nuclear radio activity and shows signs of possible restarting jet activity in its nuclear regions [arXiv:2106.13161].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1031+5915 as a confirmed remnant showing nuclear radio activity, possible restarting jets, and a giant-radio-galaxy linear size.

quotes.quote: We confirm that some of the remnants (including some classified based on the USS emission) do show the presence of some level of nuclear radio activity (J1031+5915, J1045+5631, J1047+5550, J1052+5636, J1054+5505 and J1055+5635). The presence of cores (mean core radio luminosity of remnants being log_ 10 (L^ core _ 6000 MHz / W Hz ^ 1 ) = 23) means that not all remnants have a very low CP.

On the contrary, ultra-steep sources with high levels of CP for their total power might be hinting at a restarting jet activity in the nuclear regions (e.g. sources J1031+5915, J1047+5550, J1054+5505 and J1055+5635, marked with stars above the green line).
The observed linear sizes are in the range [ 214.40, 1385 ] kpc, including 3 giant radio galaxies (linear size > 0.7 Mpc; sources J1031+5915, J1047+5550 and J1052+5636).

gmw_00009607.name: DF5
gmw_00009607.overview: DF5 is a large low-surface-brightness galaxy that closely resembles the galaxies DF2 and DF4 [arXiv:2205.08552]. It is located only 1.6 arcminutes in projection from DF4 and is interpreted as a dark-matter-free fragment formed during a galaxy collision [arXiv:2205.08552].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DF5 as a large, unusual low-surface-brightness trail galaxy resembling DF2 and DF4 and interprets it as another dark-matter-free collision fragment.

quotes.quote: We highlight three large and unusual galaxies that are part of the sample of eleven. DF5 looks very similar to DF2 and DF4 but has a much lower surface brightness. It is so close in projection to DF4 (only 1.6' away) that both galaxies were observed in the same HST/ACS pointing. In the bullet dwarf scenario this puzzling object is readily explained as another dark matter-free fragment that formed in the aftermath of the collision.

gmw_00009608.name: RCP32
gmw_00009608.overview: RCP32 is an extremely faint ultra-diffuse galaxy that may host a globular cluster system [arXiv:2205.08552]. It is tentatively identified as a candidate remnant of a pre-collision progenitor galaxy [arXiv:2205.08552].

sources.summary: The paper describes RCP32 as an extremely faint ultra-diffuse galaxy ahead of the trail, possibly containing globular clusters and representing a progenitor remnant.

quotes.quote: RCP32 and DF7 are the furthest away from the center of the structure, and are ``ahead'' of \ and \ respectively. RCP32 is the faintest ultra-diffuse galaxy (UDG) that has so far been discovered in integrated light and may have a globular cluster system. DF7 is an elongated UDG that was previously observed with HST (see Methods section). We tentatively identify RCP32 and DF7 as the candidate remnants of the original, pre-collision galaxies. In the geometry of Fig. \ geometry.fig b RCP32 could be the remnant of progenitor 1, the gas-rich object that was not bound to NGC1052, and the brighter galaxy DF7 could be the remnant of progenitor 2, the galaxy that was a NGC1052 satellite at the time of collision.

gmw_00009609.name: DF7
gmw_00009609.overview: DF7 is an elongated and distorted ultra-diffuse galaxy located at the leading edge of a trail ahead of DF4 [arXiv:2205.08552]. It is speculated to be a highly dark matter-dominated, possibly largely disrupted, remnant of a progenitor galaxy [arXiv:2205.08552].

sources.summary: The paper presents DF7 as an elongated ultra-diffuse galaxy ahead of DF4, possibly a disrupted dark-matter-dominated remnant of one progenitor galaxy.

quotes.quote: DF7 is an elongated UDG that was previously observed with HST (see Methods section). We tentatively identify RCP32 and DF7 as the candidate remnants of the original, pre-collision galaxies. In the geometry of Fig. \ geometry.fig b RCP32 could be the remnant of progenitor 1, the gas-rich object that was not bound to NGC1052, and the brighter galaxy DF7 could be the remnant of progenitor 2, the galaxy that was a NGC1052 satellite at the time of collision.

Hubble Space Telescope imaging of the candidate dark galaxy DF7. DF7 is at one of the leading edges of the trail, ``ahead" of DF4. The galaxy was observed with HST/ACS as part of an exploratory survey of Dragonfly-identified low surface brightness galaxies in several groups. The observations constituted two orbits, one orbit in F606W and one orbit in F814W. In Fig. \ df7.fig we show the HST imaging at two different contrast levels. The galaxy is elongated and appears distorted, with the elongation in the direction of DF4. DF7's apparent distortion, combined with its location at the leading edge of the trail, lead us to speculate that the galaxy is the highly dark matter-dominated remnant of one of the two progenitor galaxies. We note that DF7 may be largely disrupted in this interpretation: the observed axis ratio is b/a=0.42 , but given the extreme foreshortening of the geometry the intrinsic axis ratio could be a stream-like 1:20 .

gmw_00009610.name: J2101+0341
gmw_00009610.overview: J2101+0341 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=1.013 [arXiv:astro-ph/0410663]. It exhibits a complex radio structure featuring two components aligned along the north-south axis and diffuse, low-brightness emission extending to the northeast [arXiv:astro-ph/0410663].

sources.summary: The paper describes J2101+0341 as a quasar with complex radio morphology, including aligned components and diffuse low-brightness emission.

quotes.quote: J2101+0341 : quasar at z=1.013 . This source has a very complex structure. Two components are aligned with the NS axis for a total angular size of 14 arcsec. Furthermore there is a diffuse low-brightness emission in the NE direction with angular size of 22 arcsec (see Fig. fig:J2024+1718 ).

gmw_00009611.name: J2320+0513
gmw_00009611.overview: J2320+0513 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=0.622 [arXiv:astro-ph/0410663]. It exhibits extended radio emission in both the L and C bands, featuring two irregular components on either side of its unresolved nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0410663].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J2320+0513 as a quasar and reports extended L- and C-band radio emission with irregular components around its unresolved nucleus.

quotes.quote: J2320+0513 : quasar at z=0.622 . Extended emission is revealed in both the L and the C band. The maximum angular size in the NW-SE direction is of 19 arcsec. In the C band the extended emission has two irregular components on both sides of the unresolved nucleus, the southern one being weaker than the northern one (see Fig. fig:J2320+0513 ).

gmw_00009612.name: ALMA 24
gmw_00009612.overview: ALMA 24 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:2305.06388]. Depending on the model used to fit its spectral energy distribution, the galaxy has an estimated dust temperature of either 42.0 K or 59.0 K [arXiv:2305.06388].

sources.summary: The paper compares optically thin and opacity-plus-power-law SED fits for ALMA 24, finding both fit but give different dust temperatures and emissivity indices.

quotes.quote: In Figure opac_fit_comp , we show a comparison of this opacity + power law model and the optically thin model fits for ALMA 24. Both fit the data well but with somewhat different median likelihood values. The optically thin fit gives = 1.44 and T_d = 42.0 K, while the opacity + power law fit gives = 1.76 and T_d = 59.0 K. We also note that for this galaxy, the =2.0 power law slope does a good job of fitting the measured MIPS 70 m point, which is not included in our optically thin fit.

gmw_00009613.name: ALMA 43
gmw_00009613.overview: ALMA 43 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:2305.06388]. Photometric measurements of the galaxy constrain its dust temperature to approximately 37.0 K and provide tightly constrained dust emissivity parameters [arXiv:2305.06388].

sources.summary: The paper uses ALMA 43 as a well-sampled SED example, reporting five ALMA measurements and tightly constrained emissivity and dust temperature.

quotes.quote: We illustrate this for a concrete example in Figure sed_comp , where we compare the fits for ALMA 43, which has 5 ALMA measurements; ALMA 48, which has just 2; and ALMA 60, which is not included in the robust subset due to it only having ALMA 870 m and shorter wavelength data. We also show the marginalized likelihood distributions (histograms) and joint likelihood distributions (contour plot) for and T_d for each source. For ALMA 43, the parameters are tightly constrained, with = 1.54^ +0.27 _ 0.26 and T_d = 37.0^ +6.1 _ 4.7 K. The elongated shape of the joint likelihood distribution reflects the intrinsic degeneracy between and T_d , though in this case the parameters are well-constrained regardless.

gmw_00009614.name: ALMA 60
gmw_00009614.overview: ALMA 60 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:2305.06388]. Observations of its spectral energy distribution, which lacks millimeter data, yield a dust temperature of 36.0 Kelvin and a dust emissivity index of 1.72 [arXiv:2305.06388].

sources.summary: The paper uses ALMA 60 to show how lacking millimeter data broadens emissivity constraints despite sampled SPIRE/submillimeter SED peaks.

quotes.quote: We illustrate this for a concrete example in Figure sed_comp , where we compare the fits for ALMA 43, which has 5 ALMA measurements; ALMA 48, which has just 2; and ALMA 60, which is not included in the robust subset due to it only having ALMA 870 m and shorter wavelength data. We also show the marginalized likelihood distributions (histograms) and joint likelihood distributions (contour plot) for and T_d for each source.

In contrast, for ALMA 60, though the peak of the SED is sampled by the SPIRE and submillimeter data, the lack of millimeter data means that a much larger range of emissivities are consistent with the available data (clearly apparent in the posterior likelihood distribution for ). We find = 1.72^ +1.20 _ 0.68 and T_d=36.0^ +14.4 _ 11.1 K for this source. These errors are 2--4 higher than those for ALMA 43, which is also reflected in the 16th to 84th percentile distribution of models shown in the SED fits and the relatively wide joint likelihood distribution for and T_d .

gmw_00009615.name: ALMA 68
gmw_00009615.overview: ALMA 68 is an optically dark, ultra-massive galaxy at a spectroscopic redshift of z=5.58 [arXiv:2305.06388, arXiv:2309.02492]. It exhibits intense, dust-obscured star formation [arXiv:2309.02492] and may be undergoing a merger with an even more obscured companion [arXiv:2309.02492].

sources.summary: The paper identifies S1 as an ultra-massive, optically dark z~5.5 galaxy with heavy dust attenuation, intense obscured star formation, extended dust emission, and a possible obscured merger companion.

quotes.quote: Based on the deep JWST observations, we find that the three galaxies have extremely red properties (F182M F444W > 3.5 mag; Fig. fig1 , Extended Data Fig. Extended_color_mag ), due to their significant dust attenuation ( A_ V >3 mag). Self-consistently, we find that they also have highly dust-obscured star formation rates (SFR _ IR ), which are obtained from far-infrared SED fits with CIGALE , specifically 625 31 M_ yr ^ 1 for S1, 1030 _ 150 ^ +190 M _ yr ^ 1 for S2, and 1137 31 M _ yr ^ 1 for S3 (see Methods). This indicates that they are in the process of very efficient stellar mass build-up.

In S1, we observe dust emission (half-light radius R_ e, =2.88 0.91 kpc) significantly more extended than the stellar component ( R_ e, F444W =0.76 0.01 kpc), giving access to the circumgalactic region. We note that the dust distribution in dusty star-forming galaxies is typically more compact relative to the stellar distribution , which is thought to be caused by angular momentum loss driven by mergers or counter-rotating gas accretion. Our findings for S1 seem to favor an opposite scenario, in which the galaxy activity may be triggered by a strong event of corotating and coplanar gas accretion through the cold streams . In addition, we find a 6 off-nuclear clump in the dust distribution map of S1 (see the right panel of Fig. fig3 ). Surprisingly, no stellar emission associated with this clump is detected in the deep JWST data. It suggests that S1 might be undergoing a merger with an even more obscured galaxy that is still missed, even by JWST.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ALMA 68 as the sample source with the highest spectroscopic redshift, z=5.58, visible in JWST NIRCam but not CANDELS HST imaging.

quotes.quote: The highest specz in our sample is for ALMA 68 (numbered as in Table 4 of C18) at z=5.58 , obtained in obn+23 using JWST NIRCam/grism spectra from the FRESCO survey. A NIRCam F444W, F210M, F182M color image of this source is shown in Figure alma68cut . Although this source was undetected in the CANDELS HST images, it is clearly visible in the NIRCam image.

gmw_00009616.name: BH-NB5-6
gmw_00009616.overview: BH-NB5-6 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy classified as a broad-line active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1802.10102]. It is detected as an X-ray source and exhibits N V emission [arXiv:1802.10102].

sources.summary: BH-NB5-6 is identified as a broad-line AGN, the only X-ray source among covered luminous LAEs, and one of the objects with significant N v emission.

quotes.quote: Finally, for our Bo o tes sources, we find one X-ray detection (BH-NB5-6, a broad-line AGN), while the other sources are not detected in the X-rays for a flux limit of 4 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , about 5 times shallower than COSMOS X-day data, see Murray2005 . Therefore, using the available X-ray data, we find a single X-ray source (BH-NB5-6) out of 18 which have X-ray coverage, but we note that the available data are very heterogeneous, do not cover our most luminous LAEs in SA22, and are generally not deep enough to detect lower accretion and lower black mass AGN.

We find that sources SA22-NB5-18, BH-NB5-6, 10, 16, 27 and CALYMHA 415 all show significant N v emission (observed ratios N v /Ly 0.1-0.6 ). Half of these sources are BL-AGN (see Section BLAGN_class ), while BH-NB5-6 is also an X-ray source.

gmw_00009617.name: BH-NB5-10
gmw_00009617.overview: BH-NB5-10 is a narrow-line active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1802.10102] that is highly luminous in both the ultraviolet and Lyman-alpha bands [arXiv:1802.10102]. The galaxy exhibits broad emission-line widths [arXiv:1802.10102] along with large blue-shifted absorption features at carbon IV and Lyman-alpha [arXiv:1802.10102].

sources.summary: BH-NB5-10 is classed as a narrow-line AGN with high N v/Ly, broad emission-line widths, bright UV/Ly output, and blue-shifted absorption features.

quotes.quote: Apart from BH-NB5-10, which was already classed as a NL-AGN due to the high N v /Ly ratio of 0.17 0.03 , we identify two further narrow-line sources which are consistent with being NL-AGN: BR3, the brightest narrow-line LAE in our sample and GN-NB5-3378, with a much fainter Ly luminosity. Both BH-NB5-10 and BR3 have emission-line FWHMs of 600-700 km s ^ 1 , higher than the other NL-emitters ( 100-300 km s ^ 1 ), also being brighter in UV and Ly .

We also show even brighter broad-line emitters, and one of the brightest in the UV (BH-NB5-10), which shows large blue-shifted absorption features at C iv and Ly . Figure BR3_and_friends reveals the large variety in the rest-frame UV spectra of the most luminous LAEs at z 2--3, which we find to be consistent with being powered by AGN.

gmw_00009618.name: BR3
gmw_00009618.overview: BR3 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy and the brightest narrow-line source in its sample [arXiv:1802.10102]. It is classified as a narrow-line active galactic nucleus powered by a highly ionizing source, with emission-line widths of approximately 600 km/s [arXiv:1802.10102].

sources.summary: BR3 is the brightest narrow-line LAE in the sample, with strong high-ionization UV lines, about 600 km/s line widths, and an AGN-like powering source.

quotes.quote: Apart from BH-NB5-10, which was already classed as a NL-AGN due to the high N v /Ly ratio of 0.17 0.03 , we identify two further narrow-line sources which are consistent with being NL-AGN: BR3, the brightest narrow-line LAE in our sample and GN-NB5-3378, with a much fainter Ly luminosity. Both BH-NB5-10 and BR3 have emission-line FWHMs of 600-700 km s ^ 1 , higher than the other NL-emitters ( 100-300 km s ^ 1 ), also being brighter in UV and Ly .

In Figure BR3_and_friends we show our most luminous narrow-line LAE (BR3), which shows strong C iv , He ii , C iii ] and weaker O iii ] , revealing a highly ionising powering source. No N v is detected and all lines have FWHMs similar to Ly , of 600 km s ^ 1 .

gmw_00009619.name: GN-NB5-6712
gmw_00009619.overview: GN-NB5-6712 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha galaxy that is likely dominated by star formation [arXiv:1802.10102]. Models of its ultraviolet line ratios suggest a young 20-megayear-old burst of star formation and a sub-solar to solar gas-phase metallicity [arXiv:1802.10102].

sources.summary: GN-NB5-6712 is likely star-formation dominated; BPASS models reproduce its UV line ratios with a young burst and sub-solar to solar metallicity.

quotes.quote: For the only source with at least two UV lines which is likely dominated by star-formation (GN-NB5-6712), we find that BPASS models are able to reproduce the line ratios, although black body models with temperatures 70-100 kK also reproduce the constraints on the line ratios. The cloudy BPASS models suggest a 20 Myr-old burst, an ionisation parameter of U=-3.0^ +1.7 _ 0.8 and sub-solar to solar gas-phase metallicity ( 0.6^ +0.8 _ 0.4 Z_ ), and thus similar to properties derived for faint LAEs e.g. NakajimaOuchi2013,Suzuki2017 . We note, nonetheless, that GN-NB5-6712 is not necessarily representative of the population of bright LAEs dominated by star-formation, particularly due to the detection of both C iv and He ii .

gmw_00009620.name: UGC 7228
gmw_00009620.overview: UGC 7228 is a regular-disk galaxy that hosted the Type Ia supernova SN 2007sw [arXiv:1907.03574]. The galaxy's oxygen abundance increases toward its center, indicating an inside-out formation process [arXiv:1907.03574].

sources.summary: UGC 7228 is a regular-disk Type Ia supernova host whose oxygen abundance rises inward, with SN 2007sw close to the highest-metallicity region.

quotes.quote: According to Fig.  fig.SNIa-havd , the H velocity gradient of the host galaxies of all the four Type Ia SNe 2007sw (UGC 7228), 2006iq (PGC 1380172), 2007R (PGC 21767) and 2005cc (NGC 5383) are smooth without irregularities. There are some spaxels that have a higher in the host galaxies and they distribute in the outer regions of the field of view. We infer that this may be caused by the low signal-to-noise (S/N) for the spaxels in the outer region. Roughly, the host galaxies of all the SNe Ia only have one peak of and the peaks locate at the center of the host galaxies. Therefore, all the SNe Ia host galaxies here are almost RD.

The oxygen abundance increases towards the center of the host galaxy of 2007sw (UGC 7228), which indicate an inside-outside formation of the galaxy. In the center, the metalliciy reaches the highest value and the supernova 2007sw locates close to the highest metallicity.

gmw_00009621.name: PGC 1380172
gmw_00009621.overview: PGC 1380172 is a regular-disk galaxy characterized by a smooth velocity gradient [arXiv:1907.03574]. Globally, the galaxy is classified as star-forming, though it exhibits a lower oxygen abundance in its central regions [arXiv:1907.03574].

sources.summary: PGC 1380172 is a regular-disk Type Ia host with lower central metallicity; its global spectrum is star-forming while the SN site is composite.

quotes.quote: The global spectrum of the host galaxy of SN 2006iq (PGC 1380172) locates in the star forming region, while the supernova 2006iq locates in the composite region.

According to Fig.  fig.SNIa-havd , the H velocity gradient of the host galaxies of all the four Type Ia SNe 2007sw (UGC 7228), 2006iq (PGC 1380172), 2007R (PGC 21767) and 2005cc (NGC 5383) are smooth without irregularities. There are some spaxels that have a higher in the host galaxies and they distribute in the outer regions of the field of view. We infer that this may be caused by the low signal-to-noise (S/N) for the spaxels in the outer region. Roughly, the host galaxies of all the SNe Ia only have one peak of and the peaks locate at the center of the host galaxies. Therefore, all the SNe Ia host galaxies here are almost RD.
The oxygen abundances decrease towards the central regions for the host galaxies of SN Ia 2006iq (PGC 1380172) and 2007R (PGC 21767), which means an outside-inside formation of the galaxies.

gmw_00009622.name: PGC 21767
gmw_00009622.overview: PGC 21767 is a regular-disk galaxy that hosted the Type Ia supernova SN 2007R [arXiv:1907.03574]. The galaxy's oxygen abundance decreases toward its central region, which is consistent with an outside-inside formation history [arXiv:1907.03574].

sources.summary: PGC 21767 is analyzed as a regular-disk Type Ia host whose oxygen abundance decreases toward the center, consistent with outside-inside formation.

quotes.quote: According to Fig.  fig.SNIa-havd , the H velocity gradient of the host galaxies of all the four Type Ia SNe 2007sw (UGC 7228), 2006iq (PGC 1380172), 2007R (PGC 21767) and 2005cc (NGC 5383) are smooth without irregularities. There are some spaxels that have a higher in the host galaxies and they distribute in the outer regions of the field of view. We infer that this may be caused by the low signal-to-noise (S/N) for the spaxels in the outer region. Roughly, the host galaxies of all the SNe Ia only have one peak of and the peaks locate at the center of the host galaxies. Therefore, all the SNe Ia host galaxies here are almost RD.

The oxygen abundances decrease towards the central regions for the host galaxies of SN Ia 2006iq (PGC 1380172) and 2007R (PGC 21767), which means an outside-inside formation of the galaxies. The host galaxy of 2007R (PGC 21767) has been analyzed in . Our result for this galaxy agree well with .

gmw_00009623.name: MCG +07-34-015
gmw_00009623.overview: MCG +07-34-015 is an unbarred Sb-type spiral galaxy that hosted the Type II supernova SN 2000cs [arXiv:1907.03574]. The galaxy features an active galactic nucleus at its center, while its outer regions exhibit an increasing metallicity gradient [arXiv:1907.03574].

sources.summary: MCG +07-34-015 hosts SN 2000cs, has central H flux like an unbarred Sb spiral, and has an AGN-masked center with outward-rising metallicity.

quotes.quote: From Fig.  fig.SNII-havd , the host galaxies of Type II SN 2000cs (MCG +07-34-015), 2010ee (UGC 8652), 2012al (PGC 213664) and 2004eb (NGC 6387) have the highest H flux in the center in each galaxy, which indicate that these galaxies are unbarred Sb-type spiral galaxies. From the pseudo-color image of MaNGA, the host galaxy of 2004eb (NGC 6387) is in the process of merger, which will make effect on the SFR, oxygen abundance etc. The supernova 2004eb is on the edge of the field of view of this galaxy.

According to Fig.  fig.BPT and Table.  table.bptlines , the centers of the host galaxy of unclassified type of SN 1975K (NGC 6195) and the host galaxy of SN II 2000cs (MCG +07-34-015) are in the AGN regions. However, their global spectrum locate in the star-forming galaxies and composites regions. We can infer that for these two galaxies, the effect of AGNs is too small to change the global galaxy spectra ( st12 ).
We can see from the 2D map of the host galaxy of 2000cs (MCG +07-34-015) that the center is masked due to the presence of AGNs, and the metallicity increase in outer regions.

gmw_00009624.name: UGC 8652
gmw_00009624.overview: UGC 8652 is an unbarred Sb-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1907.03574] that hosted the Type II supernova SN 2010ee [arXiv:1907.03574]. Viewed nearly edge-on, the galaxy exhibits gas dust extinction and H-alpha flux that both increase toward its center [arXiv:1907.03574].

sources.summary: UGC 8652 hosts SN 2010ee; the paper describes central H flux, inward-increasing dust extinction, and two oxygen-abundance peaks likely in spiral arms.

quotes.quote: From Fig.  fig.SNII-havd , the host galaxies of Type II SN 2000cs (MCG +07-34-015), 2010ee (UGC 8652), 2012al (PGC 213664) and 2004eb (NGC 6387) have the highest H flux in the center in each galaxy, which indicate that these galaxies are unbarred Sb-type spiral galaxies. From the pseudo-color image of MaNGA, the host galaxy of 2004eb (NGC 6387) is in the process of merger, which will make effect on the SFR, oxygen abundance etc. The supernova 2004eb is on the edge of the field of view of this galaxy.

From Fig.  fig.SNII-havd , we can see that the gas dust extinction distribution of Type II SN 2010ee host galaxy increases towards the center, which has the similar trend as the H flux. However, according to the image and the inclination, the host of SN 2010ee is an edge-on galaxy, so it is difficult to estimate the extinction accurately.
There are two peaks of gas-phase oxygen abundance in the host galaxy of 2010ee (UGC 8652), which may be in the spiral arms, but the center has lower metallicity.

gmw_00009625.name: NGC 6387
gmw_00009625.overview: NGC 6387 is an unbarred Sb-type spiral galaxy that is currently undergoing a merger within an interactive system [arXiv:1907.03574]. The galaxy exhibits perturbed rotation, a high central hydrogen-alpha flux, and a gas-phase oxygen abundance that increases toward its center [arXiv:1907.03574].

sources.summary: NGC 6387 is the Type II SN 2004eb host, described as merging or interacting, with perturbed rotation, central H flux, and inward-increasing oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: From Fig.  fig.SNII-havd , the host galaxies of Type II SN 2000cs (MCG +07-34-015), 2010ee (UGC 8652), 2012al (PGC 213664) and 2004eb (NGC 6387) have the highest H flux in the center in each galaxy, which indicate that these galaxies are unbarred Sb-type spiral galaxies. From the pseudo-color image of MaNGA, the host galaxy of 2004eb (NGC 6387) is in the process of merger, which will make effect on the SFR, oxygen abundance etc. The supernova 2004eb is on the edge of the field of view of this galaxy.

For SN 2004eb, there is more than one peak in the map of the host galaxy (NGC 6387), which indicates that the host galaxy of SN 2004eb (NGC 6387) is PR. It can also be shown from the images of MaNGA that NGC 6387 is in an interactive system.
The gas phase oxygen abundances of host galaxies of SN 2012al (PGC 213664) and 2004eb (NGC 6387) increase towards the centers.

gmw_00009626.name: NGC 6195
gmw_00009626.overview: NGC 6195 is a disk galaxy with regular kinematics and a smooth velocity gradient [arXiv:1907.03574]. The galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus at its center, though its global spectrum is dominated by star formation [arXiv:1907.03574].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC6195 as a worked SPARC rotation-curve example: Gaussian-prior Bayesian fitting with the RAR interpolating function shows weak parameter correlations, a variable acceleration-scale posterior, and a fit similar to L18.

quotes.quote: Fig. fig:NGC6195Tri shows the example of the Bayesian analysis with Gaussian priors for galaxy NGC6195 using the RAR-inspired interpolating function. The same Bayesian analysis of this galaxy is also represented in R18 and L18. In Fig. fig:NGC6195Tri , we find that there is no strong correlation between the parameters when Gaussian priors are employed.

Fig. fig:NGC6195Tri shows that a variable acceleration scale could reduce the variation of other parameter. The maximum of marginalized posterior is _ 10 a_0=-13.115, _ d =0.325, _ b =0.714, _D=0.999, _i=1.008 . In addition, it also shows that the Gaussian prior imposed on a_0 is almost flat respect to the marginalized posterior, there is extremely weak constraint on the distribution of a_0 , which prevents a_0 too small. In Fig. fig:NGC6195RC , we show the rotation curves for galaxy NGC6195 by using the maximum of marginalized posterior. A similar fit is obtained as well as L18.

sources.summary: NGC 6195 hosts SN 1975K; its center is AGN-like but globally weakly affected, with regular disk kinematics and a centrally decreasing oxygen abundance.

quotes.quote: According to Fig.  fig.BPT and Table.  table.bptlines , the centers of the host galaxy of unclassified type of SN 1975K (NGC 6195) and the host galaxy of SN II 2000cs (MCG +07-34-015) are in the AGN regions. However, their global spectrum locate in the star-forming galaxies and composites regions. We can infer that for these two galaxies, the effect of AGNs is too small to change the global galaxy spectra ( st12 ).

According to Fig.  fig.unSN-havd , the H velocity gradient of the host galaxy of unclassified type of SN 1975K (NGC 6195) is smooth without irregularities. The map has only one peak in the galaxy center. Therefore, according to the velocity field and the map, NGC 6195 is almost RD without turbulence.
According to Fig.  fig.unSN-havd , the gas phase oxygen abundance of SN 1975K host galaxy decreases from outside towards central regions.

gmw_00009627.name: ESO 544-27
gmw_00009627.overview: ESO 544-27 is an edge-on disk galaxy located in the green valley [arXiv:2201.00566, arXiv:2405.17999]. Although its thin and thick disks are dominated by old, metal-rich stellar populations, the galaxy recently experienced a reignition of star formation [arXiv:2405.17999].

sources.summary: The paper studies ESO 544-27 with MUSE and H-alpha imaging, identifying it as green-valley, noting weak eDIG/thin-disk correlation under stringent cuts, and widespread secondary HOLMES-like ionization.

quotes.quote: We investigate the origin of the extraplanar diffuse ionized gas (eDIG) and its predominant ionization mechanisms in five nearby (17--46 Mpc) low-mass ( 10^9 - 10^ 10 M_ ) edge-on disk galaxies: ESO 157-49, ESO 469-15, ESO 544-27, IC 217, and IC 1553. We acquired Multi Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) integral field spectroscopy and deep narrowband H imaging of our sample galaxies. To investigate the connection between in-plane star formation and eDIG, we measure the star formation rates (SFRs) and perform a photometric analysis of our narrowband H imaging. Using our MUSE data, we investigate the origin of eDIG via kinematics, specifically the rotation velocity lags. We also construct standard diagnostic diagrams and emission-line maps (EW(H ), [ N   ii ] /H , [ S   ii ] //H , [ O   iii ] /H ) and search for regions consistent with ionization by hot low-mass evolved stars (HOLMES) and shocks.

ESO 157-49, ESO 469-15, and IC 217 are also marginally star-forming when star-forming galaxies are defined as those with sSFR > 10^ 10.6 yr ^ 1 hsieh2017manga , while ESO 544-27 falls into the green valley. Using the stringent eDIG definition does not greatly affect the correlation in the galaxies with the strongest correlations (ESO 469-15, IC 217, and IC 1553), but it does significantly weaken the correlation in the galaxies with the weakest correlations (ESO 157-49 and ESO 544-27), with a minimum of 0.15 in the case of ESO 544-27. The distribution of this secondary HOLMES ionization differs significantly between the galaxies, ESO 544-27 having EW(H ) < 14 Å nearly everywhere outside the disk, while IC 1553 has EW(H ) < 14 Å only in few extraplanar regions on the northern side of the disk.

sources.summary: The paper uses MUSE stellar-population fitting to show ESO 544-27 has old metal-rich thin and thick discs, alpha-element dichotomy, and recently reignited star formation.

quotes.quote: We utilise archival MUSE IFU observations of the edge-on galaxy ESO 544-27 to uncover the formation history of its thin and thick discs through its stellar populations. We find the thin disc of the galaxy is dominated by an old ( >9  Gyr) low [ /Fe ] metal-rich stellar population. Its outer thick disc is dominated by an old ( >9  Gyr) high [ /Fe ] metal-rich component that should have formed with higher star-formation efficiency than the Milky Way thick disc. We thus find [ /Fe ] dichotomy in ESO 544-27 with its thin and thick discs dominated by low and high [ /Fe ] stellar populations respectively. However, we also find a metal-rich younger ( <2  Gyr old) stellar population in ESO 544-27.

The galaxy was nearly quenched until its star-formation was reignited recently first in the outer and inner thick disc ( 1 Gyr ago) and then in the thin disc ( 600 Myr ago). We thus find that both the low [ /Fe ] thin and high [ /Fe ] thick discs of ESO 544-27 are inhabited primarily by similarly old metal-rich stellar populations, a contrast to that of other galaxies with known thin and thick disc stellar population properties.

gmw_00009628.name: SDSS J0752+2736
gmw_00009628.overview: SDSS J0752+2736 is an infrared galaxy characterized by very red mid-infrared colors [arXiv:1504.01391]. It most likely hosts a buried active galactic nucleus with biconical photoionization regions, alongside a substantial X-ray contribution from star formation [arXiv:1504.01391].

sources.summary: The paper classifies SDSS J0752+2736 as a likely single AGN, with substantial possible star-formation X-ray contribution, very red mid-infrared colors, and evidence for a buried AGN.

quotes.quote: Two of the galaxies, SDSS J0752+2736 and SDSS J1006+4647, may have significant contributions from star formation to the observed X-ray luminosities (Section wise ). In SDSS J0752+2736, 47 of the 0.5-8 keV luminosity may be due to star formation, while in SDSS J1006+4647, 41 of the 0.5-8 keV luminosity may be attributed to star formation. These two galaxies also have the reddest colors ( W1-W4 > 6.5 ; Figure fig:wise ) of the eight galaxies whose mid-infrared fluxes are dominated by star formation, which suggests that these two galaxies may host highly obscured AGNs.

However, photoionized gas likely contributes to the observed fluxes of SDSS J0752+2736 and SDSS J1448+1825, given their soft X-ray spectra and low ratios of soft X-ray to luminosities. In each of these two systems, the two observed X-ray and emission sources could be produced by an AGN and a photoionized region, or a buried AGN with a biconical photoionization region. Given the evidence from the mid-infrared colors for an obscured AGN in SDSS J0752+2736, it most likely hosts a buried AGN with biconical photoionization regions producing the observed X-ray and sources.

gmw_00009629.name: SDSS J1006+4647
gmw_00009629.overview: SDSS J1006+4647 is classified as a likely single active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1504.01391]. Its red mid-infrared color suggests it may host a highly obscured active galactic nucleus, with star formation potentially contributing forty-one percent of its observed X-ray luminosity [arXiv:1504.01391].

sources.summary: SDSS J1006+4647 is classified among likely single AGNs; its X-ray luminosity may include a large star-formation contribution and its red mid-infrared color suggests a highly obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: The remaining six galaxies (SDSS J0142 0050, SDSS J0752+2736, SDSS J0854+5026, SDSS J1006+4647, SDSS J1448+1825, and SDSS J1604+5009) are likely single AGNs where the double-peaked narrow emission lines are the result of outflows, jets, or disk rotation. HST /IR observations are useful for revealing the stellar structures of these six galaxies. Four of them were observed with HST /F160W for this program, one of them has archival HST /F105W imaging (J1604+5009; GO 12521, PI: Liu), and one of them has no published high-resolution imaging (J0752+2736). We cannot be sure of the exact morphology of J0752+2736 (in SDSS imaging it appears almost edge-on, with no obvious substructure), but for the five galaxies with HST imaging we do not find double stellar bulges associated with the double emission components.

Two of the galaxies, SDSS J0752+2736 and SDSS J1006+4647, may have significant contributions from star formation to the observed X-ray luminosities (Section wise ). In SDSS J0752+2736, 47 of the 0.5-8 keV luminosity may be due to star formation, while in SDSS J1006+4647, 41 of the 0.5-8 keV luminosity may be attributed to star formation. These two galaxies also have the reddest colors ( W1-W4 > 6.5 ; Figure fig:wise ) of the eight galaxies whose mid-infrared fluxes are dominated by star formation, which suggests that these two galaxies may host highly obscured AGNs.

gmw_00009630.name: Cloud 1
gmw_00009630.overview: Cloud 1 is a gas-rich, optically inert extragalactic hydrogen cloud located on the periphery of the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1010.6095]. It is resolved into northern and southern components with distinct velocity gradients, but it lacks any detectable optical, infrared, or ultraviolet counterparts [arXiv:1010.6095].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Cloud 1 as a gas-rich, optically inert Virgo-periphery HI cloud resolved by VLA into northern and southern components with velocity structure but no optical, IR, or UV counterpart.

quotes.quote: The two HI clouds (henceforth Cloud 1 and 2) were detected in the ALFALFA data obtained in the Spring 2005 campaign sampling the Virgo cluster and its surrounding periphery. The detections were reported in Kent (2007) as part of an initial collection of gas-rich, optically inert extragalactic objects.

The northern cloud 1N appears to be more aligned north-- south whereas the lower signal-to-noise southern cloud 1S is aligned east--west. The northern cloud 1N exhibits a clear north--south velocity gradient. The velocity gradient of the southern cloud 1S is slightly more disordered but trends along the east/west axis of the detection. There is no discernible structure on smaller scales or clumpiness as seen in the Virgo HI objects examined in Kent (2009). No counterparts are observed in the field in any available optical, IR, or UV imaging databases.

gmw_00009631.name: Cloud 2
gmw_00009631.overview: Cloud 2 is a gas-rich, optically inert neutral hydrogen cloud located on the periphery of the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1010.6095]. High-resolution observations resolve the cloud into three distinct emission peaks that show no ordered motion [arXiv:1010.6095].

sources.summary: The paper describes Cloud 2 as a gas-rich, optically inert Virgo-periphery HI cloud whose VLA emission is weaker, partly resolved out, split into three peaks, and lacks ordered motion.

quotes.quote: The two HI clouds (henceforth Cloud 1 and 2) were detected in the ALFALFA data obtained in the Spring 2005 campaign sampling the Virgo cluster and its surrounding periphery. The detections were reported in Kent (2007) as part of an initial collection of gas-rich, optically inert extragalactic objects.

We recover 41 5 of the flux (Tables AOparams and VLAparams ) compared to the Arecibo data. It is likely that lower column density emission is resolved out and we are only detecting the higher column density peaks of the source. We identify three of these peaks (Cloud 2 North, West, and South) fit for the same parameters as the Cloud 1 field. Clouds 2 North and West are elongated in an east-west direction, where the Southern component is a marginal detection in the map with low signal to noise, albeit still a 5 ^ _m detection in the map. The velocity maps of all three Cloud 2 field detections show no ordered motion.

gmw_00009632.name: IC 1277
gmw_00009632.overview: IC 1277 is an Scd-type spiral galaxy characterized by an exponential disk scale length of 6.7 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1106.3071]. The galaxy exhibits a radial oxygen-abundance gradient of -0.03 dex per kiloparsec, resulting in a metal-poor outer disk [arXiv:1106.3071].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes IC 1277 as the Scd host of SN 2006gz, measuring its disk scale length, the supernova's outer-disk location, and a radial oxygen-abundance gradient indicating a metal-poor site.

quotes.quote: SN 2006gz is located 12'' W and 28'' S from the center of the Scd host IC 1277 at a projected distance of 14.4 kpc ref:Hicken_2007 . The parameters derived by GALFIT show that IC 1277 has an exponential disk scale length of 14 4 or 6.7 kpc. This means that the SN exploded 2.1 disk scale lengths away from the center of the host at the outer edge of the disk of the galaxy (Figure fig:sn06gz ). The SN is located outside of 75 of the galaxy's light.

A linear fit to the oxygen abundance measurements has a gradient of 0.03 dex kpc ^ 1 . In Figure fig:slope , the dashed line shows the linear fit to the metallicity measurements and the solid line marks the position of the SN. It is apparent that the SN location is metal-poor (12+log(O/H) 8.26 or Z 0.25 Z_ ).

gmw_00009633.name: NGC 2608
gmw_00009633.overview: NGC 2608 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1404.0620]. While standard nuclear spectrum analysis classifies it as star-forming, its activity classification shifts toward transition object and LINER regions as the aperture size increases [arXiv:1404.0620].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2608 as a barred spiral whose aperture-dependent spectra shift from star-forming toward TO/LINER classifications as slit coverage changes.

quotes.quote: NGC 2608 : A barred spiral galaxy classified as star-forming based on the standard long-slit analysis of the nuclear spectrum. The galaxy changes its activity classification towards the TO region for intermediate aperture sizes and falls into the LINER region for the largest apertures in the long-slit approach. A large portion of the slit, oriented along the major axis, passes through an extensive interarm region. This could be responsible for the decrement in the flux of the Balmer lines (see the corresponding H equivalent width plot), producing the upward and towards the right tendency on the BPT diagrams at small to intermediate sized apertures. Then, at larger apertures, the slit falls upon the spiral arm regions where we observe a gradual increase in the Balmer line flux and decrease of the BPT line ratios.

gmw_00009634.name: NGC 2731
gmw_00009634.overview: NGC 2731 is classified as a spiral star-forming galaxy [arXiv:1404.0620]. Long-slit aperture measurements show that its emission line ratios shift toward transition object and LINER regions at larger distances from the nucleus [arXiv:1404.0620].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 2731 as a spiral star-forming galaxy whose long-slit aperture growth changes BPT placement toward TO and LINER regions.

quotes.quote: NGC 2731 : A spiral star-forming galaxy with almost no scatter at the [ O iii ] 5007/H axis but with significant variation towards the right side of the BPT diagrams mostly in the long-slit approach. As discussed previously, this is a case where the higher ionization [ O iii ] 5007 line incrementally decreases at faster rates compared to lower ionization lines as we move farther out from the nucleus of the galaxy. In the long-slit method the galaxy reaches the TO and LINER region at larger apertures.

gmw_00009635.name: NGC 3306
gmw_00009635.overview: NGC 3306 is a barred spiral galaxy that is classified as a starburst [arXiv:1404.0620]. The galaxy contains several young, extra-nuclear star-forming clumps along its spiral arms that produce a hard ionizing continuum [arXiv:1404.0620].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3306 as a barred-spiral starburst whose extra-nuclear clumps drive upward BPT movement, reaching the TO region only at largest long-slit apertures.

quotes.quote: NGC 3306 : This is a barred-spiral galaxy and one of the starbursts in the sample having an upward movement on the BPT diagrams with increasing aperture. Several younger, extra-nuclear star-forming clumps on the spiral arms produce a harder ionizing continuum, which results in the upward behavior observed. Though close to the pure star-forming distinguishing line, it only relocates to the TO region for the largest apertures in the long-slit method.

gmw_00009636.name: NGC 4412
gmw_00009636.overview: NGC 4412 is a spiral galaxy classified as a Seyfert-2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1404.0620]. Its active galactic nucleus features are increasingly obscured by starlight and aperture effects, causing its classification to shift toward transition object and star-forming regions [arXiv:1404.0620].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4412 as a spiral Seyfert-2 galaxy whose AGN features are increasingly obscured by aperture and starlight, changing its diagnostic classification.

quotes.quote: NGC 4412 : This is a typical example of a spiral Seyfert-2 galaxy having its AGN features obscured with increasing aperture and starlight. Regarding the change of its activity classification, the galaxy falls into the TO and star-forming regions in both methods and passes through the LINER region for intermediate apertures in the long-slit method.

Finally, NGC 2608 along with NGC 4412 and NGC 4704 undergo a change in classification towards the TO region in the [ N ii ] 6583/H diagnostic at an aperture covering approximately half of their surface.

gmw_00009637.name: NGC 4491
gmw_00009637.overview: NGC 4491 is classified as an early-type, barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1404.0620]. Its two-dimensional spectrum shows no evidence of extra-nuclear star-forming activity [arXiv:1404.0620].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4491 as an early-type barred spiral whose moderate-quality spectra and starlight subtraction may create an apparent upward BPT trend.

quotes.quote: NGC 4491 : An early-type, barred spiral galaxy producing moderate-quality simulated elliptical spectra that have an upward trend on the BPT diagrams. No emission lines were measured without performing starlight subtraction. The two-dimensional spectrum doesn't show any evidence of extra-nuclear star-forming activity, and because the produced elliptical spectra were of moderate-quality it is possible that the applied starlight subtraction could have overestimated the flux on the absorption Balmer lines. This would give a smaller flux on the Balmer emission lines and would result in the observed upward behavior on the diagrams.

gmw_00009638.name: NGC 4500
gmw_00009638.overview: NGC 4500 is an early-type, barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1404.0620]. It is classified as a transition object or star-forming galaxy depending on the diagnostic diagram used, with its potential active galactic nucleus fraction decreasing at larger apertures [arXiv:1404.0620].

sources.summary: The paper characterizes NGC 4500 as an early-type barred spiral with TO/star-forming classifications whose possible AGN fraction decreases as aperture increases.

quotes.quote: NGC 4500 : Another early-type, barred spiral galaxy maintaining a TO classification in the [ N ii ] 6583/H diagnostic and a star-forming classification in the [ S ii ] 6716, 6731/H , and [ O i ] 6300/H diagrams. The galaxy only passes into the LINERs' region at the largest apertures of the long-slit approach. Being in the TO region and very close to the maximum starburst line at its smallest aperture, the galaxy's possible AGN fraction appears to decrease with increasing aperture as in the case of Seyferts.

gmw_00009639.name: NGC 4704
gmw_00009639.overview: NGC 4704 is a spiral galaxy classified as a Seyfert-2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1404.0620]. The galaxy exhibits a decrease in its active galactic nucleus characteristics with increasing aperture, transitioning into star-forming diagnostics at larger apertures [arXiv:1404.0620].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4704 as a spiral Seyfert-2 whose AGN characteristics diminish with aperture, moving through TO to star-forming diagnostics.

quotes.quote: NGC 4704 : This is a spiral Seyfert-2 galaxy showing a decrement of AGN characteristics with increasing aperture. The galaxy falls into the star-forming region passing through the TOs at intermediate apertures. Without starlight subtraction emission lines emerge approximately at half major semi-axis radius.

gmw_00009640.name: NGC 4868
gmw_00009640.overview: NGC 4868 is classified as an early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:1404.0620]. It exhibits mild star-forming activity and displays a reddish color in SDSS imaging [arXiv:1404.0620].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4868 as an early-type spiral with little BPT scatter, no aperture-driven classification change, and mild star-forming activity.

quotes.quote: NGC 4868 : An early-type spiral galaxy without notable scatter along all BPT axes, presenting no change of activity classification with increasing aperture. The galaxy's reddish color on the SDSS image and inspection of the two-dimensional spectrum suggests mild star-forming activity and agrees with the star-forming classification and placement towards the lower end of the [ O iii ] 5007/H axis.
Some galaxies (NGC 4500, NGC 4868) show lower [ O iii ] 5007/H values with increasing aperture, in line with our expectations.

gmw_00009641.name: NGC 5660
gmw_00009641.overview: NGC 5660 is a late-type spiral galaxy classified as a starburst [arXiv:1404.0620]. The galaxy exhibits extra-nuclear star-forming activity, which drives an upward movement on BPT diagnostic diagrams [arXiv:1404.0620].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5660 as a late-type spiral starburst whose extra-nuclear star formation produces upward BPT movement, with only largest apertures crossing TO.

quotes.quote: NGC 5660 : Another starburst, late-type spiral galaxy presenting an upward movement on the BPTs due to extra-nuclear star-forming activity, as explained previously. Only the largest apertures cross the TO region in the [ N ii ] 6583/H diagnostic for the non-starlight-subtracted elliptical aperture and the standard long-slit methods. In the other two diagnostics the galaxy remains in the star-forming region.

gmw_00009642.name: UGC 6732
gmw_00009642.overview: UGC 6732 is a lenticular galaxy classified as a LINER based on a long-slit analysis of its nuclear spectrum [arXiv:1404.0620]. In certain diagnostic diagrams, the galaxy crosses into the Seyfert region at intermediate apertures [arXiv:1404.0620].

sources.summary: The paper classifies UGC 6732 as a lenticular LINER whose BPT trend may be insignificant, crosses Seyfert regions in two diagnostics, and needs starlight subtraction.

quotes.quote: UGC 6732 : This is a lenticular galaxy classified as LINER based on the long-slit analysis of the nuclear spectrum. It shows a small upward tendency on the BPTs which could be statistically insignificant due to the uncertainties of the emission-line measurements. In the [ S ii ] 6716, 6731/H , and [ O i ] 6300/H diagnostics, the galaxy crosses the Seyfert region at intermediate apertures. No emission lines were measured without starlight subtraction.

gmw_00009643.name: KKH 65
gmw_00009643.overview: KKH 65 is a low-metallicity dwarf spheroidal galaxy candidate with a low velocity dispersion [arXiv:1509.02784]. It is classified as an ultra-diffuse quenched galaxy and is a probable member of the NGC 3414 group [arXiv:1509.02784].

sources.summary: The paper measures KKH 65 as a low-metallicity, low-dispersion dSph candidate, probable NGC 3414 group member, and ultra-diffuse quenched galaxy.

quotes.quote: Here we measure spectroscopic and photometric parameters of three candidates for isolated dSphs, KKH 65 = BTS23, KK 180, and KK 227, outside the Local Group. The galaxies are found to be of low metallicity and low velocity dispersion. They are among the lowest surface brightness objects in the Local Universe. According to the measured radial velocities, metallicities, and structural and photometric parameters, KKH 65 and KK 227 are representatives of the ultra-diffuse quenched galaxies. KKH 65 and KK 227 belong to the outer parts of the groups NGC3414 and NGC5371, respectively.

This value agrees well with the velocity of KKH 65 V_ LG =1301 50 km s ^ 1 found by us. Thus, KKH 65 is a probable member of the group. We here adopted the distance to the NGC 3414 group measured by 2001ApJ...546..681T using SB fluctuations: D_ =25.2 Mpc.

gmw_00009644.name: KK 180
gmw_00009644.overview: KK 180 is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy candidate located within the virial radius of the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1509.02784]. It is characterized by low metallicity, low velocity dispersion, and a very low surface brightness [arXiv:1509.02784].

sources.summary: The paper measures KK 180 as a non-isolated dSph candidate in the Virgo infall region, with low metallicity/dispersion and luminosity comparable to KKH 65.

quotes.quote: Here we measure spectroscopic and photometric parameters of three candidates for isolated dSphs, KKH 65 = BTS23, KK 180, and KK 227, outside the Local Group. The galaxies are found to be of low metallicity and low velocity dispersion. They are among the lowest surface brightness objects in the Local Universe.

We here adopted the distance to KK 180 equal to the distance to the Virgo cluster: D_ =16.4 Mpc 2000ApJ...529..745F . Based on this, KK 180 is located at a projected distance of 1.39Mpc from M87, that is, within the virial radius of the Virgo cluster R_v=1.8 Mpc 1980ApJ...242..861H . Its luminosity, effective radius, and mass are M_V=-14.98 mag, Ks_o=14.14 mag, R_ e,V =1.6 kpc, Mass_ Ks =1.1 10^8 M_ . KK 180 is as luminous as KKH 65, but by 1.5 times more compact.

gmw_00009645.name: KK 227
gmw_00009645.overview: KK 227 is a low-metallicity dwarf spheroidal galaxy candidate with a low velocity dispersion [arXiv:1509.02784]. It is classified as an ultra-diffuse quenched galaxy and is likely a member of the NGC 5371 group [arXiv:1509.02784].

sources.summary: The paper measures KK 227 as a low-metallicity dSph candidate, representative of ultra-diffuse quenched galaxies, and likely member of the NGC 5371 group.

quotes.quote: Here we measure spectroscopic and photometric parameters of three candidates for isolated dSphs, KKH 65 = BTS23, KK 180, and KK 227, outside the Local Group. The galaxies are found to be of low metallicity and low velocity dispersion. They are among the lowest surface brightness objects in the Local Universe. According to the measured radial velocities, metallicities, and structural and photometric parameters, KKH 65 and KK 227 are representatives of the ultra-diffuse quenched galaxies.

This galaxy most likely belongs to the NGC 5371 group of 55 galaxies (MK11) according to its radial velocity and position in the sky (Table tab:allres ). The distance to the Sbc galaxy NGC 5371 was estimated using the Tully-Fisher relation by 2008AJ....135.1488T : D_ =29 Mpc. The radial velocity of NGC 5371 with respect to the center of the LG is V_ LG =2640 km s ^ 1 2001AA...378..370V .

gmw_00009646.name: host galaxy of GRB 051227
gmw_00009646.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 051227 is a faint, very blue, and likely irregular star-forming galaxy [arXiv:0901.4038]. It is situated at a probable redshift of z ≥ 0.8 [arXiv:0901.4038] and exhibits a very small positional offset from its associated gamma-ray burst [arXiv:0901.4038, arXiv:astro-ph/0611128].

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 051227 host as a faint, very blue, likely irregular star-forming galaxy at moderate redshift, with a small projected burst offset.

quotes.quote: Even with these large errors, the host galaxy of GRB 051227 appears very blue. Following Bergeretal07a , we can use the apparent luminosity of the host galaxy as a rough indicator of its redshift. The host galaxy, with an extinction-corrected magnitude R 25.4 , is quite faint and this could suggest a moderately high redshift. For comparison, we took a sample of galaxies with spectroscopic redshifts from the Gemini Deep Deep Survey Abraham04 . We find that 90 of the systems in the magnitude range 24 < R 25.5 have redshift in the interval 0.8 < z < 2.4 . The host galaxy of GRB 051227 is actually at the faint end of the considered brightness range, hence it is probably at even higher redshift. Conservatively, we infer z 0.8 for this system. If real, the UVOT detection of the afterglow in the U filter http://www.swift.ac.uk/filters.shtml ( = 3500 500 ) implies z 2.8 , otherwise the observed flux would be suppressed by the intergalactic hydrogen. In addition we note that, within the uncertainties of our photometry, the colors of the host galaxy are consistent with those of an irregular galaxy at z 0.8 Fukugita95 , in line with our estimates.

Even without an accurate redshift determination, we can set a limit of 0.6 kpc on the projected offset between the GRB explosion site and the host galaxy center (Sect. 3.1). The host galaxy of GRB 061006 seems to be a star-forming galaxy too (Sect. 4.1), but less luminous, and probably smaller, than the host galaxy of GRB 071227. In our VLT images we cannot spatially resolve the galaxy but, thanks to the image subtraction technique, we are able to constrain the afterglow offset with respect to its center, which is smaller than 3.5 kpc (in projection). No excess column density has been found in the X-ray spectrum (Sect. 4.1), but the limits are not very constraining due to the large foreground component ( 1.3 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 ). The small offsets and the presence of gas absorption point towards a ``primordial'' binary progenitor also for GRB 061006 and GRB 051227.

sources.summary: The paper reports a faint host coincident with the fading afterglow of GRB 051227, with a tiny positional offset and very low chance-coincidence probability.

quotes.quote: Additional observations with GMOS were obtained 38.6 and 62.4 hr after the burst for total exposures of 1500 and 1800 s, respectively, and confirmed that the object has faded between the first and second observations. The brightness of the object remains constant between the second and third observation, indicating the presence of the host galaxy. From the final observation we measure for the host r_ AB =25.78 0.15 mag (Figure fig:hosts ). The positional offset between the afterglow and host is only 0.05 0.02'' . The probability of chance coincidence at such a radius and brightness level is about 2 10^ 4 .

gmw_00009647.name: host galaxy of GRB 061006
gmw_00009647.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 061006 is an inclined, nearly edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1910.14505] with an exponential disk profile [arXiv:0909.1804] and a bulge component [arXiv:0909.1804, arXiv:1910.14505]. Located at a redshift of approximately 0.437 [arXiv:0901.4038, arXiv:1910.14505], this sub-luminous galaxy [arXiv:0901.4038] exhibits ongoing star formation [arXiv:0901.4038] and moderate metallicity [arXiv:0901.4038].

sources.summary: The GRB 061006 host is a sub-luminous blue spiral-like galaxy at z=0.436, with ongoing star formation, moderate metallicity, and a small afterglow offset.

quotes.quote: From the detection of several emission lines ( [ O ii ] , H , [ O iii ] ), we derive a redshift z = 0.436 0.002 (Fig. fig:spe ). This is consistent with the value reported by Berger07,Berger08 . The resulting rest-frame B band absolute magnitude of the host is thus M_B = -17.7 , or L_B 0.05L^* (assuming M^*_B = -21 ). We secured further multiband photometry of the host galaxy in the period 2007 Feb. -- 2007 Mar., obtaining B , V , R , I and J detections, together with a deep K band upper limit. All the results of our photometry are reported in Table tab_log2 . The extinction-corrected colors are B-V = 1.04 0.14 , V-R = 0.40 0.14 and R-I = 0.78 0.14 , which are much bluer than those of elliptical galaxies and consistent with those of spirals (Sbc or Scd type) at z 0.5 (Fukugita et al. 1995). This is in agreement with our spectroscopic redshift estimate. Considering the measured angular offset, at z = 0.436 the afterglow is located within 3.5 kpc (in projection) from the center of the host galaxy.

As indicated from several emission lines, star formation is still ongoing in this galaxy. For the [ O ii ] emission line, we measure a luminosity of 2.18 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 (corrected for slit losses). This converts to an unobscured star formation rate (SFR) of 0.3 M_ yr ^ 1 Kennicutt98 , which corresponds to a specific SFR of about 6.1 M_ yr ^ 1 L_*^ 1 . This is comparable to the typical specific SFRs measured in long GRB host galaxies Christensen04 . The detection of oxygen and hydrogen emission lines in our spectrum allows us to determine the metallicity of the host galaxy. This can be done by adopting the commonly used abundance diagnostic R_ 23 = (F_ [ O ii ] 3727 + F_ [ O iii ] 4959,5007 ) / F_ H . However, as can be seen in Fig. fig:spe , the H and [ O iii ] 4959 emission lines have very low S/N ( 2 ), which makes the R_ 23 parameter of little practical use. To overcome this problem, we used the empirical study performed by Nagao06 on a large sample of galaxies, which shows that the ratio F_ [ O iii ] 5007 / F_ [ O ii ] 3727 can be used as a useful indicator of metallicity. For the host galaxy of GRB 061006 we obtain F_ [ O iii ] 5007 / F_ [ O ii ] 3727 = 0.52 0.23 which corresponds, according to the study of these authors, to an oxygen abundance range 8.2 12 + ( O/H ) 8.9 , i.e. a metallicity in the range (0.4 - 1.7) Z_ (using as solar abundance 12 + ( O/H ) = 8.69 from Allende01 ). This is higher that what commonly found for the host galaxies of long GRBs Savaglio08 .

sources.summary: The host of GRB 061006 is modeled as an exponential disk with a significant older-stellar-population bulge coincident with the burst and a 1.3 kpc physical offset.

quotes.quote: From the galfit analysis we find that three hosts (GRBs 050709, 051221a, and 061006) are best modeled with n 1 , corresponding to an exponential disk profile, while two hosts (GRBs 050509b and 050724) are best modeled with n 3 and 5.6 , respectively, typical of elliptical galaxies. We note that GRB 050724 possibly exhibits weak spiral structure, which may explain the resulting value of n 3 (see Figure  fig:050724 and mcd+07 ), but this putative spiral structure is clearly sub-dominant relative to the elliptical structure.

Finally, we find that the hosts of GRBs 051210, 060121, and 061006 exhibit significant bulges, clearly seen in their radial surface brightness profiles (Figure  fig:sbfit_all ). For the host of GRB 061006, which was observed in two filters, the bulge component is more significant in the F814W filter than in the F555W filter, as expected for an older stellar population; the burst appears to coincide with this bulge component (Figure  fig:061006 ).

Our HST sample includes three short GRBs with strong extended emission (050709, 050724, and 061006), and one GRB (060121) with possible extended emission ( 4.5 significance; dls+06 ). The physical offsets of these bursts are about 3.7, 2.7, 1.3, and 1 kpc, respectively, leading to a mean offset of about 2.2 kpc. The physical offsets of the bursts without extended emission, but with precise afterglow positions (051221, 060313, and 061201) are 2.0, 2.3, and 14.2 or 32.5 kpc, respectively. The two events with no extended emission and with XRT positions (050509b and 051210) have offsets of about 54 12 and 28 23 kpc, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper describes the GRB 061006 host as a faint fuzzy galaxy at z=0.4377 with weak emission lines, very low SFR, inclined nearly edge-on spiral morphology, and afterglow near its bulge.

quotes.quote: In Gemini-S/GMOS images the host galaxy appears as a faint ( R_C 24.0 mag) and fuzzy object see figure 1 in BergerApJ664 . Its shows weak emission lines from the doublet, H , and at a common redshift of z=0.4377 0.0002 . The line flux corresponds to a SFR of 0.02 \ Berger2009ApJ .
The host is an inclined, nearly edge-on spiral galaxy. The optical afterglow was located close to the galactic bulge see figure 9 in Fong2010 .

gmw_00009648.name: host galaxy of GRB 071227
gmw_00009648.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 071227 is an edge-on disk galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.381 [arXiv:0901.4038]. It is characterized by ongoing star formation and has a metallicity ranging from 0.4 to 1.0 solar metallicity [arXiv:0901.4038].

sources.summary: The GRB 071227 host is an edge-on disk galaxy at z=0.381 with ongoing star formation, subsolar-to-solar metallicity, and an afterglow 15 kpc from its center.

quotes.quote: From the detection of several emission ( [ O ii ] , [ O iii ] and weak H ) and absorption lines we derived a redshift z = 0.381 0.001 (Fig. fig:spe2 ). This redshift was later confirmed by Berger et al. (2007). At the measured redshift, the isotropic-equivalent energy of GRB 071227 is E_ ,iso = 5.8 10^ 50 erg (20--1300 keV), a value typical of short GRBs. According to the offset measured from our astrometry, at this redshift the afterglow is located at 15.0 2.2 kpc from the center of the host galaxy. The host galaxy has a rest-frame B band absolute magnitude M_B = -19.3 , i.e. L_B 0.2L^* (assuming M^*_B = -21 ).

The presence of emission lines in the optical spectrum of the host galaxy of GRB 071227 indicates that star formation is ongoing in this system. We measure for the [ O ii ] emission line a luminosity of 3.9 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 (corrected for slit losses). This translates into an unobscured SFR of 0.6 M_ yr ^ 1 (Kennicutt 1998), which corresponds to a specific SFR of about 2.7 M_ yr ^ 1 L_*^ 1 . As we did for the host of GRB 061006 (Sect. 4.1), we can estimate the metallicity using the F_ [ O iii ] 5007 / F_ [ O ii ] 3727 ratio as indicator Nagao06 . We obtain a value of 0.58 0.13 which corresponds to an oxygen abundance of 8.2 12 + ( O/H ) 8.8 , i.e. a metallicity in the range (0.4 - 1.0) Z_ , using the solar abundance from Allende01 .

gmw_00009649.name: Draco
gmw_00009649.overview: Draco is a dwarf spheroidal galaxy [arXiv:1804.05052]. Its underlying dark matter halo has been modeled using both Navarro-Frenk-White and Burkert density profiles to analyze its velocity-dispersion profile [arXiv:1804.05052].

sources.summary: The paper fits Draco with NFW and Burkert dark-matter halo models, compares its velocity-dispersion profile, and evaluates velocity-dependent annihilation boosts.

quotes.quote: In figure fig:corner_draco_sculpt we show the outcome of our Bayesian fit on Sculptor and Draco datasets, describing the underlying DM halo with the NFW (red triangle plot) or with the Burkert (blue triangle plot) profile. We report the one-dimensional marginalized posterior p.d.f. for each of the estimated parameters, highlighting the 16- th , 50- th , 84- th percentiles, and their joint probability distribution with the 68 and 95 highest probability density (h.p.d.) contours. While a strong correlation between the DM halo parameters emerges in all our fits, we can observe for the case of Draco and Carina that the NFW scenario is sensitive to the physically motivated upper-limit assigned on r_ s . As a general trend, we find in NFW fits a non-negligible correlation among DM halo parameters and _ 0, , while for the fits with Burkert profile the correlation of the halo parameters with inner trend of the stellar anisotropy gets essentially milder. Heliocentric distance and half-light angle show overall mild correlations in the bi-dimensional joint distributions with the rest of the fitted parameters. Finally, to provide a concrete picture of the goodness of our fits, we show in figure fig:slos_draco_sculpt the 68 and 95 h.p.d. interval for the predicted l.o.s. velocity dispersion profile in Draco and Sculptor, together with their binned data points.
