It turned out that J1101+7225 shows a complex source structure, which makes it physically interesting but, on the other hand, also less suitable as a calibrator. Thus the nuclear region of J1101+7225 provides the rare possibility of observing the total range of jet kinematics including apparent superluminal separation velocities even far out of the central parsec-region.

gmw_00009689.name: DF03
gmw_00009689.overview: DF03 is a low surface brightness dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1703.02042]. It is identified as a field ultra-diffuse galaxy located approximately 45 megaparsecs behind the Coma Cluster [arXiv:1703.02042].

sources.summary: The paper places DF03 behind the Coma Cluster from its recessional velocity, recomputes its structural values at that distance, and identifies it as a field UDG with a spectroscopic redshift.

quotes.quote: 3 Because DF03 is located behind the Coma Cluster, the values for the effective radius and absolute magnitude have been recomputed using the new distance. Another of our UDGs (DF03) lies well outside the caustic curves and we conclude that this system is 45 Mpc behind the cluster. Along with DGSAT I, DF03 is one of the few field UDGs with a spectroscopic redshift.

gmw_00009690.name: BCG
gmw_00009690.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) of Abell 980 is a radio galaxy that possesses a large 80 kpc stellar halo [arXiv:2207.05166, arXiv:2206.13550]. It has experienced multiple episodes of jet activity, producing two diffuse, ultra-steep spectrum relic lobes alongside a younger, smaller double radio source with an active central core [arXiv:2207.05166, arXiv:2206.13550].

sources.summary: The paper argues that Abell 980's BCG produced two generations of double radio sources, drifted toward the cluster center, and now coincides with the younger active radio structure.

quotes.quote: It is argued that the new morphological and spectral information gleaned from the recently published LoFAR Two meter Sky Survey data release 2 (LoTSS-2 at 144 MHz) observations of the cluster Abell 980 (A980), in combination with its existing GMRT and VLA observations at higher frequencies, provide the much-needed evidence to strengthen the proposal that the cluster's radio emission comes mainly from two double radio sources, both produced by the brightest cluster galaxy (BCG) in two major episodes of jet activity. The two radio lobes left from the previous activity have become diffuse and developed an ultra-steep radio spectrum while rising buoyantly through the confining hot intra-cluster medium (ICM) and, concomitantly, the host galaxy has drifted to the cluster centre and entered a new active phase manifested by a coinciding younger double radio source. The new observational results and arguments presented here bolster the case that the old and young double radio sources in A980 conjointly represent a `double-double' radio galaxy whose two lobe-pairs have lost colinearity due to the (lateral) drift of their parent galaxy, making this system by far the most plausible case of a `Detached-Double-Double Radio Galaxy' (dDDRG).

The BCG of the cluster A980 (RXC J1022.5+5006, z = 0.1582; Ebeling_MNRAS_1996 ) is known to possess a stellar halo of size 80 kpc, roughly co-spatial with the X-ray emission peak (see Fig. fig:LoTSS_GMRT_optical_xray ). From the radio observations with the GMRT (150/325 MHz) and VLA (1.5 GHz), the radio emission has been found to arise mainly from 3 components (A, B C, Fig. fig:LoTSS_GMRT_optical_xray a), of which A and B are diffuse, with ultra-steep radio spectra (USS, <-2 ) and extending to the ICM outskirts, whereas the source C coinciding with the BCG is resolved into a smaller double radio source of size 55 kpc ( Salunkhe_2022A&A ; hereafter, S22). Here we present new observation-based evidence and arguments, particularly those emerging from the recently released high-sensitivity image with a 6 arcsecond resolution at 144 MHz (LoTSS/DR2, Shimwell_2022A&A ), in order to reinforce the hypothesis associating all the 3 radio components (A, B, C) with the intermittent nuclear activity occurring in the BCG (S22). Discarding this unlikely host, the plausibility favours the scenario (sec. intro ) that the relic lobes A and B were both created by the BCG during its previous major episode of jet activity when it was near the central location between A and B. Since then, the galaxy has drifted towards the cluster centre (BCG in Fig. fig:LoTSS_GMRT_optical_xray a) and entered a new phase of intense jet activity which is identified with the creation of the smaller double radio source with an active central core (L1, L2 in the inset of Fig. fig:LoTSS_GMRT_optical_xray a).

sources.summary: The paper describes Abell 980's BCG as the likely source of multiple radio episodes: aged USS lobes, a current compact-core double source, and a probable mini-halo within an extended stellar halo.

quotes.quote: The rich diversity of this cluster radio emission is found to include (i) two large diffuse sources of the ultra-steep spectrum (USS) located near the opposite extremities of the ICM, (ii) a bright double radio source ( 55 kpc) ) coincident with the BCG which lies close to the centre of the cluster's cool core, and (iii) a probable radio mini-halo of average size 110 kpc associated with the BCG which itself possesses a huge ellipsoidal stellar halo of size 80 kpc. All four radio sources may have been created by the BCG at different stages and in different circumstances. The two large diffuse USS sources may be highly aged relics of the radio lobes produced by the BCG during its previous active phase 260 Myr ago and eventually propelled buoyantly towards the outskirts of the ICM, where they are found to create the two X-ray discontinuities in the ICM. The bright small double source with a central radio core is a probable outcome of a recent episode of activity in the BCG.

Considering the absence of likely host galaxies for A and B and given that intermittent nuclear activity is a common feature of radio galaxies (e.g. 2017MNRAS_Godfrey, 2018MNRAS_turner ), it seems quite plausible that A and B are aged remnants of a powerful double radio source created by the BCG (G1 in Fig. fig:radio_high_opt a) in its previous active phase, and both have risen to the ICM outskirts due to buoyancy pressure, while remaining confined due to the thermal pressure of the hot ICM. Concurrently, the galaxy has drifted towards the cluster centre where it has entered a new active phase resulting in the small double source with an associated compact radio core (Fig. fig:radio_high_opt b fig:radio_high_opt ).

gmw_00009691.name: NGC 6041
gmw_00009691.overview: NGC 6041 is the brightest cluster galaxy of the Abell 2151 cluster [arXiv:2003.11104]. It exhibits modest hydrogen-alpha emission and contains a detectable level of molecular gas [arXiv:2003.11104].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 6041 as A2151’s BCG with modest H emission and detectable molecular gas below the cooling-threshold luminosity.

quotes.quote: McDonald_2011 recently etected H emission in it's BCG, NGC 6041, with a luminosity of L_ H 3 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 . Therefore, NGC 6041 has a detectable level, albeit, a modest level of molecular gas. However, emission at this level lies well below the luminosity where the cooling time threshold seen in becomes prominent at, L_ H 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 . An H detection of this magnitude is expected as the accumulation of gas from stellar winds, supernovae, and external accretion that may be unrelated to uplift and thermally unstable cooling.

gmw_00009692.name: NGC0873
gmw_00009692.overview: NGC0873 is a blue galaxy dominated by star formation [arXiv:2009.08383]. Its central region is well detected in carbon monoxide, and its star-forming properties are characterized by high hydrogen-equivalent-width values across almost the entire galaxy [arXiv:2009.08383].

sources.summary: NGC0873 is shown as a CO-detected, blue, centrally star-forming EDGE-CALIFA example with H-equivalent-width values above the star-forming threshold across most of the galaxy.

quotes.quote: The centres of all the galaxies in this example are well detected in CO, but the continuum and \ equivalent width ( ) maps show that the objects are in three different phases of their evolution. On the left middle panel, the continuum map indicates that the galaxy (NGC0873) is entirely blue, therefore dominated by star formation. Along with colour, \ is also a faithful proxy for the star formation properties of the galaxies e.g., sanchez2020 . In particular, W_ H >6 is found in galaxy areas dominated by HII regions, while where W_ H <6 the galaxy is quenched, or dominated by other effects than recent star formation. Indeed, the bottom left panel of Fig  F:mapspectra indicates that W_ H >6 almost everywhere and closely follows the continuum map information.

gmw_00009693.name: NGC0170
gmw_00009693.overview: NGC0170 is a galaxy that is globally dominated by star formation [arXiv:2009.08383]. Although star formation is fully quenched in its central region, stars continue to form in the galaxy's outskirts [arXiv:2009.08383].

sources.summary: NGC0170 is presented as CO-detected with a quenched central region but star-forming outskirts, leaving the full galaxy globally dominated by star formation.

quotes.quote: The centres of all the galaxies in this example are well detected in CO, but the continuum and \ equivalent width ( ) maps show that the objects are in three different phases of their evolution.

In NGC0170 (middle column), instead, the star formation in the centre is fully quenched, as indicated by the median value of \ within the APEX beam aperture, W_ H , b <6 (see Section  S:props for further details), and by the yellow colour of the continuum map in the central region. Nevertheless, on the outskirts, stars are forming, and this galaxy appears globally dominated by star formation (as suggested by the median \ across the full galaxy W_ H , g >6 ).

gmw_00009694.name: KDG 61
gmw_00009694.overview: KDG 61 is a low-metallicity stellar system [arXiv:0706.4467]. Depending on the stellar population models used, its radial surface brightness fluctuation and integrated color changes can be interpreted as a radial gradient in the age of its dominant stellar component [arXiv:0706.4467].

sources.summary: The paper interprets KDG 61 as a low-metallicity stellar system whose radial SBF-color behavior gives model-dependent age or metallicity-gradient implications.

quotes.quote: KDG 61 -- All models predict a Z < 0.004 stellar system. Within the R05 and L02 SSP scenarios, the radial change of SBF and integrated color could be interpreted by a radial change in the age of the dominant stellar component. The opposite conclusion would be drawn by using the BVA01 and MA06 models. Moreover, it must be noted the criss-crossing of data at different radii for this galaxy.

gmw_00009695.name: KDG 64
gmw_00009695.overview: KDG 64 is a member of the M 81 group of galaxies [arXiv:0706.4467]. It is modeled as a low-metallicity system with an average chemical content of Z 0.001 and an estimated age of over 5 Gyr [arXiv:0706.4467].

sources.summary: The paper places KDG 64 in a very low-metallicity model regime and notes that model discrepancies limit conclusions about its SBF-color gradient.

quotes.quote: KDG 64 -- A 0.0004 Z 0.001 , t > 5 Gyr system is predicted. It is worth mentioning that for this galaxy, and for the two previous -- all members of the M 81 group -- dacosta07 quotes an average chemical content of Z 0.001 comparing the mean RGB color to the colors of Galactic Globular Clusters. The location of these galaxies in the low metallicity regime, where significant discrepancies between models exist, does not allow us to obtain any substantial conclusion on the possible origin of SBF versus color gradients.

gmw_00009696.name: NVSS J002431 303330
gmw_00009696.overview: NVSS J002431 303330 is a double radio source located at a redshift of z=2.415 [arXiv:1510.06631]. The galaxy features a bright emission-line region spanning approximately 8 kiloparsecs, which exhibits broad line widths and perturbed velocities [arXiv:1510.06631].

sources.summary: The paper describes NVSS J002431 303330 as a z=2.415 double radio source with broad optical lines, a bright emission-line region, perturbed velocities, and fainter southwest gas.

quotes.quote: NVSS J002431 303330 NVSS J002431 303330 is a double radio source, dominated by a bright component associated with the optical counterpart, and a weaker component at 16 \ toward southwest (Fig. fig:radioMorphologies ). We find this source at a redshift of z=2.415 0.001 , which is in good agreement with the estimate of Johnston et al. (in prep.) from the rest-frame UV lines, with z_ uv =2.416 0.001 . In the H band, we detect the [ OIII ] 4959,5007 doublet and H . In the K band, we find H and [ NII ] 6548,6583, which are strongly blended owing to their large intrinsic widths. The H line has a broad component with FWHM = 3250 km s ^ 1 .

As shown in Fig. fig:maps1 , NVSS J002431 303330 has a strong continuum associated with a bright emission-line region with [ OIII ] 5007 surface brightness of (5-25) 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 . Line widths in this region are very broad, FWHM 1200 km s ^ 1 , and the velocity field is perturbed with two small, unresolved regions that show abrupt velocity jumps relative to their surroundings with relative redshifts of about 250 km s ^ 1 in each region. This area extends over 1.0 1.0 \ around the peak of the continuum and [ OIII ] 5007 line emission, corresponding to 8 kpc at z=2.415 .

gmw_00009697.name: NVSS J004000 303333
gmw_00009697.overview: NVSS J004000 303333 is a double radio source located at a redshift of z = 3.399 [arXiv:1510.06631]. The galaxy features extended line emission, faint continuum knots, and a velocity gradient of approximately 300 km/s across its emission-line region [arXiv:1510.06631].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NVSS J004000 303333 as a z=3.399 double radio source with extended line emission, faint continuum knots, velocity gradients, and a nearby blueshifted emitter.

quotes.quote: NVSS J004000 303333 NVSS J004000 303333 is a double radio source with a size of 17 \ (Fig. fig:radioMorphologies ). With SINFONI we find the [ OIII ] 4959,5007 doublet and H in the K band at wavelengths that correspond to z = 3.399 0.001 . The H and [ NII ] 6548,6583 lines fall outside the atmospheric windows.

Line emission extends over 2.5 1.5 \ with surface brightnesses between _ [ Oiii ] 5 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 s and 3 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 (Fig. fig:maps1 ). Faint continuum emission is found associated with a knot to the very east of the emission-line region, and about 1.5 \ southwest from the center, outside the bright line emission. The K-band image of Bryant2009a shows a very similar continuum morphology, likewise at low signal-to-noise ratio. The local velocities of [ OIII ] 5007 fall monotonically from the southwest to the northeast with a total gradient of about 300 km s ^ 1 . The knot in the far east shows an abrupt velocity increase of 300 km s ^ 1 relative to the nearby blueshifted gas.

gmw_00009698.name: NVSS J012932 385433
gmw_00009698.overview: NVSS J012932 385433 is a compact radio source located at a redshift of z = 2.185 [arXiv:1510.06631]. It features an extended emission-line region with a regular velocity gradient and coincident continuum emission [arXiv:1510.06631].

sources.summary: The paper presents NVSS J012932 385433 as a compact z=2.185 radio source with broad blended lines, extended central emission, continuum, and a regular velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: NVSS J012932 385433 The SINFONI maps of NVSS J012932 385433 are shown in Fig. fig:maps1 . We find the optical emission lines at redshift z = 2.185 0.001 . The radio source is compact with a deconvolved size of 0.7 , and is associated with the optical counterpart. [ OIII ] 4959,5007 is bright in the H band. H is detected at 5.6 .

The emission-line region is extended over 1.6 1.2 . It is brighter in the center with _ H (1.0 - 1.7) 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 and fades toward the periphery. We detect continuum emission coincident with the emitting gas. The [ OIII ] 4959,5007 lines show a clear, relatively regular velocity gradient of v 350 km s ^ 1 along a northeast-southwest axis.

gmw_00009699.name: NVSS J030431 315308
gmw_00009699.overview: NVSS J030431 315308 is a compact radio galaxy [arXiv:1510.06631]. It features marginally resolved line emission, including detected oxygen, hydrogen, and nitrogen lines, alongside faint continuum emission [arXiv:1510.06631].

sources.summary: The paper describes NVSS J030431 315308 as a compact radio galaxy with detected oxygen, hydrogen, and nitrogen lines, marginally resolved line emission, faint continuum, and broad western lines.

quotes.quote: NVSS J030431 315308 NVSS J030431 315308 is a single, relatively compact source at 9 GHz and 5.5 GHz, with a deconvolved size of 1.8 \ in our highest resolution data at 9 GHz (Fig. fig:radioMorphologies ). The [ OIII ] 4959,5007 doublet is clearly detected in the H band with SINFONI and is well fitted with single Gaussians (Fig fig:spec1 ). The same holds for the H and [ NII ] 6583 lines. The [ SII ] doublet is not detected.

The line emission is marginally spatially resolved with a size of 1.5 1.5 , and a PSF with FWHM=1.2 1.0 , the largest in this program (Tab. tab:obslog ). Faint continuum emission is also detected, at a slightly different position ( 0.5 \ to the west) from the peak in [ OIII ] 5007 surface brightness, but at the same position as the peak of H surface brightness. The velocity maps show two small redshifted (by 50 100 km s ^ 1 ) regions north and south of the continuum, and uniform velocities in the rest of the source.

gmw_00009700.name: NVSS J144932 385657
gmw_00009700.overview: NVSS J144932 385657 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.149 [arXiv:1510.06631]. It features a very extended emission-line region spanning nearly 30 kiloparsecs along a northeast-southwest axis, which aligns with its large radio lobes [arXiv:1510.06631].

sources.summary: The paper finds NVSS J144932 385657 has large radio lobes and very extended, jet-aligned line emission with two regions, large velocity offsets, and complex southwestern kinematics.

quotes.quote: NVSS J144932 385657 NVSS J144932 385657 has one of the largest radio sources in our sample; the lobes are offset by 7.5 \ relative to each other. We find the [ OIII ] line at z = 2.149 0.001 . In the H band, we detect the [ OIII ] 4959,5007 doublet and H (Fig. fig:spec2 ). In the K band, H and [ NII ] 6583 are narrow enough not to be blended. FWHM = 350 km s ^ 1 for H , and the width of [ NII ] 6583 is dominated by the spectral resolution.

NVSS J144932 385657 has a very extended emission-line region of nearly 4 \ ( 30 kpc at z 2 ) along a northeast-southwest axis (Fig. fig:maps1 ). We identify two parts: a large, elongated region, which coincides with the continuum and extends over another 2 \ toward the southwest, and a fainter, smaller region in the northwest. Surface brightnesses of [ OIII ] 5007 are between 1 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 and 10 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 .

gmw_00009701.name: NVSS J201943 364542
gmw_00009701.overview: NVSS J201943 364542 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.120 [arXiv:1510.06631]. It features a very extended radio source spanning 120 kpc, a compact emission-line region with bright continuum emission, and a broad H-alpha emission line [arXiv:1510.06631].

sources.summary: The paper reports NVSS J201943 364542 as a very extended z=2.120 radio source with compact line emission, bright continuum, broad H, velocity scatter, and a nearby related emitter.

quotes.quote: NVSS J201943 364542 The radio source of NVSS J201943 364542 is very extended, with LAS = 14.7 , corresponding to 120 kpc at z = 2.1 (Fig. fig:radioMorphologies ). In the H band with SINFONI, we detect the [ OIII ] 4959,5007 doublet at a redshift of z = 2.120 0.001 , but not H . In the K band, in addition to the narrow components of H and [ NII ] 6583, we find a broad H emission line (FWHM 8000 km s ^ 1 ) from a compact region aligned with the nucleus. We come back to this line in s:additionalLineEmittersTracersOfEnvironmentDynamicalProbes . Integrated line properties are listed in Tab. tab:spec1 .

NVSS J201943 364542 has a compact emission-line region of 1.0 \ with an [ OIII ] 5007 surface brightness _ [ Oiii ] = (0.5 - 1.0) 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 (Fig. fig:maps2 ). It also has relatively bright continuum emission associated with the line emission. The velocity field of the narrow-line component shows a scatter of 200 km s ^ 1 , with the highest velocities reached in the far east and west.

gmw_00009702.name: NVSS J204601 335656
gmw_00009702.overview: NVSS J204601 335656 is a compact radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.499 [arXiv:1510.06631]. It features a faint continuum source associated with marginally resolved emission lines that exhibit a small velocity offset [arXiv:1510.06631].

sources.summary: The paper describes NVSS J204601 335656 as a compact z=2.499 radio galaxy with marginally resolved line emission, faint continuum, a small velocity offset, and broad lines.

quotes.quote: NVSS J204601 335656 NVSS J204601 335656 has a compact radio source with a deconvolved size of 1.8 \ in our data, consistent with the 1.6 \ previously found by Broderick2007 . With SINFONI in the H band, we detect the [ OIII ] 4959,5007 doublet and H at z = 2.499 0.001 . In the K band, H and the [ NII ] 6548,6583 doublet are blended but well fitted with a single Gaussian component for each line (Fig. fig:spec2 ). The [ SII ] 6716,6731 doublet is not detected.

The line emission is marginally spatially resolved with a FWHM size of 1 \ compared to a 0.7 0.6 \ PSF. The emission-line region is associated with a faint continuum source; the region is roughly circular and extends over 1.0 , with _ [ OIII ] = (0.3 - 1.7) 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 . NVSS J204601 335656 has a small velocity offset of 150 km s ^ 1 which rises from northeast to southwest.

gmw_00009703.name: NVSS J234235 384526
gmw_00009703.overview: NVSS J234235 384526 is an extended radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 3.515 [arXiv:1510.06631]. The galaxy features two radio lobes [arXiv:1510.06631] and exhibits extended line emission aligned with its radio axis [arXiv:1510.06631].

sources.summary: The paper presents NVSS J234235 384526 as a z=3.515 extended radio galaxy with two lobes, two-component oxygen lines, extended jet-aligned emission, and central broadening.

quotes.quote: NVSS J234235 384526 NVSS J234235 384526 is an extended radio source with two lobes at a relative distance of 9.8 \ Fig. fig:radioMorphologies , see also Broderick2007 . It is at redshift z = 3.515 0.001 , where the [ OIII ] 4959,5007 doublet and the H emission lines fall into the K band. Fitting the [ OIII ] 4959,5007 line profiles adequately requires two components per line (Fig. fig:spec1 ), a narrow component with FWHM = 360 km s ^ 1 (which we consider to be at the systemic redshift), and a broader blue wing with FWHM = 1300 km s ^ 1 , blueshifted by 700 km s ^ 1 . In the H band, we detect the [ OII ] 3727 emission lines.

Line emission extends over 2.0 1.0 \ along an axis going from northeast to southwest, with surface brightness _ [ Oiii ] = (5 - 30) 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 arcsec ^ 2 (Fig. fig:maps2 ). The velocity map shows a gradient of about 400 km s ^ 1 rising from the northeast to the southwest, and well aligned with the axis of the radio source. Line widths are larger in the center of the galaxy (FWHM 1000-1200 km s ^ 1 ) than in the periphery (FWHM 200-400 km s ^ 1 ).

gmw_00009704.name: NVSS J094949 211508
gmw_00009704.overview: NVSS J094949 211508 is a high-redshift radio galaxy at a redshift of z = 2.427 [arXiv:1510.06631]. It features compact radio emission and an extended emission-line region that spans approximately 14 kiloparsecs with a 300 km/s velocity gradient [arXiv:1510.06631].

sources.summary: The paper describes NVSS J094949 211508 as CEN 072, a z=2.427 source with compact radio emission, extended line emission, detected continuum, and a 300 km/s velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: NVSS J094949 211508 (CEN 072) This source has a redshift of z = 2.427 0.001 , in agreement with the previous UV redshift measured by Brookes2008CENSORS . Because there was a coordinate mismatch, we do not have new radio measurements at 5.5 and 9.0 GHz for this source. Best2003CENSORS found LAS < 0.7 , which is small enough to infer that the extended emission-line region is larger than the radio emission. The integrated spectrum shown in Fig. fig:spec3 shows that [ OIII ] 4959,5007 and H are well detected in the H band, and H and [ NII ] 6548,6583 in the K band, The lines are not very strongly blended.

Line emission extends over 1.7 1.2 \ (14 kpc 10 kpc) along a northwest-southeast axis (Fig. fig:maps3 ). It has a velocity gradient of 300 km s ^ 1 . The stellar continuum is associated with the northwestern part of the emission-line region, where the line widths also reach their maximum (FWHM 800 km s ^ 1 ), compared to FWHM 300-500 km s ^ 1 in the southeast.

gmw_00009705.name: NVSS J095226 200105
gmw_00009705.overview: NVSS J095226 200105 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.422 [arXiv:1510.06631]. It features two radio lobes separated by 20 kiloparsecs, a central stellar continuum, and a velocity gradient roughly aligned with its radio axis [arXiv:1510.06631].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NVSS J095226 200105 as CEN 129, a z=2.422 source with resolved radio lobes, spherical extended emission, central continuum, and a velocity gradient.

quotes.quote: NVSS J095226 200105 (CEN 129) This source is at redshift z = 2.422 0.001 , in agreement with the previous estimate of z = 2.421 based on rest-frame UV lines Brookes2008CENSORS . The radio morphology is resolved in our ATCA observation at 5.5 and 9.0 GHz (see Fig. fig:radioMorphologies ), but was compact in the previous 1.4 GHz data of Best2003CENSORS . We find two radio lobes along an east-west axis along a position angle of 95 ^ , separated by 2.5 (20 kpc at z = 2.4). The integrated spectrum of CEN 129 shows H and the [ OIII ] 4959,5007 doublet in the H band, and H and the [ NII ] 6548,6883 lines in the K band (Fig. fig:spec3 and Tab. tab:spec2 ).

The emission-line morphology of CEN 129 is fairly spherical, with a small extension toward the northwest, and a size of 2.3 1.7 \ (19 kpc 14 kpc). The major axis is along the northwest-southeast direction. The stellar continuum is detected at the center of the line emission. The velocity field has a gradient of 200 km s ^ 1 , and is roughly aligned with the radio axis, along an east-west axis.

gmw_00009706.name: NVSS J094949 213432
gmw_00009706.overview: NVSS J094949 213432 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.355 [arXiv:1510.06631]. It features a highly extended radio structure [arXiv:1510.06631] and a large line-emission region spanning approximately 25 kiloparsecs with disturbed velocities [arXiv:1510.06631].

sources.summary: The paper presents NVSS J094949 213432 as CEN 134, a z=2.355 source with a very extended radio structure and large line-emission region with disturbed velocities.

quotes.quote: NVSS J094949 213432 (CEN 134) The integrated spectrum of CEN 134 shows H and the [ OIII ] 4959,5007 doublet in the H band, and H and [ NII ] 6583 in the K band. We find a redshift of z = 2.355 0.001 for CEN 134, in good agreement with the value estimated from rest-frame UV lines Brookes2008CENSORS . At 1.4 GHz, the radio source is very extended, with LAS=22.4 \ along a position angle of 125 ^ Best2003CENSORS . At 5.5 GHz we measure LAS = 21.9 \ and PA = 131 ^ , but we do not detect the second lobe in the 9.0 GHz observations.

Line emission in CEN 134 extends over 3.1 1.8 \ (25 kpc 15 kpc), with the major axis going from the northwest to the southeast (Fig. fig:maps3 ). Continuum emission is detected in the southern part of the emission-line region. The velocity field shows two blueshifted regions, one south of the continuum, one at the very north of the emission-line region, with velocities of about 120 km s ^ 1 relative to the velocities near the center.

gmw_00009707.name: MaNGA 1-279073
gmw_00009707.overview: MaNGA 1-279073 is a galaxy that exhibits strong indications of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2208.03383]. It displays high nuclear electron densities exceeding 1000 per cubic centimeter, along with elevated equivalent widths at its nucleus and field-of-view edges [arXiv:2208.03383].

sources.summary: The paper finds MaNGA 1-279073 has nuclear and off-nuclear ionized-gas signatures of AGN activity, high electron densities, FIRST radio detection, and red-geyser outflow evidence.

quotes.quote: The GMOS-IFU EW maps of four galaxies present nuclear region with values larger than 3 \ (MaNGA 1-279073, 1-296801, 1-385124 and 1-197230). In two of these maps, there are locations with EW greater than 6 \ (MaNGA 1-279073 and 1-296801).
The only exception is MaNGA 1-279073 that shows the greatest values ( N_ e > 1000 cm ^ 3 ) at the nucleus and at the edges of the FoV.
Only two galaxies, MaNGA 1-279073 and 1-296801, have H equivalent width greater than 3 \ with MaNGA data, which is a strong indication of the presence of an AGN.

gmw_00009708.name: MaNGA 1-296801
gmw_00009708.overview: MaNGA 1-296801 is an S0 lenticular galaxy that hosts an unresolved radio source [arXiv:1411.0361] and is classified as a red geyser [arXiv:2208.03383]. It exhibits neutral hydrogen absorption from a rotating disk [arXiv:1411.0361] and displays high nuclear electron density [arXiv:2208.03383].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MaNGA 1-296801 as a red geyser with strong H equivalent width, high electron density, low nuclear line ratios, FIRST radio detection, and AGN-host evidence.

quotes.quote: The GMOS-IFU EW maps of four galaxies present nuclear region with values larger than 3 \ (MaNGA 1-279073, 1-296801, 1-385124 and 1-197230). In two of these maps, there are locations with EW greater than 6 \ (MaNGA 1-279073 and 1-296801).
The galaxy MaNGA 1-296801 presents electron density greater than 3000 cm ^ 3 to the south-west and north-east of the nucleus, being the object with the highest N_ e in our sample.
Five galaxies have [ N ii ] /H greater than 1.0 ; only the galaxy MaNGA 1-296801 has smaller values ( 1.0).

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J161217+282546 as an S0-hosted VLA-observed radio source that remains unresolved at 0.7 kpc resolution and shows systemic H I absorption from a rotating disk.

quotes.quote: 2MASX J161217+282546 The radio source is hosted by an S0 galaxy and has been observed with the VLA-A configuration by . At the resolution of the VLA-A observations ( 1.4 1.1 arcseconds, 0.7 kpc at z=0.0320), the radio source is unresolved. We detect an absorption line at the systemic velocity of the host galaxy, indicative of neutral hydrogen rotating in a disk.

gmw_00009709.name: MaNGA 1-385124
gmw_00009709.overview: MaNGA 1-385124 is a galaxy characterized by extended [N II] 6583 emission [arXiv:2208.03383]. It exhibits hydrogen equivalent-width active galactic nucleus signatures in certain regions and an elevated electron density exceeding 1200 per cubic centimeter to the east of its nucleus [arXiv:2208.03383].

sources.summary: The paper reports MaNGA 1-385124 has extended emission, H equivalent-width AGN signatures in some regions, shell-like high line ratios, and elevated electron density east of the nucleus.

quotes.quote: The strongest [ N ii ] 6583 extended emission accross the FoV are observed for the following galaxies: MaNGA 1-385124, 1-296801,1-24104,1-114245, 1-197230 and 1-279073. MaNGA 1-385124, 1-24104 and 1-114245 show extended emission similar to the Akira galaxy riffel19 .

MaNGA 1-385124, 1-296801 ,1-197230 and 1-279073 show EW H > 3 \ in some regions. The GMOS-IFU EW maps clearly show that the bi-symmetric emission features observed in the large scale MaNGA maps extend inward to the nuclear region, except for galaxy MaNGA 1-385124.
MaNGA 1-385124 presents N_ e > 1200 cm ^ 3 to the east of the nucleus, and 1-114225 shows N_ e > 600 cm ^ 3 to the north.

gmw_00009710.name: MaNGA 1-24104
gmw_00009710.overview: MaNGA 1-24104 is a galaxy that exhibits extended [N II] 6583 emission similar to that of the Akira galaxy [arXiv:2208.03383]. It displays high nuclear and extra-nuclear emission-line ratios, with its largest [S II]/H values aligned along a bi-symmetric feature [arXiv:2208.03383].

sources.summary: The paper says MaNGA 1-24104 has Akira-like extended emission, misaligned ionization patterns, high nuclear and extra-nuclear line ratios, density hotspots, and FIRST radio detection.

quotes.quote: The strongest [ N ii ] 6583 extended emission accross the FoV are observed for the following galaxies: MaNGA 1-385124, 1-296801,1-24104,1-114245, 1-197230 and 1-279073. MaNGA 1-385124, 1-24104 and 1-114245 show extended emission similar to the Akira galaxy riffel19 .
Only MaNGA 1-24104 and 1-114245 do not present EW larger than 3 \ across the H EW map, although it presents regions with 2 .

The targets MaNGA 1-24104, 1-114245 and 1-279073 show [ N ii ] 6583/H emission-line ratios with the largest values at the nucleus, but also present extra-nuclear knots of high values. While for [ S ii ] 6716,6730/H , smaller values in the nuclear region are observed, surrounded by higher values, for MaNGA 1-385124, 1-296801, 1-279073. For MaNGA 1-24104 and 1-114245 the largest [ S ii ] /H values are observed along the EW bi-symmetric feature.

gmw_00009711.name: MaNGA 1-197230
gmw_00009711.overview: MaNGA 1-197230 is a red geyser galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2208.03383]. The galaxy exhibits nuclear enhancement of [S II]/H̑́ and high line ratios characteristic of AGN-classified ionization [arXiv:2208.03383].

sources.summary: The paper includes MaNGA 1-197230 among red geysers with H equivalent-width AGN signatures, high line ratios, nuclear [S II]/H enhancement, and AGN-classified ionization.

quotes.quote: For the galaxy MaNGA 1-197230, this pattern is less evident. The GMOS-IFU EW maps of four galaxies present nuclear region with values larger than 3 \ (MaNGA 1-279073, 1-296801, 1-385124 and 1-197230).

The [ N ii ] 6583/H values range from 0.75 (for MaNGA 1-279073) to 4.5 (MaNGA 1-197230), while [ S ii ] 6717,6731/H shows values ranging from 0.75 to 3. For three objects the [ N ii ] 6583/H ratio has the lowest values in the nucleus: 1 for MaNGA 1-296801, 1.5 for 1-385124 and 2 for 1-197230.
Unlike [ N ii ] /H , the distribution of [ S ii ] /H show higher values at the nucleus for MaNGA 1-197230.

gmw_00009712.name: host galaxy of GRB 130925A
gmw_00009712.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 130925A is an edge-on galaxy that hosted an ultra-long gamma-ray burst [arXiv:1505.04415]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy reveal a bright star-forming region in its outskirts and a super-solar metallicity at both the nucleus and this outer region [arXiv:1505.04415].

sources.summary: The paper studies the GRB 130925A host, measuring dust extinction and super-solar metallicity near the nucleus and an outer star-forming region.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present high signal-to-noise X-shooter observations of the host galaxy of GRB 130925A, which is the fourth unambiguously identified ultra-long GRB, with prompt ray emission detected for 20 ks. The GRB line of sight was close to the host galaxy nucleus, and our spectroscopic observations cover this region along the bulge/disk of the galaxy, and a bright star-forming region within the outskirts of the galaxy. From our broad wavelength coverage, we obtain accurate metallicity and dust-extinction measurements at the galaxy nucleus and at an outer star-forming region, and measure a super-solar metallicity at both locations, placing this galaxy within the 10-20 most metal-rich GRB host galaxies. Such a high metal enrichment has significant implications on the progenitor models of both long and ultra-long GRBs, although the edge-on orientation of the host galaxy does not allow us to rule out a large metallicity variation along our line of sight.

gmw_00009713.name: DC 308643
gmw_00009713.overview: DC 308643 is classified as a galaxy merger candidate [arXiv:2107.10856] or merger [arXiv:2002.00967]. Although its velocity gradient suggests a rotating disk [arXiv:2107.10856, arXiv:2002.00967], two close components in its position-velocity diagram and a non-Gaussian [CII] line shape support the merging scenario [arXiv:2107.10856].

sources.summary: The paper classifies DC 308643 as a merger candidate, noting a velocity gradient but interpreting close PVD components and a non-Gaussian [CII] line as merging evidence.

quotes.quote: DC 308643 : this source was classified as a merger by . The moment-1 map shows a clear velocity gradient suggesting the presence of a rotating disk. However, a similar velocity map could also be reproduced by two merging components in an advanced phase of merging. This is suggested by the two close components visible in the PVD along the major axis and by the shape of the [ CII ] line which deviates from that of a single Gaussian.

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 308643 as a merger, noting [CII] emission, UV/optical alignment, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 308643 : this object is classified as a merger mainly because two dynamical components are present in the position-velocity diagram (see Le F evre et al. 2020), although a rotating disk could also produce a similar velocity pattern. The spatial UV- [ CII ] and spectral [ CII ] offsets are both very small.

gmw_00009714.name: DC 372292
gmw_00009714.overview: DC 372292 is classified as a galaxy merger [arXiv:2002.00967, arXiv:2107.10856]. This classification is supported by two distinct components separated in velocity by 200 km/s, which are visible in both the spectrum and position-velocity diagrams [arXiv:2002.00967, arXiv:2107.10856].

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 372292 as a merger, noting [CII] emission, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 372292 : this object is classified as a merger mainly because two dynamical components are present in the position-velocity diagram, separated in velocity by 200 km s ^ 1 (see Le F evre et al. 2020), although a rotating disk could also produce a similar velocity pattern. Neither the spatial nor the spectral [ CII ] offset are significant.

sources.summary: The paper confirms DC 372292 as a merger, citing a possible velocity gradient plus two velocity-separated components visible in PVDs and the spectrum.

quotes.quote: DC 372292 : we confirm the merger classification of this object by . As for DC 308643, a possible velocity gradient is present in the moment-1 map. However, two components separated in velocity by 200 km s ^ 1 are clearly visible in the PVDs and in the spectrum.

gmw_00009715.name: DC 417567
gmw_00009715.overview: DC 417567 is a high-redshift galaxy classified as a merger [arXiv:2107.10856]. This classification is supported by a disturbed [CII] morphology [arXiv:2107.10856, arXiv:2002.00967], multiple peaks in its position-velocity diagrams [arXiv:2107.10856], and fainter emission components located close to the main central source [arXiv:2002.00967].

sources.summary: The paper supports a merger classification for DC 417567 from disturbed [CII] morphology, multiple PVD peaks, Gaussian spectral components, and poor disk-fit behavior.

quotes.quote: DC 417567 : this source was classified as a merger by because of the disturbed [ CII ] morphology and the presence of multiple peaks in the PVDs. These are reproduced by different Gaussian components in the overall [ CII ] spectrum. This target was also analyzed by which classified it as uncertain because of its low S/N. However, the poor ^ 3D Barolo fit supports the merger classification for this object.

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 417567 as a merger, noting [CII] emission, UV/optical alignment, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 417567 : this object is classified as a merger because the [ CII ] maps show fainter components close to the main central source. The morphology in the UV and optical rest-frame is disturbed by the presence of a nearby foreground source, nevertheless, it is clear that the emission originates from the main [ CII ] component. We confirmed that the secondary [ CII ] peaks do not contribute to moving the position of the [ CII ] velocity peak.

gmw_00009716.name: DC 434239
gmw_00009716.overview: DC 434239 is a high-redshift galaxy classified as a merger [arXiv:2107.10856]. It exhibits an extended and disturbed morphology in both [CII] emission and the K-band, alongside multiple peaks in its position-velocity diagrams [arXiv:2107.10856].

sources.summary: The paper classifies DC 434239 as a merger with extended disturbed [CII] and K-band morphology, multiple PVD peaks, and a multi-component [CII] spectrum.

quotes.quote: DC 434239 : this object was classified as a merger both by and . The [ CII ] morphology is extended and disturbed, as well as that from the UVista K_s band. The PVDs show multiple peaks and we need at least three components to reproduce the global shape of the [ CII ] spectrum.

gmw_00009717.name: DC 519281
gmw_00009717.overview: DC 519281 is a high-redshift galaxy classified as an active merger [arXiv:2002.00967, arXiv:2107.10856]. This ongoing merging activity is indicated by a complex velocity map [arXiv:2107.10856], a faint companion southwest of the main [CII] emission, and optical rest-frame light aligned with the main emission [arXiv:2002.00967].

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 519281 as a merger, noting [CII] emission, UV/optical alignment, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 519281 : this object is classified as a merger because a faint second component is present in the position velocity diagram (Le F evre et al. 2020) and because the [ CII ] map shows a fainter companion southwest of the main emission (at the edge of the spectroscopic slit). We confirmed that these secondary peaks do not affect the position of the [ CII ] velocity peak. The optical rest-frame light is well aligned with the main [ CII ] emission.

sources.summary: The paper treats DC 519281 as a merger whose complex velocity map and PVDs, together with prior analysis, indicate ongoing merging activity.

quotes.quote: DC 519281 : this source was classified as a merger by . The complex velocity map and PVDs sustain this interpretation. Further evidence for ongoing merging activity is provided by the analysis of (even if they classify this object as uncertain because of the low S/N and spatial resolution).

gmw_00009718.name: DC 680104
gmw_00009718.overview: DC 680104 is classified as a galaxy merger [arXiv:2107.10856]. It features a faint secondary component and two velocity-separated [CII] emission peaks visible in its position-velocity diagram [arXiv:2107.10856].

sources.summary: The paper classifies DC 680104 as a merger based on a faint secondary component, PVD emission offset, and two velocity-separated [CII] peaks.

quotes.quote: DC 680104 : this object was classified as a merger by . It shows a faint secondary component at 3 in the moment-0 map which is also visible in the PVD along the major axis at a spatial offset of 1 arcsec with respect to the main target. Although the S/N of this source is low, we consider it as a merger, mainly because of two peaks of emission in the PVDs at the same spatial position but separated by 150 km s ^ 1 in velocity. These are also visible in the [ CII ] spectrum.

gmw_00009719.name: DC 834764
gmw_00009719.overview: DC 834764 is a high-redshift galaxy that was previously classified as an extended dispersion-dominated system [arXiv:2107.10856]. However, its disturbed velocity field, emission line shape, and emission peaks in position-velocity diagrams indicate the presence of multiple components, leading to its reclassification as a galaxy merger [arXiv:2107.10856].

sources.summary: The paper reclassifies DC 834764 as a merger because its PVD emission peaks, line shape, and disturbed velocity field suggest multiple components.

quotes.quote: DC 834764 : this source was classified as an extended dispersion-dominated galaxy by . However, the peaks of emission in the PVDs, the shape of the emission line, and the quite disturb velocity field suggest the presence of multiple components. We thus classify this object as a merger.

gmw_00009720.name: DC 873321
gmw_00009720.overview: DC 873321 is a high-redshift galaxy system classified as a merger [arXiv:2107.10856, arXiv:2401.03020]. Its [CII] emission is elongated toward two bright K-band sources that share the same velocity [arXiv:2107.10856] and have a very small spatial separation [arXiv:2401.03020].

sources.summary: The paper classifies DC 873321 as a merger, with [CII] emission elongated toward two K-band sources at the same velocity.

quotes.quote: DC 873321 : this galaxy was classified as a merger both by and . The [ CII ] emission is elongated toward the two bright sources visible in the UVista K_s band. These two components are at the same velocity, being indistinguishable in the PVDs and their spectra are shown in the bottom panel of Fig. fig:merger_appendix11 (right). vc 5100541407 : the extended [ CII ] morphology coincident with the two optical components visible in the maps, allowed to characterize this source as a merger. classified it as uncertain instead, mainly because of their adopted classification criteria.

sources.summary: The paper treats DC 873321 as a close high-redshift merger whose similar [CII] components required special two-component fitting and likely caused diffuse-halo emission to be underestimated or misassigned.

quotes.quote: For one system, DC 873321, we had to perform a two 2D Gaussian components fit, because of the little spatial separation and similar [ CII ] luminosity ( _ [ CII ] 1 ; see Table tab:OBStargets ) of the merging galaxies, which prevent us from fitting single 2D Gaussian models.

Notice that DC 873321 is the system with the least [ CII ] emission associated with the diffuse component, according to Figure fig:spectra (top right panel), and it is also the only system where we had to perform a two components 2D Gaussian fit instead of two single component 2D Gaussian models (see previous discussion). Because of the difficulty in separating the emission from the galaxies and that of the surrounding medium, it is likely that part of the [ CII ] emission from the diffuse halo is instead wrongly associated with the individual components of the system.

gmw_00009721.name: vc 5100541407
gmw_00009721.overview: The galaxy vc 5100541407 is classified as a merger of two components emitting at the same velocity [arXiv:2107.10856]. This merger classification is supported by extended [CII] morphology, multiple bright clumps, and two optical components visible in imaging [arXiv:2107.10856, arXiv:2002.00967].

sources.summary: The paper classifies vc 5100541407 as a two-component merger at the same velocity based on extended [CII] morphology and matching optical components.

quotes.quote: vc 5100541407 : the extended [ CII ] morphology coincident with the two optical components visible in the maps, allowed to characterize this source as a merger. classified it as uncertain instead, mainly because of their adopted classification criteria. Given the morphological and kinematic information on this galaxy, we also consider it as a merger of two components emitting at the same velocity. A pseudo-slit 4.5 arcsec wide is used to compute the PVDs. vc 5101209780 : this galaxy was classified as a merger both by and , and it was the subject of a further and in-depth analysis by . The [ CII ] moment-0 map clearly shows the presence of two major components, as also confirmed by the optical images.

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 5100541407 as a merger, noting [CII] emission, UV/optical alignment, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 5100541407 : this object is classified as a merger because the [ CII ] map shows multiple bright clumps. The UltraVISTA K_s image (mapping the optical rest-frame light) also shows two components, but only one of them is in the spectroscopic slit. It is clear that the spectrum has been extracted from the region coincident with the brightest [ CII ] peak, and we verified that the position of the [ CII ] velocity peak is not affected by the faintest clumps.

gmw_00009722.name: vc 5101209780
gmw_00009722.overview: The high-redshift galaxy vc 5101209780 is classified as a merger system [arXiv:2107.10856, arXiv:2401.03020]. It features two major optical and [CII] components [arXiv:2107.10856], with roughly half of its total [CII] emission originating from a gaseous envelope distributed between these components [arXiv:2401.03020].

sources.summary: The paper treats vc 5101209780 as a merger with two major [CII] and optical components plus fainter features in the spectrum and PVDs.

quotes.quote: vc 5101209780 : this galaxy was classified as a merger both by and , and it was the subject of a further and in-depth analysis by . The [ CII ] moment-0 map clearly shows the presence of two major components, as also confirmed by the optical images. Two fainter components are also found in the spectrum and PVDs, which are computed by adopting a pseudo-slit 4.5 arcsec wide. vc 5180966608 : classified this object as a merger while considered it as uncertain, mainly because of the [ CII ] spectrum which resembles that of a single, extended source.

sources.summary: The paper confirms earlier work on vc 5101209780, finding roughly half of its total [CII] emission arises from a gaseous envelope between its merging components.

quotes.quote: Looking at the spectra we note that the brightest emission is the one coming from the peak aperture (pink) i.e. the center of each galaxy, while 1" and optimal apertures in some cases have comparable emissions (see for example component A of both DC 818760 and vc 5101209780).

Already in Ginolfi+2020a the authors analysed in detail the merging system vc 5101209780, finding that about 50 of the total [ CII ] emission arises from a gaseous envelope distributed between the individual components of the system.

On average, we find that 55 of the [ CII ] emission of the merging systems comes from the inner CGM, confirming what was previously found by Ginolfi+2020a for the system vc 5101209780 alone, and quantifying the emission we found looking at the spectra in Figure fig:spectra (green hatched area).

gmw_00009723.name: vc 5180966608
gmw_00009723.overview: The high-redshift galaxy vc 5180966608 is classified as a merger [arXiv:2107.10856, arXiv:2002.00967]. It features multiple optical components visible in imaging [arXiv:2107.10856, arXiv:2002.00967] and exhibits a disturbed velocity gradient in its extended [CII] emission [arXiv:2107.10856].

sources.summary: The paper finds vc 5180966608 more consistent with a merger than a single extended source, citing multiple optical components and disturbed [CII] kinematics.

quotes.quote: vc 5180966608 : classified this object as a merger while considered it as uncertain, mainly because of the [ CII ] spectrum which resembles that of a single, extended source. We find more evidence for a merging system. Indeed, at least two optical components are visible both from HST and UVista within the 3 [ CII ] emission contours. The velocity gradient is quite disturbed and several peaks of emission are present in the PVDs, more visible along the minor axis (which is aligned with the two optical sources). t t t t t t t t t t

sources.summary: The appendix describes Object 5180966608 as a merger, noting [CII] emission, UV/optical alignment, multiple components in the ALPINE imaging and spectra.

quotes.quote: Object 5180966608 : this object is classified as a merger because multiple clumps appear in the ancillary HST and Subaru optical images, although the [ CII ] emission is extended and is not resolved in multiple components. The line has been extracted from the UV-optical peak that is coincident with the [ CII ] emission. Because [ CII ] is extended, we re-extracted a [ CII ] spectrum from a region coincident with the UV-optical region, and we find that the position of the [ CII ] velocity peak is not affected.

gmw_00009724.name: cX
gmw_00009724.overview: The object cX is a candidate host galaxy detected within an X-ray uncertainty region [arXiv:2303.01857]. It is potentially a dwarf galaxy, though its exact redshift and distance remain undetermined due to a lack of multi-band photometric detections [arXiv:2303.01857].

sources.summary: The paper identifies cX as a candidate host inside the X-ray uncertainty region and evaluates possible offsets, luminosities, and dwarf-galaxy interpretations.

quotes.quote: Assuming cX is the host galaxy for In our second epoch GTC/HiPERCAM observations, we detect the candidate host galaxy cX with the g_s = 25.9 0.1 within the 1 X-ray uncertainty region (see Section sec:results_optical ). The lack of detections at other photometric bands preclude the determination of the photometric redshift. If at the distance of the Abell 1795 cluster ( z =0.063), cX lies at a (projected) offset of 0.7 1.2 kpc from the FXT. Alternatively, if cX is at the distance of the galaxies cNE or cW, the (projected) offset would be 4.8 8.7 kpc or 4.5 8.2 kpc, respectively. The left panel of Figure fig:sGRB shows the cumulative host galaxy offset distribution of SGRBs ( 2022arXiv220601763F ), long GRBs ( 2017MNRAS.467.1795L ), superluminous supernovae ( 2021ApJS..255...29S ), Type Ia SNe ( 2020ApJ...901..143U ) and core-collapsed SNe (CC-SNe; 2012ApJ...759..107K , 2021ApJS..255...29S ) compared with the offsets (in kpc) of GW170817 ( 2017ApJ...848L..28L ), ( Xue2019 ), and .

gmw_00009725.name: cW
gmw_00009725.overview: The galaxy cW is a photometric-redshift candidate host with a redshift of approximately 1.04 [arXiv:2303.01857]. It has a stellar mass of 10 billion solar masses and a star formation rate of 7.5 solar masses per year [arXiv:2303.01857].

sources.summary: The paper considers cW as a photometric-redshift candidate host whose offset, star-formation rate, stellar mass, and implied X-ray luminosity match short-GRB hosts.

quotes.quote: Assuming cW is the host galaxy The photometric redshift of cW z_ phot 1.04^ +0.22 _ 0.14 . If lies at the distance of cW, it had a peak luminosity of (3--7) 10 ^ 45 and a projected physical offset of 30 8 kpc (pink line in the Left panel of Figure fig:sGRB ). A few SGRBs (orange circles in the right panel of Figure fig:sGRB ) originate from hosts with similar SFR and M_* (cW has a SFR 7.5 yr ^ 1 and M_* [ ] 10 ^ 10 ; Right panel of Figure fig:sGRB ). As in the case of cNE, the host galaxy SFR and M _ , the offset between the center of the host and the FXT uncertainty region, and the peak X-ray luminosity agree with the values found for SGRBs making a BNS merger scenario possible for .

gmw_00009726.name: Arp 107 A
gmw_00009726.overview: Arp 107 A is the southwestern component of an interacting galaxy pair [arXiv:astro-ph/9811342]. The galaxy exhibits Seyfert 2 activity and displays complex, two-component emission-line profiles in its spectrum [arXiv:astro-ph/9811342].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Arp 107 A as the southwest component of an interacting pair with Seyfert 2 activity and complex two-component emission-line profiles.

quotes.quote: Arp 107 A is the SW component of an interacting galaxy pair (Arp 1966). It exhibits Seyfert 2 activity and its spectrum shows very strong \ lines ( \ = 3.0) according to Keel et al. (1985). Our spectrum shows a more normal value. In fact, the lines have a complex profile which can best be fitted by two sets of Gaussians having different widths, but similar \ ratios, namely 1.41 and 1.23.

gmw_00009727.name: N156
gmw_00009727.overview: N156 is a very low-surface-brightness dwarf galaxy that exhibits warped outer isophotes or tidal tails [arXiv:astro-ph/0410463]. These structures, which give the galaxy an integral-sign shape, likely originated from a tidal interaction with the nearby low-luminosity spiral galaxy N155 [arXiv:astro-ph/0410463].

sources.summary: The paper describes N156 as a very low-surface-brightness dwarf with warped outer isophotes or tidal tails, probably shaped by interaction with nearby spiral N155.

quotes.quote: N156 was originally classified as dE by FS90 . As discussed in Sec. s_sbmr , however, the faintest ( (g) 26 ) isophotes in our images show an ``integral sign'' shape for galaxy morphology, with a warp or tidal tail emerging at each extreme of the major axis. The a_4 Fourier coefficient from the isophote-fitting routine for this galaxy is slightly negative for 7 arcsec, indicating a mild boxiness for isophotes fainter than (g) 25 .

The low-luminosity spiral N155, a spectroscopically confirmed group member, lies at a short projected distance (1.5 arcmin 10.7 h_ o ^ 1 kpc) from the centre of N156. It is thus tempting to invoke an interaction between both objects as the origin for the warped shape of N156. The facts that the later's ``arm'' or tidal tail facing N155 points in the right direction, and that there is a hint for a counter tail at very LSB levels in N155 itself, seem to support the interaction hypothesis. Note that N155's mean effective surface brightness is 2.5 brighter than that of N156, implying a higher surface mass density for the former (if similar mass to light ratios are assumed). Unfortunately, the spectrum we obtained for N156 has an insufficient S/N ratio to derive its radial velocity; hence, its kinematics with respect to N155 cannot be stated.

gmw_00009728.name: N155
gmw_00009728.overview: N155 is a low-luminosity spiral galaxy and a spectroscopically confirmed member of a galaxy group [arXiv:astro-ph/0410463]. It lies at a short projected distance from N156, and a faint counter-tail suggests a possible tidal interaction between the two systems [arXiv:astro-ph/0410463].

sources.summary: The paper treats N155 as a low-luminosity spiral group member near N156, with possible counter-tail evidence supporting an interaction that may explain N156’s warp.

quotes.quote: The low-luminosity spiral N155, a spectroscopically confirmed group member, lies at a short projected distance (1.5 arcmin 10.7 h_ o ^ 1 kpc) from the centre of N156. It is thus tempting to invoke an interaction between both objects as the origin for the warped shape of N156. The facts that the later's ``arm'' or tidal tail facing N155 points in the right direction, and that there is a hint for a counter tail at very LSB levels in N155 itself, seem to support the interaction hypothesis. Note that N155's mean effective surface brightness is 2.5 brighter than that of N156, implying a higher surface mass density for the former (if similar mass to light ratios are assumed).

gmw_00009729.name: N153
gmw_00009729.overview: N153 is an isolated dwarf galaxy originally classified as a dS0 system [arXiv:astro-ph/0410463]. Although it has a clearly visible disk structure, its outermost isophotes exhibit a warped distortion that gives them a boxy shape [arXiv:astro-ph/0410463].

sources.summary: The paper describes N153 as a previously dS0-classified galaxy with directly visible disk structure, warped outer isophotes, isolation, and no likely interaction with nearby NGC 5054.

quotes.quote: Sharing a similar warped appearance, N153 is however a rather different case. It was originally classified as dS0, because its disky shape is clearly seen upon visual inspection. In addition, we were able to detect a warped distortion of the outermost isophotes (see Fig. f_conto ). These features are quantified by the a_4 coefficient, which is mildly positive (i.e., disky) within the range 10 20 arcsec and definitely negative for 20 arcsec, where the warping gives a boxy shape to the faintest isophotes.

N153 is fairly isolated; it has no catalogued companion (irrespective of membership class) closer than 8.3 arcmin on the sky. One of its nearest neighbours is, remarkably, the bright spiral NGC 5054, which lies at 70 h_ o ^ 1 kpc projected distance. However, both objects differ by more than 1000 in radial velocity, hence an interaction is not likely.

gmw_00009730.name: N42
gmw_00009730.overview: N42 is classified as an anomalous dwarf galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0410463]. Its structure is poorly fit by a single Sersic law, instead favoring a disk and bulge system where the disk is the more luminous component [arXiv:astro-ph/0410463].

sources.summary: The paper describes N42 as an anomalous dwarf whose surface-brightness profile is poorly fit by one Sersic law and instead favors a luminous disk plus bulge structure.

quotes.quote: N42: a bulge plus disc system The relevant case of N42 and its anomalous location in Fig. f_frfin and f_sbgt , has been extensively discussed in previous papers ( C99 ), and especially in CB01 , where we showed that, although a low- n fit produces a nominally better match to the galaxy integrated magnitude, its physical sense is questionable. Our new data for N42 confirm that, trying a single S ersic law, the resulting fit depends highly on the fitting range in . With the full useful SBP we obtain n=0.44 , while avoiding the bulge ( > 15 arcsec) a convex shape parameter is obtained ( n=1.18 ).

On the other hand, n>1 models give good fits to the outer region of the galaxy (the disc), leaving, after subtraction, a bulge-type component about 20 the total luminosity of the galaxy. We thus sustain a disc + bulge structure for N42, with the disc being the more luminous component.

gmw_00009731.name: N54
gmw_00009731.overview: N54 is a low surface brightness dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0410463]. It exhibits a smooth appearance with no signs of current or recent star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0410463].

sources.summary: The paper describes N54 as a huge dwarf elliptical with low surface brightness and structural similarities to N156, but without signs of current or recent star formation.

quotes.quote: The same is true for N54, a ``huge'' dE already mentioned because of its departure form the main trend in the surface brightness -- magnitude relation (sect. s_sbmr ). Except for the outer warped isophotes in N156, this dwarf and N54 have very similar structural properties: both are very LSB, n=1.0 systems, lying well within known ranges in size, luminosity and surface brightness of dwarf irregulars e.g., PT96 . However, there is no hint for zones of current/recent star formation in these two galaxies (neither in N42); instead, they have a very smooth appearance, which surely led to their original dE classification.

gmw_00009732.name: UGC 10045
gmw_00009732.overview: UGC 10045 is a nearly edge-on disk galaxy with a slight apparent tilt [arXiv:2203.01154]. It features a large boxy or peanut-shaped bulge that is attached to a thick ring, as well as a bright inner component resembling a nuclear disk [arXiv:2203.01154].

sources.summary: The paper highlights UGC 10045 as a nearly edge-on galaxy with a bright, large B/PS bulge, residue-image rays, thick-ring connection, and an inner component.

quotes.quote: UGC 10045 : This galaxy is observed nearly edge-on, but has a slight apparent tilt. It shows a bright B/PS bulge, albeit without such sharp X-structure. Its rays manifest themselves in the residue image. The bulge itself is very large in comparison with the entire galaxy, its linear size is about half of the disc.

We should also note that the B/PS bulge is attached to the thick ring with almost no gaps in between, which means that a thin (flat) part of the bar have very small linear size (if any) in this galaxy. This phenomenon is especially interesting in light of 's study of NGC 352 galaxy and works by and , where it was found that the typical size of the B/PS bulge should be about half or less than that of the bar. Interestingly, there is also an inner bright feature that resembles a nuclear disc or a small inner bulge. This separate component has a clear trace in the residue image.

gmw_00009733.name: NGC 1355
gmw_00009733.overview: NGC 1355 is a disk galaxy that features a boxy or peanut-shaped bulge [arXiv:2203.01154]. This bulge contains bright, sharp X-structures, while the galaxy's entire disk is warped into an S-shape [arXiv:2203.01154].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1355 as an exemplary, previously unstudied B/PS-bulge case with bright sharp X-structures and a warped S-shaped disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 1355 : Here one can point to bright and sharp X-structures. In addition, although the B/PS bulge looks symmetric with no sign of bending or buckling, the entire disc is warped and has an ``S'' shape. From our subjective point of view, this is rather an exemplary B/PS bulge, and, therefore, it is very interesting, that NGC 1355 was not specifically studied in any of the following works: , where a comprehensive analysis of B/PS bulges of some galaxies was carried out.

gmw_00009734.name: ESO 486-59
gmw_00009734.overview: ESO 486-59 is a non-edge-on disk galaxy that hosts a large, boxy bulge [arXiv:2203.01154]. The galaxy's structure features bar shoulders in the disk plane and a complex residue containing possible double X-shaped features [arXiv:2203.01154].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 486-59 as a non-edge-on galaxy with a large boxy B/PS bulge, complex residue, bar shoulders, and possible double X-shaped structures.

quotes.quote: ESO 486-59 : The B/PS bulge of this galaxy has a fairly boxy profile and is quite large, which makes it somewhat similar to the case of UGC 10045. But the structure of the residue is much more complicated here. First, there are large `shoulders', which are extensions of the thin part of the bar in the disc plane. Secondly, the inner and outer X-shaped features can be seen in the centre of the residue image.

Since ESO 486-59 is not directly observed edge-on and has double X-shaped features that are mostly seen in the residue, it is difficult to say whether such a residue is a direct trace of double X-structures or not. However, this makes this galaxy an interesting target for future research and, in particular, decomposition studies.

gmw_00009735.name: 2MFGC 17811
gmw_00009735.overview: The galaxy 2MFGC 17811 is dominated by a bar that occupies most of its structure [arXiv:2203.01154]. It features a square-like boxy/peanut-shaped bulge, a square central residue, and a small remaining disk [arXiv:2203.01154].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MFGC 17811 as dominated by a square-like B/PS bulge and bar, with a square central residue and only a small remaining disk.

quotes.quote: 2MFGC 17811 : Here, the entire B/PS bulge has a square-like profile, but at the same time, an unusual square-like central part is present in the residue. Again, there are large bar `shoulders', after which only a small part of the disc remains. The bar occupies most of this galaxy.

gmw_00009736.name: 2MFGC 15984
gmw_00009736.overview: The disk galaxy 2MFGC 15984 is primarily composed of a bar and a boxy/peanut-shaped bulge [arXiv:2203.01154]. It features a visible but less sharp X-structure, large shoulders likely mixed with a thick ring, and a bright, flat central component [arXiv:2203.01154].

sources.summary: The paper says 2MFGC 15984 has a visible but less sharp X-structure, is mostly bar and B/PS bulge, and contains a bright flat central component.

quotes.quote: 2MFGC 15984 : An X-structure in this galaxy is not as sharp, but manifest itself clearly in the residue image. Again, the entire galaxy is mostly composed of a bar and a B/PS bulge. There are large `shoulders' that are probably mixed with a thick ring. In the central region, a bright central component can be identified, resembling a disc or lens. It is possible that this component has a flat structure without a thick vertical part, since it is noticeably stretched in the disc plane.

gmw_00009737.name: ACT-S J0107+0001
gmw_00009737.overview: ACT-S J0107+0001 is a dusty star-forming galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 3.332 [arXiv:1511.06770]. Submillimeter Array astrometry places the galaxy near a candidate gravitational lens galaxy that is detected only in the mid-infrared [arXiv:1511.06770].

sources.summary: The paper treats ACT-S J0107+0001 as a z=3.332 ACT-selected DSFG near a mid-infrared lens candidate, using its CO redshift to tighten SED-model parameter constraints.

quotes.quote: For ACT-S J0107+0001, we used a CO-based spectroscopic redshift of z = 3.332 from a measurement with the Redshift Search Receiver RSR; Erickson07 on the Large Millimeter Telescope (LMT); a paper discussing this and other LMT/RSR observations of ACT DSFGs is currently in preparation.
ACT-S J0107+0001 ( z=3.332 ) has SMA astrometry placing it nearby a candidate lens galaxy only detected in the mid-IR.

As discussed in Section sec:data_other and Appendix app:0210 , we have spectroscopic redshift measurements for ACT-S J0107+0001 and ACT-S J0210+0016. Table ACTtable_2 shows the results of fitting these sources with the redshifts fixed to the spectroscopic values. The resulting parameter constraints are seen to be consistent with and (unsurprisingly) tighter than constraints from fitting the data with a flat, unbounded prior on the redshifts.

gmw_00009738.name: HSC J233107-001014
gmw_00009738.overview: HSC J233107-001014 is a high-redshift quasar that exhibits a strong C IV emission line [arXiv:2208.00612]. It hosts a supermassive black hole with an estimated mass of approximately 10^8 solar masses and an Eddington ratio of 0.6 [arXiv:2208.00612].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes HSC J233107-001014 as a high-redshift quasar with C IV detection, estimating its black-hole mass near 10^8 solar masses and Eddington ratio around 0.6.

quotes.quote: Here, we investigate the supermassive black hole mass ( M_ BH ) and _ Edd of one of our sample, HSC J233107-001014 for which C 4 is detected. First, we transferred the observed frame to rest frame by using z_ spec . Then, we modeled continuum using a power-law function ( f_ = ^ _ ).
L_ (1350 A ) is calculated from the best-fit SED model for the spectrum of HSC J233107-001014. The resulting (M_ BH /M_ sun ) is 7.92_ 0.27 ^ +0.62 for the virial factor of 5.1 Woo+2013 .

Since HSC J233107-001014 has a strong C 4 emission line as well among the quasars, we calculated the marginal BH mass for the quasar, resulting in 10^ 8 M_ . The _ Edd of the quasar is 0.6 , although most quasars with a similar luminosity ( L_ Bol 10^ 46 erg s^ 1 ) to the quasar have lower Eddington ratios.

gmw_00009739.name: NGC 5638
gmw_00009739.overview: NGC 5638 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1101.0903] that is interacting with its companion galaxy, NGC 5636 [arXiv:1101.0903]. Its stellar population is well fitted by an old age of 12 billion years [arXiv:1101.0903], and its structure supports aligned intrinsic principal axes [arXiv:1009.2147].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 5638 across radial bins, finding similar probability distributions and peak orientations that support aligned intrinsic principal axes.

quotes.quote: The radial bin 2 of NGC 5638 engulfs the radial bin 1 and extends further at inner side. Orientation of bin 2 of NGC 5638 is presented in figure 17. We find that the plots of P of the orientation of bin 1 in figure 11 and of bin 2 in figure 17 are quite similar to each other.

The peak of the orientation of bin 1 is at ( ^ ' _ p , ^ ' _ p ) = (16^ o .5, 37^ o .5) which is close to the peak (16^ o .5, 40^ o .5) of bin 2. These results support the notion that the intrinsic principle axes of NGC 5638 are aligned.

To confirm the results further, we consider bin 3, which is a narrow and overlapping region between bin 1 and 2. The peak of the orientation of bin 3 is (16^ o .5, 37^ o .5) which is the same as the peak orientation of bin 1.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 5638 as the elliptical companion of NGC 5636, comparing its smooth profiles and fitting it with an old stellar population.

quotes.quote: The sample includes five S0 galaxies: NGC 1533, NGC 2962, NGC 2974, NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 (interacting with NGC 5638, an elliptical galaxy).

In Figure  fig2 (top panel) we show the FUV, NUV and bands surface brightness profiles of NGC 5636 and, for comparison, the smooth profiles of the elliptical companion NGC 5638, obtained with the ELLPISE routine in the STSDAS package of IRAF .
The elliptical NGC 5638 is well fitted with an old age (12 Gyr) of the stellar population.

gmw_00009740.name: BST1047+1156
gmw_00009740.overview: BST1047+1156 is a gas-rich, ultradiffuse dwarf galaxy located in the intragroup environment of the Leo I group [arXiv:2401.03985]. It lacks an old red giant sequence, showing instead a young stellar population from a recent starburst that likely formed from gas tidally stripped from the spiral galaxy M96 [arXiv:2401.03985].

sources.summary: The paper argues BST1047+1156 is a gas-rich ultradiffuse dwarf with no old RGB population, a recent burst, and likely failing-tidal-dwarf origin from M96-stripped gas.

quotes.quote: We use deep Hubble Space Telescope imaging to study the resolved stellar populations in BST1047+1156, a gas-rich, ultradiffuse dwarf galaxy found in the intragroup environment of the Leo I galaxy group. While our imaging reaches approximately two magnitudes below the tip of the red giant branch at the Leo I distance of 11 Mpc, we find no evidence for an old red giant sequence that would signal an extended star formation history for the object. Instead, we clearly detect the red and blue helium burning sequences of its stellar populations, as well as the fainter blue main sequence, all indicative of a recent burst of star formation having taken place over the past 50--250 Myr.

The combination of a young, moderately metal-poor post starburst population and no old stars motivates a scenario in which BST1047 was recently formed during a weak burst of star formation in gas that was tidally stripped from the outskirts of the neighboring massive spiral M96. BST1047's extremely diffuse nature, lack of ongoing star formation, and disturbed HI morphology all argue that it is a transitory object, a ``failing tidal dwarf'' in the process of being disrupted by interactions within the Leo I group. Finally, in the environment surrounding BST1047, our imaging also reveals the old, metal-poor ( [ M/H ] =-1.3 0.2 ) stellar halo of M96 at a projected radius of 50 kpc.

gmw_00009741.name: IC3355
gmw_00009741.overview: IC3355 is a dwarf irregular galaxy that may be connected to NGC4438 by a plume, suggesting a potential recent interaction between the two systems [arXiv:1001.0980]. This possible interaction is supported by a tail associated with IC3355, which points to the west [arXiv:1001.0980].

sources.summary: The paper discusses IC3355 as a dwarf irregular potentially connected to the plume, but notes its tail direction and uncertain M86-cloud membership weaken that scenario.

quotes.quote: The plume apparently connects NGC4438 to the dwarf irregular galaxy IC3355 ( V 17 , goldmine ), perhaps suggesting a recent interaction between the two systems. This seems supported by the presence of a tail associated with IC3355 chung09 . However, contrary to what expected in case of a fly-by encounter with NGC4438, IC3355's tail points to the west, thus in the opposite direction with respect to NGC4438. Moreover, it is still unclear whether or not IC3355 is really a member of the M86 cloud chung09 . Thus, although we cannot exclude that IC3355 is playing a role, the current data do not allow us to further investigate this hypothesis.

gmw_00009742.name: SDSS J143037.09+352052.8
gmw_00009742.overview: SDSS J143037.09+352052.8 is a dwarf galaxy that is extremely faint in Pan-STARRS1 imaging [arXiv:2303.12598]. Subaru/Suprime-Cam data show that the galaxy hosts an active galactic nucleus candidate radio source within its optical footprint [arXiv:2303.12598].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J143037.09+352052.8 as the host matched to an AGN-candidate radio source, noting faint PS1 imaging but Subaru evidence for association.

quotes.quote: SDSS J143037.09+352052.8 (matched to the AGN candidate ILT 143037.30+352053.3) is also extremely faint in the PS1 filter image and may also be a chance alignment. However, data from the JVO Subaru/Suprime-Cam Aihara2019PASJ...71..114A show the radio source to be within the galaxy (Appendix sec:appendix1 ).

Finally, the AGN candidate ILT 143037.30+352053.3 was matched to the host galaxy SDSS J143037.09+352052.8. SDSS J143037.09+352052.8 is not resolved in PS1 filter shown in Figure fig:family_plot_continued . We found a JVO Subaru/Suprime-Cam image for this field, which indicates that the radio source is within the optical footprint of the host, which we show in Figure fig:agn .

gmw_00009743.name: 2MASX J12594007+2751177
gmw_00009743.overview: 2MASX J12594007+2751177 is a dwarf galaxy located within the ACO 1656 cluster [arXiv:2303.12598]. It is characterized by a very low surface density and is visible in Subaru Suprime-Cam imaging [arXiv:2303.12598].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J12594007+2751177 as a low-surface-density dwarf galaxy in ACO 1656, visible in Subaru imaging and likely unrelated to a nearby radio source.

quotes.quote: The dwarf galaxy 2MASX J12594007+2751177 was shown to be of very low surface density in PS1 (Figure fig:family_plot_continued ). To better highlight the environment in which this galaxy resides, Figure fig:HSC shows the same field as observed by PS1 in filter (right) as shown previously, and a JVO Subaru/Suprime-Cam Aihara2019PASJ...71..114A composite image (left) using all filters---where 2MASX J12594007+2751177 is apparent. Green markers (x, +) show the location of the LoTSS and CLU detections, respectively. The Subaru Suprime-Cam image strengthens the hypothesis that 2MASX J12594007+2751177 and ILTJ125944.53+275800.9 are unrelated.

gmw_00009744.name: UGC 04068
gmw_00009744.overview: The galaxy UGC 04068 hosts the radio source ILT J075257.15+401026.3 [arXiv:2303.12598]. Although previously classified as an active galactic nucleus, its SDSS spectrum is classified simply as a galaxy, which may be affected by spectral contamination from a nearby bright star [arXiv:2303.12598].

sources.summary: The paper says UGC 04068 hosts ILT J075257.15+401026.3, has prior AGN classification but a galaxy SDSS spectrum, and may have spectral contamination from a nearby bright star.

quotes.quote: Similarly, the galaxy UGC 04068, which hosts ILT J075257.15+401026.3 has been classified as an AGN by Veron2010A&A...518A..10V , while the classification based on the SDSS spectrum is simply ``galaxy'' (rather than other considered classes in SDSS nomenclature, such as QSO). There is a bright star located near the centroid of the galaxy (slightly leftward in Figure fig:family_plot ) that may impact the overall flux observed in the spectrum, especially given the location of the SDSS spectrograph fiber (pink circle) almost exactly between the galaxy centroid and that of the bright star.
