It is also the only case within the candidates where the CLU catalog contains a fitted H D25 measurement. CLU J163850.64+352900.9 and 2MASX J09133387+3000514 are unclassified in SDSS, while all other matches are classified as galaxies.

gmw_00009745.name: S2F1-142
gmw_00009745.overview: S2F1-142 is a compact early-type galaxy located at a redshift of z=1.386 [arXiv:1402.0971]. It has a small effective radius of 3.1 kiloparsecs and a virial mass of approximately 3.9e11 solar masses [arXiv:1402.0971].

sources.summary: The paper measures S2F1-142 as a compact z=1.386 early-type galaxy with high velocity dispersion, virial mass near 3.9e11 solar masses, and small effective radius.

quotes.quote: We present a high signal to noise (S/N > 10) medium resolution (R=2000) LBT-LUCIFER spectrum of the early-type galaxy (ETG) S2F1-142 at z 1.4 . By means of the CaT line at 8662 , we measured its redshift z=1.386 0.001 and we estimated its velocity dispersion _ v =340 ^ 60 _ +120 km/s. Its corresponding virial mass is 3.9 10^ 11 M _ , compatible with the stellar mass estimates obtained assuming Initial Mass Functions (IMFs) less dwarf rich than the Salpeter one. S2F1-142 is a compact galaxy with R_ e =3.1 0.2 kpc, i.e., an effective radius more than three times smaller than the average R_ e of early-type galaxies with the same mass in the local universe.

The NICMOS high resolution near-IR data allowed us to measure its effective radius R_ e and light profile in the rest-frame R-band, revealing its compact nature ( l7 , tru ). Table 1 summarizes the basic parameters and physical properties of S2F1-142, while Figure 1 shows its HST F160W image and its light profile.

gmw_00009746.name: PKS 0537-286
gmw_00009746.overview: PKS 0537-286 is a highly luminous, radio-loud blazar and flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of z = 3.104 [arXiv:1607.02053, arXiv:0707.2925]. The source exhibits variable X-ray and gamma-ray emission, featuring multiple flaring periods and source-frame variability timescales as short as 1.4 hours [arXiv:1710.02904, arXiv:2008.09675].

sources.summary: The paper presents PKS 0537-286 as a high-redshift FSRQ, extremely luminous in X-rays, with prior variability reports but no significant XMM-Newton IDV here.

quotes.quote: PKS 0537-286 PKS 0537-286, the high redshift (z = 3.104) FSRQ was studied by Reeves et al. (2001) with XMM-Newton. They reported that the source is extremely luminous in X-ray band. In their spectral study, they found that in contrast with typical AGNs, the radio to X-ray SED of the source shows dominance of X-ray power over other energy bands, which is explained by inverse Compton (IC) emission. Optical variability with different instruments and marginal X-ray variability on IDV timescale with Swift/XRT was reported by Bottacini et al. (2010).

Sambruna et al. (2007) reported that there were no soft and hard X-ray flux variability in the source with different XRT and BAT pointings within the period 2005-2007. We analyzed two observations for the blazar taken with XMM-Newton in 2000 and 2005. We did not find any significant IDV on both the occasions.

sources.summary: The paper presents NVSS J053954 283956 as a luminous z=3.104 blazar with strong X-ray jet emission, a May 2017 gamma-ray outburst, and source-frame variability as short as about 1.4 hours.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: the optimized location at this time is R.A. 85.04 ^ and DEC. -28.64 ^

quotes.quote: NVSS J053954 283956, also named as PKS 0537 286, is one of the most luminous high-redshift quasars ( z =3.104, 1978ApJ...226L..61W ). Its first detection was at radio frequencies e.g., 1975AuJPA..34....1B . It is also a bright and well-studied source in X rays e.g., 1981ApJ...245..357Z,2007ApJ...669..884S,2010A&A...509A..69B , showing an extremely high X-ray luminosity ( 10^ 47 erg s^ 1 in the 0.1-2 keV range) and a particularly hard spectrum ( _ X 1.2), indicative of a significant contribution of the nonthermal jet emission.

However, the third flare (i.e. flare-C) detected in May 2017 This flare event has also been reported in ATel by Fermi collaboration 2017ATel...10356...1C . , exhibits a totally different behavior. In the 15-day time bin light curve of the flare-C, flux of the eighth bin is significantly higher than the averaged flux, whereas the target maintains in the low flux state for the rest of time. Moreover, such a behavior is confirmed by the further 3-day time bin ray light curve, where an intense ray outburst suddenly appears.
Together with the variation amplitude (i.e. F_ bin15 /F_ bin12 8.4), _ doub,source can be estimated as short as 1.4-hr.

sources.summary: The paper presents a multi-frequency study of high-redshift blazar PKS 0537-286, finding luminous variable X-ray/gamma-ray emission and modeling flares as changes in the emitting electron distribution.

quotes.quote: We present a multi-frequency spectral and temporal study of high redshift blazar PKS 0537-286 by analyzing data from , NuSTAR Swift XRT and UVOT. Although the time averaged \ spectrum of the source is relatively soft (indicating the high-energy emission peak is below the GeV range), several prominent flares were observed when the spectrum hardened and the luminosity increased above 10^ 49 erg s^ 1 . The X-ray emission of the source varies in different observations and is characterised by a hard spectrum 1.38 with a luminosity of >10^ 47 erg s^ 1 . The broadband spectral energy distribution in the quiescent and flaring periods was modeled within a one-zone leptonic scenario assuming different locations of the emission region and considering both internal (synchrotron radiation) and external (from the disk, broad-line region and dusty torus) photon fields for the inverse Compton scattering. The modeling shows that the most optimistic scenario, from the energy requirement point of view, is when the jet energy dissipation occurs within the broad-line region.

The comparison of the model parameters obtained for the quiescent and flaring periods suggests that the flaring activities are most likely caused by the hardening of the emitting electron spectral index and shifting of the cut-off energy to higher values.

sources.summary: The paper treats 0537--286 as a z>2 radio-loud blazar detected by Swift/BAT, with soft-medium X-ray emission, hard XRT/BAT spectra, and no UVOT detection.

quotes.quote: We report on \ observations of four z>2 radio-loud quasars (0212+735, 0537--286, 0836+710, and 2149--307), classified as blazars. The sources, well-known emitters at soft-medium X-rays, were detected at >5 with the BAT experiment in 15--150 keV. No flux variability was detected within the XRT and BAT exposures, with the exception of 0836+710 which shows an increase of a factor 4 of the 15--150 keV flux on a timescale of one month. The 0.3--10 keV spectra are well fitted by power law models, with rather hard continua (photon indices _ XRT 1.3-1.5 ); similarly, the 15--150 keV spectra are described by power laws with _ BAT 1.3-1.8 .
While 0836+710 and 2149--307 are well detected with all the optical and UV filters, 0212+735 and 0537--286 were not detected in the UV, where 3 upper limits are given for the flux densities.

sources.summary: The paper reports PKS 0537-286 as a bright high-redshift blazar with sub-day gamma-ray variability, multiple flaring periods, high flare luminosity, and prominent X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: The most distant flaring blazars are MG3 J163554+3629 and PKS 0537-286 at z=3.65 and z=3.10 , respectively. Though flux amplification is observed in the 30-day bin light curve of MG3 J163554+3629, the flares are more drastic and evident for PKS 0537-286. The adaptively binned light curve PKS 0537-286 shows several bright flaring periods with a maximum flux of (1.29 0.26) 10^ 6 photon cm^ 2 s^ 1 above 100 MeV, observed on MJD 57879.2 in a time bin having a width of 16.0 hours. For the distance of PKS 0537-286, such flux amplification can be useful for investigation of the attenuation, but the photon index is _ =2.64 0.27 , so the flare is dominated by sub-GeV photons.

gmw_00009747.name: Cone Source
gmw_00009747.overview: The Cone Source is an active galactic nucleus host galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.0268 [arXiv:1610.01602]. It features a biconical ionized-gas outflow perpendicular to its stellar disk and exhibits a low star formation rate of approximately 1 solar mass per year [arXiv:1610.01602].

sources.summary: The paper describes the Cone Source as a bona-fide AGN host with biconical ionized-gas outflow, low star formation, and sufficient AGN power to drive the observed wind.

quotes.quote: This object, MaNGA 1-137883, at z = 0.0268 , shows an intriguing cone-shaped morphology of high velocity [ OIII ] gas in MaNGA, suggestive of bi-polar outflows perpendicular to the plane of the stellar disk of the galaxy. This cone-shaped region coincides with AGN/LINER-like emission according to the [ NII ] resolved BPT diagram (lower row in Figure manga_data ). In the following we dub this source as `Cone Source'.

In contrast to the Blob Source, the MaNGA data for the Cone Source already showed a biconical morphology of fast 200 km/s ionised gas as traced by the [ OIII ] emission line. By analogy to Figure GMOS_blob , we show the H velocity field as measured by both MaNGA and GMOS in Figure GMOS_cone_vel . We follow the same strategy as described above and fit rotational models to the velocity fields that are shown in the middle panels in Figure GMOS_cone_vel and compute the residuals (right panels in Figure GMOS_cone_vel ).

Based on the H luminosity, we find a SFR 1 M _ /year for the Cone Source's host galaxy. We also cross-match the Cone Source's position with the FIRST radio catalog and find a match with an unresolved source with F _ 1.4 GHz = 3.4 mJy which implies a radio luminosity of L_ [ 1.4GHz ] = 7.9 10^ 37 erg/s.

gmw_00009748.name: source to the SW
gmw_00009748.overview: The source to the SW is an optical cluster galaxy with a total flux density of 4.50 ± 0.06 mJy [arXiv:0706.3000]. High-resolution observations reveal a morphology suggesting it is a head-tail radio galaxy [arXiv:0706.3000].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the southwest cluster source as an optical cluster galaxy with radio emission; high-resolution VLA data give its position and flux and suggest head-tail radio-galaxy morphology.

quotes.quote: The source visible to the south-west (SW) of the extended emission is associated to a cluster galaxy, and has a total flux density of 4.50 0.06 mJy.

In this high resolution map the source to the SW, located at RA(J2000): 13^ h 47^ m 27.7^ s , Dec(J2000): 11^ 45' 53.4'' (not visible in Fig. mappa-A.fig ), has a total flux density of 1.90 0.04 mJy. Its morphology suggests that it is a head-tail radio galaxy. The difference in the fluxes measured at high and low resolution is thus due to the diffuse emission associated to the tail of the radio source.

gmw_00009749.name: host galaxy of the burst
gmw_00009749.overview: The host galaxy of the burst is a late-type spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.16 [arXiv:astro-ph/0510190]. The afterglows of the burst are offset by a projected distance of 3 kpc from the center of this host galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0510190].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the GRB 050709 host as a late-type spiral galaxy at z = 0.16, with the afterglows offset about 3 kpc from its center.

quotes.quote: These, in turn, have led to identification of the host galaxy of the burst as a late-type galaxy at z = 0.16 , ^ 10 showing that at least some short-hard bursts occur at cosmological distances in the outskirts of galaxies, and are likely to be due to the merging of compact binaries.

These have led to the first secure identification of a host galaxy: a late-type spiral galaxy lying at a redshift z = 0.16 . ^ 11 The X-ray and optical afterglows lie at a projected distance of 3 kpc from the center of the host galaxy and are therefore not coincident with the brightest optical emission from the host galaxy (in contrast to long bursts ^ 28 ).

The absence of any large-amplitude oscillations with a period in the range 1-10 s in the long-soft bump and the offset of GRB 050709 from the center of the host galaxy argue against an association between this short-hard burst and SGR giant flares. ^ 25,26

gmw_00009750.name: NGC 7097
gmw_00009750.overview: NGC 7097 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1203.2578] that exhibits kinematically peculiar stellar structures [arXiv:1610.01174]. Its internal regions feature a complex mixture of co-rotating and counter-rotating stellar orbits rather than a single compact rotating component [arXiv:1610.01174].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7097 as kinematically peculiar, with counter-rotating internal stellar structure and mixed co- and counter-rotating orbits rather than a compact rotating component.

quotes.quote: In the case of NGC 7097, this difference is about 27 ^o . Also here, the stellar kinematics of this object seems to be peculiar. 2001ApJ...546..903D showed that NGC 7097 has a stellar structure with < 3.5 arcsec that is counter-rotating with respect to the external regions of this galaxy. Using models to describe the stellar kinematics of NGC 7097, they demonstrated that it is not possible to define a specific radial range that contains only counter-rotating orbits and another range with only corotating orbits. Indeed, they showed that in both the internal and external structures of NGC 7097, the projection of the radial velocity is the result of the combination of a large number of stellar orbits in co-rotation and a large number of orbits in counter-rotation. In other words, the counter-rotation detected within < 3.5 arcsec is not associated with a compact rotating group of stars.

sources.summary: The paper compares 2MASS and deep K-band profiles for NGC 7097, using the galaxy to evaluate PSF distortion and preferred seeing corrections.

quotes.quote: As an additional test of the effects of the PSF, we compare the 2MASS profile for NGC 7097 with a deep K image from Kent (2012) shown in Figure 6. The profiles agree fainter than 16 K mag arcsecs ^ 2 , but the effects of the PSF distortion are visible in the 2MASS data at brighter surface brightnesses (the Kent data was taking under sub-arcsec seeing with 0.2 arcsecs pixels). We correct the 2MASS profile with two FWHM's of 2.5 (blue curve) and 3.5 (green curve) arcsecs. While a FWHM of 2.5 arcsecs is typical for 2MASS images, the 3.5 arcsecs PSF is a better fit producing agreement with pointed observations down to a radius of 1.5 arcsecs.

gmw_00009751.name: NGC 3087
gmw_00009751.overview: NGC 3087 is an early-type galaxy [arXiv:1203.2578]. Photometric analysis of the galaxy demonstrates that its outer, low-signal regions dominate its total luminosity measurements [arXiv:1203.2578].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3087 to illustrate cleaning procedures and aperture photometry, showing how outer low-signal regions dominate luminosity measurements.

quotes.quote: An example of the output from the cleaning procedures is shown in Figure 2 for the early-type galaxy NGC 3087. The visual difference is impressive, although the difference in the total flux measurements with versus without star subtraction is only 3 since the stars only obscure a very small portion of the typical galaxy.

This is demonstrated in Figure 3, a plot of elliptical aperture magnitude for the galaxy NGC 3087 as a function of radius (arcsecs). The luminosity is plotted as the ratio of the aperture luminosity to the total luminosity.

gmw_00009752.name: RGG 118
gmw_00009752.overview: RGG 118 is a nearby dwarf disk galaxy that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1709.07884]. Its structure consists of an outer spiral disk, a pseudobulge-like central component, and an active black hole with a mass of approximately 50,000 solar masses [arXiv:1709.07884, arXiv:1506.07531].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes HST imaging of RGG 118, a nearby active dwarf disk galaxy, finding an outer spiral disk, pseudobulge-like central component, AGN point source, and undermassive black hole.

quotes.quote: Greene 6 1 Department of Astronomy, Yale University, New Haven, CT 06511 2 Einstein Fellow 3 National Optical Astronomy Observatory, 950 N Cherry Ave, Tucson, AZ 85719 4 Hubble Fellow 5 Department of Astronomy, University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, MI 48109 6 Department of Astrophysical Sciences, Princeton University, Princeton, NJ 08544 RGG 118 (SDSS 1523+1145) is a nearby ( z=0.0243 ), dwarf disk galaxy ( M_ 2 10^ 9 M_ ) found to host an active 50,000 solar mass black hole at its core 2015ApJ...809L..14B . RGG 118 is one of a growing collective sample of dwarf galaxies known to contain active galactic nuclei -- a group which, until recently, contained only a handful of objects. Here, we report on new Hubble Space Telescope Wide Field Camera 3 UVIS and IR imaging of RGG 118, with the main goal of analyzing its structure. Using 2-D parametric modeling, we find that the morphology of RGG 118 is best described by an outer spiral disk, inner component consistent with a pseudobulge, and central PSF.

With a S e rsic index of n=0.8 , the central component of RGG 118 is more consistent with a pseudobulge than a classical bulge. find that RGG 118 does sit below the M_ BH M_ bulge relation defined by early-type galaxies; our results based on HST imaging are consistent with this. For the bulge mass of RGG 118 ( 10^ 8.59 M_ ), the relation given by predicts a BH mass of 8 10^ 5 M_ , or 0.2 of the bulge mass. The BH in RGG 118 is in actuality roughly an order of magnitude smaller.

sources.summary: The paper presents RGG 118 as a dwarf galaxy hosting the smallest reported nuclear black hole, with broad H emission, a nuclear X-ray source, and AGN-like emission ratios.

quotes.quote: Here we present optical and X-ray observations of the dwarf galaxy RGG 118 taken with the Magellan Echellette Spectrograph on the 6.5m Clay Telescope and Chandra X-ray Observatory. Based on Sloan Digital Sky Survey spectroscopy, RGG 118 was identified as possessing narrow emission line ratios indicative of photoionization partly due to an active galactic nucleus. Our higher resolution spectroscopy clearly reveals broad H emission in the spectrum of RGG 118.

Using virial BH mass estimate techniques, we calculate a BH mass of 50,000 . We detect a nuclear X-ray point source in RGG 118, suggesting a total accretion powered luminosity of L=4 10^ 40   erg s^ 1 , and an Eddington fraction of 1 per cent. The BH in RGG 118 is the smallest ever reported in a galaxy nucleus and we find that it lies on the extrapolation of the M_ BH _ relation to the lowest masses yet.

gmw_00009753.name: KIDS J232940-340922
gmw_00009753.overview: KIDS J232940-340922 is a gravitational lensing system featuring an early-type deflector galaxy at a redshift of z ~ 0.38 [arXiv:2011.09150]. This deflector creates an Einstein Cross configuration of a background, ultra-compact quenching galaxy at a redshift of z ~ 1.59 [arXiv:2011.09150].

sources.summary: KIDS J232940-340922 is confirmed as an Einstein Cross with a z=0.38 early-type deflector and an ultra-compact z=1.59 quenching source.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of two Einstein Crosses (ECs) in the footprint of the Kilo-Degree Survey (KiDS): KIDS J232940-340922 and KIDS J122456+005048. Using integral field spectroscopy from MUSE@VLT, we confirm their gravitational-lens nature. In both cases, the four spectra of the source clearly show a prominence of absorption features, hence revealing an evolved stellar population with little star formation.

The first EC, KIDS J232940-340922 (KIDS-EC1, hereafter, see Fig. fig:spectra ), has been found in the Southern KiDS patch. The deflector has a AB magnitude of 19.8 and red color, g-i=1.4 , while the average magnitude of the 4 lensed images of the source is 22.6 and their average color is g-i=-0.21 .

We have detected the same spectral features in the four images of the two crosses and inferred a redshift of z_ s 1.59 for KIDS-EC1 and z_ s 1.10 for KIDS-EC2, both higher that their respective deflector galaxies, i.e. two old early-type galaxies at z_ l 0.38 (KIDS J232940-340922) and z_ l 0.24 (KIDS J122456+005048). The discovery is exceptional as we only inspected 1000 deg ^2 so far and Einstein Crosses (ECs) are very rare phenomena. However, general predictions on the number of expected quads (of which ECs are a special case) are based on the assumption that these are generated by quasars (see e.g. 2010MNRAS.405.2579O ). For the two ECs presented here, we have shown that they are produced by ultra--compact, blue, quenching galaxies. In particular: to reproduce the cross configuration, the best lensing model (see Table tab:lens_mod ), assuming a singular isothermal ellipsoid (SIE) with external shear, predicts that the sources have very compact sizes: R_ e 0.9 kpc for KiDS-EC1 and R_ e 0.5 kpc for KiDS-EC2, i.e. >10 off the typical size-mass relation of normal SF galaxies at the same redshifts (see 2017ApJ...834L..11A ); the spectra of the sources show a dominance of absorption lines, typical of a quite evolved stellar population, which has been confirmed by SED fitting of the 9-band photometry performed on the average of the two highest magnified images of each system.

gmw_00009754.name: KIDS J122456+005048
gmw_00009754.overview: KIDS J122456+005048 is a gravitational lensing system that forms an Einstein Cross [arXiv:2011.09150]. The system consists of an early-type deflector galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.24 and an ultra-compact, quenching source galaxy at z = 1.10 that exhibits an evolved stellar population [arXiv:2011.09150].

sources.summary: KIDS J122456+005048 is confirmed as an Einstein Cross with a z=0.24 early-type deflector and an ultra-compact z=1.10 quenching source.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of two Einstein Crosses (ECs) in the footprint of the Kilo-Degree Survey (KiDS): KIDS J232940-340922 and KIDS J122456+005048. Using integral field spectroscopy from MUSE@VLT, we confirm their gravitational-lens nature. In both cases, the four spectra of the source clearly show a prominence of absorption features, hence revealing an evolved stellar population with little star formation.

The second EC, KIDS J122456+005048 (KIDS-EC2, hereafter, Fig. fig:spectra ), has been found in the Northern KiDS patch. The deflector has total magnitude 19.7 and color g-i=1.9 , while the 4 images have an average magnitude 22.0 and average color g-i=1.0 , i.e. bluer than the deflector but redder than KIDS-EC1 source.

We have detected the same spectral features in the four images of the two crosses and inferred a redshift of z_ s 1.59 for KIDS-EC1 and z_ s 1.10 for KIDS-EC2, both higher that their respective deflector galaxies, i.e. two old early-type galaxies at z_ l 0.38 (KIDS J232940-340922) and z_ l 0.24 (KIDS J122456+005048). The discovery is exceptional as we only inspected 1000 deg ^2 so far and Einstein Crosses (ECs) are very rare phenomena. However, general predictions on the number of expected quads (of which ECs are a special case) are based on the assumption that these are generated by quasars (see e.g. 2010MNRAS.405.2579O ). For the two ECs presented here, we have shown that they are produced by ultra--compact, blue, quenching galaxies. In particular: to reproduce the cross configuration, the best lensing model (see Table tab:lens_mod ), assuming a singular isothermal ellipsoid (SIE) with external shear, predicts that the sources have very compact sizes: R_ e 0.9 kpc for KiDS-EC1 and R_ e 0.5 kpc for KiDS-EC2, i.e. >10 off the typical size-mass relation of normal SF galaxies at the same redshifts (see 2017ApJ...834L..11A ); the spectra of the sources show a dominance of absorption lines, typical of a quite evolved stellar population, which has been confirmed by SED fitting of the 9-band photometry performed on the average of the two highest magnified images of each system.

gmw_00009755.name: RCSGA 030945-143714
gmw_00009755.overview: RCSGA 030945-143714 is a high-redshift galaxy with a redshift of z = 2.721 [arXiv:1108.1175]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy reveal a single, mildly asymmetric Lyman-alpha emission line [arXiv:1108.1175].

sources.summary: The paper reports SOAR spectroscopy of RCSGA 030945-143714, identifying a single emission feature as Ly-alpha at z=2.721 and colors consistent with a high-redshift galaxy.

quotes.quote: At the position of RCSGA 030945-143714 along the slit there is a single mildly asymmetric emission line feature is evident in all four individual exposures at 4523 , which we determine to be Lyman- 1216 at a redshift, z _ arc = 2.721 . Other plausible interpretations for the emission feature (i.e. nebular emission lines at lower redshift) are ruled out by the absence of additional lines in the wavelength range redward of the emission feature ( = 4600-7000 ). If the emission line was, in fact, O [ II ] 3727 or H- 4341 then we would expect to also see one or more of H- 4862 , O [ III ] 4960,5007 or H- 6563 ) in the wavelength range covered by our observations. We can also note that in our final photometric analysis RCSGA 030945-143714 falls into the appropriate region of the u-g vs g- space for a galaxy with redshift z: 2.7 z < 3.5 .

gmw_00009756.name: SGAS 111504+164528
gmw_00009756.overview: SGAS 111504+164528 is a high-redshift galaxy with colors that identify it in the redshift range of 2.7 to 3.5 [arXiv:1108.1175]. Spectroscopy of the galaxy reveals a single strong Lyman-alpha emission line, indicating a redshift of 3.463 [arXiv:1108.1175].

sources.summary: The paper reports DIS spectroscopy of SGAS 111504+164528, finding a single strong line interpreted as Ly-alpha at z=3.463 and colors matching a high-redshift galaxy.

quotes.quote: DIS uses a dichroic splitting optic centered at 5500 , and we observe a single strong emission line in both the blue- and red-side spectra corresponding to the slit position of SGAS 111504+164528, centered at 5425 . Similar to the case of RCSGA 030945-143714 described above, we conclude that this feature is Lyman- 1216 at a redshift, z _ arc = 3.463 , based on the lack of other visible emission features over the observed wavelength range = 3800-9800 . Similar to the case for RCSGA 030945-143714 above, the SGAS 111504+164528 has u-g vs g- colors that also identify it as a galaxy in the redshift range, 2.7 z < 3.5 .

gmw_00009757.name: SDSS J222153.87+003054.2
gmw_00009757.overview: SDSS J222153.87+003054.2 is an active galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.12355 [arXiv:1811.03694]. Originally classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy, it temporarily transitioned to a Seyfert 1-like state due to accretion disk variability [arXiv:1811.03694].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J222153.87+003054.2 as a Seyfert 2 host of PS1-13cbe whose broad lines and optical continuum temporarily changed to a Seyfert 1-like state.

quotes.quote: The outburst occurred in the nucleus of the galaxy SDSS J222153.87+003054.2 at z = 0.12355 , which was classified as a Seyfert 2 in a pre-outburst archival Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) spectrum. PS1-13cbe showed the appearance of strong broad H and H emission lines and a non-stellar continuum in a Magellan spectrum taken 57 days after the peak of the outburst that resembled the characteristics of a Seyfert 1. These broad lines were not present in the SDSS spectrum taken a decade earlier and faded away within two years, as observed in several late-time MDM spectra. We argue that the dramatic appearance and disappearance of the broad lines and factor of 8 increase in the optical continuum is most likely caused by variability in the pre-existing accretion disk than a tidal disruption event, supernova, or variable obscuration.
The host galaxy was observed as part of SDSS and given the name SDSS J222153.87+003054.2 (hereafter SDSS J2221+0030), with a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.12355 ahn2014tenth .

gmw_00009758.name: SGASJ0033-A
gmw_00009758.overview: SGASJ0033-A is a strongly lensed source galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.39 [arXiv:2107.09769]. The galaxy is gravitationally lensed into four distinct images and has confirmed detections of carbon monoxide, neutral carbon, and dust continuum emission [arXiv:2107.09769].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SGASJ0033-A as a four-image lensed source galaxy with CO, C I, and dust detections, using it as a benchmark for molecular-gas estimates and high-redshift gas scaling comparisons.

quotes.quote: The fourth target, SGAS J003341.5+024217 (hereafter SGASJ0033) at z=2.39 , was also discovered in SDSS data and it was first reported in Rigby2018MegaSauraI . The source galaxy SGASJ0033-A is lensed into four different images: the first two are blended in a ;;5 long bright arc we call SGASJ0033-A.1. The remaining less magnified images are SGASJ0033-A.2 and SGASJ0033-A.3 (see Fig. fig:rb7_continua ).

Since SGASJ0033-A has detections of CO, [ C i ] and dust continuum, we can use it as a benchmark for the three methods listed above. In what follows, we adopt the CO estimate as the fiducial value of M mol for SGASJ0033-A. SGASJ0033-A is in agreement with other (albeit more massive) sources at the same redshift, but the tension is higher for the nondetected and SGASJ1226-A, whose gas will deplete in less than 100 at the current SFR.

gmw_00009759.name: COSMOS J100054.83+023126.2
gmw_00009759.overview: COSMOS J100054.83+023126.2 is a gas-rich luminous infrared galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.376 [arXiv:1606.00013]. Optical imaging shows a regular barred spiral morphology with clear spiral arms, while its asymmetric atomic gas distribution suggests a potential interaction with a companion galaxy [arXiv:1606.00013].

sources.summary: The paper reports CHILES H I and LMT CO detections of COSMOS J100054.83+023126.2, finding a gas-rich LIRG with asymmetric atomic gas, abundant molecular gas, and barred spiral morphology.

quotes.quote: Here, we report the highest redshift 21-cm detection in emission to date of the luminous infrared galaxy (LIRG) COSMOS J100054.83+023126.2 at z=0.376 with the first 178 hours of CHILES data. The total mass is (2.9 1.0) 10^ 10 M_ , and the spatial distribution is asymmetric and extends beyond the galaxy. While optically the galaxy looks undisturbed, the distribution suggests an interaction with candidate a candidate companion.

Here, we report the detection of the Luminous Infrared Galaxy (LIRG) COSMOS J100054.83+023126.2 (henceforth J100054) at z=0.376 with the first 178 hr of CHILES data. In addition, we include CO observations from the Large Millimeter Telescope Alfonso Serrano (LMT), and follow-up optical spectroscopic data that confirm the redshift of the detection.

A deep HST I-band image of J100054 (Figure 3a) shows that it is a massive regular barred spiral with clear spiral arms and a prominent bulge. In addition, it shows a number of fainter galaxies suggesting that this may be a small group.

gmw_00009760.name: MG3 J225517+2409
gmw_00009760.overview: MG3 J225517+2409 is an intermediate synchrotron peaked blazar [arXiv:2001.09355] classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2012.15082]. The source has been observed in a flaring state [arXiv:2002.01661] and is investigated as a potential origin for astrophysical neutrino events [arXiv:2002.01661, arXiv:2012.15082].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3FGLJ2255+2409 as the only candidate blazar flaring during an IceCube track event and models its multiwavelength jet emission as a plausible neutrino source.

quotes.quote: Among the eight sources identified within 2 angular uncertainty of the IceCube track events selected in our study, we find only one source 3FGL J2255+2409 was in flaring state during the neutrino detection. We have carried out a time dependent modelling of the multi-wavelength data from this blazar, and the neutrino event including leptonic energy losses and proton-proton interactions in its jet to determine whether it could be the origin of the neutrino event. Our lepto-hadronic model estimates a jet luminosity of L_j = 3.6 10^ 47 erg/sec during the neutrino phase of 3FGL/4FGL J2255+2409.

Our analysis shows that it does not require super-Eddington luminosity or complicated geometry to explain 3FGLJ2255+2409/MG3 J225517+2409 as the origin of the neutrino event of energy 442 TeV. To the best of our knowledge this is the first attempt to model 3FG/4FGL J2255+2409 as a source of astrophysical neutrinos.

sources.summary: The paper treats MG3 J225517+2409 as the most significant Fermi 3LAC blazar in the ANTARES analysis, noting its gamma-ray spectrum and possible neutrino association.

quotes.quote: Blazars The most significant blazar from the Fermi 3LAC catalog is the BL Lac MG3 J225517+2409 ( Fermi source 3FGL J2255.1+2411, or 4FGL J2255.2+2411 in the latest version of the catalog).

The redshift of MG3 J225517+2409 is currently unknown, but a limit of z>0.86 has been recently reported in . The gamma-ray flux of MG3 J225517+2409 measured by Fermi LAT in 8 years of observation is well described by a simple power law without cut-off, with a spectral index = 2.10 0.05 , with an integral flux of =1.0 10^ 9 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 for energies between 1 and 100 GeV.

Dedicated analysis of the region around the blazar MG3 J225517+2409 The possible association between ANTARES and IceCube neutrinos and the blazar MG3 J225517+2409 is investigated in more detail in this section, by further looking at the time evolution of the gamma-ray emission of the source.

sources.summary: The paper discusses MG3 J225517+2409 as a blazar in the IceCube-100608A error region and a possible ANTARES counterpart, classifying its SED as an IBL similar to TXS 0506+056 with z > 0.863.

quotes.quote: The IceCube event IceCube-100608A is not included in the sample of 70 tracks considered above because the uncertainty in its arrival direction is > 3^ . Nevertheless, we discuss it in this section since its error region includes the blazar MG3 J225517+2409, which has been recently reported as a possible counterpart of 5 ANTARES track events with energy ranging form 3 to 40 TeV antares2019 . The SED of MG3 J225517+2409, assembled using the latest multi-frequency information available, plotted in Fig. fig:antares shows that this object is an IBL blazar similar to TXS 0506+056, and not an LBL object as initially reported in . have derived a lower limit of z > 0.863 to the redshift of this source.

gmw_00009761.name: NGC 7592B
gmw_00009761.overview: NGC 7592B is a starbursting galaxy classified as a later Hubble-type system than its companion [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377]. It is highly inclined to the line-of-sight and lacks a clearly defined optical nucleus, instead displaying a ring-like structure of bright clumps with abundant H-alpha emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377].

sources.summary: NGC 7592B is described as a starbursting, more inclined later-type component with no clear optical nucleus, ring-clump H emission, blue southern colors, and a possible red nucleus.

quotes.quote: NGC 7592A or Nuc 1, further to the west, has an active Seyfert 2 nucleus, whereas NGC 7592B or Nuc 2 is classified as a starbursting system daha85,raf92,kew01,hat02 . NGC 7592A has a much better defined nucleus or bulge than NGC 7592B, consistent with the suggestion of hat02 , who argue that NGC 7592A is of an earlier Hubble-type than NGC 7592B. Similarly, bus90 argued that NGC 7592B is more highly inclined to the line-of-sight. This, together with its later Hubble-type, explains why no clear nucleus is seen in this system.

A comparison to the ground-based K band image of bus90 suggests that the nucleus of NGC 7592B lies near the center of the ring-like structure of bright clumps seen in Figure f:n7592 . We are not able to identify any specific clump with the nucleus. th The H image shows abundant emission around the nucleus and the ``bar'' of NGC 7592A, and emission from the clumps in the ring of NGC 7592B, with a strong central depression in emission.

gmw_00009762.name: NGC 7764A
gmw_00009762.overview: NGC 7764A is an interacting triple galaxy system undergoing a merger [arXiv:astro-ph/0006084]. Its main tidal body contains a possible double nucleus separated by approximately 260 parsecs [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377, arXiv:0708.2645], along with adjacent filaments hosting bright, blue star clusters [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377].

sources.summary: NGC 7764A is the most distant sample system; HST images show one main tidal body, adjacent companions or streamers, blue clusters, H regions, and dust-marked possible nuclei.

quotes.quote: NGC 7764A This is the most distant system in our sample at D = 122 Mpc. The location of NGC 7764A in the Toomre Sequence, among galaxies with clearly separate nuclei (Fig. f:mosaic ) suggests that Toomre thought the nuclei of the two systems were still distinct. However, our HST images (Figure f:n7764A ) do not obviously support the presence of two distinct nuclei. Future NICMOS near-infrared data will be critical in identifying the likely remains of the interacting galaxies. For now we only identify one main component from which both the tails are emanating.

Our PC image reveals a shred or linear feature about 7 (4.1 kpc) to the northwest of the main body of NGC 7764A. About a dozen bright, blue ( V-I 0.6--0.8) star clusters lie within this filament. Between this filament and the center of Fig. f:n7764A is a very bright, very blue ( V-I 0.22) cluster.

sources.summary: NICMOS images of NGC 7764A reveal a possible double nucleus separated by about 260 pc, consistent with its placement in the Toomre merger sequence.

quotes.quote: The nuclear region of NGC 7764A is shown in Figure f:seventysevensixtyfourvk . When enlarged, all three NICMOS images ( J , H and K ) reveal what appears to be a double nucleus in this system (see Figure f:seventysevenenlarged ). If these truly are the two merging galaxy nuclei, it is consistent with the placement of NGC 7764A in the middle of the Toomre sequence. The two putative nuclei are separated in a nearly east-west direction by 0 43 , which corresponds to 260 pc at the distance to NGC 7764A.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7764A as an interacting triple system where NED2 is merging with NED1, tidal material is present, and NED3 shows only faint asymmetries.

quotes.quote: NGC 7764A The NGC 7764A system is an interacting triple system containing NGC 7764A NED2 (AM2350--410), 7764A NED3 to the south--east and 7764A NED1 to the north--west. The largest galaxy, NGC 7764A NED2, appears to be in the process of merging with NGC 7764A NED1 Borchkhadze et al. (1977) note the tidal material between these two galaxies. The smallest galaxy, NGC 7764A NED3 on the other hand shows only faint asymmetries in its outer optical isophotes. In this study, the NGC 7764A NED1/NED2 system is treated as a pre--merger, with two distinct galaxies present.

gmw_00009763.name: NGC 6622
gmw_00009763.overview: NGC 6622 is an early-type galaxy featuring a well-defined nucleus and a strong stellar bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377]. It exhibits a dust lane on its northern and northeastern sides, likely caused by perturbations from its neighbor NGC 6621, and shows almost no H-alpha emission [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377].

sources.summary: NGC 6622 has a well-defined nucleus, likely bar rather than edge-on orientation, dust from perturbation by NGC 6621, little H emission, red nucleus and bar colors.

quotes.quote: NGC 6622 has a well-defined nucleus and either a strong stellar bar, or a close to edge-on orientation to the line-of-sight (Fig. f:n6622 ). The J band infrared image of bus90 shows that the outer isophotes are not too far from being circular, making it unlikely that this is an edge-on galaxy. There is an obvious dust lane on the northern and northeastern side of the galaxy, most likely signifying perturbations caused by NGC 6621. There is practically no H emission associated with NGC 6622, suggesting that it is an early Hubble-type galaxy. The nucleus and the bar have red V-I colors up to 2.3.

The typical color in the remainder of the disk is 1.3. There is a star forming region in the area between NGC 6621 and NGC 6622 which has a blue V-I color with typical values around 0.8 (near the upper right edge of Figure f:n6622 ).

gmw_00009764.name: Andromeda
gmw_00009764.overview: The Andromeda galaxy is a member of the Local Group [arXiv:2202.04099]. It resides within a dark matter halo with an estimated mass of approximately 1.3 times 10 to the power of 12 solar masses [arXiv:2202.04099].

sources.summary: The simulation includes a model Andromeda halo matching observed dynamics, with distance and velocities near observed values but a stellar mass about an order of magnitude too low.

quotes.quote: At approximately the centre of the volume lies a dark-matter halo pair that matches closely the observed dynamics of our own Local Group's Milky Way and Andromeda haloes, a deliberate result of the embedment procedure that created the constrained initial condition set (see sect:constraints ). All the properties detailed below are also listed in table:lg . The model Milky Way and Andromeda have halo masses M_ 200 = 0.9 10^ 12 and M_ 200 = 1.3 10^ 12 , respectively, matching well the current observational estimates 10^ 12 , i.e., Watkins2010,Posti2019,Cautun2020 .

Viewed from the model Milky Way, the model Andromeda galaxy is at a distance of 753 kpc, putting it well within the observed range 752 27 kpc, Riess2012 ; has an infalling radial velocity of -117 km/s, very close to the observed value 109.3 4.4 km/s, vanderMarel2019 and has a low tangential velocity, 33 km/s, also well in line with observational constraints 56^ +35 _ 31 km/s from Gaia DR2 and HST data and 164.4 61.8 km/s from satellite kinematics, vanderMarel2019,Salomon2016 . It is encouraging, yet unsurprising, that the halo masses and dynamics of the model Local Group match so well the observations, as they were deliberately designed to be so.

gmw_00009765.name: SDSS J091949.16+342304.0
gmw_00009765.overview: SDSS J091949.16+342304.0 is a nearby dwarf spheroidal galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.0375 [arXiv:0810.3971]. It acts as a gravitational lens, creating an almost complete Einstein ring of a background quasar at a redshift of 0.684 [arXiv:0810.3971].

sources.summary: The paper reports SDSS J091949.16+342304.0 as an almost complete Einstein-ring system: a redshift 0.684 quasar lensed by a very low-redshift dwarf spheroid galaxy.

quotes.quote: We report the serendipitous discovery of an ``Einstein Ring" in the optical band from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) data and associated four images of a background source. The lens galaxy appears to be a nearby dwarf spheroid at a redshift of 0.0375 0.002. The lensed quasar is at a redshift of 0.6842 0.0014 and its multiple images are distributed almost 360 ^ o around the lens nearly along a ring of radius 6."0. Single component lens models require a mass of the galaxy of almost 10 ^ 12 M _ within 6".0 from the lens center.

Here we report the discovery of an almost perfect optical ``Einstein Ring" (SDSS J091949.16+342304.0), a quasar at a redshift of 0.684 lensed by a galaxy at a very low redshift of 0.0375 forming a ring of nearly 6'' radius and associated four images.

gmw_00009766.name: PGC 12443
gmw_00009766.overview: PGC 12443 is an elliptical galaxy located at the coordinates 03h 19m 55.6s, +41d 31m 23s [arXiv:0709.1336]. It is observed in absorption between two other galaxies and exhibits an intrinsic X-ray absorbing column density of 3e20 cm^-2 [arXiv:0709.1336].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PGC 12443 as an elliptical galaxy seen in absorption between two sources, gives its position, and reports an intrinsic X-ray absorbing column from spectral fitting.

quotes.quote: One galaxy is seen in absorption (Fig. 2). This is listed as 284 or PGC 12443 in the Table of Brunzendorf Meusinger (1999). It lies midway between galaxies 4 and 5 in Fig. 1 and is centred at 03^ h 19^ m 55.6^ s , +41^ 31'23'' and is listed as an elliptical galaxy 1.9 magnitudes fainter than NGC 1278. A spectral fit shows that the absorption corresponds to an intrinsic column density of 3 1 10^ 20 .

gmw_00009767.name: CXO 52
gmw_00009767.overview: CXO 52 is a type 2 quasar located at a redshift of z=3.288 [arXiv:1504.06948, arXiv:astro-ph/0512437]. The galaxy exhibits high equivalent width ultraviolet emission lines [arXiv:astro-ph/0512437] and contains gas clouds that are consistent with photoionization by an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0512437].

sources.summary: The paper treats CXO 52 as a z=3.288 type 2 quasar and models its broad UV lines, lower-FWHM forbidden lines, AGN flux, shock component, and continuum SED.

quotes.quote: CXO 52 was identified as an optical, colour selected, high redshift source (Stern et al 2002c) and by optical follow up of X-ray sources in the Lynx field (e.g. Stanford et al 1997) . Stern et al (2002) report on observations of a type 2 quasar at z=3.288 that was identified as a hard X-ray source by Chandra X-ray observatory. The optical spectrum of CXO 52 was obtained by Keck I telescope. Line profile FWHM are broad ( 1000 ). A shock dominated model with =1500 and pre-shock density 0=110 3, resembling those found in other galaxies at similar redshifts, fits most of the UV lines (MSS Table 4).

The and the forbidden lines [ OIII ] 5007+ correspond to lower FWHM (170-430 ). The lines are emitted from clouds within the galaxy with different . A radiation dominated model (MSR) with =200 and 0=350 3 reproduces the UV lines (except OVI and NV) within a factor of 2 and explains the high [ OIII ] 5007+4959/ .

sources.summary: The paper uses CXO 52 as the high-equivalent-width Type II QSO template, exploring its emission-line uncertainties, colors, metallicity effects, and detectability at high redshift.

quotes.quote: Only the Stern QSO, CXO 52 ( z=3.288 ) provides all the requirements for constructing a Type II QSO spectrum in the UV with high equivalent width emission lines. The emission line properties they fit are shown in Table tab:emlines .

Because of the uncertain equivalent widths of the metal emission lines in CXO 52, the effect of varying them is explored. This may also be considered a change in the overall metallicity, although it ignores any effect changing the metallicity may have on the thermodynamics of the emitting gas and hence the emission line strengths. For (z_ 850 J_ 110 )_ AB <1.8 , boosting the metallicity by 50 renders the Type II QSO bluer in (J_ 110 H_ 160 )_ AB , while it is reddened for (z_ 850 J_ 110 )_ AB >1.8 . Decreasing the metallicity by 50 has the opposite effect. The effect on the z_ 850 dropouts is small, as shown in Figure fig:zJ-JH .
The emission line flux ratios for CXO 52 are consistent with the photoionization models presented in Table 6 of Kwan Krolik (1981) for clouds exposed to an AGN spectrum.

gmw_00009768.name: UGC 07593
gmw_00009768.overview: UGC 07593 is a spiral galaxy in a relatively late merger stage with its companion, Mrk 212 [arXiv:1801.03981, arXiv:2010.14914]. The galaxy features ultraviolet star-forming knots, kiloparsec-scale tidal tails, and an optical spectrum characteristic of a star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2010.14914].

sources.summary: The paper presents the first dynamical model of UGC 07593, estimating its late merger stage, time since first passage, pericentric distance, eccentricity, and disk inclinations.

quotes.quote: We found a first ever model for UGC 07593, though the dynamical modeling method we used did not well converge for the other systems. Here we present the encounter parameters we found for UGC 07593 and discuss briefly the possible reasons for the failure of our method for some of the other systems. UGC 07593 Figure fig:obssys:ugc07593scoremap shows slices of the map of scores across three encounter parameters, eccentricity, pericentric distance, and time since pericenter.

This system is in a relatively late stage at 0.68 ^ 0.08 _ 0.01 of the time between the first and the second passages. The reconstructed pericentric distance and eccentricity are 0.50 ^ 0.16 _ 0.16 R_ vir and 0.8 ^ 0.05 _ 0.05 , respectively. Taking the physical length and velocity scaling into account we derive the encounter parameters with physical units (See Mortazavi:2016hv ). The reconstructed time since the first passage is 27 ^ 30 _ 3 Myr. The physical pericentric distance is 2.5 ^ 0.6 _ 0.7 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Source 2 as Mrk 212's companion spiral galaxy, with a compact radio core, star-forming optical spectrum, UV knots, tidal tails, and uncertain AGN association.

quotes.quote: It is a double-pinwheel galaxy (UGC 07593 NED02) where the companion galaxy is UGC 07593 NED01. In this paper, we refer to Mrk 212 as Source 1 (S1) and its companion as Source 2 (S2) (see Figure sdss ). S1 has a redshift ( z ) of 0.022893 0.000133 rc1991 S2 has a z of 0.022352 0.000063.

Both sources are spiral galaxies of type Sb and Sbc. The SDSS image shows that the inner arms of each galaxy are nearly merged, while the outer arms appear to be unaffected (Figure sdss ). mezcua2014 have carried out a photometric analysis of this system and obtained -band magnitudes of 21.77 and 22.86 respectively, for S1 and S2 respectively. There is an SDSS optical spectrum of S2 and it is identified as a star-forming galaxy by SDSS DR12 https://www.sdss.org/dr12/ .

New deep FUV and NUV observations with the Ultraviolet Imaging Telescope (UVIT) aboard AstroSat reveal SF knots around S2 as well as kpc-scale tidal tails; the SF knots around S2 coincide with the extended radio structure detected at 8.5 GHz.

gmw_00009769.name: Pox 36
gmw_00009769.overview: Pox 36 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy located approximately 20 Mpc away [arXiv:0812.2911]. It features strong optical emission lines [arXiv:0812.2911] and contains metal-deficient neutral gas with a metallicity significantly lower than its ionized gas [arXiv:0812.2911, arXiv:1010.2154].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes FUSE spectra of the blue compact dwarf Pox 36, finding metal-deficient neutral gas, B0-dominated FUV light, and ionized-gas abundances around one-fifth solar.

quotes.quote: The object we analyze is Pox 36 (also known as ESO 572--G 034, I SZ 63, HIPASSJ1158-19b, or IRAS 11564--1844), an emission-line galaxy first studied by Rodgers et al. \ (1978) and then rediscovered by Kunth et al. \ (1981). It is characterized by strong optical emission-lines superimposed on a very blue continuum. Its spectral characteristics and morphological properties fit the definition of a BCD galaxy (see e.g., Thuan Martin 1981, Kunth Ostlin 2000).

In particular, chemical abundances in the ionized gas of the H \ regions within Pox 36 have been derived by several independent studies (e.g., Kunth Sargent 1983; Izotov Thuan 2004). The ionized gas has an oxygen abundance 12+ (O/H) 8.05 which corresponds to Z _ /5. With a H \ velocity of 1114 km s ^ 1 (Meyer et al. \ 2004), a Hubble constant of 73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , its cosmology-corrected distance is 20 Mpc.
Our results suggest that the neutral gas of Pox 36 is metal-deficient by a factor 7 as compared to the ionized gas, and they agree with a metallicity of 1/35 Z _ .

sources.summary: The review discusses Pox 36 as a BCD where stellar-contaminated FUV lines require modelling and metal-mixing calculations imply limited enrichment of the neutral gas.

quotes.quote: As an illustration, the H \ lines from the Lyman series in Mrk 59 and Pox 36 show "V-shaped" wings typical from B stars photospheres. While Thuan et al. \ (2002) circumvented the problem by considering an artificial continuum to fit the profile of the H \ lines in Mark 59, it is also possible to model the stellar absorption. In their study of Pox 36, Lebouteiller et al. \ (2009) used the TLUSTY models for O and B stars (Lanz Hubeny 2003; Lanz Hubeny 2007) to reproduce the synthetic spectral continuum.

Given the large mass of atomic hydrogen, the metallicity of the neutral gas would increase by 5 (calculation made for Pox 36). Only a fraction of the metals mixes within the H \ gas, and the remaining mix within the H \ gas. It is possible that the H \ close to the clusters is enriched first. Lebouteiller et al. \ (2009) calculated that if metals mix with 10 of the H \ in Pox 36, the enriched H \ would acquire the metallicity observed in the ionized gas of the H \ regions.

In order to reproduce the observed abundance discontinuity between the 2 phases, the fraction of metals mixing in the neutral gas has to satisfy to the condition: f_n = 1 1 + 10^ _ H I M( H II ) M( H I ) , where M (H ) and M (H ) are the masses of the ionized gas and neutral gas respectively (see Lebouteiller et al. \ 2009) and where _ H I is the abundance discontinuity between the ionized gas and neutral gas as defined in Sect. sec:results . In Pox 36, _ H I 10 translates into f_n of about 1 . In I Zw 18, _ H I 0 (following the result of Lecavelier et al. \ 2004) translates into a fraction f_n of 5 . The large difference in behavior between f_n and _ H I in these 2 galaxies is due to the fact that the ratio of ionized gas mass over neutral gas mass is much larger in I Zw 18 than in Pox 36.

gmw_00009770.name: BG1429+1202
gmw_00009770.overview: BG1429+1202 is a quadruply lensed Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a systemic redshift of z = 2.8224 [arXiv:1702.00989]. The galaxy is gravitationally magnified by a total factor of 8.8 and has an effective radius of approximately 1.28 kiloparsecs [arXiv:1702.00989].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BG1429+1202 as a quadruply lensed Ly-alpha emitter at z=2.8224, measuring strong UV/Ly-alpha luminosity, modest dust, high star formation, and large lensing magnification.

quotes.quote: The lensed image pair, A and B, is identified in SDSS as a single source, SDSS J142954.88+120238.3 (hereafter BG1429+1202 for the lensed LAE, where BG stands for BELLS GALLERY), showing blue colors in DECaLS and SDSS bands (Table tab1 ). The lensed A and B images are also detected in the UKIRT Infrared Deep Sky Survey UKIDSS: lawrence Large Area Survey (LAS), only in Y-band with 21.06 0.19 mag and 21.12 0.20 mag, respectively.

We identified several photospheric absorption features, from which we derived the systemic redshift z_ sys = 2.8224 0.0013 using the cleanest among them: O iv 1343 , and a close blend of C ii and N iii at 1324 . The Ly emission line has an observed (A + B) flux of F_ Ly = (2.1 0.3) 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , much higher than the Ly flux measured within the 1'' radius BOSS fibre ( F_ Ly ^ BOSS = 0.256 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ).

For the source, the lens model gives local magnifications of 3.2, 3.1, 1.8 and 0.7 for images A, B, C and D, respectively, which means that the total magnification is 8.8 0.4 . The source has an effective radius R_ eff = 0 159 0 007, which corresponds to R_ eff = 1.28 0.06 kpc for the adopted cosmology. The source has a minor-to-major axis ratio q = 0.56 and S e rsic index n = 3.9 .

gmw_00009771.name: SDSS J142954.80+120235.6
gmw_00009771.overview: SDSS J142954.80+120235.6 is a massive early-type galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.5531 [arXiv:1702.00989]. It acts as a gravitational lens, producing a four-image fold configuration of a background Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:1912.04033, arXiv:1702.00989].

sources.summary: The paper re-observes BG1429+1202, a four-image lensed LAE magnified by 8.8, deriving z_sys=2.8244 and favoring a stellar metallicity around 0.4-0.6 solar.

quotes.quote: We also re-observed BG1429+1202, discussed already in see also rigby2017a, chisholm2019 , this time with higher spectral resolution and deeper imaging. This system comprises four images in the observer plane with a total magnification of 8.8 marques2017 , forming a fold configuration: a bright lensed image pair, A and B (with a separation of 1.5^ ), and two fainter images, C and D (see Figure fig:2rgb ). The GTC long slit was positioned so as to encompass the two brightest lensed images A and B.

The new spectroscopic observations of BG1429+2102 allow us to identify several photospheric absorption features, C iii 1247 , Si iii 1294,1298 , the close blend of C ii and N iii 1324 , S v 1501 , and N iv 1718 , and redefine the systemic redshift of BG1429+1202 as z_ sys = 2.8244 0.0006 , similar to the one measured by ( z_ sys = 2.8241 0.0006 ). Emission in nebular lines of O iii ] 1666 and C iii ] 1906,1908 is also detected, but with low significance. Fine-structure emission lines Si ii* 1264 , Si ii* 1309 , and Si ii* 1533 are also clearly detected and their peak lie at z_ FS = 2.8243 0.0008 , consistent with the systemic redshift derived using photospheric absorption lines (see top right panel of Figure fig:fs ).

On the other hand, the spectrum of BG1429+1202 is better explained by a model with stellar metallicity of Z_ * / Z_ 0.4 . It is worth noting that a slightly large metallicity of Z_ * / Z_ 0.6 was inferred recently by for BG1429+1202.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J142954.80+120235.6 as the z=0.5531 early-type foreground lens whose Sersic light and SIE mass model produce the observed lensed images and external shear.

quotes.quote: By visual inspection of SDSS and DECaLS Dark Energy Camera Legacy Survey: http://legacysurvey.org/decamls/ color images of the BELLS GALLERY sample, we found one candidate showing bluish features 3 3 from the z=0.5531 massive early-type galaxy, SDSS J142954.80+120235.6 (hereafter ETG). Its BOSS spectrum (Plate-MJD-Fiber: 5463-56003-121) shows a secondary emission line at 4652 Ly at z = 2.8253 ; shu_a . The positions of the bluish features with respect to the ETG are consistent with a fold lensing configuration: a bright lensed image pair, A and B (with a separation of 1 5), and two fainter images, C and D (see Fig. fig:image ).

The foreground-light is modeled using the elliptical S e rsic profile and similar to its subtraction is performed jointly with the lens modeling. The lens model includes a mass distribution of the foreground lens parameterized as a singular isothermal ellipsoid (SIE), and an additional external shear is included to model the higher-order effect from the environment.

The foreground-light model is combined with the predicted lensed images and convolved with the point-spread function (PSF) which was modeled with a star in the GTC/OSIRIS g band field-of-view. The best fit lens model ( ^ 2 / dof = 3494/3709 ) predicts for the lens an Einstein radius b_ SIE = 2 95 0 10, minor-to-major axis ratio q = 0.34 and a position angle P.A. = 165.5 deg.

gmw_00009772.name: IC 4745
gmw_00009772.overview: IC 4745 is a galaxy characterized by a thick, boxy bulge that exhibits a highly irregular morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/0401057]. Its inner bulge system is asymmetric relative to the disk, and the outer region contains a structure that may represent a dissolving satellite galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0401057].

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 4745 as a prototypical thick-boxy-bulge galaxy, emphasizing irregular morphology, asymmetric bulge and disk structure, possible merger remnants, satellites, and recent accretion.

quotes.quote: IC 4745 (Fig. 4745ccd ) is one of the most prominent member of the galaxies with a TBB and shows a lot of irregularities. Therefore it is surprising that Bell Whitmore ( ) do not mention its peculiar structure. The inner bulge system is asymmetric with respect to the disk and the outer part of the bulge (Dettmar , Dettmar Barteldrees ) as shown by a twist of the isophotes visible in NIR images (L utticke ). The boxiness is most pronounced at lower surface brightness levels. Similar to NGC 1055 the disk is on one side more extended and separated from the bulge, while on the other side bulge and disk are merged at larger radial distances.

It can be associated with a possible merger remnant. Perhaps related to this structure is a further feature which is in the outer region of the galaxy at (R,z) = ( 55 , 35 ). A dissolving satellite could be an explanation for this structure.

gmw_00009773.name: G1
gmw_00009773.overview: G1 is an elliptical companion galaxy located approximately 11 kiloparsecs from a nearby quasar host [arXiv:astro-ph/0405309]. The galaxy lacks emission lines, and it likely experienced a recent close interaction with the host that swept out its interstellar medium [arXiv:astro-ph/0405309].

sources.summary: The paper identifies G1 as the closest companion galaxy to the quasar host, with an elliptical profile, shared group environment, no emission lines, and a likely recent interaction with the host.

quotes.quote: A close companion, labeled G1 on Fig. field , can be seen next to , located 4 from the center, or 11 kpc. Its redshift (see Section neighb ) is the same as that of . A ^ 1/4 law fits well its 1D intensity profile, while an exponential disk does not. Moreover, its shape is that of an elliptical galaxy.

We propose that the ISM of both galaxies (host and G1) has been swept out during a recent close interaction and has had insufficient time to relax. Using the velocity dispersion in the group as an estimate of the relative velocity of the two interacting galaxies, we estimate that it would have taken 3 10^7 years for the two galaxies to reach their present projected separation.

gmw_00009774.name: NGC547
gmw_00009774.overview: NGC547 is a luminous radio galaxy located in the nearby poor cluster Abell 194 [arXiv:1106.3434]. The active galactic nucleus of NGC547 hosts a giant southern radio lobe that has formed a large, 34-kiloparsec X-ray cavity [arXiv:1106.3434].

sources.summary: The paper places NGC547/3C 40B in Abell 194 and links its giant southern radio lobe to a 34 kpc X-ray cavity with estimated age, work, and cavity power.

quotes.quote: Based on Chandra and ROSAT observations, we investigated the nearby poor cluster Abell 194, which hosts two luminous radio galaxies, NGC547 (3C 40B) and NGC541 (3C 40A). We demonstrated the presence of a large X-ray cavity ( 34 kpc) formed by the giant southern radio lobe arising from 3C 40B in NGC547. The estimated age of the cavity is t=7.9 10^7 years and the total work of the AGN is 3.3 10^ 59 \ erg , hence the cavity power is P_ cav =1.3 10^ 44 \ erg \ s^ 1 .

gmw_00009775.name: NGC545
gmw_00009775.overview: NGC545 is an early-type galaxy located within the Abell 194 cluster [arXiv:1106.3434]. Chandra observations reveal that the galaxy exhibits sharp surface brightness edges on its north-eastern side and possesses an extended tail [arXiv:1106.3434].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC545 has Chandra surface-brightness edges and an extended tail in Abell 194, using surface-brightness profiles to demonstrate the cold-front-like structure.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies in Abell 194, namely NGC545 and NGC541, exhibit sharp surface brightness edges and extended tails in the soft band Chandra images (Fig. fig:edge upper panels). The edges are located on the north-eastern and western side of NGC545 and NGC541, respectively. To demonstrate the existence of the edges and tails, we extracted surface brightness profiles of the galaxies using the regions displayed in the upper panels of Fig. fig:edge .

gmw_00009776.name: host galaxy of GRB 191019A
gmw_00009776.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 191019A is an ancient stellar system at a redshift of z=0.248 [arXiv:2303.12912]. It features a very compact core, Balmer absorption lines, little star formation, and a high nuclear stellar interaction rate [arXiv:2303.12912].

sources.summary: The paper describes GRB 191019A’s host as an old z=0.248 galaxy with a nuclear burst location, compact core, Balmer absorption, little star formation, and high dynamical interaction rate.

quotes.quote: Here we report the case of GRB 191019A, a long GRB ( T_ 90 = 64.4 4.5 s) which we pinpoint close ( 100 pc projected) to the nucleus of an ancient ( >1 Gyr old) host galaxy at z=0.248 . The lack of evidence for star formation and deep limits on any supernova emission make a massive star origin difficult to reconcile with observations, while the timescales of the emission rule out a direct interaction with the supermassive black hole in the nucleus of the galaxy, We suggest that the most likely route for progenitor formation is via dynamical interactions in the dense nucleus of the host, consistent with the centres of such galaxies exhibiting interaction rates up to two orders of magnitude larger than typical field galaxies .

However, the nuclear location of the GRB on its host galaxy differs from compact object merger expectations. Systems formed via standard stellar evolution channels involve two supernovae; at each supernova, the combination of natal kicks and those induced from mass loss combined to give the binary a substantial (50--500 km s ^ 1 ) systemic velocity. Indeed, no short GRB with sub-arcsecond localisation is consistent with the nucleus of its host galaxy . We suggest instead that the binary which created GRB 191019A formed via dynamical interactions in the dense nucleus of its host galaxy. Dynamical channels for compact object formation may be due to many-body interactions in dense stellar systems such as globular clusters or nuclear star clusters in galaxies . Alternatively, they may also form at a significantly enhanced rate in the gaseous discs that surround supermassive black holes . The host galaxy of GRB 191019A appears similar to those that preferentially host tidal disruption events, with a very compact core and Balmer absorption lines. The Lick indices for H in absorption and H in emission are 1.54^ +1.44 _ 0.74 and 2.51^ +1.81 _ 2.51 , consistent with those of the TDE population which make up only 2 of SDSS galaxies, but 75 of the TDE hosts . The TDE rate effectively measures the stellar interaction rate close to the black hole. Scattering events are responsible for placing stars on paths which cross closer than the tidal radius for the star around the SMBH. The preference for TDEs in galaxies of certain types is related directly to their dense stellar environments, and interaction rates . At face value, then, the host galaxy of GRB 191019A may have a dynamical interaction rate one to two orders of magnitude larger than typical galaxies.

gmw_00009777.name: PKS 1229-021
gmw_00009777.overview: PKS 1229-021 is a jetted gamma-ray quasar located at a redshift of z=1.043 [arXiv:1201.5395]. It is hosted by a central cluster galaxy surrounded by hot intracluster gas, with X-ray emission consistent with a cool-core cluster environment [arXiv:1201.5395].

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1229-021 as a z=1.043 jetted gamma-ray quasar with compact and extended radio structure, extended Chandra X-ray emission, and a cool-core cluster host environment.

quotes.quote: The bright quasar PKS 1229-021 (4C 02.55) at redshift z=1.043 is also a jetted radio source acting as a powerful gamma-ray source (Thompson et al. 1995; Celotti Ghisellini 2008). On parsec-scales VLBA images show a strong compact core and a 10 mas jet to the west (Kovalev et al. 2007). Its extended radio structure was mapped at several frequencies with VLA by Kronberg et al (1992). They attribute the observed variations in Faraday rotation measure across the source as due to an intervening absorber, having discounted the possibility that it is embedded in a cool core cluster on the grounds of the relaxed appearance for the radio source. A short ( 19 ) archival Chandra image suggested the presence of extended X-ray emission (Tavecchio et al. 2007).

Here we report on a 100 Chandra observation of the object clearly showing X-ray emission extended in a circular symmetric manner around the quasar. It is indeed hosted by a central cluster galaxy surrounded by hot intracluster gas. The hardness ratio of the X-ray emission drops towards the centre consistent with the cluster having a cool core.

gmw_00009778.name: NGC 6621/22
gmw_00009778.overview: NGC 6621/22 is an interacting galaxy system undergoing a merger [arXiv:0708.2645]. The circumnuclear region of NGC 6621 exhibits a complex morphology in the infrared, whereas NGC 6622 contains a simpler, single concentrated nuclear source [arXiv:0708.2645].

sources.summary: The paper contrasts the complex infrared circumnuclear region of NGC 6621 with the simpler, single concentrated nuclear source identified in NGC 6622.

quotes.quote: The nucleus of NGC 6621 is detected in the infrared, and has a fainter counterpart in the optical images (see Figure f:sixtysixtwentyonevk ). The circumnuclear region has a complex morphology, even in the K band. In contrast, the nuclear region of NGC 6622 appears much less complex, showing only a single concentrated source which we identify as the nucleus (Figure f:sixtysixtwentytwovk ).

gmw_00009779.name: cE _ AGN3
gmw_00009779.overview: The galaxy cE _ AGN3 is classified as an isolated compact elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2107.10289]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus and is located far from the nearest group, making its formation via tidal stripping and subsequent ejection highly unlikely [arXiv:2107.10289].

sources.summary: The paper treats cE _ AGN3 as an isolated compact elliptical AGN whose location makes ejection from a group through tidal stripping unlikely.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, cE _ AGN3 , which is listed as a member of a very small group with only two members, is located at a distance of 5 Mpc (three-dimensional) and 0.4 Mpc (projected) from the nearest group. If the cE _ AGN3 was formed through a stripping process at a very early epoch, followed by ejection from a group with a velocity comparable to the velocity dispersion of the group ( 30 ), it would take longer than a Hubble time for the cE _ AGN3 to move to its current location. Unless cE _ AGN3 is an abnormal high-speed object on an extremely eccentric orbit Sales2007,Paudel2016 , we can rule out the possibility that cE _ AGN3 is an object ejected from a group.

gmw_00009780.name: galaxy A
gmw_00009780.overview: Galaxy A is the most massive member of a compact galaxy group [arXiv:2203.05614]. Its neighboring group members, including galaxies B and C, are located within a projected distance of 50 kiloparsecs and are aligned with galaxy A's major axis [arXiv:2203.05614].

sources.summary: Galaxy A is the most massive group member; the other galaxies are labeled by distance from it, and nearby members are compactly arranged and aligned around it.

quotes.quote: where galaxy A is the most massive one (see Sect. sec:32 ). The other letters (from B to E) are attributed to galaxies depending on their projected distance to galaxy A (from the closest to the furthest, see middle panel of Fig. fig:group_presentation ). We note that galaxy B has a lower redshift confidence level (ZCONF=1) than the other group members in the MUSE UDF catalog (Bacon et al. in prep). Based on a careful analysis of the \ NB image (see Sect. sec:51 ) -- where \ emission is clearly detected at the position of galaxy B when setting low flux cuts -- and the rather well constrained photometric redshift (1.25 < z_ BPZ < 1.47), we are confident about the redshift of this source and therefore about its group membership. The group members are located within 50 kpc in projection and 120 in redshift space (middle and right panels of Fig. fig:group_presentation ). The projected distances and velocity offsets from galaxy A are (9 kpc, + 43 ), (13 kpc, 76 ), (37 kpc, + 2 ) and (41 kpc, + 43 ) for galaxies B, C, D and E, respectively (see right panel of Fig. fig:group_presentation and Table tab:gal_prop ). Galaxies B and C are aligned with the major axis of galaxy A.

sources.summary: Galaxy B is a newly detected, lower-confidence group member whose redshift is supported by Mg emission and photometric-redshift constraints near galaxy A.
