gmw_00009781.name: galaxy C
gmw_00009781.overview: Galaxy C is located at a projected distance of 9 kpc from galaxy A, aligned along its major axis [arXiv:2203.05614]. The galaxy also spatially coincides with the strongest magnesium emission detected within the surrounding nebula [arXiv:2203.05614].

sources.summary: Galaxy C is close to galaxy A, aligned with its major axis along with galaxy B, and coincides spatially with the strongest Mg emission in the nebula.

quotes.quote: The projected distances and velocity offsets from galaxy A are (9 kpc, + 43 ), (13 kpc, 76 ), (37 kpc, + 2 ) and (41 kpc, + 43 ) for galaxies B, C, D and E, respectively (see right panel of Fig. fig:group_presentation and Table tab:gal_prop ). Galaxies B and C are aligned with the major axis of galaxy A.
The strongest \ emission coincides spatially with galaxy C.

gmw_00009782.name: galaxy D
gmw_00009782.overview: Galaxy D is a member of a compact, interacting five-galaxy group [arXiv:2203.05614]. It is aligned with the major axis of its neighbor, galaxy E, and is surrounded by an extended, magnesium-enriched intragroup medium that exhibits offset nebular emission nearby [arXiv:2203.05614].

sources.summary: Galaxy D lies within the compact group, aligns with galaxy E, has offset nebular emission nearby, and shows redshifted Mg-line hints in its spectrum.

quotes.quote: The projected distances and velocity offsets from galaxy A are (9 kpc, + 43 ), (13 kpc, 76 ), (37 kpc, + 2 ) and (41 kpc, + 43 ) for galaxies B, C, D and E, respectively (see right panel of Fig. fig:group_presentation and Table tab:gal_prop ). Galaxies B and C are aligned with the major axis of galaxy A. Similarly, galaxy D is aligned with the major axis of galaxy E.

Interestingly, while \ emission lines are detected in the integrated spectra of the galaxies D and E, the \ map shows no flux at the location of galaxy E and offset emission near galaxy D. This can be caused by the galaxy's absorption being completely filled by the emission (e.g. Zabl21 ). The spectra of the galaxies D and E indeed show hints of low S/N absorption lines which are within the \ NB spectral bandwidth (see Fig. fig:integ_sp ). We also note that the segmentation map of galaxy D extends beyond the stellar body of the galaxy (Fig. fig:integ_sp ). This implies that the \ emission observed in the spectrum of galaxy D likely originates from circumgalactic regions (as seen in the \ map in Fig. fig:nb_mgii a). The ODHIN spectrum of BKG (z 1.8463, see Fig. fig:group_presentation ) which is located outside the detected \ emission nebula and at a projected distance of 19 kpc from galaxy D, shows \ absorption at the same redshift as the nebula (see Fig. fig:nb_mgii ). Such a detection indicates that the \ gas even extends beyond the detected \ emission nebula. Using the line fitting procedure described in Sect. sec:33 , we measured an \ 2796 rest frame EW of 0.5 ^ +0.6 _ 0.4 \ for the absorption. This measurement falls well on the \ EW versus impact parameter relation found in several absorption studies (e.g. Lundgren21 ). Using the relation between the \ column density and the rest frame \ 2796 EW we obtained the rough estimate of 10 ^ 19 atoms cm ^ 2 for the extended gas column density in this line of sight. We refrain from analysing the absorption further because of the low S/N of the absorption lines. The spatially resolved MUSE view of the nebula also reveals that the \ distribution is different from that of the continuum: the \ gas is more extended than the stellar content of the galaxies (see Sect. sec:43 ) and a projected bridge seems to link galaxy subgroups [ A,B,C ] and [ D,E ] (see Sect. sec:43 ). This bridge might not be a projection effect because of its tentative detection (S/N 2) in the single 1.25 (58 km s ^ 1 ) cube slice between +54 and +112 km s ^ 1 relative to the systemic redshift of galaxy A (bottom left panel of Fig. fig:NBlayer_mgii ). We can also mention that the \ spatial distribution is not homogeneous in between the galaxies: several intensity peaks are detected at S/N>3. They suggest the presence of gas clumps or satellite galaxies (undetected in the HST and MUSE data) residing in the IGrM (see discussion in Sect. sec:621 ). Radial \ surface brightness profile In order to highlight the extended nature of the \ emission around and in between the galaxy group members, we computed azimuthally averaged radial surface brightness profiles of the \ and continuum emission centered on galaxy A (Fig. fig:sbprof_mgii , first panel). The \ profile (blue) is measured on the unsmoothed \ NB image (Sect. sec:41 ) after masking pixels located outside an ellipse corresponding to the 1 significance level in order to increase the S/N in the two-pixel-wide annuli (or truncated annuli) used for the aperture photometry (see inset panels). The width of the annuli (0.4") is comparable to the spatial resolution element of the data (PSF FWHM of 0.52"). The \ continuum radial SB profile (black) is measured from a continuum image extracted from the continuum-only cube within a window of 20 000 around the \ doublet. This broad spectral window does not include strong absorption or emission lines and ensures a good enough S/N. The neighboring sources were rigorously masked in order to avoid contamination and the same elliptical mask used on the \ image was applied to compute the continuum SB profile. Errors were measured in each (truncated) annulus using the estimated variance from the MUSE data cube. To aid the visual comparison, the continuum profile has been rescaled to the \ SB at the position of galaxy C which is where the brightest \ intensity peak is observed. Galaxy C is unlikely to be affected by \ absorption as its measured \ line ratio 2796 / 2803 is very close to two, which is the expected value for optically thin emission (see Appendix ap:3 ). Moreover, no fluorescent \ lines are significantly detected for galaxy C which is in good agreement with no infilling effect and therefore no absorption (see Appendix ap:3 , Mauerhofer21 ). The \ SB profile appears more extended than the continuum, especially in between the galaxies [ A,B,C ] and [ D,E ] - radial positions are indicated by vertical dashed lines -- where a low SB projected "bridge" at 2 10 ^ 19 level can be identified. In order to differentiate between the extended emission around the galaxy subgroup [ A,B,C ] and in between the galaxy subgroups [ A,B,C ] and [ D,E ] , we compute the radial SB profiles above and below the [ A,B,C ] axis (see insets for illustration) in the middle and right panels of Fig. fig:sbprof_mgii , respectively. The \ emission appears more extended than the rescaled continuum in both directions (above and below the [ A,B,C ] axis), confirming the detection of diffuse \ emission around the group members as well as a low SB \ bridge ( 2 10 ^ 19 ) detected with more than 1.5 significance spanning 50 kpc in projection between the galaxy subgroups [ A,B,C ] and [ D,E ] (see Fig. fig:nb_mgii a). We go further into the analysis of the \ nebula in the next section by looking at the spatial variation of the \ spectral properties. Spatially resolved \ properties The kinematics of the gas connecting the galaxy group can provide crucial information to understand its nature and origin. In order to reveal the spatial variations of the \ spectral profile within the nebula, we build a 2D binned map using the weighted Voronoi tesselation method C03,Diehl&Statler06 . This method allows us to increase the S/N in the surroundings of the galaxies where the surface brightness is the lowest. The binning is performed on the \ NB image constructed from the MXDF datacube (see Sect. sec:41 ) for which the pixels with S/N < 1.5 (corresponding to the outer SB contour in Fig. fig:nb_mgii a) have been masked in order to remove the noise-dominated pixels. The resulting binned \ map consists of 20 bins with S/N > 3 and probes the detected \ nebula above 2 10 ^ 19 . The spectral extraction is performed from the MUSE continuum-subtracted cube smoothed with a 0.6 \ FWHM Gaussian in order to increase the S/N while keeping a good spatial resolution (see Appendix ap:1 ). We extract the \ doublet in each resulting bin and measure the properties of the emission lines by modelling the doublet as a sum of two Gaussian profiles with fixed peak separation. The errors on the line parameters (peak position and FWHM) were estimated using bootstrapping: for each segmented area, we generated 1000 realizations of the extracted line where each pixel is randomly drawn from a normal distribution centered on the original pixel value and with standard deviation derived from the estimated noise value. The noise of each extracted spectrum corresponds to the standard deviation of the data measured in two 200 wide spectral windows located on each side of the \ doublet. We note that because of smoothing and because of the PSF the line parameters between neighboring pixels are somewhat correlated. However, spatial variations are still visible. Figure fig:velmap_mgii (top left panel) shows the resolved map of the \ 2796 peak position relative to the systemic redshift of galaxy A. The observed \ velocity gradient suggests an overall rotation of the structure along the major axis of galaxy A consistent with an extension of the ISM rotation (as probed by the \ emission, see Sect. sec:51 ) at the CGM scale within which the satellite galaxies B and C are embedded. This ISM/CGM co-rotation is also observed in simulations and observations (e.g. S19,Zabl19,Ho20,Zabl21 ). The top right panels of Fig. fig:velmap_mgii show the unsmoothed \ doublet extracted in the elliptical areas traced in purple and designated by the same number on the map. These apertures are independent of the Voronoi bins and aim to show the \ line profile in some diffuse regions of the nebula. The velocity variations are small (< 50 ) in the diffuse regions. The kinematics of the nebula appear to be overall shifted towards higher velocities (by + 50 km s ^ 1 on average) compared to the \ emission (bottom left panel, see Sect. sec:51 ). The highest velocity variations are observed along the minor axis of galaxy A and reach +120 and 80 with respect to the systemic redshift of galaxy A. As discussed in Sect. sec:621 ), this supports the presence of an outflow emerging from galaxy A. 0.29 0.71 0.29 0.71 At the redshift of the group, MUSE covers the non-resonant collisionally excited \ doublet and the fluorescent \ lines. The corresponding NB images and radial SB profiles are shown in Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb and are created following the same procedure as for the \ emission (Sects. sec:41 and sec:43 , respectively). A low surface brightness \ nebula The \ NB map (Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb , top left) is obtained by summing the continuum-subtracted cube in the wavelength range [ 8590, 8605 ] ( 500 ). In order to improve the S/N, the image was smoothed using a 0 6 FWHM Gaussian function. The white contours correspond to the 1.5, 2, and 3 significance levels with 1 corresponding to 2.5 10^ 19 . The total \ flux and luminosity within the 2 significance level contour (dashed white line) are 5.95 0.07 10^ 17 \ and 6.36 0.07 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 , respectively. The \ azimuthally averaged radial SB profiles are shown in the lower left panels of Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb . The profiles are constructed following the same procedure as for \ (see Sect. sec:43 ) with an additional step for the construction of the continuum image where the cube slices within 20 000 km s ^ 1 polluted by skylines have been masked. This is particularly important because at the redshift of the nebula, the \ line falls next to many sky lines. The \ doublet is itself contaminated by a skyline (see vertical grey line in the lower right panels of Fig. fig:velmap_mgii ) preventing us from in-depth analysis. When comparing the \ and rescaled continuum radial SB profiles in Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb , only one data point is above the rescaled continuum profile with more than 2 significance. The inner profile has the same shape as the continuum one implying that the \ emission is not significantly more extended than the continuum inside a radius of 2 \ from galaxy A. Extended \ emission at the same position as the \ bridge, i.e. in between the galaxies [ A,B,C ] and [ D,E ] , is detected with 2 significance level in the \ NB image (Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb , top left). However, due to averaging effects, the \ bridge is less significant on the radial profiles computed below the [ A,B,C ] axis, i.e. in the direction of the [ D,E ] subgroup (first column third row of Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb ). The radial SB profile computed above the galaxies A, B and C (bottom left panel) indicates extended \ emission at radii > 2.5 \ from galaxy A, suggesting the presence of an ionized \ halo around this galaxy subgroup. Comparisons of the \ and \ spatial extent and radial SB profiles are presented in Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb (top two right panels). Since the MUSE PSF is narrower at the \ wavelength than at the \ wavelength, we degraded the \ NB image by convolving it by a Moffat function whose parameters ( a=0.22 and b=2.87 , see B17 ) have been empirically determined so that the \ and \ images have the same PSF. While the \ and \ spatial distributions are consistent, the \ radial SB profiles (azimuthally averaged and in directions, bottom right panels) are all flatter than the \ profiles. This can be attributed to the resonant nature of the \ transition (see Sect. sec:622 ). The binned line-of-sight velocity map of the \ emission is shown in the bottom left panel of Fig. fig:velmap_mgii . The map is constructed following the same procedure as for \ (Sect. sec:44 ) and consists of more bins (57) because of the higher S/N of the \ emission near the galaxies. At the position of the galaxies, the observed spatial variations of the \ peak positions are consistent with their redshift relative to galaxy A (right panel of Fig. fig:group_presentation ). The lower right panels of Fig. fig:velmap_mgii show the unsmoothed \ lines extracted in the same elliptical apertures as for \ (top panels). Interestingly, in region 4 the \ emission is stronger than . This can be explained by the resonant nature of the \ transition (see Sect. sec:622 ) resulting in \ being more extended that . The existence of the \ bridge is reinforced by the detection of the \ doublet in this area (region 3 in Fig. fig:velmap_mgii ). 0.94 Extended \ emission The \ NB image (top middle panel of Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb ) is obtained by summing the 2365, 2396, 2612 and 2626 NB images, of which the observed spectral windows are [ 5448.75, 5456.25 ] , [ 5522.5, 5530.0 ] , [ 6021.25, 6027.5 ] and [ 6041.25, 6066.25 ] , respectively. The total \ flux and luminosity within the significance level contour of 2 is 5.3 0.5 10^ 18 \ and 5.7 0.6 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , respectively. Given that the \ and \ lines are close in wavelength, we consider the same stellar continuum (see Sect. sec:41 ). Therefore, the radial SB profiles of the continuum (black profile in the middle bottom panels) are the same as for . The comparison between the \ emission and the rescaled continuum radial SB profile reveals that the \ emission is more extended than the continuum (see central panel of Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb ) second row. The \ extended emission appears more significant in the direction of the galaxies D and E (compare the central panels of the Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb third and fourth rows). This is confirmed in the NB image where the \ emission indeed extends along the minor axis of galaxy A and towards galaxy D. We discuss the origin for the extended nature of the \ emission in Sect. sec:62 . Contrary to \ and \ emission, the \ emission does not surround the five-galaxy group but is rather centered on the most massive galaxy A (see the , \ and \ contours and radial SB profiles comparison in the last column of Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb ). Similarly, using MUSE found the \ emission of a z=1.29 galaxy to be more extended than the stellar continuum and aligned with the minor axis of the galaxy. The velocity gradient and shape of the \ emission suggest the presence of a conical outflow. The faintness of the \ emission of galaxy A hampers a kinematic analysis. Existence of an Mg-enriched intragroup medium Historically the existence of an intragroup medium enriched in magnesium has been suggested by absorption studies in order to explain the fact that, while the strong \ absorbers (rest frame equivalent width EW>0.8 ) are generally associated to a single galaxy (within 100 kpc, e.g. Bouche07, Bouche12, Ho17, Schroetter16, Schroetter19, Zabl19, Lundgren21 ), sometimes weaker \ absorbers (EW < 1 , e.g. Muzahid18,Du20 ) or \ absorbers (e.g. P17,Ra18 ) can be matched to groups of multiple galaxies ( < 500 kpc and v < 500 ), in agreement with clustering studies Bouche06,Lundgren09,Gauthier09 . Whether observed absorption features (with EW < 1 ) imprinted in the spectrum of a background source are tracing gas coupled to the group or circumgalactic gas of an individual galaxy is still a matter of debate. Indeed, absorption studies reported contradictory results, some suggesting that \ absorbing systems were associated with the CGM of the individual group members (e.g. B11,Fo19 ) and others to a widespread IGrM (e.g. Bi17, P17, N18 ) originating from a mixture of previous tidal interactions between group members and outflowing winds. Disentangling the two is very challenging, especially when there is only one-dimensional information for a given system. Our discovery of the first extended \ nebula surrounding and connecting the members of a 5-galaxy group provides a panoramic view of the neutral enriched gas residing in between galaxies. This group is rather small and compact (see Sect. sec:31 ), meaning that it is likely an interacting system where the CGM of individual galaxies are mixed. This idea is reinforced by the fact that the detected \ nebula resides inside the virial radius of galaxy A estimated at 100 kpc. Moreover, the detection of a gaseous bridge in between the two subgroups [ A,B,C ] and [ D,E ] suggests that we are observing a widespread low surface brightness structure embracing the five galaxies, also called intragroup medium. We note that there is no precise definition of the IGrM in the literature. Our analysis brings the first observational evidence for a low SB diffuse component of neutral gas residing within a galaxy group at z>1 that we define as the intragroup medium. The detection of \ absorption in the spectrum of the background source BKG (see Sect. sec:42 ) indicates that the IGrM is actually even more extended than the detected nebula. Our EW measurement of the \ absorption (Sect. sec:42 ) is in good agreement with the EW versus impact parameter relation established by quasar absorption line studies (e.g. Lundgren21 ). This result highlights the complementarity of the absorption and emission methods to study the gas around galaxies. Origin of the intragroup nebula In this section we discuss two points: the presence of enriched gas beyond the galaxies and the mechanisms that make it shine. Why is there enriched gas so far from the stars ? Several scenarios have been suggested to explain the presence of magnesium-enriched gas in between the galaxies of a group. Magnesium atoms are elements released in the ISM and CGM by core collapse supernovae. One can therefore naturally imagine that the magnesium gas residing outside galaxies has been ejected through strong supernovae-driven outflows. The P-Cygni shape of the \ doublet in the integrated spectrum of Galaxy A (see Fig. fig:integ_sp top) is in good agreement with this scenario. Indeed, according to radiative transfer (RT) models, this line profile is a signature of resonant scattering in an optically thick outflowing medium where the redshifted emission corresponds to back-scattered photons reflected by the receding medium H00,D06,V06,K10 . The \ lines of galaxies D and E seem to be redshifted compared to their systemic redshifts (Fig. fig:integ_sp ), however the continuum is too faint to detect significant absorption lines and therefore P-Cygni profiles, i.e. the presence of outflows for those galaxies.

gmw_00009783.name: galaxy E
gmw_00009783.overview: Galaxy E is a massive, edge-on group member aligned with galaxy D [arXiv:2203.05614]. Although magnesium emission lines are detected in its integrated spectrum, the corresponding emission map shows no flux at the galaxy's physical location [arXiv:2203.05614].

sources.summary: Galaxy E is an edge-on massive group member aligned with galaxy D; its spectrum shows Mg emission but the map lacks flux at its location.

quotes.quote: The two most massive galaxies (A and E) are seen edge-on with projected axis ratios of q = 0.329 0.005 and q = 0.247 0.015, respectively (corresponding to inclinations of 71 and 76 degrees, respectively, when considering a flat disk model) and a similar position angle PA 27 ^ (measurements from VDW12 in the WFC3/F105W band, see Table tab:gal_prop ). The three other galaxies have rounder shapes with q 0.7 and smaller sizes.

Interestingly, while \ emission lines are detected in the integrated spectra of the galaxies D and E, the \ map shows no flux at the location of galaxy E and offset emission near galaxy D. This can be caused by the galaxy's absorption being completely filled by the emission (e.g. Zabl21 ). The spectra of the galaxies D and E indeed show hints of low S/N absorption lines which are within the \ NB spectral bandwidth (see Fig. fig:integ_sp ). We also note that the segmentation map of galaxy D extends beyond the stellar body of the galaxy (Fig. fig:integ_sp ). This implies that the \ emission observed in the spectrum of galaxy D likely originates from circumgalactic regions (as seen in the \ map in Fig. fig:nb_mgii a). The ODHIN spectrum of BKG (z 1.8463, see Fig. fig:group_presentation ) which is located outside the detected \ emission nebula and at a projected distance of 19 kpc from galaxy D, shows \ absorption at the same redshift as the nebula (see Fig. fig:nb_mgii ). Such a detection indicates that the \ gas even extends beyond the detected \ emission nebula. Using the line fitting procedure described in Sect. sec:33 , we measured an \ 2796 rest frame EW of 0.5 ^ +0.6 _ 0.4 \ for the absorption. This measurement falls well on the \ EW versus impact parameter relation found in several absorption studies (e.g. Lundgren21 ). Using the relation between the \ column density and the rest frame \ 2796 EW we obtained the rough estimate of 10 ^ 19 atoms cm ^ 2 for the extended gas column density in this line of sight. We refrain from analysing the absorption further because of the low S/N of the absorption lines. The spatially resolved MUSE view of the nebula also reveals that the \ distribution is different from that of the continuum: the \ gas is more extended than the stellar content of the galaxies (see Sect. sec:43 ) and a projected bridge seems to link galaxy subgroups [ A,B,C ] and [ D,E ] (see Sect. sec:43 ). This bridge might not be a projection effect because of its tentative detection (S/N 2) in the single 1.25 (58 km s ^ 1 ) cube slice between +54 and +112 km s ^ 1 relative to the systemic redshift of galaxy A (bottom left panel of Fig. fig:NBlayer_mgii ). We can also mention that the \ spatial distribution is not homogeneous in between the galaxies: several intensity peaks are detected at S/N>3. They suggest the presence of gas clumps or satellite galaxies (undetected in the HST and MUSE data) residing in the IGrM (see discussion in Sect. sec:621 ). Radial \ surface brightness profile In order to highlight the extended nature of the \ emission around and in between the galaxy group members, we computed azimuthally averaged radial surface brightness profiles of the \ and continuum emission centered on galaxy A (Fig. fig:sbprof_mgii , first panel). The \ profile (blue) is measured on the unsmoothed \ NB image (Sect. sec:41 ) after masking pixels located outside an ellipse corresponding to the 1 significance level in order to increase the S/N in the two-pixel-wide annuli (or truncated annuli) used for the aperture photometry (see inset panels). The width of the annuli (0.4") is comparable to the spatial resolution element of the data (PSF FWHM of 0.52"). The \ continuum radial SB profile (black) is measured from a continuum image extracted from the continuum-only cube within a window of 20 000 around the \ doublet. This broad spectral window does not include strong absorption or emission lines and ensures a good enough S/N. The neighboring sources were rigorously masked in order to avoid contamination and the same elliptical mask used on the \ image was applied to compute the continuum SB profile. Errors were measured in each (truncated) annulus using the estimated variance from the MUSE data cube. To aid the visual comparison, the continuum profile has been rescaled to the \ SB at the position of galaxy C which is where the brightest \ intensity peak is observed. Galaxy C is unlikely to be affected by \ absorption as its measured \ line ratio 2796 / 2803 is very close to two, which is the expected value for optically thin emission (see Appendix ap:3 ). Moreover, no fluorescent \ lines are significantly detected for galaxy C which is in good agreement with no infilling effect and therefore no absorption (see Appendix ap:3 , Mauerhofer21 ). The \ SB profile appears more extended than the continuum, especially in between the galaxies [ A,B,C ] and [ D,E ] - radial positions are indicated by vertical dashed lines -- where a low SB projected "bridge" at 2 10 ^ 19 level can be identified. In order to differentiate between the extended emission around the galaxy subgroup [ A,B,C ] and in between the galaxy subgroups [ A,B,C ] and [ D,E ] , we compute the radial SB profiles above and below the [ A,B,C ] axis (see insets for illustration) in the middle and right panels of Fig. fig:sbprof_mgii , respectively. The \ emission appears more extended than the rescaled continuum in both directions (above and below the [ A,B,C ] axis), confirming the detection of diffuse \ emission around the group members as well as a low SB \ bridge ( 2 10 ^ 19 ) detected with more than 1.5 significance spanning 50 kpc in projection between the galaxy subgroups [ A,B,C ] and [ D,E ] (see Fig. fig:nb_mgii a). We go further into the analysis of the \ nebula in the next section by looking at the spatial variation of the \ spectral properties. Spatially resolved \ properties The kinematics of the gas connecting the galaxy group can provide crucial information to understand its nature and origin. In order to reveal the spatial variations of the \ spectral profile within the nebula, we build a 2D binned map using the weighted Voronoi tesselation method C03,Diehl&Statler06 . This method allows us to increase the S/N in the surroundings of the galaxies where the surface brightness is the lowest. The binning is performed on the \ NB image constructed from the MXDF datacube (see Sect. sec:41 ) for which the pixels with S/N < 1.5 (corresponding to the outer SB contour in Fig. fig:nb_mgii a) have been masked in order to remove the noise-dominated pixels. The resulting binned \ map consists of 20 bins with S/N > 3 and probes the detected \ nebula above 2 10 ^ 19 . The spectral extraction is performed from the MUSE continuum-subtracted cube smoothed with a 0.6 \ FWHM Gaussian in order to increase the S/N while keeping a good spatial resolution (see Appendix ap:1 ). We extract the \ doublet in each resulting bin and measure the properties of the emission lines by modelling the doublet as a sum of two Gaussian profiles with fixed peak separation. The errors on the line parameters (peak position and FWHM) were estimated using bootstrapping: for each segmented area, we generated 1000 realizations of the extracted line where each pixel is randomly drawn from a normal distribution centered on the original pixel value and with standard deviation derived from the estimated noise value. The noise of each extracted spectrum corresponds to the standard deviation of the data measured in two 200 wide spectral windows located on each side of the \ doublet. We note that because of smoothing and because of the PSF the line parameters between neighboring pixels are somewhat correlated. However, spatial variations are still visible. Figure fig:velmap_mgii (top left panel) shows the resolved map of the \ 2796 peak position relative to the systemic redshift of galaxy A. The observed \ velocity gradient suggests an overall rotation of the structure along the major axis of galaxy A consistent with an extension of the ISM rotation (as probed by the \ emission, see Sect. sec:51 ) at the CGM scale within which the satellite galaxies B and C are embedded. This ISM/CGM co-rotation is also observed in simulations and observations (e.g. S19,Zabl19,Ho20,Zabl21 ). The top right panels of Fig. fig:velmap_mgii show the unsmoothed \ doublet extracted in the elliptical areas traced in purple and designated by the same number on the map. These apertures are independent of the Voronoi bins and aim to show the \ line profile in some diffuse regions of the nebula. The velocity variations are small (< 50 ) in the diffuse regions. The kinematics of the nebula appear to be overall shifted towards higher velocities (by + 50 km s ^ 1 on average) compared to the \ emission (bottom left panel, see Sect. sec:51 ). The highest velocity variations are observed along the minor axis of galaxy A and reach +120 and 80 with respect to the systemic redshift of galaxy A. As discussed in Sect. sec:621 ), this supports the presence of an outflow emerging from galaxy A. 0.29 0.71 0.29 0.71 At the redshift of the group, MUSE covers the non-resonant collisionally excited \ doublet and the fluorescent \ lines. The corresponding NB images and radial SB profiles are shown in Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb and are created following the same procedure as for the \ emission (Sects. sec:41 and sec:43 , respectively). A low surface brightness \ nebula The \ NB map (Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb , top left) is obtained by summing the continuum-subtracted cube in the wavelength range [ 8590, 8605 ] ( 500 ). In order to improve the S/N, the image was smoothed using a 0 6 FWHM Gaussian function. The white contours correspond to the 1.5, 2, and 3 significance levels with 1 corresponding to 2.5 10^ 19 . The total \ flux and luminosity within the 2 significance level contour (dashed white line) are 5.95 0.07 10^ 17 \ and 6.36 0.07 10^ 41 erg s ^ 1 , respectively. The \ azimuthally averaged radial SB profiles are shown in the lower left panels of Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb . The profiles are constructed following the same procedure as for \ (see Sect. sec:43 ) with an additional step for the construction of the continuum image where the cube slices within 20 000 km s ^ 1 polluted by skylines have been masked. This is particularly important because at the redshift of the nebula, the \ line falls next to many sky lines. The \ doublet is itself contaminated by a skyline (see vertical grey line in the lower right panels of Fig. fig:velmap_mgii ) preventing us from in-depth analysis. When comparing the \ and rescaled continuum radial SB profiles in Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb , only one data point is above the rescaled continuum profile with more than 2 significance. The inner profile has the same shape as the continuum one implying that the \ emission is not significantly more extended than the continuum inside a radius of 2 \ from galaxy A. Extended \ emission at the same position as the \ bridge, i.e. in between the galaxies [ A,B,C ] and [ D,E ] , is detected with 2 significance level in the \ NB image (Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb , top left). However, due to averaging effects, the \ bridge is less significant on the radial profiles computed below the [ A,B,C ] axis, i.e. in the direction of the [ D,E ] subgroup (first column third row of Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb ). The radial SB profile computed above the galaxies A, B and C (bottom left panel) indicates extended \ emission at radii > 2.5 \ from galaxy A, suggesting the presence of an ionized \ halo around this galaxy subgroup. Comparisons of the \ and \ spatial extent and radial SB profiles are presented in Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb (top two right panels). Since the MUSE PSF is narrower at the \ wavelength than at the \ wavelength, we degraded the \ NB image by convolving it by a Moffat function whose parameters ( a=0.22 and b=2.87 , see B17 ) have been empirically determined so that the \ and \ images have the same PSF. While the \ and \ spatial distributions are consistent, the \ radial SB profiles (azimuthally averaged and in directions, bottom right panels) are all flatter than the \ profiles. This can be attributed to the resonant nature of the \ transition (see Sect. sec:622 ). The binned line-of-sight velocity map of the \ emission is shown in the bottom left panel of Fig. fig:velmap_mgii . The map is constructed following the same procedure as for \ (Sect. sec:44 ) and consists of more bins (57) because of the higher S/N of the \ emission near the galaxies. At the position of the galaxies, the observed spatial variations of the \ peak positions are consistent with their redshift relative to galaxy A (right panel of Fig. fig:group_presentation ). The lower right panels of Fig. fig:velmap_mgii show the unsmoothed \ lines extracted in the same elliptical apertures as for \ (top panels). Interestingly, in region 4 the \ emission is stronger than . This can be explained by the resonant nature of the \ transition (see Sect. sec:622 ) resulting in \ being more extended that . The existence of the \ bridge is reinforced by the detection of the \ doublet in this area (region 3 in Fig. fig:velmap_mgii ). 0.94 Extended \ emission The \ NB image (top middle panel of Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb ) is obtained by summing the 2365, 2396, 2612 and 2626 NB images, of which the observed spectral windows are [ 5448.75, 5456.25 ] , [ 5522.5, 5530.0 ] , [ 6021.25, 6027.5 ] and [ 6041.25, 6066.25 ] , respectively. The total \ flux and luminosity within the significance level contour of 2 is 5.3 0.5 10^ 18 \ and 5.7 0.6 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 , respectively. Given that the \ and \ lines are close in wavelength, we consider the same stellar continuum (see Sect. sec:41 ). Therefore, the radial SB profiles of the continuum (black profile in the middle bottom panels) are the same as for . The comparison between the \ emission and the rescaled continuum radial SB profile reveals that the \ emission is more extended than the continuum (see central panel of Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb ) second row. The \ extended emission appears more significant in the direction of the galaxies D and E (compare the central panels of the Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb third and fourth rows). This is confirmed in the NB image where the \ emission indeed extends along the minor axis of galaxy A and towards galaxy D. We discuss the origin for the extended nature of the \ emission in Sect. sec:62 . Contrary to \ and \ emission, the \ emission does not surround the five-galaxy group but is rather centered on the most massive galaxy A (see the , \ and \ contours and radial SB profiles comparison in the last column of Fig. fig:oiifeii_nbsb ). Similarly, using MUSE found the \ emission of a z=1.29 galaxy to be more extended than the stellar continuum and aligned with the minor axis of the galaxy. The velocity gradient and shape of the \ emission suggest the presence of a conical outflow. The faintness of the \ emission of galaxy A hampers a kinematic analysis. Existence of an Mg-enriched intragroup medium Historically the existence of an intragroup medium enriched in magnesium has been suggested by absorption studies in order to explain the fact that, while the strong \ absorbers (rest frame equivalent width EW>0.8 ) are generally associated to a single galaxy (within 100 kpc, e.g. Bouche07, Bouche12, Ho17, Schroetter16, Schroetter19, Zabl19, Lundgren21 ), sometimes weaker \ absorbers (EW < 1 , e.g. Muzahid18,Du20 ) or \ absorbers (e.g. P17,Ra18 ) can be matched to groups of multiple galaxies ( < 500 kpc and v < 500 ), in agreement with clustering studies Bouche06,Lundgren09,Gauthier09 . Whether observed absorption features (with EW < 1 ) imprinted in the spectrum of a background source are tracing gas coupled to the group or circumgalactic gas of an individual galaxy is still a matter of debate. Indeed, absorption studies reported contradictory results, some suggesting that \ absorbing systems were associated with the CGM of the individual group members (e.g. B11,Fo19 ) and others to a widespread IGrM (e.g. Bi17, P17, N18 ) originating from a mixture of previous tidal interactions between group members and outflowing winds. Disentangling the two is very challenging, especially when there is only one-dimensional information for a given system. Our discovery of the first extended \ nebula surrounding and connecting the members of a 5-galaxy group provides a panoramic view of the neutral enriched gas residing in between galaxies. This group is rather small and compact (see Sect. sec:31 ), meaning that it is likely an interacting system where the CGM of individual galaxies are mixed. This idea is reinforced by the fact that the detected \ nebula resides inside the virial radius of galaxy A estimated at 100 kpc. Moreover, the detection of a gaseous bridge in between the two subgroups [ A,B,C ] and [ D,E ] suggests that we are observing a widespread low surface brightness structure embracing the five galaxies, also called intragroup medium. We note that there is no precise definition of the IGrM in the literature. Our analysis brings the first observational evidence for a low SB diffuse component of neutral gas residing within a galaxy group at z>1 that we define as the intragroup medium. The detection of \ absorption in the spectrum of the background source BKG (see Sect. sec:42 ) indicates that the IGrM is actually even more extended than the detected nebula. Our EW measurement of the \ absorption (Sect. sec:42 ) is in good agreement with the EW versus impact parameter relation established by quasar absorption line studies (e.g. Lundgren21 ). This result highlights the complementarity of the absorption and emission methods to study the gas around galaxies. Origin of the intragroup nebula In this section we discuss two points: the presence of enriched gas beyond the galaxies and the mechanisms that make it shine. Why is there enriched gas so far from the stars ? Several scenarios have been suggested to explain the presence of magnesium-enriched gas in between the galaxies of a group. Magnesium atoms are elements released in the ISM and CGM by core collapse supernovae. One can therefore naturally imagine that the magnesium gas residing outside galaxies has been ejected through strong supernovae-driven outflows. The P-Cygni shape of the \ doublet in the integrated spectrum of Galaxy A (see Fig. fig:integ_sp top) is in good agreement with this scenario. Indeed, according to radiative transfer (RT) models, this line profile is a signature of resonant scattering in an optically thick outflowing medium where the redshifted emission corresponds to back-scattered photons reflected by the receding medium H00,D06,V06,K10 . The \ lines of galaxies D and E seem to be redshifted compared to their systemic redshifts (Fig. fig:integ_sp ), however the continuum is too faint to detect significant absorption lines and therefore P-Cygni profiles, i.e. the presence of outflows for those galaxies.

gmw_00009784.name: BZQJ1148+5254
gmw_00009784.overview: BZQJ1148+5254 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of z=1.632 [arXiv:1004.0239]. Its 9000-day Asiago light curve reveals a monotonic fading trend of approximately 1.8 magnitudes, with no definite shorter-term variability detected [arXiv:1004.0239].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BZQJ1148+5254 as a z=1.632 flat-spectrum radio quasar and measures a roughly 9000-day Asiago light curve with a monotonic fading trend.

quotes.quote: BZQJ1148+5254 The spectrum of this source in the SDSS shows strong emission lines and a redshift z=1.632: it is therefore classified as a Flat Spectrum Radio Quasar in the Roma BZCat. The image is point-like in the SDSS, as expected for a high-redshift AGN.

The Asiago light curve covers about 9000 days and shows a monotonic decreasing trend of about 1.8 mag (0.073 mag/year) with a slope quite similar to that of 1058+56. The data are scattered around this trend, with no definite shorter time-scales. The photometric accuracy is however lower than in the case of 1058+56, due to the lower flux level of this source. As expected from the visual inspection, the DFT analysis did not show any remarkable frequency in the data besides the seasonal one year time scale of the observations.

gmw_00009785.name: BZBJ1209+4119
gmw_00009785.overview: BZBJ1209+4119 is a BL Lacertae object located at a redshift of z=0.377 [arXiv:1004.0239]. It exhibits a point-like image and real long-term optical variations, including a slow brightening trend and a major dimming episode [arXiv:1004.0239].

sources.summary: The paper classifies BZBJ1209+4119 as a z=0.377 BL Lac and finds a real long-term optical variation, including slow brightening and a strong dimming episode.

quotes.quote: BZBJ1209+4119 The source has an SDSS nearly featureless spectrum, and can be classified therefore as a BL Lac. In the SDSS database it is given a redshift z=0.377 and the image is point-like.

The Asiago light curve covers about 9500 days. The source is rather faint for the 67/92 Schmidt so the photometric accuracy is worse than for the other two sources. In this case a slow brightening trend (0.04 mag/year) seems to be present, with a large dimming episode around JD 45000. The rms deviations of the comparison stars of comparable faintness (S,G,T,Q, see Table 3) from their average values are anyway much smaller than that of the AGN, so that at least in a statistical sense the overall source variation is real.

gmw_00009786.name: J2
gmw_00009786.overview: J2 is an extremely compact emission line galaxy with a physical size of about 1.7 kpc [arXiv:1412.7909]. It is characterized as a young, low-mass starburst galaxy with strong emission lines [arXiv:1412.7909].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J2 as an extremely compact EELG with EW above 3000, multi-band confirmation, strong line boosts, and properties consistent with young low-mass starbursts.

quotes.quote: There are two EELGs (J2 and J29) among the 40 EELGs showing EWs above 3000 , which are 3737 726 and 3332 439 . As seen in Figure highew , both of these two EELGs are extremely compact. The FWHM of the instrument point-spread function (PSF) is 0 14 for the J_ 125 . hence the EELGs are all resolved. After subtracting the instrument PSF and the lensing effect, the measured sizes correspond to physical sizes of about 1.7 kpc and 0.9 kpc.

As the spectra in Figure highew and the results in Table table1 , the large EWs are confirmed with three bands for J2 candidate and two bands for J29 candidate. Both the two EELG candidates show similar spectral shapes, moreover, the significant boosts in Z_ 850 and Y_ 105 due to \ line are also in agreement with the strong [ O III ] 5007 \ line. The EW of Y9 candidate can only be estimate with the boost of Y_ 105 , therefore, the noise fluctuation cannot be totally excluded and the strength could be confirmed with further observations. VDW11 found these candidates are low-mass ( 10^8 M_ ) galaxies, starbursting ( 5 M_ \ yr^ 1 ), young ( 5 40 \ Myr ) galaxies.

gmw_00009788.name: Y0
gmw_00009788.overview: Y0 is a galaxy located in the Abell 209 cluster field [arXiv:1412.7909]. Although initially identified as a high-photometric-redshift candidate [arXiv:1412.7909], its observed spectral energy distribution is better explained by a strong emission-line model [arXiv:1412.7909].

sources.summary: The paper treats Y0 as a high-photometric-redshift candidate whose colors can instead be explained by a strong emission-line model favored over the high-redshift fit.

quotes.quote: Another galaxy in Abell 209 (RA: 22.954264, DEC: -13.611176), which is removed out from the Y sample because the I_ 814 band detection is below 3 , is also included for its high photometric redshift (5.89) from BPZ and is called Y0. These candidates have similar spectra shapes as shown with solid black circles in Figure highz . The best BPZ results are shown with open orange boxes. We also fit the I_ 814 band and redward bands with our strong emission line model which is shown with blue boxes.

While the high redshift assumption often fails to fit the H_ 160 band, our emiison line model can explain the drop of flux in H_ 160 bands in three galaxies. The emission line model also overestimate the flux in the Y_ 814 band. The ^2 values are shown with the same colors in the figure. For all the three candidates, the spectra favor the emission line models for the slightly lower ^2 values.

sources.summary: The paper links Y8 to a z~6 catalog candidate but argues its photometry is better fit by a strong emission-line model than by the high-redshift assumption.

quotes.quote: Two EELGs, Y8 and Y9, are also selected as m1115--0352 (z=6.2) and m1720--1114 (z=5.9) in the z 6 sample of B13. Another galaxy in Abell 209 (RA: 22.954264, DEC: -13.611176), which is removed out from the Y sample because the I_ 814 band detection is below 3 , is also included for its high photometric redshift (5.89) from BPZ and is called Y0. These candidates have similar spectra shapes as shown with solid black circles in Figure highz . The best BPZ results are shown with open orange boxes. We also fit the I_ 814 band and redward bands with our strong emission line model which is shown with blue boxes.

While the high redshift assumption often fails to fit the H_ 160 band, our emiison line model can explain the drop of flux in H_ 160 bands in three galaxies. The emission line model also overestimate the flux in the Y_ 814 band. The ^2 values are shown with the same colors in the figure. For all the three candidates, the spectra favor the emission line models for the slightly lower ^2 values.

sources.summary: The paper links Y9 to a z~6 catalog candidate but argues its photometry is better fit by a strong emission-line model; its EW estimate is high but uncertain.

gmw_00009791.name: ID 1
gmw_00009791.overview: ID 1 is a high-redshift galaxy that exhibits extended carbon emission and a more compact continuum emitting region [arXiv:1810.01640]. This system shows signs of disruption, including a velocity offset in its emission lines and an extended northwestern continuum structure that may indicate a gaseous outflow or a tidal tail from a galaxy interaction [arXiv:1810.01640].

sources.summary: The paper finds ID 1 has extended CI emission and off-galaxy continuum, suggesting disrupted gas, a tidal tail, or a gaseous outflow associated with nearby companions.

quotes.quote: ID 1 The first row of images in Fig. fig:mom0 contains apertures of varying sizes indicating the varying extent of continuum, \ and CO emission from source ID 1. There is strong \ emission in a broad line extended over an area larger than the optical extent of the galaxy, while the CO and especially the continuum emitting regions are more compact. The central continuum peak is in fact only at the 1- level, but much stronger continuum emission is apparent in an extended structure to the NW. This is unlikely to be a noise peak, since it is extended and has a peak S/N =3.2 and an integrated S/N >5.0 .

If either of these has been involved in an interaction with ID 1 it is possible that some ISM has been stripped in a tidal tail, which would not be visible in the stellar emission seen in the optical. Further evidence that this system is not completely relaxed can be seen in the fact that the \ line centre is offset from the CO and from the optical grism redshift by V_p 390 in the rest frame ( z=0.0026 ). In addition, the extended nature of the \ emission in comparison with the optical size of the galaxy may indicate a gaseous outflow.

gmw_00009792.name: ID 2
gmw_00009792.overview: ID 2 is a compact galaxy at a redshift of z=1.09 [arXiv:1810.01640]. The bulk of its emission, including the continuum and CO emission, is concentrated in a compact core, though a weak southern extension is also visible [arXiv:1810.01640].

sources.summary: The paper describes ID 2 as compact, with CI, continuum, and CO concentrated in a core plus weak southern CI extension and unusual double-peaked velocity structure.

quotes.quote: ID 2 Due to the compactness of this source, the area of the images in Fig. fig:mom0 is 6 6 arcsec ^2 for ID 2, compared with 12 12 arcsec ^2 for the other sources. The bulk of the \ emission and all of the continuum and CO emission are located in a compact core. A 2-dimensional Gaussian fit to the continuum image has a beam-deconvolved FWHM of 0.37 0.22 arcsec, equivalent to 3.0 1.8 kpc at z=1.09 . However, we have allowed a larger aperture for this source, since there is weak \ emission extending to the south (comprising around 20 per cent of the total measured flux), consistent with a tail seen in the optical image.

Furthermore, the \ velocity structure is strongly double-peaked, which may be due to two distinct sources separated by V_p 400 , or possibly a rotating disk-like structure which is either optically thick or deficient in the centre. Conversely, the CO emission is very weakly detected, with a central velocity 156100 close to that of one of the \ peaks, although it is unclear whether there is also weaker CO emission at 156300 (close to the second \ peak). The CO deficiency may be related to optical thickness or low gas excitation, and this will be explored in Section sec:co_ci .

gmw_00009793.name: ID 5
gmw_00009793.overview: The high-redshift galaxy ID 5 is weakly detected in carbon monoxide emission [arXiv:1810.01640]. It exhibits a spatial mismatch between its gas and continuum emitting regions, which may indicate kinematic disruption from a neighboring galaxy at a similar redshift [arXiv:1810.01640].

sources.summary: The paper reports weak CI detection in ID 5, mismatched gas and continuum regions, and possible kinematic disruption near a same-redshift neighboring galaxy.

quotes.quote: ID 5 Source ID 5 is only weakly detected in \ but we note that there appears to be a mismatch between the , \ and continuum emitting regions, similar to what was seen in ID 1. This may suggest some kinematic disruption, perhaps related to the proximity of a neighbouring galaxy at a similar redshift (CID 14680), but without deeper imaging we cannot be certain of this. We have therefore simply chosen an aperture similar in size and shape to the optical emission, which encloses the continuum and \ emission.

gmw_00009794.name: ID 7
gmw_00009794.overview: ID 7 is a high-redshift galaxy located close to a much bluer, less massive neighbor galaxy [arXiv:1810.01640]. The disturbed morphology of this neighboring galaxy suggests that the pair may be interacting [arXiv:1810.01640].

sources.summary: The paper notes ID 7 lies beside a much bluer, less massive disturbed neighbor, defines an aperture around the target, and lacks CO coverage at its redshift.

quotes.quote: ID 7 We note that this galaxy is very close to a neighbouring galaxy at a similar redshift (CID 15107, most easily seen in Fig. fig:rgb to the left of the central target). This galaxy is much bluer and a factor 300 less massive than our target according to the 3DHST catalogue, and its disturbed morphology indicates that the pair may be interacting. The aperture we have defined encloses only the target, but the neighbour has no detectable \ flux (either due to its redshift or its low mass) while its continuum flux is roughly 25 per cent that of ID 7. We also note that ID 7 has no CO data because it is at a redshift where CO is unobservable with ALMA.

gmw_00009795.name: ID 9
gmw_00009795.overview: ID 9 is a high-redshift galaxy [arXiv:1810.01640] that exhibits an extended spatial configuration [arXiv:1810.01640]. Its aperture spectrum reveals a broad, skewed carbon monoxide emission line profile [arXiv:1810.01640].

sources.summary: The paper combines ALMA cycles for ID 9, matches apertures to its extended optical size, and finds broad, skewed CI emission with low but significant detection.

quotes.quote: ID 9 As described in Section sec:imaging , the continuum image for ID 9 is the result of combining data taken in Cycles 4 and 5, in order to maximise signal-to-noise, although we note that consistent fluxes were measured in each of the individual data sets. We have imaged the continuum and emission lines with a beam size of 1.1 1.1 arcsec due to the extended nature of the source, and we have used an aperture matched to the optical size of the galaxy. The full \ line in the aperture spectrum in Fig. fig:mom0 has a broad and skewed profile, with a peak at around 158220 , and weaker but significant emission extending to 158050 \ (hence the central velocity is 158140 ).

The spectrum in the central beam peaks at 158080 , as shown in Fig. fig:spectra . The profile of the CO aperture spectrum is more closely approximated by a narrow Gaussian centred at 158140 . The broad line-width and extended spatial configuration of \ leads to a low signal-to-noise image, but the line is nevertheless detected at S/N 2.5 as shown in Table tab:almaresults .

gmw_00009796.name: NVSS J095643-095544
gmw_00009796.overview: NVSS J095643-095544 is a merging system composed of two disk galaxies [arXiv:0811.3890]. This system exhibits low surface brightness tidal features, strong far-infrared emission, and is a strong radio source [arXiv:0811.3890].

sources.summary: The paper describes the brightest 24 micron galaxy as a merging disk-galaxy system with tidal features, far-infrared brightness, and resolved radio emission associated with NVSS J095643-095544.

quotes.quote: The brightest 24 galaxy is a system of two merging disk galaxies with a total magnitude of R 16 . The Northern system (44635) has a very blue SED (U-V)_ rest =0.14 and implies a very strong H line given its elevated R band flux (see Fig. SED_merger ). The Southern system (45154) has an extremely red SED (U-V)_ rest =1.97 and implies strong dust-reddening. Their reshifts are estimated as z_ phot =0.084 and 0.053 , but the blue SED is better constrained by emission lines.

The 20 ks R band image of COMBO-17 shows tidal features with very low surface brightness (Fig. Im_merger ). The arm that reaches once around the entire galaxy has 5000 lower surface brightness than the main disks of the two merging galaxies. The system is also a strong radio source (NVSS J095643-095544) and was seen by IRAS. In our Spitzer MIPS images it shows 50 mJy of flux, but such bright FIR measurements are missing from our matched catalogue due to matching difficulties.

gmw_00009797.name: Object 41435
gmw_00009797.overview: Object 41435 is a massive red-sequence elliptical galaxy that is likely a member of the A901a cluster [arXiv:0811.3890]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus and is the brightest X-ray and radio source in the STAGES field [arXiv:0811.3890].

sources.summary: Object 41435 is described as a massive red-sequence elliptical, likely an A901a cluster member, with AGN light plus the brightest X-ray and radio emission in STAGES.

quotes.quote: Object 41435 is a massive red-sequence elliptical with excess blue light in its SED see Fig. 7 of gilmour2007 that has biased the redshift estimation. While it has z_ phot 0.33 , it is almost certainly a cluster member and a z=0.16 template fitted by hand works well and leaves over some room for AGN light. It is the brightest X-ray source in the STAGES field observed by XMM and a point source with a luminosity (assuming z=0.16 ) of L_X=1.55 10^ 44 erg/s. It is also the brightest radio source at 1280 MHz and is unresolved with total flux 46.33 0.01 mJy/bm. At 610 MHz it is partially resolved with an integrated flux density of 171.3 0.1 mJy.

gmw_00009798.name: Object 14049
gmw_00009798.overview: Object 14049 is an S0 cluster galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of 0.168 [arXiv:0811.3890]. The galaxy displays a full optical Einstein ring, which is produced by a lensed background source at a redshift of 1.5 [arXiv:0811.3890].

sources.summary: Object 14049 is identified as an S0 cluster galaxy whose photometry is contaminated by a full optical Einstein ring from a lensed background source at redshift 1.5.

quotes.quote: Object 14049 (Fig. Im_3 , left) is an S0 galaxy displaying a full optical Einstein ring. It has z_ phot =0.23 , but 2dF spectroscopy confirms it is a cluster member with z_ spec =0.168 , implying that the SED is contaminated by light from the lensed galaxy. Subsequent targetted spectroscopy revealed a source redshift z_s=1.5 (Arag o n-Salamanca et al, in prep.).

gmw_00009799.name: STAGES I
gmw_00009799.overview: STAGES I is a nearby dwarf irregular galaxy with a blue, irregular morphology [arXiv:0811.3890]. It has a low stellar mass, a small Sersic index of 0.55, and contains resolved point sources that suggest it is located at a redshift of less than 0.01 [arXiv:0811.3890].

sources.summary: STAGES I is presented as a nearby dwarf irregular galaxy with resolved point sources, low inferred redshift, low stellar mass, a small Sersic index, and blue irregular morphology.

quotes.quote: The object with the COMBO-17 number 59586 is a nearby dwarf irregular galaxy (see Fig. Im_3 , right and Fig. SED_dSph ) estimated at z_ phot = 0.044 0.026 (consistent with z=0 at 1.6 ). At the estimated redshift it would have M_V 16.7 and M_*/M_ 8.7 ; however given the brightness of the resolved point sources it is most likely at z<0.01 . It has a \ index of n = 0.55 and shows clear signs of irregularity besides a blue colour.

gmw_00009800.name: object 54511
gmw_00009800.overview: Object 54511 is a galaxy classified as strange due to its template fit [arXiv:0811.3890]. It exhibits extremely strong emission lines [arXiv:0811.3890], which constrain its redshift to a range between 0.27 and 0.32 [arXiv:0811.3890].

sources.summary: Object 54511 is singled out as the only strange COMBO-17 galaxy, with extremely strong emission lines and a line-constrained redshift around 0.27 to 0.32.

quotes.quote: The COMBO-17 catalogue contains only one object classified as `strange' as a result of having a ^2_ red >30 for its best template fit, while having good flags: object 54511 is a galaxy with extremely strong emission lines and R 22.5 . The emission-line flux in the R band and the 646-band both suggest EW 150 nm, which would need to be the combined H and OIII lines. A line in the filter 485/30 shows EW 14 nm and is possibly OII. The redshift of the object appears to be constrained to 0.27<z_ lines <0.32 by a third line signal in the filter 855 (H ; see Fig. SED_StrObj ).

gmw_00009801.name: Mrk 32
gmw_00009801.overview: Mrk 32 is a faint galaxy classified as an Im/BCD [arXiv:1004.2858]. Its starburst activity is resolved into three smaller star-forming regions aligned along a north-south axis, and its velocity field is flat [arXiv:1004.2858].

sources.summary: Mrk 32 is described as a faint Im/BCD whose PMAS maps resolve multiple star-forming regions, compare continuum and emission morphology, and measure spectral and abundance properties.

quotes.quote: Mrk 32 (= UGCA 211, SBS 1023+565) With M_ B =-14.99 , Mrk 32 is one of the faintest galaxies in our sample. In the Atlas it is classified as an Im/BCD. Broad-band imaging and optical spectroscopy were published in and , respectively. The PMAS FOV covers an area of 1.27 1.27 kpc with a sampling of 80 pc per spaxel.

In Mrk 32 the starburst is resolved into three smaller SF regions, aligned along a north-south axis, while in Tololo 1434+032 the current star formation activity spreads all over the mapped region. In general the different excitation maps for a same object display the same pattern and trace the star-forming regions, as expected for objects ionized by hot stars; only the outer regions of Mrk 32, I Zw 123 and I Zw 159 display higher [ S ii ] / \ values, suggestive of shocks. Regarding the kinematics, Mrk 750, Mrk 206 and I Zw 159 display a clear rotation pattern, while in Mrk 32, Mrk 475 and I Zw 123 the velocity fields are flat.
